/home/xshadow/panoptic-v1-core-private/contracts/CollateralTracker.sol 1 | | // SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1 2 | | pragma solidity ^0.8.18; 3 | | 4 | | // Interfaces 5 | | import {PanopticPool} from "./PanopticPool.sol"; 6 | | // Inherited implementations 7 | | import {ERC20Minimal} from "@tokens/ERC20Minimal.sol"; 8 | * | import {Multicall} from "@base/Multicall.sol"; 9 | | // Libraries 10 | | import {Constants} from "@libraries/Constants.sol"; 11 | | import {Errors} from "@libraries/Errors.sol"; 12 | * | import {InteractionHelper} from "@libraries/InteractionHelper.sol"; 13 | | import {Math} from "@libraries/Math.sol"; 14 | * | import {PanopticMath} from "@libraries/PanopticMath.sol"; 15 | * | import {SafeTransferLib} from "@libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol"; 16 | | // Custom types 17 | | import {LeftRightUnsigned, LeftRightSigned} from "@types/LeftRight.sol"; 18 | | import {LiquidityChunk} from "@types/LiquidityChunk.sol"; 19 | | import {TokenId} from "@types/TokenId.sol"; 20 | | 21 | | /// @title Collateral Tracking System / Margin Accounting used in conjunction with a Panoptic Pool. 22 | | /// @author Axicon Labs Limited 23 | | // 24 | | /// @notice Tracks collateral of users which is key to ensure the correct level of collateralization is achieved. 25 | | /// This is represented as an ERC20 share token. A Panoptic pool has 2 tokens, each issued by its own instance of a CollateralTracker. 26 | | /// All math within this contract pertains to a single token. 27 | | // 28 | * | /// @notice This contract uses the ERC4626 standard allowing the minting and burning of "shares" (represented using ERC20 inheritance) in exchange for underlying "assets". 29 | * | /// Panoptic uses a collateral tracking system that is similar to TradFi margin accounts. While users can borrow and 30 | * | /// effectively control funds several times larger than the collateral they deposited, they cannot withdraw those funds 31 | | /// from the Panoptic-Uniswap ecosystem. All funds are always owned by the Panoptic protocol, but users will: 32 | | // 33 | | /// @notice 1) collect any fees generated by selling an option. 34 | | // 35 | | /// @notice 2) get any gain in capital that results from buying an option that becomes in-the-money. 36 | * | contract CollateralTracker is ERC20Minimal, Multicall { 37 | | using Math for uint256; 38 | | 39 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 40 | | EVENTS 41 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 42 | | 43 | | /// @notice Emitted when assets are deposited into the Collateral Tracker. 44 | | /// @param sender The address of the caller (and depositor) 45 | | /// @param owner The address of the recipient of the newly minted shares 46 | | /// @param assets The amount of assets deposited by 'sender' in exchange for 'shares' 47 | | /// @param shares The amount of shares minted to 'owner' 48 | | event Deposit(address indexed sender, address indexed owner, uint256 assets, uint256 shares); 49 | | 50 | | /// @notice Emitted when assets are withdrawn from the Collateral Tracker. 51 | | /// @param sender The address of the caller 52 | | /// @param receiver The address of the recipient of the withdrawn assets 53 | | /// @param owner The address of the owner of the shares being burned 54 | | /// @param assets The amount of assets withdrawn to 'receiver' 55 | | /// @param shares The amount of shares burned by 'owner' in exchange for 'assets' 56 | | event Withdraw( 57 | | address indexed sender, 58 | | address indexed receiver, 59 | | address indexed owner, 60 | | uint256 assets, 61 | | uint256 shares 62 | | ); 63 | | 64 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 65 | | CONSTANTS 66 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 67 | | 68 | | /// @notice Prefix for the token symbol (i.e. poUSDC). 69 | | string internal constant TICKER_PREFIX = "po"; 70 | | 71 | | /// @notice Prefix for the token name (i.e POPT-V1 USDC LP on ETH/USDC 30bps). 72 | | string internal constant NAME_PREFIX = "POPT-V1"; 73 | | 74 | | /// @notice Decimals for computation (1 bps (basis point) precision: 0.01%). 75 | | /// @dev uint type for composability with unsigned integer based mathematical operations. 76 | * | uint256 internal constant DECIMALS = 10_000; 77 | | 78 | | /// @notice Decimals for computation (1 bps (basis point) precision: 0.01%). 79 | | /// @dev int type for composability with signed integer based mathematical operations. 80 | | int128 internal constant DECIMALS_128 = 10_000; 81 | | 82 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 83 | | UNISWAP POOL DATA 84 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 85 | | 86 | | /// @notice The address of underlying token0 or token1 from the Uniswap Pool. 87 | | /// @dev Whether this is token0 or token1 depends on which collateral token is being tracked in this CollateralTracker instance. 88 | | address internal s_underlyingToken; 89 | | 90 | | /// @notice Boolean which tracks whether this CollateralTracker has been initialized. 91 | | /// @dev As each instance is deployed via proxy clone, initial parameters must only be initalized once via startToken(). 92 | | bool internal s_initialized; 93 | | 94 | | /// @notice Stores address of token0 from the underlying Uniswap V3 pool. 95 | | address internal s_univ3token0; 96 | | 97 | | /// @notice Stores address of token1 from the underlying Uniswap V3 pool. 98 | | address internal s_univ3token1; 99 | | 100 | | /// @notice Store whether the current collateral token is token0 of the AMM (true) or token1 (false). 101 | | bool internal s_underlyingIsToken0; 102 | | 103 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 104 | | PANOPTIC POOL DATA 105 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 106 | | 107 | | /// @notice The Collateral Tracker keeps a reference to the Panoptic Pool using it. 108 | | PanopticPool internal s_panopticPool; 109 | | 110 | | /// @notice Cached amount of assets accounted to be held by the Panoptic Pool — ignores donations, pending fee payouts, and other untracked balance changes. 111 | | uint128 internal s_poolAssets; 112 | | 113 | | /// @notice Amount of assets moved from the Panoptic Pool to the AMM. 114 | | uint128 internal s_inAMM; 115 | | 116 | | /// @notice Additional risk premium charged on intrinsic value of ITM positions, 117 | * | /// defined in hundredths of basis points. 118 | | /// @dev The result of the calculation is stored instead of the multiple to save gas during usage. 119 | | /// When the fee is set, the multiple is calculated and stored. 120 | | uint128 internal s_ITMSpreadFee; 121 | | 122 | | /// @notice The fee of the Uniswap pool in hundredths of basis points. 123 | | uint24 internal s_poolFee; 124 | | 125 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 126 | | RISK PARAMETERS 127 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 128 | | 129 | | /// @notice The commission fee, in basis points, collected from PLPs at option mint. 130 | | /// @dev In Panoptic, options never expire, commissions are only paid when a new position is minted. 131 | | /// @dev We believe that this will eliminate the impact of the commission fee on the user's decision-making process when closing a position. 132 | | uint256 immutable COMMISSION_FEE; 133 | | 134 | | // base collateral ratios 135 | | 136 | * | /// @notice Required collateral ratios for buying, represented as percentage * 10_000. 137 | * | /// @dev i.e 20% -> 0.2 * 10_000 = 2_000. 138 | * | uint256 immutable SELLER_COLLATERAL_RATIO; 139 | | 140 | | /// @notice Required collateral ratios for selling, represented as percentage * 10_000. 141 | | /// @dev i.e 10% -> 0.1 * 10_000 = 1_000. 142 | | uint256 immutable BUYER_COLLATERAL_RATIO; 143 | | 144 | | // miscellaneous parameters 145 | | 146 | | /// @notice Basal cost (in bps of notional) to force exercise a position that is barely far-the-money (out-of-range). 147 | | int256 immutable FORCE_EXERCISE_COST; 148 | | 149 | | // Targets a pool utilization (balance between buying and selling) 150 | | /// @notice Target pool utilization below which buying+selling is optimal, represented as percentage * 10_000. 151 | | /// @dev i.e 50% -> 0.5 * 10_000 = 5_000. 152 | | uint256 immutable TARGET_POOL_UTIL; 153 | | 154 | | /// @notice Pool utilization above which selling is 100% collateral backed, represented as percentage * 10_000. 155 | | /// @dev i.e 90% -> 0.9 * 10_000 = 9_000. 156 | | uint256 immutable SATURATED_POOL_UTIL; 157 | | 158 | | /// @notice Multiplier, in basis points, to the pool fee that is charged on the intrinsic value of ITM positions. 159 | | /// @dev e.g. ITM_SPREAD_MULTIPLIER = 20_000, s_ITMSpreadFee = 2 * s_poolFee. 160 | | uint256 immutable ITM_SPREAD_MULTIPLIER; 161 | | 162 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 163 | | ACCESS CONTROL 164 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 165 | | 166 | | /// @notice Ensure that the associated Panoptic pool is the caller. Revert if not. 167 | | modifier onlyPanopticPool() { 168 | | if (msg.sender != address(s_panopticPool)) revert Errors.NotPanopticPool(); 169 | | _; 170 | | } 171 | | 172 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 173 | | INITIALIZATION & PARAMETER SETTINGS 174 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 175 | | 176 | | /// @notice Set immutable parameters for the Collateral Tracker. 177 | | /// @param _commissionFee The commission fee, in basis points, collected from PLPs at option mint. 178 | | /// @param _sellerCollateralRatio Required collateral ratios for buying, represented as percentage * 10_000. 179 | | /// @param _buyerCollateralRatio Required collateral ratios for selling, represented as percentage * 10_000. 180 | | /// @param _forceExerciseCost Basal cost (in bps of notional) to force exercise a position that is barely far-the-money (out-of-range). 181 | | /// @param _targetPoolUtilization Target pool utilization below which buying+selling is optimal, represented as percentage * 10_000. 182 | | /// @param _saturatedPoolUtilization Pool utilization above which selling is 100% collateral backed, represented as percentage * 10_000. 183 | | /// @param _ITMSpreadMultiplier Multiplier, in basis points, to the pool fee that is charged on the intrinsic value of ITM positions. 184 | | constructor( 185 | | uint256 _commissionFee, 186 | | uint256 _sellerCollateralRatio, 187 | | uint256 _buyerCollateralRatio, 188 | | int256 _forceExerciseCost, 189 | | uint256 _targetPoolUtilization, 190 | | uint256 _saturatedPoolUtilization, 191 | | uint256 _ITMSpreadMultiplier 192 | | ) { 193 | | COMMISSION_FEE = _commissionFee; 194 | | SELLER_COLLATERAL_RATIO = _sellerCollateralRatio; 195 | | BUYER_COLLATERAL_RATIO = _buyerCollateralRatio; 196 | | FORCE_EXERCISE_COST = _forceExerciseCost; 197 | | TARGET_POOL_UTIL = _targetPoolUtilization; 198 | | SATURATED_POOL_UTIL = _saturatedPoolUtilization; 199 | | ITM_SPREAD_MULTIPLIER = _ITMSpreadMultiplier; 200 | | } 201 | | 202 | | /// @notice Initialize a new collateral tracker for a specific token corresponding to the Panoptic Pool being created by the factory that called it. 203 | | /// @dev The factory calls this function to start a new collateral tracking system for the incoming token at 'underlyingToken'. 204 | | /// @dev The factory will do this for each of the two tokens being tracked. Thus, the collateral tracking system does not track *both* tokens at once. 205 | | /// @param underlyingIsToken0 whether this collateral tracker is for token0 (true) or token1 (false) 206 | | /// @param token0 token 0 of the Uniswap pool 207 | | /// @param token1 token 1 of the Uniswap pool 208 | | /// @param fee the fee of the Uniswap pool 209 | | /// @param panopticPool the address of the Panoptic Pool being created and linked to this Collateral Tracker 210 | | function startToken( 211 | | bool underlyingIsToken0, 212 | | address token0, 213 | | address token1, 214 | | uint24 fee, 215 | | PanopticPool panopticPool 216 | | ) external { 217 | | // fails if already initialized 218 | | if (s_initialized) revert Errors.CollateralTokenAlreadyInitialized(); 219 | | s_initialized = true; 220 | | 221 | | // these virtual shares function as a multiplier for the capital requirement to manipulate the pool price 222 | | // e.g if the virtual shares are 10**6, then the capital requirement to manipulate the price to 10**12 is 10**18 223 | | totalSupply = 10 ** 6; 224 | | 225 | | // set total assets to 1 226 | | // the initial share price is defined by 1/virtualShares 227 | | s_poolAssets = 1; 228 | | 229 | | // store the address of the underlying ERC20 token 230 | | s_underlyingToken = underlyingIsToken0 ? token0 : token1; 231 | | 232 | | // store the Panoptic pool for this collateral token 233 | | s_panopticPool = panopticPool; 234 | | 235 | | // cache the pool fee in hundredths of basis points 236 | | s_poolFee = fee; 237 | | 238 | | // Stores the addresses of the underlying tracked tokens. 239 | | s_univ3token0 = token0; 240 | | s_univ3token1 = token1; 241 | | 242 | | // store whether the current collateral token is token0 (true) or token1 (false; since there's always exactly two tokens it could be) 243 | | s_underlyingIsToken0 = underlyingIsToken0; 244 | | 245 | | // Additional risk premium charged on intrinsic value of ITM positions 246 | | unchecked { 247 | | s_ITMSpreadFee = uint128((ITM_SPREAD_MULTIPLIER * fee) / DECIMALS); 248 | | } 249 | | } 250 | | 251 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 252 | | COLLATERAL TOKEN INFORMATION 253 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 254 | | 255 | | /// @notice Get information about the utilization of this collateral vault. 256 | | /// @return poolAssets cached amount of assets accounted to be held by the Panoptic Pool - ignores donations, pending fee payouts, and other untracked balance changes 257 | | /// @return insideAMM the underlying token amount held in the AMM 258 | | /// @return currentPoolUtilization Packing of the pool utilization (how much funds are in the Panoptic pool versus the AMM pool at the time of minting), 259 | | /// right 64bits for token0 and left 64bits for token1, defined as (inAMM * 10_000) / totalAssets() 260 | | /// where totalAssets is the total tracked assets in the AMM and PanopticPool minus fees and donations to the Panoptic pool 261 | * | function getPoolData() 262 | | external 263 | | view 264 | * | returns (uint256 poolAssets, uint256 insideAMM, int256 currentPoolUtilization) 265 | | { 266 | * | poolAssets = s_poolAssets; 267 | * | insideAMM = s_inAMM; 268 | * | currentPoolUtilization = _poolUtilization(); 269 | | } 270 | | 271 | | /// @notice Returns name of token composed of underlying token symbol and pool data. 272 | | /// @return The name of the token 273 | | function name() external view returns (string memory) { 274 | | // this logic requires multiple external calls and error handling, so we do it in a delegatecall to a library to save bytecode size 275 | | return 276 | | InteractionHelper.computeName( 277 | | s_univ3token0, 278 | | s_univ3token1, 279 | | s_underlyingIsToken0, 280 | | s_poolFee, 281 | | NAME_PREFIX 282 | | ); 283 | | } 284 | | 285 | | /// @notice Returns symbol as prefixed symbol of underlying token. 286 | | /// @return The symbol of the token 287 | | function symbol() external view returns (string memory) { 288 | | // this logic requires multiple external calls and error handling, so we do it in a delegatecall to a library to save bytecode size 289 | | return InteractionHelper.computeSymbol(s_underlyingToken, TICKER_PREFIX); 290 | | } 291 | | 292 | | /// @notice Returns decimals of underlying token (0 if not present). 293 | | /// @return The decimals of the token 294 | | function decimals() external view returns (uint8) { 295 | | // this logic requires multiple external calls and error handling, so we do it in a delegatecall to a library to save bytecode size 296 | | return InteractionHelper.computeDecimals(s_underlyingToken); 297 | | } 298 | | 299 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 300 | | LIMITED TRANSFER FUNCTIONS 301 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 302 | | 303 | | /// @dev See {IERC20-transfer}. 304 | | /// Requirements: 305 | | /// - the caller must have a balance of at least 'amount'. 306 | | /// - the msg.sender must not have any position on the panoptic pool 307 | * | function transfer( 308 | | address recipient, 309 | | uint256 amount 310 | * | ) public override(ERC20Minimal) returns (bool) { 311 | | // make sure the caller does not have any open option positions 312 | | // if they do: we don't want them sending panoptic pool shares to others 313 | | // as this would reduce their amount of collateral against the opened positions 314 | | 315 | * | if (s_panopticPool.numberOfPositions(msg.sender) != 0) revert Errors.PositionCountNotZero(); 316 | | 317 | * | return ERC20Minimal.transfer(recipient, amount); 318 | | } 319 | | 320 | | /// @dev See {IERC20-transferFrom}. 321 | | /// Requirements: 322 | | /// - the 'from' must have a balance of at least 'amount'. 323 | | /// - the caller must have allowance for 'from' of at least 'amount' tokens. 324 | | /// - 'from' must not have any open positions on the panoptic pool. 325 | * | function transferFrom( 326 | | address from, 327 | | address to, 328 | | uint256 amount 329 | * | ) public override(ERC20Minimal) returns (bool) { 330 | | // make sure the caller does not have any open option positions 331 | | // if they do: we don't want them sending panoptic pool shares to others 332 | | // as this would reduce their amount of collateral against the opened positions 333 | | 334 | * | if (s_panopticPool.numberOfPositions(from) != 0) revert Errors.PositionCountNotZero(); 335 | | 336 | * | return ERC20Minimal.transferFrom(from, to, amount); 337 | | } 338 | | 339 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 340 | | STANDARD ERC4626 INTERFACE 341 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 342 | | 343 | | /// @notice Get the token contract address of the underlying asset being managed. 344 | | /// @return assetTokenAddress The address of the underlying asset 345 | * | function asset() external view returns (address assetTokenAddress) { 346 | * | return s_underlyingToken; 347 | | } 348 | | 349 | | /// @notice Get the total amount of assets managed by the CollateralTracker vault. 350 | | /// @dev This returns the total tracked assets in the AMM and PanopticPool, 351 | | /// @dev - EXCLUDING the amount of collected fees (because they are reserved for short options) 352 | | /// @dev - EXCLUDING any donations that have been made to the pool 353 | | /// @return totalManagedAssets The total amount of assets managed 354 | * | function totalAssets() public view returns (uint256 totalManagedAssets) { 355 | | unchecked { 356 | * | return s_poolAssets + s_inAMM; 357 | | } 358 | | } 359 | | 360 | | /// @notice Returns the amount of shares that can be minted for the given amount of assets. 361 | | /// @param assets The amount of assets to be deposited 362 | | /// @return shares The amount of shares that can be minted 363 | * | function convertToShares(uint256 assets) public view returns (uint256 shares) { 364 | * | return Math.mulDiv(assets, totalSupply, totalAssets()); 365 | | } 366 | | 367 | | /// @notice Returns the amount of assets that can be redeemed for the given amount of shares. 368 | | /// @param shares The amount of shares to be redeemed 369 | | /// @return assets The amount of assets that can be redeemed 370 | * | function convertToAssets(uint256 shares) public view returns (uint256 assets) { 371 | * | return Math.mulDiv(shares, totalAssets(), totalSupply); 372 | | } 373 | | 374 | | /// @notice Returns the maximum deposit amount. 375 | | /// @return maxAssets The maximum amount of assets that can be deposited 376 | | function maxDeposit(address) external pure returns (uint256 maxAssets) { 377 | | return type(uint104).max; 378 | | } 379 | | 380 | | /// @notice Returns shares received for depositing given amount of assets. 381 | | /// @param assets The amount of assets to be deposited 382 | | /// @return shares The amount of shares that can be minted 383 | * | function previewDeposit(uint256 assets) public view returns (uint256 shares) { 384 | | // compute the MEV tax, which is equal to a single payment of the commissionRate on the FINAL (post mev-tax) assets paid 385 | | unchecked { 386 | * | shares = Math.mulDiv( 387 | * | assets * (DECIMALS - COMMISSION_FEE), 388 | * | totalSupply, 389 | * | totalAssets() * DECIMALS 390 | | ); 391 | | } 392 | | } 393 | | 394 | | /// @notice Deposit underlying tokens (assets) to the Panoptic pool from the LP and mint corresponding amount of shares. 395 | | /// @dev There is a maximum asset deposit limit of (2 ** 104) - 1. 396 | | /// @dev An MEV tax is levied, which is equal to a single payment of the commissionRate BEFORE adding the funds. 397 | | /// @dev Shares are minted and sent to the LP ('receiver'). 398 | | /// @param assets Amount of assets deposited 399 | | /// @param receiver User to receive the shares 400 | | /// @return shares The amount of Panoptic pool shares that were minted to the recipient 401 | * | function deposit(uint256 assets, address receiver) external returns (uint256 shares) { 402 | * | if (assets > type(uint104).max) revert Errors.DepositTooLarge(); 403 | | 404 | * | shares = previewDeposit(assets); 405 | | 406 | | // transfer assets (underlying token funds) from the user/the LP to the PanopticPool 407 | | // in return for the shares to be minted 408 | * | SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom( 409 | * | s_underlyingToken, 410 | * | msg.sender, 411 | * | address(s_panopticPool), 412 | * | assets 413 | | ); 414 | | 415 | | // mint collateral shares of the Panoptic Pool funds (this ERC20 token) 416 | * | _mint(receiver, shares); 417 | | 418 | | // update tracked asset balance 419 | | unchecked { 420 | * | s_poolAssets += uint128(assets); 421 | | } 422 | | 423 | * | emit Deposit(msg.sender, receiver, assets, shares); 424 | | } 425 | | 426 | | /// @notice Returns the maximum shares received for a deposit. 427 | | /// @return maxShares The maximum amount of shares that can be minted. 428 | * | function maxMint(address) external view returns (uint256 maxShares) { 429 | | unchecked { 430 | * | return (convertToShares(type(uint104).max) * (DECIMALS - COMMISSION_FEE)) / DECIMALS; 431 | | } 432 | | } 433 | | 434 | | /// @notice Returns the amount of assets that would be deposited to mint a given amount of shares. 435 | | /// @param shares The amount of shares to be minted. 436 | | /// @return assets The amount of assets that would be deposited. 437 | * | function previewMint(uint256 shares) public view returns (uint256 assets) { 438 | | // round up depositing assets to avoid protocol loss 439 | | // This prevents minting of shares where the assets provided is rounded down to zero 440 | | // compute the MEV tax, which is equal to a single payment of the commissionRate on the FINAL (post mev-tax) assets paid 441 | | // finalAssets - convertedAssets = commissionRate * finalAssets 442 | | // finalAssets - commissionRate * finalAssets = convertedAssets 443 | | // finalAssets * (1 - commissionRate) = convertedAssets 444 | | // finalAssets = convertedAssets / (1 - commissionRate) 445 | * | assets = Math.mulDivRoundingUp( 446 | * | shares * DECIMALS, 447 | * | totalAssets(), 448 | * | totalSupply * (DECIMALS - COMMISSION_FEE) 449 | | ); 450 | | } 451 | | 452 | | /// @notice Deposit required amount of assets to receive specified amount of shares. 453 | | /// @dev There is a maximum asset deposit limit of (2 ** 104) - 1. 454 | | /// An MEV tax is levied, which is equal to a single payment of the commissionRate BEFORE adding the funds. 455 | | /// @dev Shares are minted and sent to the LP ('receiver'). 456 | | /// @param shares Amount of shares to be minted. 457 | | /// @param receiver User to receive the shares. 458 | | /// @return assets The amount of assets deposited to mint the desired amount of shares. 459 | * | function mint(uint256 shares, address receiver) external returns (uint256 assets) { 460 | * | assets = previewMint(shares); 461 | | 462 | * | if (assets > type(uint104).max) revert Errors.DepositTooLarge(); 463 | | 464 | | // transfer assets (underlying token funds) from the user/the LP to the PanopticPool 465 | | // in return for the shares to be minted 466 | * | SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom( 467 | * | s_underlyingToken, 468 | * | msg.sender, 469 | * | address(s_panopticPool), 470 | * | assets 471 | | ); 472 | | 473 | | // mint collateral shares of the Panoptic Pool funds (this ERC20 token) 474 | * | _mint(receiver, shares); 475 | | 476 | | // update tracked asset balance 477 | | unchecked { 478 | * | s_poolAssets += uint128(assets); 479 | | } 480 | | 481 | * | emit Deposit(msg.sender, receiver, assets, shares); 482 | | } 483 | | 484 | | /// @notice Returns The maximum amount of assets that can be withdrawn for a given user. 485 | | /// If the user has any open positions, the max withdrawable balance is zero. 486 | | /// @dev Calculated from the balance of the user; limited by the assets the pool has available. 487 | | /// @param owner The address being withdrawn for. 488 | | /// @return maxAssets The maximum amount of assets that can be withdrawn. 489 | * | function maxWithdraw(address owner) public view returns (uint256 maxAssets) { 490 | | // We can only use the standard 4626 withdraw function if the user has no open positions 491 | | // For the sake of simplicity assets can only be withdrawn through the redeem function 492 | * | uint256 available = s_poolAssets; 493 | * | uint256 balance = convertToAssets(balanceOf[owner]); 494 | * | return s_panopticPool.numberOfPositions(owner) == 0 ? Math.min(available, balance) : 0; 495 | | } 496 | | 497 | | /// @notice Returns the amount of shares that would be burned to withdraw a given amount of assets. 498 | | /// @param assets The amount of assets to be withdrawn. 499 | | /// @return shares The amount of shares that would be burned. 500 | * | function previewWithdraw(uint256 assets) public view returns (uint256 shares) { 501 | * | uint256 supply = totalSupply; // Saves an extra SLOAD if totalSupply is non-zero. 502 | | 503 | * | return Math.mulDivRoundingUp(assets, supply, totalAssets()); 504 | | } 505 | | 506 | | /// @notice Redeem the amount of shares required to withdraw the specified amount of assets. 507 | | /// We can only use this standard 4626 withdraw function if the user has no open positions. 508 | | /// @dev Shares are burned and assets are sent to the LP ('receiver'). 509 | | /// @param assets Amount of assets to be withdrawn. 510 | | /// @param receiver User to receive the assets. 511 | | /// @param owner User to burn the shares from. 512 | | /// @return shares The amount of shares burned to withdraw the desired amount of assets. 513 | * | function withdraw( 514 | | uint256 assets, 515 | | address receiver, 516 | | address owner 517 | * | ) external returns (uint256 shares) { 518 | * | if (assets > maxWithdraw(owner)) revert Errors.ExceedsMaximumRedemption(); 519 | | 520 | | shares = previewWithdraw(assets); 521 | | 522 | | // check/update allowance for approved withdraw 523 | | if (msg.sender != owner) { 524 | | uint256 allowed = allowance[owner][msg.sender]; // Saves gas for limited approvals. 525 | | 526 | | if (allowed != type(uint256).max) allowance[owner][msg.sender] = allowed - shares; 527 | | } 528 | | 529 | | // burn collateral shares of the Panoptic Pool funds (this ERC20 token) 530 | | _burn(owner, shares); 531 | | 532 | | // update tracked asset balance 533 | | unchecked { 534 | | s_poolAssets -= uint128(assets); 535 | | } 536 | | 537 | | // transfer assets (underlying token funds) from the PanopticPool to the LP 538 | | SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom( 539 | | s_underlyingToken, 540 | | address(s_panopticPool), 541 | | receiver, 542 | | assets 543 | | ); 544 | | 545 | | emit Withdraw(msg.sender, receiver, owner, assets, shares); 546 | | 547 | | return shares; 548 | | } 549 | | 550 | | /// @notice Redeem the amount of shares required to withdraw the specified amount of assets. 551 | | /// @dev Reverts if the account is not solvent with the given `positionIdList`. 552 | | /// @dev Shares are burned and assets are sent to the LP ('receiver'). 553 | | /// @param assets Amount of assets to be withdrawn 554 | | /// @param receiver User to receive the assets 555 | | /// @param owner User to burn the shares from 556 | | /// @param positionIdList The list of all option positions held by `owner` 557 | | /// @return shares The amount of shares burned to withdraw the desired amount of assets 558 | * | function withdraw( 559 | | uint256 assets, 560 | | address receiver, 561 | | address owner, 562 | | TokenId[] calldata positionIdList 563 | * | ) external returns (uint256 shares) { 564 | * | shares = previewWithdraw(assets); 565 | | 566 | | // check/update allowance for approved withdraw 567 | * | if (msg.sender != owner) { 568 | * | uint256 allowed = allowance[owner][msg.sender]; // Saves gas for limited approvals. 569 | | 570 | * | if (allowed != type(uint256).max) allowance[owner][msg.sender] = allowed - shares; 571 | | } 572 | | 573 | | // burn collateral shares of the Panoptic Pool funds (this ERC20 token) 574 | * | _burn(owner, shares); 575 | | 576 | | // update tracked asset balance 577 | | s_poolAssets -= uint128(assets); 578 | | 579 | | // reverts if account is not solvent/eligible to withdraw 580 | | s_panopticPool.validateCollateralWithdrawable(owner, positionIdList); 581 | | 582 | | // transfer assets (underlying token funds) from the PanopticPool to the LP 583 | | SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom( 584 | | s_underlyingToken, 585 | | address(s_panopticPool), 586 | | receiver, 587 | | assets 588 | | ); 589 | | 590 | | emit Withdraw(msg.sender, receiver, owner, assets, shares); 591 | | 592 | | return shares; 593 | | } 594 | | 595 | | /// @notice Returns the maximum amount of shares that can be redeemed for a given user. 596 | | /// If the user has any open positions, the max redeemable balance is zero. 597 | | /// @param owner The redeeming address. 598 | | /// @return maxShares The maximum amount of shares that can be redeemed. 599 | * | function maxRedeem(address owner) public view returns (uint256 maxShares) { 600 | * | uint256 available = convertToShares(s_poolAssets); 601 | * | uint256 balance = balanceOf[owner]; 602 | * | return s_panopticPool.numberOfPositions(owner) == 0 ? Math.min(available, balance) : 0; 603 | | } 604 | | 605 | | /// @notice returns the amount of assets resulting from a given amount of shares being redeemed 606 | | /// @param shares the amount of shares to be redeemed 607 | | /// @return assets the amount of assets resulting from the redemption 608 | | function previewRedeem(uint256 shares) public view returns (uint256 assets) { 609 | | return convertToAssets(shares); 610 | | } 611 | | 612 | | /// @notice Redeem exact shares for underlying assets 613 | | /// We can only use this standard 4626 redeem function if the user has no open positions. 614 | | /// @param shares Amount of shares to be redeemed 615 | | /// @param receiver User to receive the assets 616 | | /// @param owner User to burn the shares from 617 | | /// @return assets the amount of assets resulting from the redemption 618 | * | function redeem( 619 | | uint256 shares, 620 | | address receiver, 621 | | address owner 622 | * | ) external returns (uint256 assets) { 623 | * | if (shares > maxRedeem(owner)) revert Errors.ExceedsMaximumRedemption(); 624 | | 625 | | // check/update allowance for approved redeem 626 | | if (msg.sender != owner) { 627 | | uint256 allowed = allowance[owner][msg.sender]; // Saves gas for limited approvals. 628 | | 629 | | if (allowed != type(uint256).max) allowance[owner][msg.sender] = allowed - shares; 630 | | } 631 | | 632 | | assets = previewRedeem(shares); 633 | | 634 | | // burn collateral shares of the Panoptic Pool funds (this ERC20 token) 635 | | _burn(owner, shares); 636 | | 637 | | // update tracked asset balance 638 | | unchecked { 639 | | s_poolAssets -= uint128(assets); 640 | | } 641 | | 642 | | // transfer assets (underlying token funds) from the PanopticPool to the LP 643 | | SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom( 644 | | s_underlyingToken, 645 | | address(s_panopticPool), 646 | | receiver, 647 | | assets 648 | | ); 649 | | 650 | | emit Withdraw(msg.sender, receiver, owner, assets, shares); 651 | | 652 | | return assets; 653 | | } 654 | | 655 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 656 | | ACCOUNTING LOGIC 657 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 658 | | 659 | | /// @notice Get the cost of exercising an option. Used during a forced exercise. 660 | | /// @dev This one computes the cost of calling the forceExercise function on a position: 661 | | /// - The forceExercisor will have to *pay* the exercisee because their position will be closed "against their will" 662 | | /// - The cost must be larger when the position is close to being in-range, and should be minimal when it is far from being in range. eg. Exercising a (1000, 1050) 663 | | /// position will cost more if the price is 999 than if it is 100 664 | | /// - The cost is an exponentially decaying function of the distance between the position's strike and the current price 665 | | /// - The cost decreases by a factor of 2 for every "position's width" 666 | | /// - Note that the cost is the largest among all active legs, not the sum 667 | | /// @dev Example exercise costs: 668 | | /// - 10% if the position is liquidated when the price is between 950 and 1000, or if it is between 1050 and 1100 669 | | /// - 5% if the price is between 900 and 950 or (1100, 1150) 670 | | /// - 2.5% if between (850, 900) or (1150, 1200) 671 | | /// @param currentTick The current price tick 672 | | /// @param oracleTick The price oracle tick 673 | | /// @param positionId The position to be exercised 674 | | /// @param positionBalance The balance in `account` of the position to be exercised 675 | | /// @param longAmounts The amount of longs in the position 676 | | /// @return exerciseFees The fees for exercising the option position 677 | * | function exerciseCost( 678 | | int24 currentTick, 679 | | int24 oracleTick, 680 | | TokenId positionId, 681 | | uint128 positionBalance, 682 | | LeftRightSigned longAmounts 683 | | ) external view returns (LeftRightSigned exerciseFees) { 684 | | // find the leg furthest to the strike price 'currentTick'; this will have the lowest exercise cost 685 | | // we don't need the leg information itself, really just "the number of half ranges" from the strike price: 686 | | uint256 maxNumRangesFromStrike = 1; // technically "maxNum(Half)RangesFromStrike" but the name is long 687 | | 688 | | unchecked { 689 | | for (uint256 leg = 0; leg < positionId.countLegs(); ++leg) { 690 | | // short legs are not counted - exercise is intended to be based on long legs 691 | | if (positionId.isLong(leg) == 0) continue; 692 | | 693 | | { 694 | | int24 range = int24( 695 | | int256( 696 | | Math.unsafeDivRoundingUp( 697 | | uint24(positionId.width(leg) * positionId.tickSpacing()), 698 | | 2 699 | | ) 700 | | ) 701 | | ); 702 | | maxNumRangesFromStrike = Math.max( 703 | | maxNumRangesFromStrike, 704 | | uint256(Math.abs(currentTick - positionId.strike(leg)) / range) 705 | | ); 706 | | } 707 | | 708 | | uint256 currentValue0; 709 | | uint256 currentValue1; 710 | | uint256 oracleValue0; 711 | | uint256 oracleValue1; 712 | | 713 | | { 714 | | LiquidityChunk liquidityChunk = PanopticMath.getLiquidityChunk( 715 | | positionId, 716 | | leg, 717 | | positionBalance 718 | | ); 719 | | 720 | | (currentValue0, currentValue1) = Math.getAmountsForLiquidity( 721 | | currentTick, 722 | | liquidityChunk 723 | | ); 724 | | 725 | | (oracleValue0, oracleValue1) = Math.getAmountsForLiquidity( 726 | | oracleTick, 727 | | liquidityChunk 728 | | ); 729 | | } 730 | | 731 | | // compensate user for loss in value if chunk has lost money between current and median tick 732 | | // NOTE: the delta for one token will be positive and the other will be negative. This cancels out any moves in their positions 733 | | exerciseFees = exerciseFees.sub( 734 | | LeftRightSigned 735 | | .wrap(0) 736 | | .toRightSlot(int128(uint128(oracleValue0)) - int128(uint128(currentValue0))) 737 | | .toLeftSlot(int128(uint128(oracleValue1)) - int128(uint128(currentValue1))) 738 | | ); 739 | | } 740 | | 741 | | // NOTE: we HAVE to start with a negative number as the base exercise cost because when shifting a negative number right by n bits, 742 | | // the result is rounded DOWN and NOT toward zero 743 | | // this divergence is observed when n (the number of half ranges) is > 10 (ensuring the floor is not zero, but -1 = 1bps at that point) 744 | | // subtract 1 from max half ranges from strike so fee starts at FORCE_EXERCISE_COST when moving OTM 745 | | int256 fee = (FORCE_EXERCISE_COST >> (maxNumRangesFromStrike - 1)); // exponential decay of fee based on number of half ranges away from the price 746 | | 747 | | // store the exercise fees in the exerciseFees variable 748 | * | exerciseFees = exerciseFees 749 | | .toRightSlot(int128((longAmounts.rightSlot() * fee) / DECIMALS_128)) 750 | | .toLeftSlot(int128((longAmounts.leftSlot() * fee) / DECIMALS_128)); 751 | | } 752 | | } 753 | | 754 | | /// @notice Get the pool utilization; it is a measure of the ratio of assets in the AMM vs the total assets managed by the pool. 755 | | // With total assets being the current Panoptic pool balance + the amount in the AMM. 756 | | /// @dev compute: inAMM/totalAssets(). 757 | | /// @return poolUtilization The pool utilization in basis points 758 | * | function _poolUtilization() internal view returns (int256 poolUtilization) { 759 | | unchecked { 760 | * | return int256((s_inAMM * DECIMALS) / totalAssets()); 761 | | } 762 | | } 763 | | 764 | | /// @notice Get the (sell) collateral ratio that is paid when a short option is minted at a specific pool utilization. 765 | | /// @dev This is computed at the time the position is minted. 766 | | /// @param utilization The fraction of totalAssets() that belongs to the Uniswap Pool 767 | | /// @return sellCollateralRatio The sell collateral ratio at `utilization` 768 | * | function _sellCollateralRatio( 769 | | int256 utilization 770 | * | ) internal view returns (uint256 sellCollateralRatio) { 771 | | // the sell ratio is on a straight line defined between two points (x0,y0) and (x1,y1): 772 | | // (x0,y0) = (targetPoolUtilization,min_sell_ratio) and 773 | | // (x1,y1) = (saturatedPoolUtilization,max_sell_ratio) 774 | | // the line's formula: y = a * (x - x0) + y0, where a = (y1 - y0) / (x1 - x0) 775 | | /** 776 | | SELL 777 | | COLLATERAL 778 | | RATIO 779 | | ^ 780 | | | max ratio = 100% 781 | | 100% - | _------ 782 | | | _-¯ 783 | | | _-¯ 784 | | 20% - |---------¯ 785 | | | . . . 786 | | +---------+-------+-+---> POOL_ 787 | | 50% 90% 100% UTILIZATION 788 | | */ 789 | | 790 | * | uint256 min_sell_ratio = SELLER_COLLATERAL_RATIO; 791 | | /// if utilization is less than zero, this is the calculation for a strangle, which gets 2x the capital efficiency at low pool utilization 792 | | /// at 0% utilization, strangle legs do not compound efficiency 793 | * | if (utilization < 0) { 794 | | unchecked { 795 | * | min_sell_ratio /= 2; 796 | * | utilization = -utilization; 797 | | } 798 | | } 799 | | 800 | | // return the basal sell ratio if pool utilization is lower than target 801 | * | if (uint256(utilization) < TARGET_POOL_UTIL) { 802 | | return min_sell_ratio; 803 | | } 804 | | 805 | | // return 100% collateral ratio if utilization is above saturated pool utilization 806 | | // this means all new positions are fully collateralized, which reduces risks of insolvency at high pool utilization 807 | * | if (uint256(utilization) > SATURATED_POOL_UTIL) { 808 | | return DECIMALS; 809 | | } 810 | | 811 | | unchecked { 812 | | return 813 | | min_sell_ratio + 814 | | ((DECIMALS - min_sell_ratio) * (uint256(utilization) - TARGET_POOL_UTIL)) / 815 | | (SATURATED_POOL_UTIL - TARGET_POOL_UTIL); 816 | | } 817 | | } 818 | | 819 | | /// @notice Get the (buy) collateral ratio that is paid when a long option is minted at a specific pool utilization. 820 | | /// @dev This is computed at the time the position is minted. 821 | | /// @param utilization The fraction of totalBalance() that belongs to the Uniswap Pool 822 | | /// @return buyCollateralRatio The buy collateral ratio at `utilization` 823 | * | function _buyCollateralRatio( 824 | | uint256 utilization 825 | * | ) internal view returns (uint256 buyCollateralRatio) { 826 | | // linear from BUY to BUY/2 between 50% and 90% 827 | | // the buy ratio is on a straight line defined between two points (x0,y0) and (x1,y1): 828 | | // (x0,y0) = (targetPoolUtilization,buyCollateralRatio) and 829 | | // (x1,y1) = (saturatedPoolUtilization,buyCollateralRatio / 2) 830 | | // note that y1<y0 so the slope is negative: 831 | | // aka the buy ratio starts high and drops to a lower value with increased utilization; the sell ratio does the opposite (slope is positive) 832 | | // the line's formula: y = a * (x - x0) + y0, where a = (y1 - y0) / (x1 - x0) 833 | | // but since a<0, we rewrite as: 834 | | // y = a' * (x0 - x) + y0, where a' = (y0 - y1) / (x1 - x0) 835 | | 836 | | // HOWEVER, if the utilization is larger than 10_000, then default to 100% buying power requirement. 837 | | // this denotes a situation where the median is too far away from the current price, so we need to require fully collateralized positions for safety 838 | | /** 839 | | BUY 840 | | COLLATERAL 841 | | RATIO 842 | | ^ 843 | | | buy_ratio = 10% 844 | | 10% - |----------__ min_ratio = 5% 845 | | 5% - | . . . . . ¯¯¯--______ 846 | | | . . . 847 | | +---------+-------+-+---> POOL_ 848 | | 50% 90% 100% UTILIZATION 849 | | */ 850 | | 851 | | // return the basal buy ratio if pool utilization is lower than target 852 | * | if (utilization < TARGET_POOL_UTIL) { 853 | | return BUYER_COLLATERAL_RATIO; 854 | | } 855 | | 856 | | // return the basal ratio divided by 2 if pool utilization is above saturated pool utilization 857 | | /// this is incentivized buying, which returns funds to the panoptic pool 858 | * | if (utilization > SATURATED_POOL_UTIL) { 859 | | unchecked { 860 | * | return BUYER_COLLATERAL_RATIO / 2; 861 | | } 862 | | } 863 | | 864 | | unchecked { 865 | | return 866 | | (BUYER_COLLATERAL_RATIO + 867 | | (BUYER_COLLATERAL_RATIO * (SATURATED_POOL_UTIL - utilization)) / 868 | | (SATURATED_POOL_UTIL - TARGET_POOL_UTIL)) / 2; // do the division by 2 at the end after all addition and multiplication; b/c y1 = buyCollateralRatio / 2 869 | | } 870 | | } 871 | | 872 | | /*//////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 873 | | LIFECYCLE OF A COLLATERAL TOKEN AND DELEGATE/REVOKE LOGIC 874 | | ////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 875 | | 876 | | /// @notice Delegate and transfer shares corresponding to the incoming assets 'from' delegator 'to' delegatee. 877 | | /// @dev This is controlled by the Panoptic Pool - not individual users. 878 | | /// @param delegator The delegator to send shares from - the sender of the shares 879 | | /// @param delegatee The delegatee to send shares to - the recipient of the shares 880 | | /// @param assets The assets to which the shares delegated correspond 881 | | function delegate( 882 | | address delegator, 883 | | address delegatee, 884 | | uint256 assets 885 | | ) external onlyPanopticPool { 886 | | /* 887 | | ┌────────┐ 888 | | │Panoptic│ 889 | | │Pool │ 890 | | └────┬───┘ 891 | | │(1) convert 'assets' to shares (this ERC20 contract) 892 | | ┌─────────┐ │ ┌─────────┐ 893 | | │delegator├──▼──►delegatee│ 894 | | └─────────┘(2) └─────────┘ 895 | | move shares 896 | | from delegator 897 | | to delegatee 898 | | */ 899 | | 900 | | uint256 shares = convertToShares(assets); 901 | | 902 | | // transfer shares from the delegator to the delegatee 903 | | _transferFrom(delegator, delegatee, shares); 904 | | } 905 | | 906 | | /// @notice Increase the share balance of a user by shares worth `assets` without updating the total supply. 907 | | /// @dev This is controlled by the Panoptic Pool - not individual users. 908 | | /// @param delegatee The account to increase the balance of 909 | | /// @param assets The amount of assets worth of shares to mint to `delegatee` 910 | | /// @return shares The amount of shares minted to `delegatee` 911 | | function delegate( 912 | | address delegatee, 913 | | uint256 assets 914 | | ) external onlyPanopticPool returns (uint256 shares) { 915 | | shares = convertToShares(assets); 916 | | balanceOf[delegatee] += shares; 917 | | } 918 | | 919 | | /// @notice Decrease the share balance of a user without updating the total supply. 920 | | /// @dev Assumes that `delegatee` has enough money to pay for the refund, will revert otherwise. 921 | | /// @dev This is controlled by the Panoptic Pool - not individual users. 922 | | /// @param delegatee The account to decrease the balance of 923 | | /// @param shares The amount of shares to burn from `delegatee` 924 | | function revoke(address delegatee, uint256 shares) external onlyPanopticPool { 925 | | balanceOf[delegatee] -= shares; 926 | | } 927 | | 928 | | /// @notice Revoke previously delegated shares. The opposite of 'delegate'. 929 | | /// @param delegator The delegator to send shares *to* (because we are revoking - opposite when we delegate) 930 | | /// @param delegatee The delegatee to send shares *from* (because we are revoking - opposite when we delegate) 931 | | /// @param assets The assets to which the shares revoked correspond 932 | * | function revoke( 933 | | address delegator, 934 | | address delegatee, 935 | | uint256 assets 936 | * | ) external onlyPanopticPool { 937 | | /** 938 | | ┌────────┐ 939 | | │Panoptic│ 940 | | │Pool │ 941 | | └────┬───┘ 942 | | │(1) convert 'assets' to shares (this ERC20 contract) 943 | | ┌─────────┐ │ ┌─────────┐ 944 | | │delegator◄──▼─┤delegatee│ 945 | | └─────────┘(2) └─────────┘ 946 | | moves shares 947 | | from delegatee 948 | | back to delegator 949 | | */ 950 | | 951 | | uint256 shares = convertToShares(assets); 952 | | 953 | | // get the delegateeBalance and compare later against requestedAmount 954 | | uint256 delegateeBalance = balanceOf[delegatee]; 955 | | 956 | | // if requested amount is larger than user balance, transfer shares back, 957 | | // then issue new shares 958 | | if (shares > delegateeBalance) { 959 | | // transfer delegatee balance to delegator 960 | | _transferFrom(delegatee, delegator, delegateeBalance); 961 | | 962 | | // this is paying out protocol loss, so correct for that in the amount of shares to be minted 963 | | // X: total assets in vault 964 | | // Y: total supply of shares 965 | | // Z: desired value (assets) of shares to be minted 966 | | // N: total shares corresponding to Z 967 | | // T: transferred shares from liquidatee which are a component of N but do not contribute toward protocol loss 968 | | // Z = N * X / (Y + N - T) 969 | | // Z * (Y + N - T) = N * X 970 | | // ZY + ZN - ZT = NX 971 | | // ZY - ZT = N(X - Z) 972 | | // N = (ZY - ZT) / (X - Z) 973 | | // N = Z(Y - T) / (X - Z) 974 | | // subtract delegatee balance from N since it was already transferred to the delegator 975 | | _mint( 976 | | delegator, 977 | | Math.mulDiv( 978 | | assets, 979 | | totalSupply - delegateeBalance, 980 | | uint256(Math.max(1, int256(totalAssets()) - int256(assets))) 981 | | ) - delegateeBalance 982 | | ); 983 | | } 984 | | // if requested amount < delegatee balance, then just transfer shares back 985 | | else { 986 | * | _transferFrom(delegatee, delegator, shares); 987 | | } 988 | | } 989 | | 990 | | /// @notice Refunds delegated tokens to 'refunder' from 'refundee', similar to 'revoke'. 991 | | /// @dev Assumes that the refunder has enough money to pay for the refund. 992 | | /// @dev Can handle negative refund amounts that go from refundee to refunder in the case of high exercise fees. 993 | | /// @param refunder The account refunding tokens to 'refundee' 994 | | /// @param refundee The account being refunded to 995 | | /// @param assets The amount of assets to refund. Positive means a transfer from refunder to refundee, vice versa for negative 996 | | function refund(address refunder, address refundee, int256 assets) external onlyPanopticPool { 997 | | if (assets > 0) { 998 | | _transferFrom(refunder, refundee, convertToShares(uint256(assets))); 999 | | } else { 1000 | | unchecked { 1001 | | _transferFrom(refundee, refunder, convertToShares(uint256(-assets))); 1002 | | } 1003 | | } 1004 | | } 1005 | | 1006 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 1007 | | OPTION EXERCISE AND COMMISSION 1008 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 1009 | | 1010 | | /// @notice Take commission on option creation/opening (commissions will not be taken on closing). 1011 | | /// @param optionOwner The user minting the option 1012 | | /// @param longAmount The amount of longs 1013 | | /// @param shortAmount The amount of shorts 1014 | | /// @param swappedAmount The amount of tokens moved during creation of the option position 1015 | | /// @return utilization The final utilization of the collateral vault 1016 | * | function takeCommissionAddData( 1017 | | address optionOwner, 1018 | | int128 longAmount, 1019 | | int128 shortAmount, 1020 | | int128 swappedAmount 1021 | | ) external onlyPanopticPool returns (int256 utilization) { 1022 | | unchecked { 1023 | | // current available assets belonging to PLPs (updated after settlement) excluding any premium paid 1024 | | int256 updatedAssets = int256(uint256(s_poolAssets)) - swappedAmount; 1025 | | 1026 | | // constrict premium to only assets not belonging to PLPs (i.e premium paid by sellers or collected from the pool earlier) 1027 | | int256 tokenToPay = _getExchangedAmount(longAmount, shortAmount, swappedAmount); 1028 | | 1029 | | // compute tokens to be paid due to swap 1030 | | // mint or burn tokens due to minting in-the-money 1031 | | if (tokenToPay > 0) { 1032 | | // if user must pay tokens, burn them from user balance 1033 | | uint256 sharesToBurn = Math.mulDivRoundingUp( 1034 | | uint256(tokenToPay), 1035 | | totalSupply, 1036 | | totalAssets() 1037 | | ); 1038 | | _burn(optionOwner, sharesToBurn); 1039 | | } else if (tokenToPay < 0) { 1040 | | // if user must receive tokens, mint them 1041 | | uint256 sharesToMint = convertToShares(uint256(-tokenToPay)); 1042 | | _mint(optionOwner, sharesToMint); 1043 | | } 1044 | | 1045 | | // update stored asset balances with net moved amounts 1046 | | // the inflow or outflow of pool assets is defined by the swappedAmount: it includes both the ITM swap amounts and the short/long amounts used to create the position 1047 | | // however, any intrinsic value is paid for by the users, so we only add the portion that comes from PLPs: the short/long amounts 1048 | | // premia is not included in the balance since it is the property of options buyers and sellers, not PLPs 1049 | | s_poolAssets = uint256(updatedAssets).toUint128(); 1050 | | s_inAMM = uint256(int256(uint256(s_inAMM)) + (shortAmount - longAmount)).toUint128(); 1051 | | 1052 | * | utilization = _poolUtilization(); 1053 | | } 1054 | | } 1055 | | 1056 | | /// @notice Exercise an option and pay to the seller what is owed from the buyer. 1057 | | /// @dev Called when a position is burnt because it may need to be exercised. 1058 | | /// @param optionOwner The owner of the option being burned and potentially exercised 1059 | | /// @param longAmount The amount of longs to be exercised (if any) 1060 | | /// @param shortAmount The amount of shorts to be exercised (if any) 1061 | | /// @param swappedAmount The amount of tokens moved during the option close 1062 | | /// @param realizedPremium Premium to settle on the current positions 1063 | | /// @return The amount of tokens paid when closing that position 1064 | | function exercise( 1065 | | address optionOwner, 1066 | | int128 longAmount, 1067 | | int128 shortAmount, 1068 | | int128 swappedAmount, 1069 | | int128 realizedPremium 1070 | | ) external onlyPanopticPool returns (int128) { 1071 | | unchecked { 1072 | | // current available assets belonging to PLPs (updated after settlement) excluding any premium paid 1073 | | int256 updatedAssets = int256(uint256(s_poolAssets)) - swappedAmount; 1074 | | 1075 | | // add premium and token deltas not covered by swap to be paid/collected on position close 1076 | | int256 tokenToPay = swappedAmount - (longAmount - shortAmount) - realizedPremium; 1077 | | 1078 | | if (tokenToPay > 0) { 1079 | | // if user must pay tokens, burn them from user balance (revert if balance too small) 1080 | * | uint256 sharesToBurn = Math.mulDivRoundingUp( 1081 | | uint256(tokenToPay), 1082 | | totalSupply, 1083 | * | totalAssets() 1084 | | ); 1085 | | _burn(optionOwner, sharesToBurn); 1086 | | } else if (tokenToPay < 0) { 1087 | | // if user must receive tokens, mint them 1088 | | uint256 sharesToMint = convertToShares(uint256(-tokenToPay)); 1089 | | _mint(optionOwner, sharesToMint); 1090 | | } 1091 | | 1092 | | // update stored asset balances with net moved amounts 1093 | | // any intrinsic value is paid for by the users, so we do not add it to s_inAMM 1094 | | // premia is not included in the balance since it is the property of options buyers and sellers, not PLPs 1095 | | s_poolAssets = uint256(updatedAssets + realizedPremium).toUint128(); 1096 | | s_inAMM = uint256(int256(uint256(s_inAMM)) - (shortAmount - longAmount)).toUint128(); 1097 | | 1098 | | return (int128(tokenToPay)); 1099 | | } 1100 | | } 1101 | | 1102 | | /// @notice Get the amount exchanged to mint an option. 1103 | | /// @param longAmount The amount of long options held 1104 | | /// @param shortAmount The amount of short options held 1105 | | /// @param swappedAmount The amount of tokens moved during creation of the option position 1106 | | /// @return exchangedAmount The amount of funds to be exchanged for minting an option (includes commission, swapFee, and intrinsic value) 1107 | | function _getExchangedAmount( 1108 | | int128 longAmount, 1109 | | int128 shortAmount, 1110 | | int128 swappedAmount 1111 | | ) internal view returns (int256 exchangedAmount) { 1112 | | // If amount swapped is positive, the amount of tokens to pay is the ITM amount 1113 | | 1114 | | unchecked { 1115 | | // intrinsic value is the amount that need to be exchanged due to minting in-the-money 1116 | | int256 intrinsicValue = swappedAmount - (shortAmount - longAmount); 1117 | | 1118 | | if (intrinsicValue != 0) { 1119 | | // the swap commission is paid on the intrinsic value, and it is always positive 1120 | | uint256 swapCommission = Math.unsafeDivRoundingUp( 1121 | | s_ITMSpreadFee * uint256(Math.abs(intrinsicValue)), 1122 | | DECIMALS * 100 1123 | | ); 1124 | | 1125 | | // set the exchanged amount to the sum of the intrinsic value and swapCommission 1126 | | exchangedAmount = intrinsicValue + int256(swapCommission); 1127 | | } 1128 | | 1129 | | //compute total commission amount = commission rate + spread fee 1130 | | exchangedAmount += int256( 1131 | | Math.unsafeDivRoundingUp( 1132 | | uint256(uint128(shortAmount + longAmount)) * COMMISSION_FEE, 1133 | | DECIMALS 1134 | | ) 1135 | | ); 1136 | | } 1137 | | } 1138 | | 1139 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 1140 | | HEALTH AND COLLATERAL TRACKING 1141 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 1142 | | 1143 | | /// @notice Get the collateral status/margin details of an account/user. 1144 | | /// @dev NOTE: It's up to the caller to confirm from the returned result that the account has enough collateral. 1145 | | /// @dev This can be used to check the health: how many tokens a user has compared to the margin threshold. 1146 | | /// @param user The account to check collateral/margin health for 1147 | | /// @param currentTick The tick at which to evaluate the account's positions 1148 | | /// @param positionBalanceArray The list of all historical positions held by the 'optionOwner', stored as [[tokenId, balance/poolUtilizationAtMint], ...] 1149 | | /// @param premiumAllPositions The premium collected thus far across all positions 1150 | | /// @return tokenData Information collected for the tokens about the health of the account 1151 | | /// The collateral balance of the user is in the right slot and the threshold for margin call is in the left slot. 1152 | * | function getAccountMarginDetails( 1153 | | address user, 1154 | | int24 currentTick, 1155 | | uint256[2][] memory positionBalanceArray, 1156 | | int128 premiumAllPositions 1157 | * | ) public view returns (LeftRightUnsigned tokenData) { 1158 | * | tokenData = _getAccountMargin(user, currentTick, positionBalanceArray, premiumAllPositions); 1159 | | } 1160 | | 1161 | | /// @notice Get the collateral status/margin details of an account/user. 1162 | | /// @dev NOTE: It's up to the caller to confirm from the returned result that the account has enough collateral. 1163 | | /// @dev This can be used to check the health: how many tokens a user has compared to the margin threshold. 1164 | | /// @param user The account to check collateral/margin health for. 1165 | | /// @param atTick The tick at which to evaluate the account's positions 1166 | | /// @param positionBalanceArray The list of all historical positions held by the 'optionOwner', stored as [[tokenId, balance/poolUtilizationAtMint], ...] 1167 | | /// @param premiumAllPositions The premium collected thus far across all positions 1168 | | /// @return tokenData Information collected for the tokens about the health of the account; 1169 | | /// the collateral balance of the user is in the right slot and the threshold for margin call is in the left slot 1170 | * | function _getAccountMargin( 1171 | | address user, 1172 | | int24 atTick, 1173 | | uint256[2][] memory positionBalanceArray, 1174 | | int128 premiumAllPositions 1175 | * | ) internal view returns (LeftRightUnsigned tokenData) { 1176 | * | uint256 tokenRequired; 1177 | | 1178 | | // if the account has active options, compute the required collateral to keep account in good health 1179 | * | if (positionBalanceArray.length > 0) { 1180 | | // get all collateral required for the incoming list of positions 1181 | * | tokenRequired = _getTotalRequiredCollateral(atTick, positionBalanceArray); 1182 | | 1183 | | // If premium is negative (ie. user has to pay for their purchased options), add this long premium to the token requirement 1184 | * | if (premiumAllPositions < 0) { 1185 | | unchecked { 1186 | | tokenRequired += uint128(-premiumAllPositions); 1187 | | } 1188 | | } 1189 | | } 1190 | | 1191 | | // if premium is positive (ie. user will receive funds due to selling options), add this premum to the user's balance 1192 | * | uint256 netBalance = convertToAssets(balanceOf[user]); 1193 | * | if (premiumAllPositions > 0) { 1194 | | unchecked { 1195 | | netBalance += uint256(uint128(premiumAllPositions)); 1196 | | } 1197 | | } 1198 | | 1199 | | // store assetBalance and tokens required in tokenData variable 1200 | * | tokenData = tokenData.toRightSlot(netBalance.toUint128()).toLeftSlot( 1201 | * | tokenRequired.toUint128() 1202 | | ); 1203 | | return tokenData; 1204 | | } 1205 | | 1206 | | /// @notice Get the total required amount of collateral tokens of a user/account across all active positions to stay above the margin requirement. 1207 | | /// @dev Returns the token amounts required for the entire account with active positions in 'positionIdList' (list of tokenIds). 1208 | | /// @param atTick The tick at which to evaluate the account's positions 1209 | | /// @param positionBalanceArray The list of all historical positions held by the 'optionOwner', stored as [[tokenId, balance/poolUtilizationAtMint], ...] 1210 | | /// @return tokenRequired The amount of tokens required to stay above the margin threshold for all active positions of user 1211 | * | function _getTotalRequiredCollateral( 1212 | | int24 atTick, 1213 | | uint256[2][] memory positionBalanceArray 1214 | * | ) internal view returns (uint256 tokenRequired) { 1215 | | // loop through each active position. 1216 | | // Offset determined whether to consider the last tokenId from the list 1217 | | // (a potentially newly minted position) 1218 | * | uint256 totalIterations = positionBalanceArray.length; 1219 | * | for (uint256 i = 0; i < totalIterations; ) { 1220 | | // read the ith tokenId from the account 1221 | * | TokenId tokenId = TokenId.wrap(positionBalanceArray[i][0]); 1222 | | 1223 | | // read the position size and the pool utilization at mint 1224 | * | uint128 positionSize = LeftRightUnsigned.wrap(positionBalanceArray[i][1]).rightSlot(); 1225 | | 1226 | | // read the pool utilization at mint 1227 | * | uint128 poolUtilization = LeftRightUnsigned.wrap(positionBalanceArray[i][1]).leftSlot(); 1228 | | 1229 | | // Get tokens required for the current tokenId (a single active position) 1230 | * | uint256 _tokenRequired = _getRequiredCollateralAtTickSinglePosition( 1231 | * | tokenId, 1232 | * | positionSize, 1233 | * | atTick, 1234 | * | poolUtilization 1235 | | ); 1236 | | 1237 | | // add to the tokenRequired accumulator 1238 | | unchecked { 1239 | * | tokenRequired += _tokenRequired; 1240 | | } 1241 | | unchecked { 1242 | * | ++i; 1243 | | } 1244 | | } 1245 | | 1246 | * | return tokenRequired; 1247 | | } 1248 | | 1249 | | /// @notice Get the required amount of collateral tokens corresponding to a specific single position 'tokenId' at a price 'tick'. 1250 | | /// The required collateral of an account depends on the price ('tick') in the AMM pool: if in the position's favor less collateral needed, etc. 1251 | | /// @param tokenId The option position 1252 | | /// @param positionSize The size of the option position 1253 | | /// @param atTick The tick at which to evaluate the account's positions 1254 | | /// @param poolUtilization The utilization of the collateral vault (balance of buying and selling) 1255 | | /// @return tokenRequired Total required tokens for all legs of the specified tokenId. 1256 | * | function _getRequiredCollateralAtTickSinglePosition( 1257 | | TokenId tokenId, 1258 | | uint128 positionSize, 1259 | | int24 atTick, 1260 | | uint128 poolUtilization 1261 | * | ) internal view returns (uint256 tokenRequired) { 1262 | * | bool underlyingIsToken0 = s_underlyingIsToken0; 1263 | * | uint256 numLegs = tokenId.countLegs(); 1264 | | 1265 | | unchecked { 1266 | * | for (uint256 index = 0; index < numLegs; ++index) { 1267 | | // revert if the tokenType does not match the current collateral token 1268 | * | if (tokenId.tokenType(index) != (underlyingIsToken0 ? 0 : 1)) continue; 1269 | | // Increment the tokenRequired accumulator 1270 | * | tokenRequired += _getRequiredCollateralSingleLeg( 1271 | * | tokenId, 1272 | * | index, 1273 | * | positionSize, 1274 | * | atTick, 1275 | * | poolUtilization 1276 | | ); 1277 | | } 1278 | | } 1279 | | } 1280 | | 1281 | | /// @notice Calculate the required amount of collateral for a single leg 'index' of position 'tokenId'. 1282 | | /// @param tokenId The option position 1283 | | /// @param index The leg index (associated with a liquidity chunk) to consider a partner for 1284 | | /// @param positionSize The size of the position 1285 | | /// @param atTick The tick at which to evaluate the account's positions 1286 | | /// @param poolUtilization The pool utilization: how much funds are in the Panoptic pool versus the AMM pool 1287 | | /// @return required The required amount collateral needed for this leg 'index' 1288 | * | function _getRequiredCollateralSingleLeg( 1289 | | TokenId tokenId, 1290 | | uint256 index, 1291 | | uint128 positionSize, 1292 | | int24 atTick, 1293 | | uint128 poolUtilization 1294 | * | ) internal view returns (uint256 required) { 1295 | | return 1296 | * | tokenId.riskPartner(index) == index // does this leg have a risk partner? Affects required collateral 1297 | * | ? _getRequiredCollateralSingleLegNoPartner( 1298 | * | tokenId, 1299 | * | index, 1300 | * | positionSize, 1301 | * | atTick, 1302 | * | poolUtilization 1303 | | ) 1304 | * | : _getRequiredCollateralSingleLegPartner( 1305 | * | tokenId, 1306 | * | index, 1307 | * | positionSize, 1308 | * | atTick, 1309 | * | poolUtilization 1310 | | ); 1311 | | } 1312 | | 1313 | | /// @notice Calculate the required amount of collateral for leg 'index' of position 'tokenId' when the leg does not have a risk partner. 1314 | | /// @param tokenId The option position 1315 | | /// @param index The leg index (associated with a liquidity chunk) to consider a partner for 1316 | | /// @param positionSize The size of the position 1317 | | /// @param atTick The tick at which to evaluate the account's positions 1318 | | /// @param poolUtilization The pool utilization: ratio of how much funds are in the Panoptic pool versus the AMM pool 1319 | | /// @return required The required amount collateral needed for this leg 'index' 1320 | * | function _getRequiredCollateralSingleLegNoPartner( 1321 | | TokenId tokenId, 1322 | | uint256 index, 1323 | | uint128 positionSize, 1324 | | int24 atTick, 1325 | | uint128 poolUtilization 1326 | * | ) internal view returns (uint256 required) { 1327 | | // extract the tokenType (token0 or token1) 1328 | * | uint256 tokenType = tokenId.tokenType(index); 1329 | | 1330 | | // compute the total amount of funds moved for that position 1331 | * | LeftRightUnsigned amountsMoved = PanopticMath.getAmountsMoved(tokenId, positionSize, index); 1332 | | 1333 | | // amount moved is right slot if tokenType=0, left slot otherwise 1334 | * | uint128 amountMoved = tokenType == 0 ? amountsMoved.rightSlot() : amountsMoved.leftSlot(); 1335 | | 1336 | | // match tokenType with the correct pool utilization 1337 | * | int64 utilization = tokenType == 0 1338 | * | ? int64(uint64(poolUtilization)) 1339 | * | : int64(uint64(poolUtilization >> 64)); 1340 | | 1341 | * | uint256 isLong = tokenId.isLong(index); 1342 | | 1343 | | // start with base requirement, which is based on isLong value 1344 | * | required = _getRequiredCollateralAtUtilization(amountMoved, isLong, utilization); 1345 | | 1346 | | // if the position is long, required tokens does not depend on price 1347 | | unchecked { 1348 | * | if (isLong == 0) { 1349 | | // if position is short, check whether the position is out-the-money 1350 | | 1351 | * | (int24 tickLower, int24 tickUpper) = tokenId.asTicks(index); 1352 | | 1353 | | // compute the collateral requirement as a fixed amount that doesn't depend on price 1354 | * | if ( 1355 | * | ((atTick >= tickUpper) && (tokenType == 1)) || // strike OTM when price >= upperTick for tokenType=1 1356 | * | ((atTick < tickLower) && (tokenType == 0)) // strike OTM when price < lowerTick for tokenType=0 1357 | | ) { 1358 | | // position is out-the-money, collateral requirement = SCR * amountMoved 1359 | | required; 1360 | * | } else { 1361 | * | int24 strike = tokenId.strike(index); 1362 | | // if position is ITM or ATM, then the collateral requirement depends on price: 1363 | | 1364 | | // compute the ratio of strike to price for calls (or price to strike for puts) 1365 | | // (- and * 2 in tick space are / and ^ 2 in price space so sqrtRatioAtTick(2 *(a - b)) = a/b (*2^96) 1366 | | // both of these ratios decrease as the position becomes deeper ITM, and it is possible 1367 | | // for the ratio of the prices to go under the minimum price 1368 | | // (which is the limit of what getSqrtRatioAtTick supports) 1369 | | // so instead we cap it at the minimum price, which is acceptable because 1370 | | // a higher ratio will result in an increased slope for the collateral requirement 1371 | * | uint160 ratio = tokenType == 1 // tokenType 1372 | * | ? Math.getSqrtRatioAtTick( 1373 | * | Math.max24(2 * (atTick - strike), Constants.MIN_V3POOL_TICK) 1374 | | ) // puts -> price/strike 1375 | * | : Math.getSqrtRatioAtTick( 1376 | * | Math.max24(2 * (strike - atTick), Constants.MIN_V3POOL_TICK) 1377 | | ); // calls -> strike/price 1378 | | 1379 | | // compute the collateral requirement depending on whether the position is ITM & out-of-range or ITM and in-range: 1380 | | 1381 | | /// ITM and out-of-range 1382 | * | if ( 1383 | * | ((atTick < tickLower) && (tokenType == 1)) || // strike ITM but out of range price < lowerTick for tokenType=1 1384 | * | ((atTick >= tickUpper) && (tokenType == 0)) // strike ITM but out of range when price >= upperTick for tokenType=0 1385 | * | ) { 1386 | | /* 1387 | | Short put BPR = 100% - (price/strike) + SCR 1388 | | 1389 | | BUYING 1390 | | POWER 1391 | | REQUIREMENT 1392 | | 1393 | | ^ . . 1394 | | | <- ITM . <-ATM-> . OTM -> 1395 | | 100% + SCR% - |--__ . . . 1396 | | 100% - | . .¯¯--__ . . . 1397 | | | . ¯¯--__ . . 1398 | | SCR - | . .¯¯--__________ 1399 | | | . . . . 1400 | | +----+----------+----+----+---> current 1401 | | 0 Liqui- Pa strike Pb price 1402 | | dation 1403 | | price = SCR*strike 1404 | | */ 1405 | | 1406 | * | uint256 c2 = Constants.FP96 - ratio; 1407 | | 1408 | | // compute the tokens required 1409 | | // position is in-the-money, collateral requirement = amountMoved*(1-ratio) + SCR*amountMoved 1410 | * | required += Math.mulDiv96RoundingUp(amountMoved, c2); 1411 | | } else { 1412 | | // position is in-range (ie. current tick is between upper+lower tick): we draw a line between the 1413 | | // collateral requirement at the lowerTick and the one at the upperTick. We use that interpolation as 1414 | | // the collateral requirement when in-range, which always over-estimates the amount of token required 1415 | | // Specifically: 1416 | | // required = amountMoved * (scaleFactor - ratio) / (scaleFactor + 1) + sellCollateralRatio*amountMoved 1417 | * | uint160 scaleFactor = Math.getSqrtRatioAtTick( 1418 | * | (tickUpper - strike) + (strike - tickLower) 1419 | | ); 1420 | * | uint256 c3 = Math.mulDivRoundingUp( 1421 | * | amountMoved, 1422 | * | scaleFactor - ratio, 1423 | * | scaleFactor + Constants.FP96 1424 | | ); 1425 | | // position is in-the-money, collateral requirement = amountMoved*(1-SRC)*(scaleFactor-ratio)/(scaleFactor+1) + SCR*amountMoved 1426 | * | required += c3; 1427 | | } 1428 | | } 1429 | | } 1430 | | } 1431 | | } 1432 | | 1433 | | /// @notice Calculate the required amount of collateral for leg 'index' for position 'tokenId' accounting for its partner leg. 1434 | | /// @dev If the two token long-types are different (one is a long, the other a short, e.g.) but the tokenTypes are the same, this is a spread 1435 | | /// a spread is a defined risk position which has a max loss given by difference between the long and short strikes. 1436 | | /// @dev If the two token long-types are the same but the tokenTypes are different (one is a call, the other a put, e.g.), this is a strangle - 1437 | | /// a strangle benefits from enhanced capital efficiency because only one side can be ITM at a time. 1438 | | /// @dev if the position is a spread, then the collateral requirement consists of two components: 1439 | | /// @dev 1) The difference in notional value at both strikes: abs(strikeLong - strikeShort) or abs(strikeShort - strikeLong) 1440 | | /// @dev 2) A spread term which is relevant for legs that have different widths (calendar spreads) 1441 | | /// @param tokenId The option position 1442 | | /// @param index The leg index (associated with a liquidity chunk) to consider a partner for 1443 | | /// @param positionSize The size of the position 1444 | | /// @param atTick The tick at which to evaluate the account's positions 1445 | | /// @param poolUtilization The pool utilization: how much funds are in the Panoptic pool versus the AMM pool 1446 | | /// @return required The required amount collateral needed for this leg 'index', accounting for what the leg's risk partner is 1447 | * | function _getRequiredCollateralSingleLegPartner( 1448 | | TokenId tokenId, 1449 | | uint256 index, 1450 | | uint128 positionSize, 1451 | | int24 atTick, 1452 | | uint128 poolUtilization 1453 | * | ) internal view returns (uint256 required) { 1454 | | // extract partner index (associated with another liquidity chunk) 1455 | * | uint256 partnerIndex = tokenId.riskPartner(index); 1456 | | 1457 | * | uint256 isLong = tokenId.isLong(index); 1458 | * | if (isLong != tokenId.isLong(partnerIndex)) { 1459 | * | if (isLong == 1) { 1460 | | // compute the total amount of funds moved for that position 1461 | * | required = _computeSpread( 1462 | * | tokenId, 1463 | * | positionSize, 1464 | * | index, 1465 | * | partnerIndex, 1466 | * | poolUtilization 1467 | | ); 1468 | | } 1469 | | } else { 1470 | * | required = _computeStrangle(tokenId, index, positionSize, atTick, poolUtilization); 1471 | | } 1472 | | } 1473 | | 1474 | | /// @notice Get the base collateral requirement for an 'amount' at the current Panoptic pool 'utilization' level. 1475 | | /// @notice For a given incoming 'amount' - which is the size of a user position (e.g. opening a position), what is the corresponding required collateral to have. 1476 | | /// @dev NOTE: this does not depend on the price of the AMM pool. This only computes what is needed in response to the current utilization. 1477 | | /// @param amount The amount from which required collateral is computed 1478 | | /// @param isLong Whether the position is long (=1) or short (=0) 1479 | | /// @param utilization The utilization of the Panoptic pool (balance between sellers and buyers) 1480 | | /// @return required The required collateral corresponding to the incoming 'amount' 1481 | * | function _getRequiredCollateralAtUtilization( 1482 | | uint128 amount, 1483 | | uint256 isLong, 1484 | | int256 utilization 1485 | * | ) internal view returns (uint256 required) { 1486 | | // if position is short, use sell collateral ratio 1487 | * | if (isLong == 0) { 1488 | | // compute the sell collateral ratio, which depends on the pool utilization 1489 | * | uint256 sellCollateral = _sellCollateralRatio(utilization); 1490 | | 1491 | | // compute required as amount*collateralRatio 1492 | | // can use unsafe because denominator is always nonzero 1493 | | unchecked { 1494 | * | required = Math.unsafeDivRoundingUp(amount * sellCollateral, DECIMALS); 1495 | | } 1496 | * | } else if (isLong == 1) { 1497 | | // if options is long, use buy collateral ratio 1498 | | // compute the buy collateral ratio, which depends on the pool utilization 1499 | * | uint256 buyCollateral = _buyCollateralRatio(uint256(utilization)); 1500 | | 1501 | | // compute required as amount*collateralRatio 1502 | | // can use unsafe because denominator is always nonzero 1503 | | unchecked { 1504 | * | required = Math.unsafeDivRoundingUp(amount * buyCollateral, DECIMALS); 1505 | | } 1506 | | } 1507 | | } 1508 | | 1509 | | /// @notice Calculate the required amount of collateral for the spread portion of the spread position. 1510 | | /// @dev (long leg requirement + 100% collateralized risk) 1511 | | /// @dev May be higher than the requirement of non risk-partnered legs if the spread is very wide (risky). 1512 | | /// @param tokenId The option position 1513 | | /// @param positionSize The size of the position 1514 | | /// @param index The leg index of the LONG leg in the spread position 1515 | | /// @param partnerIndex The index of the partnered SHORT leg in the spread position 1516 | | /// @param poolUtilization The pool utilization: how much funds are in the Panoptic pool versus the AMM pool 1517 | | /// @return spreadRequirement The required amount of collateral needed for the spread portion 1518 | * | function _computeSpread( 1519 | | TokenId tokenId, 1520 | | uint128 positionSize, 1521 | | uint256 index, 1522 | | uint256 partnerIndex, 1523 | | uint128 poolUtilization 1524 | * | ) internal view returns (uint256 spreadRequirement) { 1525 | | // compute the total amount of funds moved for the position's current leg 1526 | * | LeftRightUnsigned amountsMoved = PanopticMath.getAmountsMoved(tokenId, positionSize, index); 1527 | | 1528 | | // compute the total amount of funds moved for the position's partner leg 1529 | * | LeftRightUnsigned amountsMovedPartner = PanopticMath.getAmountsMoved( 1530 | * | tokenId, 1531 | * | positionSize, 1532 | * | partnerIndex 1533 | | ); 1534 | | 1535 | | // amount moved is right slot if tokenType=0, left slot otherwise 1536 | * | uint128 movedRight = amountsMoved.rightSlot(); 1537 | | uint128 movedLeft = amountsMoved.leftSlot(); 1538 | | 1539 | | // amounts moved for partner 1540 | | uint128 movedPartnerRight = amountsMovedPartner.rightSlot(); 1541 | | uint128 movedPartnerLeft = amountsMovedPartner.leftSlot(); 1542 | | 1543 | * | uint256 tokenType = tokenId.tokenType(index); 1544 | | 1545 | | // compute the max loss of the spread 1546 | | 1547 | | // if asset is NOT the same as the tokenType, the required amount is simply the difference in notional values 1548 | | // ie. asset = 1, tokenType = 0: 1549 | * | if (tokenId.asset(index) != tokenType) { 1550 | | unchecked { 1551 | | // always take the absolute values of the difference of amounts moved 1552 | | if (tokenType == 0) { 1553 | | spreadRequirement = movedRight < movedPartnerRight 1554 | | ? movedPartnerRight - movedRight 1555 | | : movedRight - movedPartnerRight; 1556 | | } else { 1557 | | spreadRequirement = movedLeft < movedPartnerLeft 1558 | | ? movedPartnerLeft - movedLeft 1559 | | : movedLeft - movedPartnerLeft; 1560 | | } 1561 | | } 1562 | | } else { 1563 | * | unchecked { 1564 | * | uint256 notional; 1565 | * | uint256 notionalP; 1566 | * | uint128 contracts; 1567 | * | if (tokenType == 1) { 1568 | * | notional = movedRight; 1569 | * | notionalP = movedPartnerRight; 1570 | * | contracts = movedLeft; 1571 | | } else { 1572 | | notional = movedLeft; 1573 | | notionalP = movedPartnerLeft; 1574 | | contracts = movedRight; 1575 | | } 1576 | | // the required amount is the amount of contracts multiplied by (notional1 - notional2)/min(notional1, notional2) 1577 | | // can use unsafe because denominator is always nonzero 1578 | * | spreadRequirement = (notional < notionalP) 1579 | * | ? Math.unsafeDivRoundingUp((notionalP - notional) * contracts, notional) 1580 | * | : Math.unsafeDivRoundingUp((notional - notionalP) * contracts, notionalP); 1581 | | } 1582 | | } 1583 | | 1584 | | // calculate the spread requirement as max(max_loss, long_leg_col_req) 1585 | | // narrower spreads will be very capital efficient (1/3 of non-partnered CR!), but 1586 | | // wider spreads (an uncommon position w/ high max loss) may not benefit from risk partnering 1587 | * | spreadRequirement = Math.max( 1588 | * | spreadRequirement, 1589 | * | _getRequiredCollateralAtUtilization( 1590 | * | tokenType == 0 ?
