movedRight : movedLeft, 1591 | * | 1, 1592 | * | tokenType == 0 1593 | | ? int64(uint64(poolUtilization)) 1594 | * | : int64(uint64(poolUtilization >> 64)) 1595 | | ) 1596 | | ); 1597 | | } 1598 | | 1599 | | /// @notice Calculate the required amount of collateral for a strangle leg. 1600 | | /// @dev Strangle legs are evaluated at 2x capital efficiency at low pool utilizations. 1601 | | /// @dev A strangle can only have only one of its leg tested at the same time, so this reduces the total risk and collateral requirement. 1602 | | /// @param tokenId The option position 1603 | | /// @param positionSize The size of the position 1604 | | /// @param index The leg index (associated with a liquidity chunk) to consider a partner for 1605 | | /// @param atTick The tick at which to evaluate the account's positions 1606 | | /// @param poolUtilization The pool utilization: how much funds are in the Panoptic pool versus the AMM pool 1607 | | /// @return strangleRequired The required amount of collateral needed for the strangle leg 1608 | * | function _computeStrangle( 1609 | | TokenId tokenId, 1610 | | uint256 index, 1611 | | uint128 positionSize, 1612 | | int24 atTick, 1613 | | uint128 poolUtilization 1614 | * | ) internal view returns (uint256 strangleRequired) { 1615 | | // If both tokenTypes are the same, then this is a long or short strangle. 1616 | | // A strangle is an options strategy in which the investor holds a position 1617 | | // in both a call and a put option with different strike prices, 1618 | | // but with the same expiration date and underlying asset. 1619 | | 1620 | | /// collateral requirement is for short strangles depicted: 1621 | | /** 1622 | | Put side of a short strangle, BPR = 100% - (100% - SCR/2)*(price/strike) 1623 | | BUYING 1624 | | POWER 1625 | | REQUIREMENT 1626 | | ^ . 1627 | | | <- ITM . OTM -> 1628 | | 100% - |--__ . 1629 | | | ¯¯--__ . 1630 | | | ¯¯--__ . 1631 | | SCR/2 - | ¯¯--______ <------ base collateral is half that of a single-leg 1632 | | +--------------------+---> current 1633 | | 0 strike price 1634 | | */ 1635 | | unchecked { 1636 | | // A negative pool utilization is used to denote a position which is a strangle 1637 | | // at low pool utilization's strangle legs are evaluated at 2x capital efficiency 1638 | | 1639 | * | uint64 poolUtilization0 = uint64(poolUtilization); 1640 | * | uint64 poolUtilization1 = uint64(poolUtilization >> 64); 1641 | | 1642 | | // add 1 to handle poolUtilization = 0 1643 | | 1644 | * | poolUtilization = 1645 | * | uint128(uint64(-int64(poolUtilization0 == 0 ? 1 : poolUtilization0))) + 1646 | * | (uint128(uint64(-int64(poolUtilization1 == 0 ? 1 : poolUtilization1))) << 64); 1647 | | 1648 | | return 1649 | * | strangleRequired = _getRequiredCollateralSingleLegNoPartner( 1650 | * | tokenId, 1651 | * | index, 1652 | * | positionSize, 1653 | * | atTick, 1654 | * | poolUtilization 1655 | | ); 1656 | | } 1657 | | } 1658 | | } 1659 | |
/home/xshadow/panoptic-v1-core-private/contracts/PanopticFactory.sol 1 | | // SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1 2 | | pragma solidity ^0.8.18; 3 | | 4 | | // Interfaces 5 | | import {CollateralTracker} from "@contracts/CollateralTracker.sol"; 6 | | import {PanopticPool} from "@contracts/PanopticPool.sol"; 7 | | import {SemiFungiblePositionManager} from "@contracts/SemiFungiblePositionManager.sol"; 8 | | import {IUniswapV3Factory} from "univ3-core/interfaces/IUniswapV3Factory.sol"; 9 | | import {IUniswapV3Pool} from "univ3-core/interfaces/IUniswapV3Pool.sol"; 10 | | // Inherited implementations 11 | | import {Multicall} from "@base/Multicall.sol"; 12 | | import {FactoryNFT} from "@base/FactoryNFT.sol"; 13 | | // OpenZeppelin libraries 14 | | import {Clones} from "@openzeppelin/contracts/proxy/Clones.sol"; 15 | | // Libraries 16 | | import {CallbackLib} from "@libraries/CallbackLib.sol"; 17 | | import {Constants} from "@libraries/Constants.sol"; 18 | | import {Errors} from "@libraries/Errors.sol"; 19 | | import {Math} from "@libraries/Math.sol"; 20 | | import {PanopticMath} from "@libraries/PanopticMath.sol"; 21 | | import {SafeTransferLib} from "@libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol"; 22 | | // Custom types 23 | | import {Pointer} from "@types/Pointer.sol"; 24 | | 25 | | /// @title Panoptic Factory which creates and registers Panoptic Pools. 26 | | /// @author Axicon Labs Limited 27 | | /// @notice Facilitates deployment of Panoptic pools. 28 | | contract PanopticFactory is FactoryNFT, Multicall { 29 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 30 | | EVENTS 31 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 32 | | 33 | | /// @notice Emitted when a Panoptic Pool is created. 34 | | /// @param poolAddress Address of the deployed Panoptic pool 35 | | /// @param uniswapPool Address of the underlying Uniswap V3 pool 36 | | /// @param collateralTracker0 Address of the collateral tracker contract for token0 37 | | /// @param collateralTracker1 Address of the collateral tracker contract for token1 38 | | /// @param amount0 The amount of token0 deployed at full range 39 | | /// @param amount1 The amount of token1 deployed at full range 40 | | event PoolDeployed( 41 | | PanopticPool indexed poolAddress, 42 | | IUniswapV3Pool indexed uniswapPool, 43 | | CollateralTracker collateralTracker0, 44 | | CollateralTracker collateralTracker1, 45 | | uint256 amount0, 46 | | uint256 amount1 47 | | ); 48 | | 49 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 50 | | TYPES 51 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 52 | | 53 | | using Clones for address; 54 | | 55 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 56 | | CONSTANTS & IMMUTABLE 57 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 58 | | 59 | | /// @notice The Uniswap V3 factory contract to use. 60 | | IUniswapV3Factory internal immutable UNIV3_FACTORY; 61 | | 62 | | /// @notice The Semi Fungible Position Manager (SFPM) which tracks option positions across Panoptic Pools. 63 | | SemiFungiblePositionManager internal immutable SFPM; 64 | | 65 | | /// @notice Reference implementation of the `PanopticPool` to clone. 66 | | address internal immutable POOL_REFERENCE; 67 | | 68 | | /// @notice Reference implementation of the `CollateralTracker` to clone. 69 | | address internal immutable COLLATERAL_REFERENCE; 70 | | 71 | | /// @notice Address of the Wrapped Ether (or other numeraire token) contract. 72 | | address internal immutable WETH; 73 | | 74 | | /// @notice An amount of `WETH` deployed when initializing the SFPM against a new AMM pool. 75 | | /// @dev If we know one of the tokens is WETH, we deploy 0.1 ETH worth in tokens. 76 | | uint256 internal constant FULL_RANGE_LIQUIDITY_AMOUNT_WETH = 0.1 ether; 77 | | 78 | | /// @notice An amount of another token that's deployed when initializing the SFPM against a new AMM pool. 79 | | /// @dev Deploy 1e6 worth of tokens if not WETH. 80 | | uint256 internal constant FULL_RANGE_LIQUIDITY_AMOUNT_TOKEN = 1e6; 81 | | 82 | | /// @notice The `observationCardinalityNext` to set on the Uniswap pool when a new PanopticPool is deployed. 83 | | uint16 internal constant CARDINALITY_INCREASE = 100; 84 | | 85 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 86 | | STORAGE 87 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 88 | | 89 | | /// @notice Mapping from address(UniswapV3Pool) to address(PanopticPool) that stores the address of all deployed Panoptic Pools. 90 | | mapping(IUniswapV3Pool univ3pool => PanopticPool panopticPool) internal s_getPanopticPool; 91 | | 92 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 93 | | INITIALIZATION 94 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 95 | | 96 | | /// @notice Set immutable variables and store metadata pointers. 97 | | /// @param _WETH9 Address of the Wrapped Ether (or other numeraire token) contract 98 | | /// @param _SFPM The canonical `SemiFungiblePositionManager` deployment 99 | | /// @param _univ3Factory The canonical Uniswap V3 Factory deployment 100 | | /// @param _poolReference The reference implementation of the `PanopticPool` to clone 101 | | /// @param _collateralReference The reference implementation of the `CollateralTracker` to clone 102 | | /// @param properties An array of identifiers for different categories of metadata 103 | | /// @param indices A nested array of keys for K-V metadata pairs for each property in `properties` 104 | | /// @param pointers Contains pointers to the metadata values stored in contract data slices for each index in `indices` 105 | | constructor( 106 | | address _WETH9, 107 | | SemiFungiblePositionManager _SFPM, 108 | | IUniswapV3Factory _univ3Factory, 109 | | address _poolReference, 110 | | address _collateralReference, 111 | | bytes32[] memory properties, 112 | | uint256[][] memory indices, 113 | | Pointer[][] memory pointers 114 | | ) FactoryNFT(properties, indices, pointers) { 115 | | WETH = _WETH9; 116 | | SFPM = _SFPM; 117 | | UNIV3_FACTORY = _univ3Factory; 118 | | POOL_REFERENCE = _poolReference; 119 | | COLLATERAL_REFERENCE = _collateralReference; 120 | | } 121 | | 122 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 123 | | CALLBACK HANDLERS 124 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 125 | | 126 | | /// @notice Called after minting liquidity to a position. 127 | | /// @dev Pays the pool tokens owed for the minted liquidity from the payer (always the caller). 128 | | /// @param amount0Owed The amount of token0 due to the pool for the minted liquidity 129 | | /// @param amount1Owed The amount of token1 due to the pool for the minted liquidity 130 | | /// @param data Contains the payer address and the pool features required to validate the callback 131 | | function uniswapV3MintCallback( 132 | | uint256 amount0Owed, 133 | | uint256 amount1Owed, 134 | | bytes calldata data 135 | | ) external { 136 | | CallbackLib.CallbackData memory decoded = abi.decode(data, (CallbackLib.CallbackData)); 137 | | 138 | | CallbackLib.validateCallback(msg.sender, UNIV3_FACTORY, decoded.poolFeatures); 139 | | 140 | | if (amount0Owed > 0) 141 | | SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom( 142 | | decoded.poolFeatures.token0, 143 | | decoded.payer, 144 | | msg.sender, 145 | | amount0Owed 146 | | ); 147 | | if (amount1Owed > 0) 148 | | SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom( 149 | | decoded.poolFeatures.token1, 150 | | decoded.payer, 151 | | msg.sender, 152 | | amount1Owed 153 | | ); 154 | | } 155 | | 156 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 157 | | POOL DEPLOYMENT 158 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 159 | | 160 | | /// @notice Create a new Panoptic Pool linked to the given Uniswap pool identified uniquely by the incoming parameters. 161 | | /// @dev There is a 1:1 mapping between a Panoptic Pool and a Uniswap Pool. 162 | | /// @dev A Uniswap pool is uniquely identified by its tokens and the fee. 163 | | /// @dev Salt used in PanopticPool CREATE2 is [leading 20 msg.sender chars][leading 20 pool address chars][salt]. 164 | | /// @param token0 Address of token0 for the underlying Uniswap v3 pool 165 | | /// @param token1 Address of token1 for the underlying Uniswap v3 pool 166 | | /// @param fee The fee tier of the underlying Uniswap v3 pool, denominated in hundredths of bips 167 | | /// @param salt User-defined component of salt used in CREATE2 for the PanopticPool (must be a uint96 number) 168 | | /// @param amount0Max The maximum amount of token0 to spend on the full-range deployment, which serves as a slippage check 169 | | /// @param amount1Max The maximum amount of token1 to spend on the full-range deployment, which serves as a slippage check 170 | | /// @return newPoolContract The address of the newly deployed Panoptic pool 171 | | function deployNewPool( 172 | | address token0, 173 | | address token1, 174 | | uint24 fee, 175 | | uint96 salt, 176 | | uint256 amount0Max, 177 | | uint256 amount1Max 178 | | ) external returns (PanopticPool newPoolContract) { 179 | | // sort the tokens, if necessary: 180 | | (token0, token1) = token0 < token1 ? (token0, token1) : (token1, token0); 181 | | 182 | | IUniswapV3Pool v3Pool = IUniswapV3Pool(UNIV3_FACTORY.getPool(token0, token1, fee)); 183 | | if (address(v3Pool) == address(0)) revert Errors.UniswapPoolNotInitialized(); 184 | | 185 | | if (address(s_getPanopticPool[v3Pool]) != address(0)) 186 | | revert Errors.PoolAlreadyInitialized(); 187 | | 188 | | // initialize pool in SFPM if it has not already been initialized 189 | | SFPM.initializeAMMPool(token0, token1, fee); 190 | | 191 | | // Users can specify a salt, the aim is to incentivize the mining of addresses with leading zeros 192 | | // salt format: (first 20 characters of deployer address) + (first 20 characters of UniswapV3Pool) + (uint96 user supplied salt) 193 | | bytes32 salt32 = bytes32( 194 | | abi.encodePacked( 195 | | uint80(uint160(msg.sender) >> 80), 196 | | uint80(uint160(address(v3Pool)) >> 80), 197 | | salt 198 | | ) 199 | | ); 200 | | 201 | | // This creates a new Panoptic Pool (proxy to the PanopticPool implementation) 202 | | newPoolContract = PanopticPool(POOL_REFERENCE.cloneDeterministic(salt32)); 203 | | 204 | | // Deploy collateral token proxies 205 | | CollateralTracker collateralTracker0 = CollateralTracker( 206 | | Clones.clone(COLLATERAL_REFERENCE) 207 | | ); 208 | | CollateralTracker collateralTracker1 = CollateralTracker( 209 | | Clones.clone(COLLATERAL_REFERENCE) 210 | | ); 211 | | 212 | | // Run state initialization sequence for pool and collateral tokens 213 | | collateralTracker0.startToken(true, token0, token1, fee, newPoolContract); 214 | | collateralTracker1.startToken(false, token0, token1, fee, newPoolContract); 215 | | 216 | | newPoolContract.startPool(v3Pool, token0, token1, collateralTracker0, collateralTracker1); 217 | | 218 | | s_getPanopticPool[v3Pool] = newPoolContract; 219 | | 220 | | // The Panoptic pool won't be safe to use until the observation cardinality is at least CARDINALITY_INCREASE 221 | | // If this is not the case, we increase the next cardinality during deployment so the cardinality can catch up over time 222 | | // When that happens, there will be a period of time where the PanopticPool is deployed, but not (safely) usable 223 | | v3Pool.increaseObservationCardinalityNext(CARDINALITY_INCREASE); 224 | | 225 | | // Mints the full-range initial deposit 226 | | // which is why the deployer becomes also a "donor" of full-range liquidity 227 | | (uint256 amount0, uint256 amount1) = _mintFullRange(v3Pool, token0, token1, fee); 228 | | 229 | | if (amount0 > amount0Max || amount1 > amount1Max) revert Errors.PriceBoundFail(); 230 | | 231 | | // Issue reward NFT to donor 232 | | uint256 tokenId = uint256(uint160(address(newPoolContract))); 233 | | _mint(msg.sender, tokenId); 234 | | 235 | | emit PoolDeployed( 236 | | newPoolContract, 237 | | v3Pool, 238 | | collateralTracker0, 239 | | collateralTracker1, 240 | | amount0, 241 | | amount1 242 | | ); 243 | | } 244 | | 245 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 246 | | HELPERS 247 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 248 | | 249 | | /// @notice Find the salt which would give a Panoptic Pool the highest rarity within the search parameters. 250 | | /// @dev The rarity is defined in terms of how many leading zeros the Panoptic pool address has. 251 | | /// @dev Note that the final salt may overflow if too many loops are given relative to the amount in `salt`. 252 | | /// @param deployerAddress Address of the account that deploys the new PanopticPool 253 | | /// @param v3Pool Address of the underlying UniswapV3Pool 254 | | /// @param salt Salt value ([96-bit nonce]) to start from, useful as a checkpoint across multiple calls 255 | | /// @param loops The number of mining operations starting from 'salt' in trying to find the highest rarity 256 | | /// @param minTargetRarity The minimum target rarity to mine for. The internal loop stops when this is reached *or* when no more iterations 257 | | /// @return bestSalt The salt of the rarest pool (potentially at the specified minimum target) 258 | | /// @return highestRarity The rarity of `bestSalt` 259 | | function minePoolAddress( 260 | | address deployerAddress, 261 | | address v3Pool, 262 | | uint96 salt, 263 | | uint256 loops, 264 | | uint256 minTargetRarity 265 | | ) external view returns (uint96 bestSalt, uint256 highestRarity) { 266 | | // Start at the given 'salt' value (a checkpoint used to continue mining across multiple calls) 267 | | 268 | | // Runs until 'bestSalt' reaches 'minTargetRarity' or for 'loops', whichever comes first 269 | | uint256 maxSalt; 270 | | unchecked { 271 | | maxSalt = uint256(salt) + loops; 272 | | } 273 | | 274 | | for (; uint256(salt) < maxSalt; ) { 275 | | bytes32 newSalt = bytes32( 276 | | abi.encodePacked( 277 | | uint80(uint160(deployerAddress) >> 80), 278 | | uint80(uint160(v3Pool) >> 80), 279 | | salt 280 | | ) 281 | | ); 282 | | 283 | | uint256 rarity = PanopticMath.numberOfLeadingHexZeros( 284 | | POOL_REFERENCE.predictDeterministicAddress(newSalt) 285 | | ); 286 | | 287 | | if (rarity > highestRarity) { 288 | | // found a more rare address at this nonce 289 | | highestRarity = rarity; 290 | | bestSalt = salt; 291 | | } 292 | | 293 | | if (rarity >= minTargetRarity) { 294 | | // desired target met 295 | | highestRarity = rarity; 296 | | bestSalt = salt; 297 | | break; 298 | | } 299 | | 300 | | unchecked { 301 | | // increment the nonce of `currentSalt` (lower 96 bits) 302 | | salt += 1; 303 | | } 304 | | } 305 | | } 306 | | 307 | | /// @notice Seeds Uniswap V3 pool with a full-tick-range liquidity deployment using funds from caller. 308 | | /// @param v3Pool The address of the Uniswap V3 pool to deploy liquidity in 309 | | /// @param token0 The address of the first token in the Uniswap V3 pool 310 | | /// @param token1 The address of the second token in the Uniswap V3 pool 311 | | /// @param fee The fee level of the of the underlying Uniswap v3 pool, denominated in hundredths of bips 312 | | /// @return The amount of token0 deployed at full range 313 | | /// @return The amount of token1 deployed at full range 314 | | function _mintFullRange( 315 | | IUniswapV3Pool v3Pool, 316 | | address token0, 317 | | address token1, 318 | | uint24 fee 319 | | ) internal returns (uint256, uint256) { 320 | | (uint160 currentSqrtPriceX96, , , , , , ) = v3Pool.slot0(); 321 | | 322 | | // For full range: L = Δx * sqrt(P) = Δy / sqrt(P) 323 | | // We start with fixed token amounts and apply this equation to calculate the liquidity 324 | | // Note that for pools with a tickSpacing that is not a power of 2 or greater than 8 (887272 % ts != 0), 325 | | // a position at the maximum and minimum allowable ticks will be wide, but not necessarily full-range. 326 | | // In this case, the `fullRangeLiquidity` will always be an underestimate in respect to the token amounts required to mint. 327 | | uint128 fullRangeLiquidity; 328 | | unchecked { 329 | | // Since we know one of the tokens is WETH, we simply add 0.1 ETH + worth in tokens 330 | | if (token0 == WETH) { 331 | | fullRangeLiquidity = uint128( 332 | | Math.mulDiv96RoundingUp(FULL_RANGE_LIQUIDITY_AMOUNT_WETH, currentSqrtPriceX96) 333 | | ); 334 | | } else if (token1 == WETH) { 335 | | fullRangeLiquidity = uint128( 336 | | Math.mulDivRoundingUp( 337 | | FULL_RANGE_LIQUIDITY_AMOUNT_WETH, 338 | | Constants.FP96, 339 | | currentSqrtPriceX96 340 | | ) 341 | | ); 342 | | } else { 343 | | // Find the resulting liquidity for providing 1e6 of both tokens 344 | | uint128 liquidity0 = uint128( 345 | | Math.mulDiv96RoundingUp(FULL_RANGE_LIQUIDITY_AMOUNT_TOKEN, currentSqrtPriceX96) 346 | | ); 347 | | uint128 liquidity1 = uint128( 348 | | Math.mulDivRoundingUp( 349 | | FULL_RANGE_LIQUIDITY_AMOUNT_TOKEN, 350 | | Constants.FP96, 351 | | currentSqrtPriceX96 352 | | ) 353 | | ); 354 | | 355 | | // Pick the greater of the liquidities - i.e the more "expensive" option 356 | | // This ensures that the liquidity added is sufficiently large 357 | | fullRangeLiquidity = liquidity0 > liquidity1 ? liquidity0 : liquidity1; 358 | | } 359 | | } 360 | | 361 | | // The maximum range we can mint is determined by the tickSpacing of the pool 362 | | // The upper and lower ticks must be divisible by `tickSpacing`, so 363 | | // tickSpacing = 1: tU/L = +/-887272 364 | | // tickSpacing = 10: tU/L = +/-887270 365 | | // tickSpacing = 60: tU/L = +/-887220 366 | | // tickSpacing = 200: tU/L = +/-887200 367 | | int24 tickLower; 368 | | int24 tickUpper; 369 | | unchecked { 370 | | int24 tickSpacing = v3Pool.tickSpacing(); 371 | | tickLower = (Constants.MIN_V3POOL_TICK / tickSpacing) * tickSpacing; 372 | | tickUpper = -tickLower; 373 | | } 374 | | 375 | | bytes memory mintCallback = abi.encode( 376 | | CallbackLib.CallbackData({ 377 | | poolFeatures: CallbackLib.PoolFeatures({token0: token0, token1: token1, fee: fee}), 378 | | payer: msg.sender 379 | | }) 380 | | ); 381 | | 382 | | return 383 | | IUniswapV3Pool(v3Pool).mint( 384 | | address(this), 385 | | tickLower, 386 | | tickUpper, 387 | | fullRangeLiquidity, 388 | | mintCallback 389 | | ); 390 | | } 391 | | 392 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 393 | | QUERIES 394 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 395 | | 396 | | /// @notice Return the address of the Panoptic Pool associated with 'univ3pool'. 397 | | /// @param univ3pool The Uniswap V3 pool address to query 398 | | /// @return Address of the Panoptic Pool associated with 'univ3pool' 399 | | function getPanopticPool(IUniswapV3Pool univ3pool) external view returns (PanopticPool) { 400 | | return s_getPanopticPool[univ3pool]; 401 | | } 402 | | } 403 | |
/home/xshadow/panoptic-v1-core-private/contracts/PanopticPool.sol 1 | | // SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1 2 | | pragma solidity ^0.8.18; 3 | | 4 | | // Interfaces 5 | | import {CollateralTracker} from "@contracts/CollateralTracker.sol"; 6 | | import {SemiFungiblePositionManager} from "@contracts/SemiFungiblePositionManager.sol"; 7 | | import {IUniswapV3Pool} from "univ3-core/interfaces/IUniswapV3Pool.sol"; 8 | | // Inherited implementations 9 | | import {ERC1155Holder} from "@openzeppelin/contracts/token/ERC1155/utils/ERC1155Holder.sol"; 10 | | import {Multicall} from "@base/Multicall.sol"; 11 | | // Libraries 12 | | import {Constants} from "@libraries/Constants.sol"; 13 | | import {Errors} from "@libraries/Errors.sol"; 14 | | import {FeesCalc} from "@libraries/FeesCalc.sol"; 15 | | import {InteractionHelper} from "@libraries/InteractionHelper.sol"; 16 | | import {Math} from "@libraries/Math.sol"; 17 | | import {PanopticMath} from "@libraries/PanopticMath.sol"; 18 | | // Custom types 19 | * | import {LeftRightUnsigned, LeftRightSigned} from "@types/LeftRight.sol"; 20 | * | import {LiquidityChunk} from "@types/LiquidityChunk.sol"; 21 | | import {TokenId} from "@types/TokenId.sol"; 22 | | 23 | * | /// @title The Panoptic Pool: Create permissionless options on a CLAMM. 24 | * | /// @author Axicon Labs Limited 25 | | /// @notice Manages positions, collateral, liquidations and forced exercises. 26 | | contract PanopticPool is ERC1155Holder, Multicall { 27 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 28 | | EVENTS 29 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 30 | | 31 | | /// @notice Emitted when an account is liquidated. 32 | | /// @dev Need to unpack bonusAmounts to get raw numbers, which are always positive. 33 | | /// @param liquidator Address of the caller liquidating the distressed account 34 | | /// @param liquidatee Address of the distressed/liquidatable account 35 | | /// @param bonusAmounts LeftRight encoding for the the bonus paid for token 0 (right slot) and 1 (left slot) from the Panoptic Pool to the liquidator 36 | | event AccountLiquidated( 37 | | address indexed liquidator, 38 | | address indexed liquidatee, 39 | | LeftRightSigned bonusAmounts 40 | | ); 41 | | 42 | | /// @notice Emitted when a position is force exercised. 43 | | /// @param exercisor Address of the account that forces the exercise of the position 44 | | /// @param user Address of the owner of the liquidated position 45 | | /// @param tokenId TokenId of the liquidated position 46 | | /// @param exerciseFee LeftRight encoding for the cost paid by the exercisor to force the exercise of the token; 47 | | /// the cost for token 0 (right slot) and 1 (left slot) is represented as negative 48 | | event ForcedExercised( 49 | | address indexed exercisor, 50 | | address indexed user, 51 | | TokenId indexed tokenId, 52 | | LeftRightSigned exerciseFee 53 | | ); 54 | | 55 | | /// @notice Emitted when premium is settled independent of a mint/burn (e.g. during `settleLongPremium`). 56 | | /// @param user Address of the owner of the settled position 57 | | /// @param tokenId TokenId of the settled position 58 | | /// @param settledAmounts LeftRight encoding for the amount of premium settled for token0 (right slot) and token1 (left slot) 59 | | event PremiumSettled( 60 | | address indexed user, 61 | | TokenId indexed tokenId, 62 | | LeftRightSigned settledAmounts 63 | | ); 64 | | 65 | | /// @notice Emitted when an option is burned. 66 | | /// @param recipient User that burnt the option 67 | | /// @param positionSize The number of contracts burnt, expressed in terms of the asset 68 | | /// @param tokenId TokenId of the burnt option 69 | | /// @param premia LeftRight packing for the amount of premia collected for token0 (right) and token1 (left) 70 | | event OptionBurnt( 71 | | address indexed recipient, 72 | | uint128 positionSize, 73 | | TokenId indexed tokenId, 74 | | LeftRightSigned premia 75 | | ); 76 | | 77 | | /// @notice Emitted when an option is minted. 78 | | /// @param recipient User that minted the option 79 | | /// @param positionSize The number of contracts minted, expressed in terms of the asset 80 | | /// @param tokenId TokenId of the created option 81 | | /// @param poolUtilizations Packing of the pool utilization (how much funds are in the Panoptic pool versus the AMM pool at the time of minting), 82 | | /// right 64bits for token0 and left 64bits for token1, defined as (inAMM * 10_000) / totalAssets() 83 | | /// where totalAssets is the total tracked assets in the AMM and PanopticPool minus fees and donations to the Panoptic pool 84 | | event OptionMinted( 85 | | address indexed recipient, 86 | | uint128 positionSize, 87 | | TokenId indexed tokenId, 88 | | uint128 poolUtilizations 89 | | ); 90 | | 91 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 92 | | IMMUTABLES & CONSTANTS 93 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 94 | | 95 | | /// @notice Lower price bound used when no slippage check is required. 96 | | int24 internal constant MIN_SWAP_TICK = Constants.MIN_V3POOL_TICK - 1; 97 | | 98 | | /// @notice Upper price bound used when no slippage check is required. 99 | | int24 internal constant MAX_SWAP_TICK = Constants.MAX_V3POOL_TICK + 1; 100 | | 101 | | /// @notice Flag that signals to compute premia for both the short and long legs of a position. 102 | * | bool internal constant COMPUTE_ALL_PREMIA = true; 103 | | /// @notice Flag that signals to compute premia only for the long legs of a position. 104 | | bool internal constant COMPUTE_LONG_PREMIA = false; 105 | | 106 | | /// @notice Flag that indicates only to include the share of (settled) premium that is available to collect when calling `_calculateAccumulatedPremia`. 107 | | bool internal constant ONLY_AVAILABLE_PREMIUM = false; 108 | | 109 | | /// @notice Flag that signals to commit both collected Uniswap fees and settled long premium to `s_settledTokens`. 110 | * | bool internal constant COMMIT_LONG_SETTLED = true; 111 | | /// @notice Flag that signals to only commit collected Uniswap fees to `s_settledTokens`. 112 | | bool internal constant DONOT_COMMIT_LONG_SETTLED = false; 113 | | 114 | | /// @notice Flag that signals to add a new position to the user's positions hash (as opposed to removing an existing position). 115 | | bool internal constant ADD = true; 116 | | 117 | | /// @notice The minimum window (in seconds) used to calculate the TWAP price for solvency checks during liquidations. 118 | | uint32 internal constant TWAP_WINDOW = 600; 119 | | 120 | | /// @notice The maximum allowed delta between the currentTick and the Uniswap TWAP tick during a liquidation (~5% down, ~5.26% up). 121 | | /// @dev Mitigates manipulation of the currentTick that causes positions to be liquidated at a less favorable price. 122 | | int256 internal constant MAX_TWAP_DELTA_LIQUIDATION = 513; 123 | | 124 | | /// @notice The maximum allowed cumulative delta between: the fast & slow oracle tick, the current & slow oracle tick, and the last-observed & slow oracle tick. 125 | | /// @dev Falls back on the more conservative (less solvent) tick during times of extreme volatility, where the price moves ~10% in <4 minutes. 126 | * | int256 internal constant MAX_TICKS_DELTA = 953; 127 | | 128 | | /// @notice The maximum allowed ratio for a single chunk, defined as: removedLiquidity / netLiquidity. 129 | | /// @dev The long premium spread multiplier that corresponds with the MAX_SPREAD value depends on VEGOID, 130 | | /// which can be explored in this calculator: https://www.desmos.com/calculator/mdeqob2m04. 131 | | uint64 internal constant MAX_SPREAD = 9 * (2 ** 32); 132 | | 133 | | /// @notice The maximum allowed number of opened positions for a user. 134 | | uint64 internal constant MAX_POSITIONS = 32; 135 | | 136 | | /// @notice Multiplier in basis points for the collateral requirement in the event of a buying power decrease, such as minting or force exercising another user. 137 | | uint256 internal constant BP_DECREASE_BUFFER = 13_333; 138 | | 139 | | /// @notice Multiplier for the collateral requirement in the general case. 140 | | uint256 internal constant NO_BUFFER = 10_000; 141 | | 142 | | /// @notice The "engine" of Panoptic - manages AMM liquidity and executes all mints/burns/exercises. 143 | | SemiFungiblePositionManager internal immutable SFPM; 144 | | 145 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 146 | | STORAGE 147 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 148 | | 149 | | /// @notice The Uniswap v3 pool that this instance of Panoptic is deployed on. 150 | | IUniswapV3Pool internal s_univ3pool; 151 | | 152 | | /// @notice Stores a sorted set of 8 price observations used to compute the internal median oracle price. 153 | | // The data for the last 8 interactions is stored as such: 154 | | // LAST UPDATED BLOCK TIMESTAMP (40 bits) 155 | | // [BLOCK.TIMESTAMP] 156 | | // (00000000000000000000000000000000) // dynamic 157 | | // 158 | | // ORDERING of tick indices least --> greatest (24 bits) 159 | | // The value of the bit codon ([#]) is a pointer to a tick index in the tick array. 160 | | // The position of the bit codon from most to least significant is the ordering of the 161 | | // tick index it points to from least to greatest. 162 | | // 163 | | // [7] [5] [3] [1] [0] [2] [4] [6] 164 | | // 111 101 011 001 000 010 100 110 165 | | // 166 | | // [Constants.MIN_V3POOL_TICK-1] [7] 167 | | // 111100100111011000010111 168 | | // 169 | | // [Constants.MAX_V3POOL_TICK+1] [0] 170 | | // 000011011000100111101001 171 | | // 172 | | // [Constants.MIN_V3POOL_TICK-1] [6] 173 | | // 111100100111011000010111 174 | | // 175 | | // [Constants.MAX_V3POOL_TICK+1] [1] 176 | | // 000011011000100111101001 177 | | // 178 | | // [Constants.MIN_V3POOL_TICK-1] [5] 179 | | // 111100100111011000010111 180 | | // 181 | | // [Constants.MAX_V3POOL_TICK+1] [2] 182 | | // 000011011000100111101001 183 | | // 184 | | // [CURRENT TICK] [4] 185 | | // (000000000000000000000000) // dynamic 186 | | // 187 | | // [CURRENT TICK] [3] 188 | | // (000000000000000000000000) // dynamic 189 | | uint256 internal s_miniMedian; 190 | | 191 | | // ERC4626 vaults that users collateralize their positions with 192 | | // Each token has its own vault, listed in the same order as the tokens in the pool 193 | | // In addition to collateral deposits, these vaults also handle various collateral/bonus/exercise computations 194 | | 195 | | /// @notice Collateral vault for token0 in the Uniswap pool. 196 | | CollateralTracker internal s_collateralToken0; 197 | | /// @notice Collateral vault for token1 in the Uniswap pool. 198 | | CollateralTracker internal s_collateralToken1; 199 | | 200 | | /// @notice Nested mapping that tracks the option formation: address => tokenId => leg => premiaGrowth. 201 | | /// @dev Premia growth is taking a snapshot of the chunk premium in SFPM, which is measuring the amount of fees 202 | | /// collected for every chunk per unit of liquidity (net or short, depending on the isLong value of the specific leg index). 203 | | mapping(address account => mapping(TokenId tokenId => mapping(uint256 leg => LeftRightUnsigned premiaGrowth))) 204 | | internal s_options; 205 | | 206 | | /// @notice Per-chunk `last` value that gives the aggregate amount of premium owed to all sellers when multiplied by the total amount of liquidity `totalLiquidity`. 207 | | /// @dev totalGrossPremium = totalLiquidity * (grossPremium(perLiquidityX64) - lastGrossPremium(perLiquidityX64)) / 2**64. 208 | | /// @dev Used to compute the denominator for the fraction of premium available to sellers to collect. 209 | | /// @dev LeftRight - right slot is token0, left slot is token1. 210 | | mapping(bytes32 chunkKey => LeftRightUnsigned lastGrossPremium) internal s_grossPremiumLast; 211 | | 212 | | /// @notice Per-chunk accumulator for tokens owed to sellers that have been settled and are now available. 213 | | /// @dev This number increases when buyers pay long premium and when tokens are collected from Uniswap. 214 | | /// @dev It decreases when sellers close positions and collect the premium they are owed. 215 | | /// @dev LeftRight - right slot is token0, left slot is token1. 216 | | mapping(bytes32 chunkKey => LeftRightUnsigned settledTokens) internal s_settledTokens; 217 | | 218 | | /// @notice Tracks the amount of liquidity for a user+tokenId (right slot) and the initial pool utilizations when that position was minted (left slot). 219 | | // poolUtilizations when minted (left) liquidity=ERC1155 balance (right) 220 | | // token0 token1 221 | | // |<-- 64 bits -->|<-- 64 bits -->|<---------- 128 bits ---------->| 222 | | // |<-------------------------- 256 bits -------------------------->| 223 | | mapping(address account => mapping(TokenId tokenId => LeftRightUnsigned balanceAndUtilizations)) 224 | | internal s_positionBalance; 225 | | 226 | | // numPositions (32 positions max) user positions hash 227 | | // |<-- 8 bits -->|<------------------ 248 bits ------------------->| 228 | | // |<---------------------- 256 bits ------------------------------>| 229 | | /// @notice Tracks the position list hash i.e keccak256(XORs of abi.encodePacked(positionIdList)). 230 | | /// @dev The order and content of this list (the preimage for the hash) is emitted in an event every time it is changed. 231 | | /// @dev A component of this hash also tracks the total number of positions (i.e. makes sure the length of the provided positionIdList matches). 232 | | /// @dev The purpose of this system is to reduce storage usage when a user has more than one active position. 233 | | /// @dev Instead of having to manage an unwieldy storage array and do lots of loads, we just store a hash of the array. 234 | | /// @dev This hash can be cheaply verified on every operation with a user provided positionIdList - which can then be used for operations 235 | | /// without having to every load any other data from storage. 236 | | // numPositions (32 positions max) user positions hash 237 | | // |<-- 8 bits -->|<------------------ 248 bits ------------------->| 238 | | // |<---------------------- 256 bits ------------------------------>| 239 | | mapping(address account => uint256 positionsHash) internal s_positionsHash; 240 | | 241 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 242 | | INITIALIZATION 243 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 244 | | 245 | | /// @notice Store the address of the canonical SemiFungiblePositionManager (SFPM) contract. 246 | | /// @param _sfpm The address of the SFPM 247 | | constructor(SemiFungiblePositionManager _sfpm) { 248 | | SFPM = _sfpm; 249 | | } 250 | | 251 | | /// @notice Initializes a Panoptic Pool on top of an existing Uniswap v3 + collateral vault pair. 252 | | /// @dev Must be called first (by a factory contract) before any transaction can occur. 253 | | /// @param _univ3pool Address of the target Uniswap v3 pool 254 | | /// @param token0 Address of the pool's token0 255 | | /// @param token1 Address of the pool's token1 256 | | /// @param collateralTracker0 Address of the collateral vault for token0 257 | | /// @param collateralTracker1 Address of the collateral vault for token1 258 | | function startPool( 259 | | IUniswapV3Pool _univ3pool, 260 | | address token0, 261 | | address token1, 262 | | CollateralTracker collateralTracker0, 263 | | CollateralTracker collateralTracker1 264 | | ) external { 265 | | // reverts if the Uniswap pool has already been initialized 266 | | if (address(s_univ3pool) != address(0)) revert Errors.PoolAlreadyInitialized(); 267 | | 268 | | // Store the univ3Pool variable 269 | | s_univ3pool = IUniswapV3Pool(_univ3pool); 270 | | 271 | | (, int24 currentTick, , , , , ) = IUniswapV3Pool(_univ3pool).slot0(); 272 | | 273 | | // Store the median data 274 | | unchecked { 275 | | s_miniMedian = 276 | | (uint256(block.timestamp) << 216) + 277 | | // magic number which adds (7,5,3,1,0,2,4,6) order and minTick in positions 7, 5, 3 and maxTick in 6, 4, 2 278 | | // see comment on s_miniMedian initialization for format of this magic number 279 | | (uint256(0xF590A6F276170D89E9F276170D89E9F276170D89E9000000000000)) + 280 | | (uint256(uint24(currentTick)) << 24) + // add to slot 4 281 | | (uint256(uint24(currentTick))); // add to slot 3 282 | | } 283 | | 284 | | // Store the collateral token0 285 | | s_collateralToken0 = collateralTracker0; 286 | | s_collateralToken1 = collateralTracker1; 287 | | 288 | | // consolidate all 4 approval calls to one library delegatecall in order to reduce bytecode size 289 | | // approves: 290 | | // SFPM: token0, token1 291 | | // CollateralTracker0 - token0 292 | | // CollateralTracker1 - token1 293 | | InteractionHelper.doApprovals(SFPM, collateralTracker0, collateralTracker1, token0, token1); 294 | | } 295 | | 296 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 297 | | QUERY HELPERS 298 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 299 | | 300 | | /// @notice Reverts if the caller has a lower collateral balance than required to meet the provided `minValue0` and `minValue1`. 301 | | /// @dev Can be used for composable slippage checks with `multicall` (such as for a force exercise or liquidation). 302 | | /// @param minValue0 The minimum acceptable `token0` value of collateral 303 | | /// @param minValue1 The minimum acceptable `token1` value of collateral 304 | | function assertMinCollateralValues(uint256 minValue0, uint256 minValue1) external view { 305 | | CollateralTracker ct0 = s_collateralToken0; 306 | | CollateralTracker ct1 = s_collateralToken1; 307 | | if ( 308 | | ct0.convertToAssets(ct0.balanceOf(msg.sender)) < minValue0 || 309 | | ct1.convertToAssets(ct1.balanceOf(msg.sender)) < minValue1 310 | | ) revert Errors.AccountInsolvent(); 311 | | } 312 | | 313 | | /// @notice Determines if account is eligible to withdraw or transfer collateral. 314 | | /// @dev Checks whether account is solvent with `BP_DECREASE_BUFFER` according to `_validateSolvency`. 315 | | /// @dev Prevents insolvent and near-insolvent accounts from withdrawing collateral before they are liquidated. 316 | | /// @dev Reverts if account is not solvent with `BP_DECREASE_BUFFER`. 317 | | /// @param user The account to check for collateral withdrawal eligibility 318 | | /// @param positionIdList The list of all option positions held by `user` 319 | | function validateCollateralWithdrawable( 320 | | address user, 321 | | TokenId[] calldata positionIdList 322 | | ) external view { 323 | | _validateSolvency(user, positionIdList, BP_DECREASE_BUFFER); 324 | | } 325 | | 326 | | /// @notice Returns the total number of contracts owned by user for a specified position. 327 | | /// @param user Address of the account to be checked 328 | | /// @param tokenId TokenId of the option position to be checked 329 | | /// @return balance Number of contracts of tokenId owned by the user 330 | | /// @return poolUtilization0 The utilization of token0 in the Panoptic pool at mint 331 | | /// @return poolUtilization1 The utilization of token1 in the Panoptic pool at mint 332 | | function optionPositionBalance( 333 | | address user, 334 | | TokenId tokenId 335 | | ) external view returns (uint128 balance, uint64 poolUtilization0, uint64 poolUtilization1) { 336 | | // Extract the data stored in s_positionBalance for the provided user + tokenId 337 | | LeftRightUnsigned balanceData = s_positionBalance[user][tokenId]; 338 | | 339 | | // Return the unpacked data: balanceOf(user, tokenId) and packed pool utilizations at the time of minting 340 | | balance = balanceData.rightSlot(); 341 | | 342 | | // pool utilizations are packed into a single uint128 343 | | 344 | | // the 64 least significant bits are the utilization of token0, so we can simply cast to uint64 to extract it 345 | | // (cutting off the 64 most significant bits) 346 | | poolUtilization0 = uint64(balanceData.leftSlot()); 347 | | 348 | | // the 64 most significant bits are the utilization of token1, so we can shift the number to the right by 64 to extract it 349 | | // (shifting away the 64 least significant bits) 350 | | poolUtilization1 = uint64(balanceData.leftSlot() >> 64); 351 | | } 352 | | 353 | | /// @notice Compute the total amount of premium accumulated for a list of positions. 354 | | /// @param user Address of the user that owns the positions 355 | | /// @param positionIdList List of positions. Written as [tokenId1, tokenId2, ...] 356 | | /// @param includePendingPremium If true, include premium that is owed to the user but has not yet settled; if false, only include premium that is available to collect 357 | | /// @return premium0 Premium for token0 (negative = amount is owed) 358 | | /// @return premium1 Premium for token1 (negative = amount is owed) 359 | | /// @return A list of balances and pool utilization for each position, of the form [[tokenId0, balances0], [tokenId1, balances1], ...] 360 | * | function calculateAccumulatedFeesBatch( 361 | | address user, 362 | | bool includePendingPremium, 363 | | TokenId[] calldata positionIdList 364 | * | ) external view returns (int128 premium0, int128 premium1, uint256[2][] memory) { 365 | | // Get the current tick of the Uniswap pool 366 | * | (, int24 currentTick, , , , , ) = s_univ3pool.slot0(); 367 | | 368 | | // Compute the accumulated premia for all tokenId in positionIdList (includes short+long premium) 369 | * | (LeftRightSigned premia, uint256[2][] memory balances) = _calculateAccumulatedPremia( 370 | * | user, 371 | * | positionIdList, 372 | | COMPUTE_ALL_PREMIA, 373 | * | includePendingPremium, 374 | * | currentTick 375 | | ); 376 | | 377 | | // Return the premia as (token0, token1) 378 | * | return (premia.rightSlot(), premia.leftSlot(), balances); 379 | | } 380 | | 381 | | /// @notice Compute the net token amounts owned by a given positionIdList in the Uniswap pool at the given price tick. 382 | | /// @param user Address of the user that owns the positions. 383 | | /// @param atTick Tick at which the portfolio value is evaluated 384 | | /// @param positionIdList List of positions. Written as [tokenId1, tokenId2, ...] 385 | | /// @return value0 Net amount of token0 in the Uniswap pool owned by the positionIdList (negative = borrowed liquidity owed to sellers) 386 | | /// @return value1 Net amount of token1 in the Uniswap pool owned by the positionIdList (negative = borrowed liquidity owed to sellers) 387 | | function calculatePortfolioValue( 388 | | address user, 389 | | int24 atTick, 390 | | TokenId[] calldata positionIdList 391 | | ) external view returns (int256 value0, int256 value1) { 392 | | (value0, value1) = FeesCalc.getPortfolioValue( 393 | | atTick, 394 | | s_positionBalance[user], 395 | | positionIdList 396 | | ); 397 | | } 398 | | 399 | | /// @notice Calculate the accumulated premia owed from the option buyer to the option seller. 400 | | /// @param user The holder of options 401 | | /// @param positionIdList The list of all option positions held by user 402 | | /// @param computeAllPremia Whether to compute accumulated premia for all legs held by the user (true), or just owed premia for long legs (false) 403 | | /// @param includePendingPremium If true, include premium that is owed to the user but has not yet settled; if false, only include premium that is available to collect 404 | | /// @return portfolioPremium The computed premia of the user's positions, where premia contains the accumulated premia for token0 in the right slot and for token1 in the left slot 405 | | /// @return balances A list of balances and pool utilization for each position, of the form [[tokenId0, balances0], [tokenId1, balances1], ...] 406 | * | function _calculateAccumulatedPremia( 407 | | address user, 408 | | TokenId[] calldata positionIdList, 409 | | bool computeAllPremia, 410 | | bool includePendingPremium, 411 | | int24 atTick 412 | * | ) internal view returns (LeftRightSigned portfolioPremium, uint256[2][] memory balances) { 413 | * | uint256 pLength = positionIdList.length; 414 | * | balances = new uint256[2][](pLength); 415 | | 416 | * | address c_user = user; 417 | | // loop through each option position/tokenId 418 | * | for (uint256 k = 0; k < pLength; ) { 419 | | TokenId tokenId = positionIdList[k]; 420 | | 421 | | balances[k][0] = TokenId.unwrap(tokenId); 422 | | balances[k][1] = LeftRightUnsigned.unwrap(s_positionBalance[c_user][tokenId]); 423 | | 424 | | ( 425 | | LeftRightSigned[4] memory premiaByLeg, 426 | | uint256[2][4] memory premiumAccumulatorsByLeg 427 | | ) = _getPremia( 428 | | tokenId, 429 | | LeftRightUnsigned.wrap(balances[k][1]).rightSlot(), 430 | | c_user, 431 | | computeAllPremia, 432 | | atTick 433 | | ); 434 | | 435 | | uint256 numLegs = tokenId.countLegs(); 436 | | for (uint256 leg = 0; leg < numLegs; ) { 437 | | if (tokenId.isLong(leg) == 0 && !includePendingPremium) { 438 | | bytes32 chunkKey = keccak256( 439 | | abi.encodePacked( 440 | | tokenId.strike(leg), 441 | | tokenId.width(leg), 442 | | tokenId.tokenType(leg) 443 | | ) 444 | | ); 445 | | 446 | | (uint256 totalLiquidity, , ) = _getLiquidities(tokenId, leg); 447 | | LeftRightUnsigned availablePremium = _getAvailablePremium( 448 | | totalLiquidity, 449 | | s_settledTokens[chunkKey], 450 | | s_grossPremiumLast[chunkKey], 451 | | LeftRightUnsigned.wrap(uint256(LeftRightSigned.unwrap(premiaByLeg[leg]))), 452 | | premiumAccumulatorsByLeg[leg] 453 | | ); 454 | | portfolioPremium = portfolioPremium.add( 455 | | LeftRightSigned.wrap(int256(LeftRightUnsigned.unwrap(availablePremium))) 456 | | ); 457 | | } else { 458 | | portfolioPremium = portfolioPremium.add(premiaByLeg[leg]); 459 | | } 460 | | unchecked { 461 | | ++leg; 462 | | } 463 | | } 464 | | 465 | | unchecked { 466 | | ++k; 467 | | } 468 | | } 469 | * | return (portfolioPremium, balances); 470 | | } 471 | | 472 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 473 | | ONBOARD MEDIAN TWAP 474 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 475 | | 476 | | /// @notice Updates the internal median with the last Uniswap observation if the MEDIAN_PERIOD has elapsed. 477 | | function pokeMedian() external { 478 | | (, , uint16 observationIndex, uint16 observationCardinality, , , ) = s_univ3pool.slot0(); 479 | | 480 | | (, uint256 medianData) = PanopticMath.computeInternalMedian( 481 | | observationIndex, 482 | | observationCardinality, 483 | | Constants.MEDIAN_PERIOD, 484 | | s_miniMedian, 485 | | s_univ3pool 486 | | ); 487 | | 488 | | if (medianData != 0) s_miniMedian = medianData; 489 | | } 490 | | 491 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 492 | | MINT/BURN INTERFACE 493 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 494 | | 495 | | /// @notice Validates the current options of the user, and mints a new position. 496 | | /// @param positionIdList The list of currently held positions by the user, where the newly minted position(token) will be the last element in 'positionIdList' 497 | | /// @param positionSize The size of the position to be minted, expressed in terms of the asset 498 | | /// @param effectiveLiquidityLimitX32 Maximum amount of "spread" defined as removedLiquidity/netLiquidity for a new position and 499 | | /// denominated as X32 = (ratioLimit * 2**32) 500 | | /// @param tickLimitLow The lower bound of an acceptable open interval for the ending price 501 | | /// @param tickLimitHigh The upper bound of an acceptable open interval for the ending price 502 | * | function mintOptions( 503 | | TokenId[] calldata positionIdList, 504 | | uint128 positionSize, 505 | | uint64 effectiveLiquidityLimitX32, 506 | | int24 tickLimitLow, 507 | | int24 tickLimitHigh 508 | | ) external { 509 | * | _mintOptions( 510 | * | positionIdList, 511 | * | positionSize, 512 | * | effectiveLiquidityLimitX32, 513 | * | tickLimitLow, 514 | * | tickLimitHigh 515 | | ); 516 | | } 517 | | 518 | | /// @notice Closes and burns the caller's entire balance of `tokenId`. 519 | | /// @param tokenId The tokenId of the option position to be burnt 520 | | /// @param newPositionIdList The new positionIdList without the token being burnt 521 | | /// @param tickLimitLow The lower bound of an acceptable open interval for the ending price 522 | | /// @param tickLimitHigh The upper bound of an acceptable open interval for the ending price 523 | * | function burnOptions( 524 | | TokenId tokenId, 525 | | TokenId[] calldata newPositionIdList, 526 | | int24 tickLimitLow, 527 | | int24 tickLimitHigh 528 | | ) external { 529 | * | _burnOptions(COMMIT_LONG_SETTLED, tokenId, msg.sender, tickLimitLow, tickLimitHigh); 530 | | 531 | | uint256 medianData = _validateSolvency(msg.sender, newPositionIdList, NO_BUFFER); 532 | | 533 | | // Update `s_miniMedian` with a new observation if the last observation is old enough (returned medianData is nonzero) 534 | | if (medianData != 0) s_miniMedian = medianData; 535 | | } 536 | | 537 | | /// @notice Closes and burns the caller's entire balance of each `tokenId` in `positionIdList. 538 | | /// @param positionIdList The list of tokenIds for the option positions to be burnt 539 | | /// @param newPositionIdList The new positionIdList without the token(s) being burnt 540 | | /// @param tickLimitLow The lower bound of an acceptable open interval for the ending price 541 | | /// @param tickLimitHigh The upper bound of an acceptable open interval for the ending price 542 | * | function burnOptions( 543 | | TokenId[] calldata positionIdList, 544 | | TokenId[] calldata newPositionIdList, 545 | | int24 tickLimitLow, 546 | | int24 tickLimitHigh 547 | * | ) external { 548 | | _burnAllOptionsFrom( 549 | | msg.sender, 550 | | tickLimitLow, 551 | | tickLimitHigh, 552 | | COMMIT_LONG_SETTLED, 553 | | positionIdList 554 | | ); 555 | | 556 | | uint256 medianData = _validateSolvency(msg.sender, newPositionIdList, NO_BUFFER); 557 | | 558 | | // Update `s_miniMedian` with a new observation if the last observation is old enough (returned medianData is nonzero) 559 | * | if (medianData != 0) s_miniMedian = medianData; 560 | | } 561 | | 562 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 563 | | POSITION MINTING LOGIC 564 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 565 | | 566 | | /// @notice Validates the current options of the user, and mints a new position. 567 | | /// @param positionIdList The list of currently held positions by the user, where the newly minted position(token) will be the last element in 'positionIdList' 568 | | /// @param positionSize The size of the position to be minted, expressed in terms of the asset 569 | | /// @param effectiveLiquidityLimitX32 Maximum amount of "spread" defined as removedLiquidity/netLiquidity for a new position and 570 | | /// denominated as X32 = (ratioLimit * 2**32) 571 | | /// @param tickLimitLow The lower bound of an acceptable open interval for the ending price 572 | | /// @param tickLimitHigh The upper bound of an acceptable open interval for the ending price 573 | * | function _mintOptions( 574 | | TokenId[] calldata positionIdList, 575 | | uint128 positionSize, 576 | | uint64 effectiveLiquidityLimitX32, 577 | | int24 tickLimitLow, 578 | | int24 tickLimitHigh 579 | | ) internal { 580 | | // the new tokenId will be the last element in 'positionIdList' 581 | * | TokenId tokenId; 582 | | unchecked { 583 | * | tokenId = positionIdList[positionIdList.length - 1]; 584 | | } 585 | | 586 | | // do duplicate checks and the checks related to minting and positions 587 | * | _validatePositionList(msg.sender, positionIdList, 1); 588 | | 589 | | // make sure the tokenId is for this Panoptic pool 590 | * | if (tokenId.poolId() != SFPM.getPoolId(address(s_univ3pool))) 591 | | revert Errors.InvalidTokenIdParameter(0); 592 | | 593 | | // disallow user to mint exact same position 594 | | // in order to do it, user should burn it first and then mint 595 | * | if (LeftRightUnsigned.unwrap(s_positionBalance[msg.sender][tokenId]) != 0) 596 | | revert Errors.PositionAlreadyMinted(); 597 | | 598 | | // Mint in the SFPM and update state of collateral 599 | * | uint128 poolUtilizations = _mintInSFPMAndUpdateCollateral( 600 | * | tokenId, 601 | * | positionSize, 602 | * | effectiveLiquidityLimitX32, 603 | * | tickLimitLow, 604 | * | tickLimitHigh 605 | | ); 606 | | 607 | | // update the users options balance of position 'tokenId' 608 | | // NOTE: user can't mint same position multiple times, so set the positionSize instead of adding 609 | | s_positionBalance[msg.sender][tokenId] = LeftRightUnsigned 610 | | .wrap(0) 611 | | .toLeftSlot(poolUtilizations) 612 | | .toRightSlot(positionSize); 613 | | 614 | | // Perform solvency check on user's account to ensure they had enough buying power to mint the option 615 | | // Add an initial buffer to the collateral requirement to prevent users from minting their account close to insolvency 616 | | uint256 medianData = _validateSolvency(msg.sender, positionIdList, BP_DECREASE_BUFFER); 617 | | 618 | | // Update `s_miniMedian` with a new observation if the last observation is old enough (returned medianData is nonzero) 619 | | if (medianData != 0) s_miniMedian = medianData; 620 | | 621 | | emit OptionMinted(msg.sender, positionSize, tokenId, poolUtilizations); 622 | | } 623 | | 624 | | /// @notice Move all the required liquidity to/from the AMM and settle any required collateral deltas. 625 | | /// @param tokenId The option position to be minted 626 | | /// @param positionSize The size of the position, expressed in terms of the asset 627 | | /// @param effectiveLiquidityLimitX32 Maximum amount of "spread" defined as `removedLiquidity/netLiquidity` 628 | | /// @param tickLimitLow The lower bound of an acceptable open interval for the ending price. 629 | | /// @param tickLimitHigh The upper bound of an acceptable open interval for the ending price. 630 | | /// @return poolUtilizations Packing of the pool utilization (how much funds are in the Panoptic pool versus the AMM pool) at the time of minting, 631 | | /// right 64bits for token0 and left 64bits for token1. When safeMode is active, it returns 100% pool utilization for both tokens. 632 | * | function _mintInSFPMAndUpdateCollateral( 633 | | TokenId tokenId, 634 | | uint128 positionSize, 635 | | uint64 effectiveLiquidityLimitX32, 636 | | int24 tickLimitLow, 637 | | int24 tickLimitHigh 638 | * | ) internal returns (uint128) { 639 | * | bool safeMode = _isSafeMode(); 640 | | // if safeMode, enforce covered deployment 641 | * | if (safeMode) { 642 | * | if (tickLimitLow > tickLimitHigh) { 643 | | (tickLimitLow, tickLimitHigh) = (tickLimitHigh, tickLimitLow); 644 | | } 645 | | } 646 | | 647 | * | (LeftRightUnsigned[4] memory collectedByLeg, LeftRightSigned totalSwapped) = SFPM 648 | * | .mintTokenizedPosition(tokenId, positionSize, tickLimitLow, tickLimitHigh); 649 | | 650 | | _updateSettlementPostMint( 651 | | tokenId, 652 | | collectedByLeg, 653 | | positionSize, 654 | | effectiveLiquidityLimitX32 655 | | ); 656 | | 657 | | uint128 poolUtilizations = _payCommissionAndWriteData(tokenId, positionSize, totalSwapped); 658 | | 659 | | if (safeMode) { 660 | | return uint128(10_000) + uint128(10_000 << 64); 661 | | } else { 662 | | return poolUtilizations; 663 | | } 664 | | } 665 | | 666 | | /// @notice Take the commission fees for minting `tokenId` and settle any other required collateral deltas. 667 | | /// @param tokenId The option position 668 | | /// @param positionSize The size of the position, expressed in terms of the asset 669 | | /// @param totalSwapped The amount of tokens moved during creation of the option position 670 | | /// @return Packing of the pool utilization (how much funds are in the Panoptic pool versus the AMM pool at the time of minting), 671 | | /// right 64bits for token0 and left 64bits for token1, defined as (inAMM * 10_000) / totalAssets() 672 | | /// where totalAssets is the total tracked assets in the AMM and PanopticPool minus fees and donations to the Panoptic pool 673 | | function _payCommissionAndWriteData( 674 | | TokenId tokenId, 675 | | uint128 positionSize, 676 | | LeftRightSigned totalSwapped 677 | | ) internal returns (uint128) { 678 | | // compute how much of tokenId is long and short positions 679 | | (LeftRightSigned longAmounts, LeftRightSigned shortAmounts) = PanopticMath 680 | | .computeExercisedAmounts(tokenId, positionSize); 681 | | 682 | | int256 utilization0 = s_collateralToken0.takeCommissionAddData( 683 | | msg.sender, 684 | | longAmounts.rightSlot(), 685 | | shortAmounts.rightSlot(), 686 | | totalSwapped.rightSlot() 687 | | ); 688 | | int256 utilization1 = s_collateralToken1.takeCommissionAddData( 689 | | msg.sender, 690 | | longAmounts.leftSlot(), 691 | | shortAmounts.leftSlot(), 692 | | totalSwapped.leftSlot() 693 | | ); 694 | | 695 | | // return pool utilizations as a uint128 (pool Utilization is always < 10000) 696 | | unchecked { 697 | | return uint128(uint256(utilization0) + uint128(uint256(utilization1) << 64)); 698 | | } 699 | | } 700 | | 701 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 702 | | POSITION BURNING LOGIC 703 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 704 | | 705 | | /// @notice Close all options in `positionIdList. 706 | | /// @param owner The owner of the option position to be closed 707 | | /// @param tickLimitLow The lower bound of an acceptable open interval for the ending price on each option close 708 | | /// @param tickLimitHigh The upper bound of an acceptable open interval for the ending price on each option close 709 | | /// @param commitLongSettled Whether to commit the long premium that will be settled to storage (disabled during liquidations) 710 | | /// @param positionIdList The list of option positions to close 711 | | /// @return netPaid The net amount of tokens paid after closing the positions 712 | | /// @return premiasByLeg The amount of premia owed to the user for each leg of the position 713 | | function _burnAllOptionsFrom( 714 | | address owner, 715 | | int24 tickLimitLow, 716 | | int24 tickLimitHigh, 717 | | bool commitLongSettled, 718 | | TokenId[] calldata positionIdList 719 | | ) internal returns (LeftRightSigned netPaid, LeftRightSigned[4][] memory premiasByLeg) { 720 | | premiasByLeg = new LeftRightSigned[4][](positionIdList.length); 721 | | for (uint256 i = 0; i < positionIdList.length; ) { 722 | | LeftRightSigned paidAmounts; 723 | | (paidAmounts, premiasByLeg[i]) = _burnOptions( 724 | | commitLongSettled, 725 | | positionIdList[i], 726 | | owner, 727 | | tickLimitLow, 728 | | tickLimitHigh 729 | | ); 730 | | netPaid = netPaid.add(paidAmounts); 731 | | unchecked { 732 | | ++i; 733 | | } 734 | | } 735 | | } 736 | | 737 | | /// @notice Close a single option position. 738 | | /// @param tokenId The option position to burn 739 | | /// @param owner The owner of the option position to be burned 740 | | /// @param tickLimitLow The lower bound of an acceptable open interval for the ending price on each option close 741 | | /// @param tickLimitHigh The upper bound of an acceptable open interval for the ending price on each option close 742 | | /// @param commitLongSettled Whether to commit the long premium that will be settled to storage (disabled during liquidations) 743 | | /// @return paidAmounts The net amount of tokens paid after closing the position 744 | | /// @return premiaByLeg The amount of premia owed to the user for each leg of the position 745 | * | function _burnOptions( 746 | | bool commitLongSettled, 747 | | TokenId tokenId, 748 | | address owner, 749 | | int24 tickLimitLow, 750 | | int24 tickLimitHigh 751 | * | ) internal returns (LeftRightSigned paidAmounts, LeftRightSigned[4] memory premiaByLeg) { 752 | * | uint128 positionSize = s_positionBalance[owner][tokenId].rightSlot(); 753 | | 754 | | LeftRightSigned premiaOwed; 755 | | // burn position and do exercise checks 756 | * | (premiaOwed, premiaByLeg, paidAmounts) = _burnAndHandleExercise( 757 | * | commitLongSettled, 758 | * | tickLimitLow, 759 | * | tickLimitHigh, 760 | | tokenId, 761 | | positionSize, 762 | | owner 763 | | ); 764 | | 765 | | emit OptionBurnt(owner, positionSize, tokenId, premiaOwed); 766 | | } 767 | | 768 | | /// @notice Validates the solvency of `user`. 769 | | /// @dev Falls back to the more conservative tick if the delta between the fast and slow oracle exceeds `MAX_TICKS_DELTA`. 770 | | /// @dev Effectively, this means that the users must be solvent at both the fast and slow oracle ticks if one of them is stale to mint or burn options. 771 | | /// @param user The account to validate 772 | | /// @param positionIdList The list of positions to validate solvency for 773 | | /// @param buffer The buffer to apply to the collateral requirement for `user` 774 | | /// @return medianData If nonzero (enough time has passed since last observation), the updated value for `s_miniMedian` with a new observation 775 | | function _validateSolvency( 776 | | address user, 777 | | TokenId[] calldata positionIdList, 778 | | uint256 buffer 779 | | ) internal view returns (uint256 medianData) { 780 | | // check that the provided positionIdList matches the positions in memory 781 | | _validatePositionList(user, positionIdList, 0); 782 | | 783 | | ( 784 | | int24 currentTick, 785 | | int24 fastOracleTick, 786 | | int24 slowOracleTick, 787 | | int24 lastObservedTick, 788 | | uint256 _medianData 789 | | ) = PanopticMath.getOracleTicks(s_univ3pool, s_miniMedian); 790 | | 791 | | medianData = _medianData; 792 | | 793 | | int24[] memory atTicks; 794 | | // Fall back to a conservative approach if there's high deviation between internal ticks: 795 | | // Check solvency at the slowOracleTick, currentTick, and lastObservedTick instead of just the fastOracleTick. 796 | | // Deviation is measured as the magnitude of a 3D vector: 797 | | // (fastOracleTick - slowOracleTick, lastObservedTick - slowOracleTick, currentTick - slowOracleTick) 798 | | // This approach is more conservative than checking each tick difference individually, 799 | | // as the Euclidean norm is always greater than or equal to the maximum of the individual differences. 800 | | if ( 801 | | int256(fastOracleTick - slowOracleTick) ** 2 + 802 | | int256(lastObservedTick - slowOracleTick) ** 2 + 803 | | int256(currentTick - slowOracleTick) ** 2 > 804 | | MAX_TICKS_DELTA ** 2 805 | | ) { 806 | | atTicks = new int24[](4); 807 | | atTicks[0] = fastOracleTick; 808 | | atTicks[1] = slowOracleTick; 809 | | atTicks[2] = lastObservedTick; 810 | | atTicks[3] = currentTick; 811 | | } else { 812 | | atTicks = new int24[](1); 813 | | atTicks[0] = fastOracleTick; 814 | | } 815 | | 816 | | if (!_checkSolvencyAtTicks(user, positionIdList, currentTick, atTicks, buffer)) 817 | | revert Errors.AccountInsolvent(); 818 | | } 819 | | 820 | | /// @notice Burns and handles the exercise of options. 821 | | /// @param commitLongSettled Whether to commit the long premium that will be settled to storage (disabled during liquidations) 822 | | /// @param tickLimitLow The lower bound of an acceptable open interval for the ending price 823 | | /// @param tickLimitHigh The upper bound of an acceptable open interval for the ending price 824 | | /// @param tokenId The option position to burn 825 | | /// @param positionSize The size of the option position, expressed in terms of the asset 826 | | /// @param owner The owner of the option position 827 | | /// @return realizedPremia The net premia paid/received from the option position 828 | | /// @return premiaByLeg The premia owed to the user for each leg of the option position 829 | | /// @return paidAmounts The net amount of tokens paid after closing the position 830 | * | function _burnAndHandleExercise( 831 | | bool commitLongSettled, 832 | | int24 tickLimitLow, 833 | | int24 tickLimitHigh, 834 | | TokenId tokenId, 835 | | uint128 positionSize, 836 | | address owner 837 | | ) 838 | | internal 839 | | returns ( 840 | * | LeftRightSigned realizedPremia, 841 | * | LeftRightSigned[4] memory premiaByLeg, 842 | * | LeftRightSigned paidAmounts 843 | | ) 844 | | { 845 | * | { 846 | * | bool safeMode = _isSafeMode(); 847 | | // if safeMode, enforce covered at assignment 848 | * | if (safeMode) { 849 | * | if (tickLimitLow > tickLimitHigh) { 850 | | (tickLimitLow, tickLimitHigh) = (tickLimitHigh, tickLimitLow); 851 | | } 852 | | } 853 | | } 854 | | 855 | * | (LeftRightUnsigned[4] memory collectedByLeg, LeftRightSigned totalSwapped) = SFPM 856 | * | .burnTokenizedPosition(tokenId, positionSize, tickLimitLow, tickLimitHigh); 857 | | 858 | | (realizedPremia, premiaByLeg) = _updateSettlementPostBurn( 859 | | owner, 860 | | tokenId, 861 | | collectedByLeg, 862 | | positionSize, 863 | | commitLongSettled 864 | | ); 865 | | 866 | | (LeftRightSigned longAmounts, LeftRightSigned shortAmounts) = PanopticMath 867 | | .computeExercisedAmounts(tokenId, positionSize); 868 | | 869 | | { 870 | | int128 paid0 = s_collateralToken0.exercise( 871 | | owner, 872 | | longAmounts.rightSlot(), 873 | | shortAmounts.rightSlot(), 874 | | totalSwapped.rightSlot(), 875 | | realizedPremia.rightSlot() 876 | | ); 877 | | paidAmounts = paidAmounts.toRightSlot(paid0); 878 | | } 879 | | 880 | | { 881 | | int128 paid1 = s_collateralToken1.exercise( 882 | | owner, 883 | | longAmounts.leftSlot(), 884 | | shortAmounts.leftSlot(), 885 | | totalSwapped.leftSlot(), 886 | | realizedPremia.leftSlot() 887 | | ); 888 | | paidAmounts = paidAmounts.toLeftSlot(paid1); 889 | | } 890 | | } 891 | | 892 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 893 | | LIQUIDATIONS & FORCED EXERCISES 894 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 895 | | 896 | | /// @notice Liquidates a distressed account. Will burn all positions and issue a bonus to the liquidator. 897 | | /// @dev Will revert if liquidated account is solvent at the TWAP tick or if TWAP tick is too far away from the current tick. 898 | | /// @param positionIdListLiquidator List of positions owned by the liquidator 899 | | /// @param liquidatee Address of the distressed account 900 | | /// @param delegations LeftRight amounts of token0 and token1 (token0:token1 right:left) delegated to the liquidatee by the liquidator so the option can be smoothly exercised 901 | | /// @param positionIdList List of positions owned by the user. Written as [tokenId1, tokenId2, ...] 902 | | function liquidate( 903 | | TokenId[] calldata positionIdListLiquidator, 904 | | address liquidatee, 905 | | LeftRightUnsigned delegations, 906 | | TokenId[] calldata positionIdList 907 | | ) external { 908 | | _validatePositionList(liquidatee, positionIdList, 0); 909 | | 910 | | // Assert the account we are liquidating is actually insolvent 911 | | int24 twapTick = getUniV3TWAP(); 912 | | 913 | | LeftRightUnsigned tokenData0; 914 | | LeftRightUnsigned tokenData1; 915 | | LeftRightSigned premia; 916 | | (, int24 currentTick, , , , , ) = s_univ3pool.slot0(); 917 | | { 918 | | // Enforce maximum delta between TWAP and currentTick to prevent extreme price manipulation 919 | | if (Math.abs(currentTick - twapTick) > MAX_TWAP_DELTA_LIQUIDATION) 920 | | revert Errors.StaleTWAP(); 921 | | 922 | | uint256[2][] memory positionBalanceArray = new uint256[2][](positionIdList.length); 923 | | (premia, positionBalanceArray) = _calculateAccumulatedPremia( 924 | | liquidatee, 925 | | positionIdList, 926 | | COMPUTE_ALL_PREMIA, 927 | | ONLY_AVAILABLE_PREMIUM, 928 | | currentTick 929 | | ); 930 | | tokenData0 = s_collateralToken0.getAccountMarginDetails( 931 | | liquidatee, 932 | | twapTick, 933 | | positionBalanceArray, 934 | | premia.rightSlot() 935 | | ); 936 | | 937 | | tokenData1 = s_collateralToken1.getAccountMarginDetails( 938 | | liquidatee, 939 | | twapTick, 940 | | positionBalanceArray, 941 | | premia.leftSlot() 942 | | ); 943 | | 944 | | (uint256 balanceCross, uint256 thresholdCross) = _getSolvencyBalances( 945 | | tokenData0, 946 | | tokenData1, 947 | | Math.getSqrtRatioAtTick(twapTick) 948 | | ); 949 | | 950 | | if (balanceCross >= thresholdCross) revert Errors.NotMarginCalled(); 951 | | } 952 | | 953 | | // Perform the specified delegation from `msg.sender` to `liquidatee` 954 | | // Works like a transfer, so the liquidator must possess all the tokens they are delegating, resulting in no net supply change 955 | | // If not enough tokens are delegated for the positions of `liquidatee` to be closed, the liquidation will fail 956 | | s_collateralToken0.delegate(msg.sender, liquidatee, delegations.rightSlot()); 957 | | s_collateralToken1.delegate(msg.sender, liquidatee, delegations.leftSlot()); 958 | | 959 | | int256 liquidationBonus0; 960 | | int256 liquidationBonus1; 961 | | { 962 | | LeftRightSigned netExchanged; 963 | | LeftRightSigned[4][] memory premiasByLeg; 964 | | // burn all options from the liquidatee 965 | | 966 | | // Do not commit any settled long premium to storage - we will do this after we determine if any long premium must be revoked 967 | | // This is to prevent any short positions the liquidatee has being settled with tokens that will later be revoked 968 | | // NOTE: tick limits are not applied here since it is not the liquidator's position being liquidated 969 | | (netExchanged, premiasByLeg) = _burnAllOptionsFrom( 970 | | liquidatee, 971 | | MIN_SWAP_TICK, 972 | | MAX_SWAP_TICK, 973 | | DONOT_COMMIT_LONG_SETTLED, 974 | | positionIdList 975 | | ); 976 | | 977 | | LeftRightSigned collateralRemaining; 978 | | // compute bonus amounts using latest tick data 979 | | (liquidationBonus0, liquidationBonus1, collateralRemaining) = PanopticMath 980 | | .getLiquidationBonus( 981 | | tokenData0, 982 | | tokenData1, 983 | | Math.getSqrtRatioAtTick(twapTick), 984 | | Math.getSqrtRatioAtTick(currentTick), 985 | | netExchanged, 986 | | premia 987 | | ); 988 | | 989 | | // premia cannot be paid if there is protocol loss associated with the liquidatee 990 | | // otherwise, an economic exploit could occur if the liquidator and liquidatee collude to 991 | | // manipulate the fees in a liquidity area they control past the protocol loss threshold 992 | | // such that the PLPs are forced to pay out premia to the liquidator 993 | | // thus, we haircut any premium paid by the liquidatee (converting tokens as necessary) until the protocol loss is covered or the premium is exhausted 994 | | // note that the haircutPremia function also commits the settled amounts (adjusted for the haircut) to storage, so it will be called even if there is no haircut 995 | | 996 | | // if premium is haircut from a token that is not in protocol loss, some of the liquidation bonus will be converted into that token 997 | | // reusing variables to save stack space; netExchanged = deltaBonus0, premia = deltaBonus1 998 | | address _liquidatee = liquidatee; 999 | | int24 _currentTick = currentTick; 1000 | | TokenId[] memory _positionIdList = positionIdList; 1001 | | int256 deltaBonus0; 1002 | | int256 deltaBonus1; 1003 | | (deltaBonus0, deltaBonus1) = PanopticMath.haircutPremia( 1004 | | _liquidatee, 1005 | | _positionIdList, 1006 | | premiasByLeg, 1007 | | collateralRemaining, 1008 | | s_collateralToken0, 1009 | | s_collateralToken1, 1010 | | Math.getSqrtRatioAtTick(_currentTick), 1011 | | s_settledTokens 1012 | | ); 1013 | | 1014 | | unchecked { 1015 | | liquidationBonus0 += deltaBonus0; 1016 | | liquidationBonus1 += deltaBonus1; 1017 | | } 1018 | | } 1019 | | 1020 | | LeftRightUnsigned _delegations = delegations; 1021 | | // revoke the delegated amount plus the bonus amount. 1022 | | s_collateralToken0.revoke( 1023 | | msg.sender, 1024 | | liquidatee, 1025 | | uint256(int256(uint256(_delegations.rightSlot())) + liquidationBonus0) 1026 | | ); 1027 | | s_collateralToken1.revoke( 1028 | | msg.sender, 1029 | | liquidatee, 1030 | | uint256(int256(uint256(_delegations.leftSlot())) + liquidationBonus1) 1031 | | ); 1032 | | 1033 | | // ensure the liquidator is still solvent after the liquidation 1034 | | _validateSolvency(msg.sender, positionIdListLiquidator, BP_DECREASE_BUFFER); 1035 | | 1036 | | LeftRightSigned bonusAmounts = LeftRightSigned 1037 | | .wrap(0) 1038 | | .toRightSlot(int128(liquidationBonus0)) 1039 | | .toLeftSlot(int128(liquidationBonus1)); 1040 | | emit AccountLiquidated(msg.sender, liquidatee, bonusAmounts); 1041 | | } 1042 | | 1043 | | /// @notice Force the exercise of a single position. Exercisor will have to pay a fee to the force exercisee. 1044 | | /// @param account Address of the distressed account 1045 | | /// @param touchedId List of position to be force exercised. Can only contain one tokenId, written as [tokenId] 1046 | | /// @param positionIdListExercisee Post-burn list of open positions in the exercisee's (account) account 1047 | | /// @param positionIdListExercisor List of open positions in the exercisor's (msg.sender) account 1048 | | function forceExercise( 1049 | | address account, 1050 | | TokenId[] calldata touchedId, 1051 | | TokenId[] calldata positionIdListExercisee, 1052 | | TokenId[] calldata positionIdListExercisor 1053 | | ) external { 1054 | | // revert if multiple positions are specified 1055 | | // the reason why the singular touchedId is an array is so it composes well with the rest of the system 1056 | | // '_calculateAccumulatedPremia' expects a list of positions to be touched, and this is the only way to pass a single position 1057 | | if (touchedId.length != 1) revert Errors.InputListFail(); 1058 | | 1059 | | // validate the exercisor's position list (the exercisee's list will be evaluated after their position is force exercised) 1060 | | _validatePositionList(msg.sender, positionIdListExercisor, 0); 1061 | | 1062 | | uint128 positionBalance = s_positionBalance[account][touchedId[0]].rightSlot(); 1063 | | 1064 | | int24 twapTick = getUniV3TWAP(); 1065 | | 1066 | | // on forced exercise, the price *must* be outside the position's range for at least 1 leg 1067 | | touchedId[0].validateIsExercisable(twapTick); 1068 | | 1069 | | LeftRightSigned exerciseFees; 1070 | | { 1071 | | (, int24 currentTick, , , , , ) = s_univ3pool.slot0(); 1072 | | 1073 | | // compute the notional value of the short legs (the maximum amount of tokens required to exercise - premia) 1074 | | // and the long legs (from which the exercise cost is computed) 1075 | | (LeftRightSigned longAmounts, ) = PanopticMath.computeExercisedAmounts( 1076 | | touchedId[0], 1077 | | positionBalance 1078 | | ); 1079 | | 1080 | | // Compute the exerciseFee, this will decrease the further away the price is from the forcedExercised position 1081 | | // Use an oracle tick to prevent price manipulations based on swaps. 1082 | | exerciseFees = s_collateralToken0.exerciseCost( 1083 | | currentTick, 1084 | | twapTick, 1085 | | touchedId[0], 1086 | | positionBalance, 1087 | | longAmounts 1088 | | ); 1089 | | } 1090 | | 1091 | | // The protocol delegates some virtual shares to ensure the burn can be settled. 1092 | | uint256 delegated0 = s_collateralToken0.delegate( 1093 | | account, 1094 | | uint256(type(uint104).max) * 10_000 1095 | | ); 1096 | | uint256 delegated1 = s_collateralToken1.delegate( 1097 | | account, 1098 | | uint256(type(uint104).max) * 10_000 1099 | | ); 1100 | | 1101 | | // Exercise the option 1102 | | // Turn off ITM swapping to prevent swap at potentially unfavorable price 1103 | | _burnAllOptionsFrom(account, MIN_SWAP_TICK, MAX_SWAP_TICK, COMMIT_LONG_SETTLED, touchedId); 1104 | | 1105 | | // redistribute token composition of refund amounts if user doesn't have enough of one token to pay 1106 | | LeftRightSigned refundAmounts = PanopticMath.getExerciseDeltas( 1107 | | account, 1108 | | delegated0, 1109 | | delegated1, 1110 | | exerciseFees, 1111 | | twapTick, 1112 | | s_collateralToken0, 1113 | | s_collateralToken1 1114 | | ); 1115 | | 1116 | | // settle difference between delegated amounts (from the protocol) and exercise fees/substituted tokens 1117 | | s_collateralToken0.refund(account, msg.sender, refundAmounts.rightSlot()); 1118 | | s_collateralToken1.refund(account, msg.sender, refundAmounts.leftSlot()); 1119 | | 1120 | | // revoke the virual shares that were delegated after settling the difference with the exercisor 1121 | | s_collateralToken0.revoke(account, delegated0); 1122 | | s_collateralToken1.revoke(account, delegated1); 1123 | | 1124 | | _validateSolvency(account, positionIdListExercisee, NO_BUFFER); 1125 | | 1126 | | // the exercisor's position list is validated above 1127 | | // we need to assert their solvency against their collateral requirement plus a buffer 1128 | | // force exercises involve a collateral decrease with open positions, so there is a higher standard for solvency 1129 | | // a similar buffer is also invoked when minting options, which also decreases the available collateral 1130 | | if (positionIdListExercisor.length > 0) 1131 | | _validateSolvency(msg.sender, positionIdListExercisor, BP_DECREASE_BUFFER);
