847 | | 848 | | // swap tokens in the Uniswap pool 849 | | // NOTE: this triggers our swap callback function 850 | * | (int256 swap0, int256 swap1) = _univ3pool.swap( 851 | * | msg.sender, 852 | * | zeroForOne, 853 | * | swapAmount, 854 | * | zeroForOne 855 | * | ? Constants.MIN_V3POOL_SQRT_RATIO + 1 856 | * | : Constants.MAX_V3POOL_SQRT_RATIO - 1, 857 | * | data 858 | | ); 859 | | 860 | | // Add amounts swapped to totalSwapped variable 861 | * | totalSwapped = LeftRightSigned.wrap(0).toRightSlot(swap0.toInt128()).toLeftSlot( 862 | * | swap1.toInt128() 863 | | ); 864 | | } 865 | | } 866 | | 867 | | /// @notice Create the position in the AMM given in the tokenId. 868 | | /// @dev Loops over each leg in the tokenId and calls _createLegInAMM for each, which does the mint/burn in the AMM. 869 | | /// @param univ3pool The Uniswap pool 870 | | /// @param tokenId The option position 871 | | /// @param positionSize The size of the option position 872 | | /// @param isBurn Whether a position is being minted (true) or burned (false) 873 | | /// @return totalMoved The net amount of funds moved to/from Uniswap 874 | | /// @return collectedByLeg An array of LeftRight encoded words containing the amount of token0 and token1 collected as fees for each leg 875 | | /// @return itmAmounts The amount of tokens swapped due to legs being in-the-money 876 | * | function _createPositionInAMM( 877 | | IUniswapV3Pool univ3pool, 878 | | TokenId tokenId, 879 | | uint128 positionSize, 880 | | bool isBurn 881 | | ) 882 | | internal 883 | | returns ( 884 | * | LeftRightSigned totalMoved, 885 | * | LeftRightUnsigned[4] memory collectedByLeg, 886 | * | LeftRightSigned itmAmounts 887 | | ) 888 | * | { 889 | | // upper bound on amount of tokens contained across all legs of the position at any given tick 890 | * | uint256 amount0; 891 | * | uint256 amount1; 892 | | 893 | * | uint256 numLegs = tokenId.countLegs(); 894 | | // loop through up to the 4 potential legs in the tokenId 895 | * | for (uint256 leg = 0; leg < numLegs; ) { 896 | * | LeftRightSigned _moved; 897 | | LeftRightSigned _itmAmounts; 898 | | LeftRightUnsigned _collectedSingleLeg; 899 | | 900 | * | { 901 | | // cache the univ3pool, tokenId, isBurn, and _positionSize variables to get rid of stack too deep error 902 | * | IUniswapV3Pool _univ3pool = univ3pool; 903 | * | TokenId _tokenId = tokenId; 904 | * | bool _isBurn = isBurn; 905 | * | uint128 _positionSize = positionSize; 906 | | uint256 _leg; 907 | | 908 | | unchecked { 909 | | // Reverse the order of the legs if this call is burning a position (LIFO) 910 | | // We loop in reverse order if burning a position so that any dependent long liquidity is returned to the pool first, 911 | | // allowing the corresponding short liquidity to be removed 912 | * | _leg = _isBurn ? numLegs - leg - 1 : leg; 913 | | } 914 | | 915 | * | LiquidityChunk liquidityChunk = PanopticMath.getLiquidityChunk( 916 | * | _tokenId, 917 | * | _leg, 918 | * | _positionSize 919 | | ); 920 | | 921 | * | (_moved, _itmAmounts, _collectedSingleLeg) = _createLegInAMM( 922 | * | _univ3pool, 923 | * | _tokenId, 924 | * | _leg, 925 | * | liquidityChunk, 926 | * | _isBurn 927 | | ); 928 | | 929 | * | collectedByLeg[_leg] = _collectedSingleLeg; 930 | | 931 | | unchecked { 932 | | // increment accumulators of the upper bound on tokens contained across all legs of the position at any given tick 933 | * | amount0 += Math.getAmount0ForLiquidity(liquidityChunk); 934 | | 935 | * | amount1 += Math.getAmount1ForLiquidity(liquidityChunk); 936 | | } 937 | | } 938 | | 939 | * | totalMoved = totalMoved.add(_moved); 940 | * | itmAmounts = itmAmounts.add(_itmAmounts); 941 | | 942 | | unchecked { 943 | * | ++leg; 944 | | } 945 | | } 946 | | 947 | | // Ensure upper bound on amount of tokens contained across all legs of the position on any given tick does not exceed a maximum of (2**127-1). 948 | | // This is the maximum value of the `int128` type we frequently use to hold token amounts, so a given position's size should be guaranteed to 949 | | // fit within that limit at all times. 950 | * | if (amount0 > uint128(type(int128).max - 4) || amount1 > uint128(type(int128).max - 4)) 951 | | revert Errors.PositionTooLarge(); 952 | | } 953 | | 954 | | /// @notice Create the position in the AMM for a specific leg in the tokenId. 955 | | /// @dev For the leg specified by the _leg input: 956 | | /// @dev - mints any new liquidity in the AMM needed (via _mintLiquidity) 957 | | /// @dev - burns any new liquidity in the AMM needed (via _burnLiquidity) 958 | | /// @dev - tracks all amounts minted and burned 959 | | /// @dev To burn a position, the opposing position is "created" through this function, 960 | | /// but we need to pass in a flag to indicate that so the removedLiquidity is updated. 961 | | /// @param univ3pool The Uniswap pool 962 | | /// @param tokenId The option position 963 | | /// @param leg The leg index that needs to be modified 964 | | /// @param liquidityChunk The liquidity chunk in Uniswap represented by the leg 965 | | /// @param isBurn Whether a position is being minted (true) or burned (false) 966 | | /// @return moved The net amount of funds moved to/from Uniswap 967 | | /// @return itmAmounts The amount of tokens to swap due to legs being in-the-money 968 | | /// @return collectedSingleLeg LeftRight encoded words containing the amount of token0 and token1 collected as fees 969 | * | function _createLegInAMM( 970 | | IUniswapV3Pool univ3pool, 971 | | TokenId tokenId, 972 | | uint256 leg, 973 | | LiquidityChunk liquidityChunk, 974 | | bool isBurn 975 | | ) 976 | | internal 977 | | returns ( 978 | * | LeftRightSigned moved, 979 | | LeftRightSigned itmAmounts, 980 | | LeftRightUnsigned collectedSingleLeg 981 | | ) 982 | * | { 983 | * | emit LogUint256("real is long ?", tokenId.isLong(leg)); 984 | | 985 | * | uint256 tokenType = tokenId.tokenType(leg); 986 | | // unique key to identify the liquidity chunk in this uniswap pool 987 | * | bytes32 positionKey = keccak256( 988 | * | abi.encodePacked( 989 | * | address(univ3pool), 990 | * | msg.sender, 991 | * | tokenType, 992 | * | liquidityChunk.tickLower(), 993 | * | liquidityChunk.tickUpper() 994 | | ) 995 | | ); 996 | | 997 | | // update our internal bookkeeping of how much liquidity we have deployed in the AMM 998 | | // for example: if this _leg is short, we add liquidity to the amm, make sure to add that to our tracking 999 | * | uint128 updatedLiquidity; 1000 | * | uint256 isLong = tokenId.isLong(leg); 1001 | * | LeftRightUnsigned currentLiquidity = s_accountLiquidity[positionKey]; //cache 1002 | * | { 1003 | | // did we have liquidity already deployed in Uniswap for this chunk range from some past mint? 1004 | | 1005 | | // s_accountLiquidity is a LeftRight. The right slot represents the liquidity currently sold (added) in the AMM owned by the user 1006 | | // the left slot represents the amount of liquidity currently bought (removed) that has been removed from the AMM - the user owes it to a seller 1007 | | // the reason why it is called "removedLiquidity" is because long options are created by removing - ie. short selling LP positions 1008 | | uint128 startingLiquidity = currentLiquidity.rightSlot(); 1009 | * | uint128 removedLiquidity = currentLiquidity.leftSlot(); 1010 | * | uint128 chunkLiquidity = liquidityChunk.liquidity(); 1011 | | 1012 | | // 0-liquidity interactions are asymmetrical in Uniswap (burning 0 liquidity is permitted and functions as a poke, but minting is prohibited) 1013 | | // thus, we prohibit all 0-liquidity chunks to prevent users from creating positions that cannot be closed 1014 | * | if (chunkLiquidity == 0) revert Errors.ZeroLiquidity(); 1015 | | 1016 | * | if (isLong == 0) { 1017 | | // selling/short: so move from msg.sender *to* uniswap 1018 | | // we're minting more liquidity in uniswap: so add the incoming liquidity chunk to the existing liquidity chunk 1019 | * | updatedLiquidity = startingLiquidity + chunkLiquidity; 1020 | | 1021 | | /// @dev If the isLong flag is 0=short but the position was burnt, then this is closing a long position 1022 | | /// @dev so the amount of removed liquidity should decrease. 1023 | * | if (isBurn) { 1024 | | removedLiquidity -= chunkLiquidity; 1025 | | } 1026 | | } else { 1027 | | // the _leg is long (buying: moving *from* uniswap to msg.sender) 1028 | | // so we seek to move the incoming liquidity chunk *out* of uniswap - but was there sufficient liquidity sitting in uniswap 1029 | | // in the first place? 1030 | * | if (startingLiquidity < chunkLiquidity) { 1031 | | // the amount we want to move (liquidityChunk.legLiquidity()) out of uniswap is greater than 1032 | | // what the account that owns the liquidity in uniswap has (startingLiquidity) 1033 | | // we must ensure that an account can only move its own liquidity out of uniswap 1034 | | // so we revert in this case 1035 | * | revert Errors.NotEnoughLiquidity(); 1036 | | } else { 1037 | | // startingLiquidity is >= chunkLiquidity, so no possible underflow 1038 | | unchecked { 1039 | | // we want to move less than what already sits in uniswap, no problem: 1040 | | updatedLiquidity = startingLiquidity - chunkLiquidity; 1041 | | } 1042 | | } 1043 | | 1044 | | /// @dev If the isLong flag is 1=long and the position is minted, then this is opening a long position 1045 | | /// @dev so the amount of removed liquidity should increase. 1046 | * | if (!isBurn) { 1047 | | removedLiquidity += chunkLiquidity; 1048 | | } 1049 | | } 1050 | | 1051 | | // update the starting liquidity for this position for next time around 1052 | * | s_accountLiquidity[positionKey] = LeftRightUnsigned 1053 | * | .wrap(0) 1054 | * | .toLeftSlot(removedLiquidity) 1055 | * | .toRightSlot(updatedLiquidity); 1056 | | } 1057 | | 1058 | | // track how much liquidity we need to collect from uniswap 1059 | | // add the fees that accumulated in uniswap within the liquidityChunk: 1060 | | { 1061 | | /* if the position is NOT long (selling a put or a call), then _mintLiquidity to move liquidity 1062 | | from the msg.sender to the uniswap v3 pool: 1063 | | Selling(isLong=0): Mint chunk of liquidity in Uniswap (defined by upper tick, lower tick, and amount) 1064 | | ┌─────────────────────────────────┐ 1065 | | ▲ ┌▼┐ liquidityChunk │ 1066 | | │ ┌──┴─┴──┐ ┌───┴──┐ 1067 | | │ │ │ │ │ 1068 | | └──┴───────┴──► └──────┘ 1069 | | Uniswap v3 msg.sender 1070 | | 1071 | | else: the position is long (buying a put or a call), then _burnLiquidity to remove liquidity from univ3 1072 | | Buying(isLong=1): Burn in Uniswap 1073 | | ┌─────────────────┐ 1074 | | ▲ ┌┼┐ │ 1075 | | │ ┌──┴─┴──┐ ┌───▼──┐ 1076 | | │ │ │ │ │ 1077 | | └──┴───────┴──► └──────┘ 1078 | | Uniswap v3 msg.sender 1079 | | */ 1080 | * | (uint128 uniLiqBefore, , , , ) = univ3pool.positions(positionKey); 1081 | * | emit LogUint256("real uniLiqBefore", uniLiqBefore); 1082 | | 1083 | * | moved = isLong == 0 1084 | * | ? _mintLiquidity(liquidityChunk, univ3pool) 1085 | | : _burnLiquidity(liquidityChunk, univ3pool); // from msg.sender to Uniswap 1086 | | // add the moved liquidity chunk to amount we need to collect from uniswap: 1087 | | 1088 | | // Is this _leg ITM? 1089 | | // if tokenType is 1, and we transacted some token0: then this leg is ITM! 1090 | * | if (tokenType == 1) { 1091 | | // extract amount moved out of UniswapV3 pool 1092 | * | itmAmounts = itmAmounts.toRightSlot(moved.rightSlot()); 1093 | | } 1094 | | // if tokenType is 0, and we transacted some token1: then this leg is ITM 1095 | * | if (tokenType == 0) { 1096 | | // Add this in-the-money amount transacted. 1097 | * | itmAmounts = itmAmounts.toLeftSlot(moved.leftSlot()); 1098 | | } 1099 | | } 1100 | | 1101 | * | emit LogUint256("curr leg", leg); 1102 | | 1103 | | // if there was liquidity at that tick before the transaction, collect any accumulated fees 1104 | * | if (currentLiquidity.rightSlot() > 0) { 1105 | | collectedSingleLeg = _collectAndWritePositionData( 1106 | | liquidityChunk, 1107 | | univ3pool, 1108 | | currentLiquidity, 1109 | | positionKey, 1110 | | moved, 1111 | | isLong 1112 | | ); 1113 | | } 1114 | | 1115 | | // position has been touched, update s_accountFeesBase with the latest values from the pool.positions 1116 | | // round up the stored feesbase to minimize Δfeesbase when we next calculate it 1117 | * | s_accountFeesBase[positionKey] = _getFeesBase( 1118 | * | univ3pool, 1119 | * | updatedLiquidity, 1120 | * | liquidityChunk, 1121 | * | true 1122 | | ); 1123 | | 1124 | | // true stored fees base 1125 | * | emit LogInt256("real feesbase 0", s_accountFeesBase[positionKey].rightSlot()); 1126 | * | emit LogInt256("real feesbase 1", s_accountFeesBase[positionKey].leftSlot()); 1127 | | } 1128 | | 1129 | | /// @notice Updates the premium accumulators for a chunk with the latest collected tokens. 1130 | | /// @param positionKey A key representing a liquidity chunk/range in Uniswap 1131 | | /// @param currentLiquidity The total amount of liquidity in the AMM for the specified chunk 1132 | | /// @param collectedAmounts The amount of tokens (token0 and token1) collected from Uniswap 1133 | | function _updateStoredPremia( 1134 | | bytes32 positionKey, 1135 | | LeftRightUnsigned currentLiquidity, 1136 | | LeftRightUnsigned collectedAmounts 1137 | | ) private { 1138 | | ( 1139 | | LeftRightUnsigned deltaPremiumOwed, 1140 | | LeftRightUnsigned deltaPremiumGross 1141 | | ) = _getPremiaDeltas(currentLiquidity, collectedAmounts); 1142 | | 1143 | | // add deltas to accumulators and freeze both accumulators (for a token) if one of them overflows 1144 | | // (i.e if only token0 (right slot) of the owed premium overflows, then stop accumulating both token0 owed premium and token0 gross premium for the chunk) 1145 | | // this prevents situations where the owed premium gets out of sync with the gross premium due to one of them overflowing 1146 | | (s_accountPremiumOwed[positionKey], s_accountPremiumGross[positionKey]) = LeftRightLibrary 1147 | | .addCapped( 1148 | | s_accountPremiumOwed[positionKey], 1149 | | deltaPremiumOwed, 1150 | | s_accountPremiumGross[positionKey], 1151 | | deltaPremiumGross 1152 | | ); 1153 | | } 1154 | | 1155 | | /// @notice Compute an up-to-date feeGrowth value without a poke. 1156 | | /// @dev Stored fees base is rounded up and the current fees base is rounded down to minimize the amount of fees collected (Δfeesbase) in favor of the protocol. 1157 | | /// @param univ3pool The Uniswap pool 1158 | | /// @param liquidity The total amount of liquidity in the AMM for the specific position 1159 | | /// @param liquidityChunk The liquidity chunk in Uniswap to compute the feesBase for 1160 | | /// @param roundUp If true, round up the feesBase, otherwise round down 1161 | * | function _getFeesBase( 1162 | | IUniswapV3Pool univ3pool, 1163 | | uint128 liquidity, 1164 | | LiquidityChunk liquidityChunk, 1165 | | bool roundUp 1166 | * | ) private returns (LeftRightSigned feesBase) { 1167 | | // read the latest feeGrowth directly from the Uniswap pool 1168 | * | (, uint256 feeGrowthInside0LastX128, uint256 feeGrowthInside1LastX128, , ) = univ3pool 1169 | | .positions( 1170 | * | keccak256( 1171 | * | abi.encodePacked( 1172 | * | address(this), 1173 | * | liquidityChunk.tickLower(), 1174 | * | liquidityChunk.tickUpper() 1175 | | ) 1176 | | ) 1177 | | ); 1178 | | 1179 | * | emit LogUint256("real fg0", feeGrowthInside0LastX128); 1180 | * | emit LogUint256("real fg1", feeGrowthInside1LastX128); 1181 | * | emit LogUint256("real liquidity", liquidity); 1182 | | 1183 | | // (feegrowth * liquidity) / 2 ** 128 1184 | | // here we're converting the value to an int128 even though all values (feeGrowth, liquidity, Q128) are strictly positive. 1185 | | // That's because of the way feeGrowthInside works in Uniswap v3, where it can underflow when stored for the first time. 1186 | | // This is not a problem in Uniswap v3 because the fees are always calculated by taking the difference of the feeGrowths, 1187 | | // so that the net different is always positive. 1188 | | // So by using int128 instead of uint128, we remove the need to handle extremely large underflows and simply allow it to be negative 1189 | * | feesBase = roundUp 1190 | * | ? LeftRightSigned 1191 | | .wrap(0) 1192 | | .toRightSlot( 1193 | * | int128(int256(Math.mulDiv128RoundingUp(feeGrowthInside0LastX128, liquidity))) 1194 | | ) 1195 | | .toLeftSlot( 1196 | * | int128(int256(Math.mulDiv128RoundingUp(feeGrowthInside1LastX128, liquidity))) 1197 | | ) 1198 | | : LeftRightSigned 1199 | | .wrap(0) 1200 | | .toRightSlot(int128(int256(Math.mulDiv128(feeGrowthInside0LastX128, liquidity)))) 1201 | | .toLeftSlot(int128(int256(Math.mulDiv128(feeGrowthInside1LastX128, liquidity)))); 1202 | | 1203 | * | emit LogBool("real roundUp", roundUp); 1204 | * | emit LogInt256("real feesBase 0", feesBase.rightSlot()); 1205 | * | emit LogInt256("real feesBase 1", feesBase.leftSlot()); 1206 | | } 1207 | | 1208 | | /// @notice Mint a chunk of liquidity (`liquidityChunk`) in the Uniswap v3 pool; return the amount moved. 1209 | | /// @dev Note that "moved" means: mint in Uniswap and move tokens from msg.sender. 1210 | | /// @param liquidityChunk The liquidity chunk in Uniswap to mint 1211 | | /// @param univ3pool The Uniswap v3 pool to mint liquidity in/to 1212 | | /// @return movedAmounts How many tokens were moved from msg.sender to Uniswap 1213 | * | function _mintLiquidity( 1214 | | LiquidityChunk liquidityChunk, 1215 | | IUniswapV3Pool univ3pool 1216 | * | ) internal returns (LeftRightSigned movedAmounts) { 1217 | | // build callback data 1218 | * | bytes memory mintdata = abi.encode( 1219 | * | CallbackLib.CallbackData({ // compute by reading values from univ3pool every time 1220 | * | poolFeatures: CallbackLib.PoolFeatures({ 1221 | * | token0: univ3pool.token0(), 1222 | * | token1: univ3pool.token1(), 1223 | * | fee: univ3pool.fee() 1224 | | }), 1225 | * | payer: msg.sender 1226 | | }) 1227 | | ); 1228 | | 1229 | | /// mint the required amount in the Uniswap pool 1230 | | /// @dev this triggers the uniswap mint callback function 1231 | * | (uint256 amount0, uint256 amount1) = univ3pool.mint( 1232 | * | address(this), 1233 | * | liquidityChunk.tickLower(), 1234 | * | liquidityChunk.tickUpper(), 1235 | * | liquidityChunk.liquidity(), 1236 | * | mintdata 1237 | | ); 1238 | | 1239 | | // amount0 The amount of token0 that was paid to mint the given amount of liquidity 1240 | | // amount1 The amount of token1 that was paid to mint the given amount of liquidity 1241 | | // no need to safecast to int from uint here as the max position size is int128 1242 | * | movedAmounts = LeftRightSigned.wrap(0).toRightSlot(int128(int256(amount0))).toLeftSlot( 1243 | | int128(int256(amount1)) 1244 | | ); 1245 | | } 1246 | | 1247 | | /// @notice Burn a chunk of liquidity (`liquidityChunk`) in the Uniswap v3 pool and send to msg.sender; return the amount moved. 1248 | | /// @dev Note that "moved" means: burn position in Uniswap and send tokens to msg.sender. 1249 | | /// @param liquidityChunk The liquidity chunk in Uniswap to burn 1250 | | /// @param univ3pool The Uniswap v3 pool to burn liquidity in/from 1251 | | /// @return movedAmounts How many tokens were moved from Uniswap to msg.sender 1252 | | function _burnLiquidity( 1253 | | LiquidityChunk liquidityChunk, 1254 | | IUniswapV3Pool univ3pool 1255 | | ) internal returns (LeftRightSigned movedAmounts) { 1256 | | // burn that option's liquidity in the Uniswap Pool. 1257 | | // This will send the underlying tokens back to the Panoptic Pool (msg.sender) 1258 | | (uint256 amount0, uint256 amount1) = univ3pool.burn( 1259 | | liquidityChunk.tickLower(), 1260 | | liquidityChunk.tickUpper(), 1261 | | liquidityChunk.liquidity() 1262 | | ); 1263 | | 1264 | | // amount0 The amount of token0 that was sent back to the Panoptic Pool 1265 | | // amount1 The amount of token1 that was sent back to the Panoptic Pool 1266 | | // no need to safecast to int from uint here as the max position size is int128 1267 | | // decrement the amountsOut with burnt amounts. amountsOut = notional value of tokens moved 1268 | | unchecked { 1269 | | movedAmounts = LeftRightSigned.wrap(0).toRightSlot(-int128(int256(amount0))).toLeftSlot( 1270 | | -int128(int256(amount1)) 1271 | | ); 1272 | | } 1273 | | } 1274 | | 1275 | | /// @notice Helper to collect amounts between msg.sender and Uniswap and also to update the Uniswap fees collected to date from the AMM. 1276 | | /// @param liquidityChunk The liquidity chunk in Uniswap to collect from 1277 | | /// @param univ3pool The Uniswap pool where the position is deployed 1278 | | /// @param currentLiquidity The existing liquidity msg.sender owns in the AMM for this chunk before the SFPM was called 1279 | | /// @param positionKey The unique key to identify the liquidity chunk/tokenType pairing in this uniswap pool 1280 | | /// @param movedInLeg How much liquidity has been moved between msg.sender and Uniswap before this function call 1281 | | /// @param isLong Whether the leg in question is long (=1) or short (=0) 1282 | | /// @return collectedChunk The amount of tokens collected from Uniswap 1283 | | function _collectAndWritePositionData( 1284 | | LiquidityChunk liquidityChunk, 1285 | | IUniswapV3Pool univ3pool, 1286 | | LeftRightUnsigned currentLiquidity, 1287 | | bytes32 positionKey, 1288 | | LeftRightSigned movedInLeg, 1289 | | uint256 isLong 1290 | | ) internal returns (LeftRightUnsigned collectedChunk) { 1291 | | uint128 startingLiquidity = currentLiquidity.rightSlot(); 1292 | | // round down current fees base to minimize Δfeesbase 1293 | | // If the current feesBase is close or identical to the stored one, the amountToCollect can be negative. 1294 | | // This is because the stored feesBase is rounded up, and the current feesBase is rounded down. 1295 | | // When this is the case, we want to behave as if there are 0 fees, so we just rectify the values. 1296 | | LeftRightSigned amountToCollect = _getFeesBase( 1297 | | univ3pool, 1298 | | startingLiquidity, 1299 | | liquidityChunk, 1300 | | false 1301 | | ).subRect(s_accountFeesBase[positionKey]); 1302 | | 1303 | | emit LogInt256("real old fees base 0", s_accountFeesBase[positionKey].rightSlot()); 1304 | | emit LogInt256("real old fees base 1", s_accountFeesBase[positionKey].leftSlot()); 1305 | | 1306 | | if (isLong == 1) { 1307 | | amountToCollect = amountToCollect.sub(movedInLeg); 1308 | | } 1309 | | 1310 | | emit LogInt256("real amount to collect 0", amountToCollect.rightSlot()); 1311 | | emit LogInt256("real amount to collect 1", amountToCollect.leftSlot()); 1312 | | 1313 | | if (LeftRightSigned.unwrap(amountToCollect) != 0) { 1314 | | // first collect amounts from Uniswap corresponding to this position 1315 | | // Collect only if there was existing startingLiquidity=liquidities.rightSlot() at that position: collect all fees 1316 | | 1317 | | // Collects tokens owed to a liquidity chunk 1318 | | (uint128 receivedAmount0, uint128 receivedAmount1) = univ3pool.collect( 1319 | | msg.sender, 1320 | | liquidityChunk.tickLower(), 1321 | | liquidityChunk.tickUpper(), 1322 | | uint128(amountToCollect.rightSlot()), 1323 | | uint128(amountToCollect.leftSlot()) 1324 | | ); 1325 | | 1326 | | emit LogUint256("real receivedAmount0", receivedAmount0); 1327 | | emit LogUint256("real receivedAmount1", receivedAmount1); 1328 | | 1329 | | // moved will be negative if the leg was long (funds left the caller, don't count it in collected fees) 1330 | | uint128 collected0; 1331 | | uint128 collected1; 1332 | | unchecked { 1333 | | collected0 = movedInLeg.rightSlot() < 0 1334 | | ? receivedAmount0 - uint128(-movedInLeg.rightSlot()) 1335 | | : receivedAmount0; 1336 | | collected1 = movedInLeg.leftSlot() < 0 1337 | | ? receivedAmount1 - uint128(-movedInLeg.leftSlot()) 1338 | | : receivedAmount1; 1339 | | } 1340 | | 1341 | | emit LogUint256("real collected0", collected0); 1342 | | emit LogUint256("real collected1", collected1); 1343 | | 1344 | | // CollectedOut is the amount of fees accumulated+collected (received - burnt) 1345 | | // That's because receivedAmount contains the burnt tokens and whatever amount of fees collected 1346 | | collectedChunk = LeftRightUnsigned.wrap(0).toRightSlot(collected0).toLeftSlot( 1347 | | collected1 1348 | | ); 1349 | | 1350 | | // record the collected amounts in the s_accountPremiumOwed and s_accountPremiumGross accumulators 1351 | | _updateStoredPremia(positionKey, currentLiquidity, collectedChunk); 1352 | | } 1353 | | } 1354 | | 1355 | | /// @notice Compute deltas for Owed/Gross premium given quantities of tokens collected from Uniswap. 1356 | | /// @dev Returned accumulators are capped at the max value (2**128 - 1) for each token if they overflow. 1357 | | /// @param currentLiquidity NetLiquidity (right) and removedLiquidity (left) at the start of the transaction 1358 | | /// @param collectedAmounts Total amount of tokens (token0 and token1) collected from Uniswap 1359 | | /// @return deltaPremiumOwed The extra premium (per liquidity X64) to be added to the owed accumulator for token0 (right) and token1 (left) 1360 | | /// @return deltaPremiumGross The extra premium (per liquidity X64) to be added to the gross accumulator for token0 (right) and token1 (left) 1361 | | function _getPremiaDeltas( 1362 | | LeftRightUnsigned currentLiquidity, 1363 | | LeftRightUnsigned collectedAmounts 1364 | | ) 1365 | | private 1366 | | pure 1367 | | returns (LeftRightUnsigned deltaPremiumOwed, LeftRightUnsigned deltaPremiumGross) 1368 | | { 1369 | | // extract liquidity values 1370 | | uint256 removedLiquidity = currentLiquidity.leftSlot(); 1371 | | uint256 netLiquidity = currentLiquidity.rightSlot(); 1372 | | 1373 | | // premia spread equations are graphed and documented here: https://www.desmos.com/calculator/mdeqob2m04 1374 | | // explains how we get from the premium per liquidity (calculated here) to the total premia collected and the multiplier 1375 | | // as well as how the value of VEGOID affects the premia 1376 | | // note that the "base" premium is just a common factor shared between the owed (long) and gross (short) 1377 | | // premia, and is only seperated to simplify the calculation 1378 | | // (the graphed equations include this factor without separating it) 1379 | | unchecked { 1380 | | uint256 totalLiquidity = netLiquidity + removedLiquidity; 1381 | | 1382 | | uint256 premium0X64_base; 1383 | | uint256 premium1X64_base; 1384 | | 1385 | | { 1386 | | uint128 collected0 = collectedAmounts.rightSlot(); 1387 | | uint128 collected1 = collectedAmounts.leftSlot(); 1388 | | 1389 | | // compute the base premium as collected * total / net^2 (from Eqn 3) 1390 | | premium0X64_base = Math.mulDiv( 1391 | | collected0, 1392 | | totalLiquidity * 2 ** 64, 1393 | | netLiquidity ** 2 1394 | | ); 1395 | | premium1X64_base = Math.mulDiv( 1396 | | collected1, 1397 | | totalLiquidity * 2 ** 64, 1398 | | netLiquidity ** 2 1399 | | ); 1400 | | } 1401 | | 1402 | | { 1403 | | uint128 premium0X64_owed; 1404 | | uint128 premium1X64_owed; 1405 | | { 1406 | | // compute the owed premium (from Eqn 3) 1407 | | uint256 numerator = netLiquidity + (removedLiquidity / 2 ** VEGOID); 1408 | | 1409 | | premium0X64_owed = Math 1410 | | .mulDiv(premium0X64_base, numerator, totalLiquidity) 1411 | | .toUint128Capped(); 1412 | | premium1X64_owed = Math 1413 | | .mulDiv(premium1X64_base, numerator, totalLiquidity) 1414 | | .toUint128Capped(); 1415 | | 1416 | | deltaPremiumOwed = LeftRightUnsigned 1417 | | .wrap(0) 1418 | | .toRightSlot(premium0X64_owed) 1419 | | .toLeftSlot(premium1X64_owed); 1420 | | } 1421 | | } 1422 | | 1423 | | { 1424 | | uint128 premium0X64_gross; 1425 | | uint128 premium1X64_gross; 1426 | | { 1427 | | // compute the gross premium (from Eqn 4) 1428 | | uint256 numerator = totalLiquidity ** 2 - 1429 | | totalLiquidity * 1430 | | removedLiquidity + 1431 | | ((removedLiquidity ** 2) / 2 ** (VEGOID)); 1432 | | 1433 | | premium0X64_gross = Math 1434 | | .mulDiv(premium0X64_base, numerator, totalLiquidity ** 2) 1435 | | .toUint128Capped(); 1436 | | premium1X64_gross = Math 1437 | | .mulDiv(premium1X64_base, numerator, totalLiquidity ** 2) 1438 | | .toUint128Capped(); 1439 | | 1440 | | deltaPremiumGross = LeftRightUnsigned 1441 | | .wrap(0) 1442 | | .toRightSlot(premium0X64_gross) 1443 | | .toLeftSlot(premium1X64_gross); 1444 | | } 1445 | | } 1446 | | } 1447 | | } 1448 | | 1449 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 1450 | | SFPM PROPERTIES 1451 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 1452 | | 1453 | | /// @notice Return the liquidity associated with a given liquidity chunk/tokenType. 1454 | | /// @dev Computes accountLiquidity[keccak256(abi.encodePacked(univ3pool, owner, tokenType, tickLower, tickUpper))]. 1455 | | /// @param univ3pool The address of the Uniswap v3 Pool 1456 | | /// @param owner The address of the account that is queried 1457 | | /// @param tokenType The tokenType of the position 1458 | | /// @param tickLower The lower end of the tick range for the position (int24) 1459 | | /// @param tickUpper The upper end of the tick range for the position (int24) 1460 | | /// @return accountLiquidities The amount of liquidity that has been shorted/added to the Uniswap contract (netLiquidity:removedLiquidity -> rightSlot:leftSlot) 1461 | * | function getAccountLiquidity( 1462 | | address univ3pool, 1463 | | address owner, 1464 | | uint256 tokenType, 1465 | | int24 tickLower, 1466 | | int24 tickUpper 1467 | * | ) external view returns (LeftRightUnsigned accountLiquidities) { 1468 | | // Extract the account liquidity for a given uniswap pool, owner, token type, and ticks 1469 | | // tokenType input here is the asset of the positions minted, this avoids put liquidity to be used for call, and vice-versa 1470 | * | accountLiquidities = s_accountLiquidity[ 1471 | * | keccak256(abi.encodePacked(univ3pool, owner, tokenType, tickLower, tickUpper)) 1472 | | ]; 1473 | | } 1474 | | 1475 | | /// @notice Return the premium associated with a given position, where premium is an accumulator of feeGrowth for the touched position. 1476 | | /// @dev Computes s_accountPremium{isLong ? Owed : Gross}[keccak256(abi.encodePacked(univ3pool, owner, tokenType, tickLower, tickUpper))]. 1477 | | /// @dev If an atTick parameter is provided that is different from type(int24).max, then it will update the premium up to the current 1478 | | /// block at the provided atTick value. We do this because this may be called immediately after the Uni v3 pool has been touched, 1479 | | /// so no need to read the feeGrowths from the Uni v3 pool. 1480 | | /// @param univ3pool The address of the Uniswap v3 Pool 1481 | | /// @param owner The address of the account that is queried 1482 | | /// @param tokenType The tokenType of the position 1483 | | /// @param tickLower The lower end of the tick range for the position (int24) 1484 | | /// @param tickUpper The upper end of the tick range for the position (int24) 1485 | | /// @param atTick The current tick. Set atTick < type(int24).max = 8388608 to get latest premium up to the current block 1486 | | /// @param isLong Whether the position is long (=1) or short (=0) 1487 | | /// @return The amount of premium (per liquidity X64) for token0 = sum (feeGrowthLast0X128) over every block where the position has been touched 1488 | | /// @return The amount of premium (per liquidity X64) for token1 = sum (feeGrowthLast0X128) over every block where the position has been touched 1489 | * | function getAccountPremium( 1490 | | address univ3pool, 1491 | | address owner, 1492 | | uint256 tokenType, 1493 | | int24 tickLower, 1494 | | int24 tickUpper, 1495 | | int24 atTick, 1496 | | uint256 isLong 1497 | * | ) external view returns (uint128, uint128) { 1498 | * | bytes32 positionKey = keccak256( 1499 | * | abi.encodePacked(univ3pool, owner, tokenType, tickLower, tickUpper) 1500 | | ); 1501 | | 1502 | * | LeftRightUnsigned acctPremia; 1503 | | 1504 | | // Compute the premium up to the current block (ie. after last touch until now). Do not proceed if atTick == type(int24).max = 8388608 1505 | * | if (atTick < type(int24).max) { 1506 | | // unique key to identify the liquidity chunk in this uniswap pool 1507 | | LeftRightUnsigned accountLiquidities = s_accountLiquidity[positionKey]; 1508 | | uint128 netLiquidity = accountLiquidities.rightSlot(); 1509 | | if (netLiquidity != 0) { 1510 | | LeftRightUnsigned amountToCollect; 1511 | | { 1512 | | IUniswapV3Pool _univ3pool = IUniswapV3Pool(univ3pool); 1513 | | int24 _tickLower = tickLower; 1514 | | int24 _tickUpper = tickUpper; 1515 | | 1516 | | // how much fees have been accumulated within the liquidity chunk since last time we updated this chunk? 1517 | | // Compute (currentFeesGrowth - oldFeesGrowth), the amount to collect 1518 | | // currentFeesGrowth (calculated from FeesCalc.calculateAMMSwapFeesLiquidityChunk) is (ammFeesCollectedPerLiquidity * liquidityChunk.liquidity()) 1519 | | // oldFeesGrowth is the last stored update of fee growth within the position range in the past (feeGrowthRange*liquidityChunk.liquidity()) (s_accountFeesBase[positionKey]) 1520 | | LeftRightSigned feesBase = FeesCalc.calculateAMMSwapFees( 1521 | | _univ3pool, 1522 | | atTick, 1523 | | _tickLower, 1524 | | _tickUpper, 1525 | | netLiquidity 1526 | | ); 1527 | | 1528 | | // If the current feesBase is close or identical to the stored one, the amountToCollect can be negative. 1529 | | // This is because the stored feesBase is rounded up, and the current feesBase is rounded down. 1530 | | // When this is the case, we want to behave as if there are 0 fees, so we just rectify the values. 1531 | | // Guaranteed to be positive, so swap to unsigned type 1532 | | amountToCollect = LeftRightUnsigned.wrap( 1533 | | uint256( 1534 | | LeftRightSigned.unwrap(feesBase.subRect(s_accountFeesBase[positionKey])) 1535 | | ) 1536 | | ); 1537 | | } 1538 | | 1539 | | (LeftRightUnsigned premiumOwed, LeftRightUnsigned premiumGross) = _getPremiaDeltas( 1540 | | accountLiquidities, 1541 | | amountToCollect 1542 | | ); 1543 | | 1544 | | // add deltas to accumulators and freeze both accumulators (for a token) if one of them overflows 1545 | | // (i.e if only token0 (right slot) of the owed premium overflows, then stop accumulating both token0 owed premium and token0 gross premium for the chunk) 1546 | | // this prevents situations where the owed premium gets out of sync with the gross premium due to one of them overflowing 1547 | | (premiumOwed, premiumGross) = LeftRightLibrary.addCapped( 1548 | | s_accountPremiumOwed[positionKey], 1549 | | premiumOwed, 1550 | | s_accountPremiumGross[positionKey], 1551 | | premiumGross 1552 | | ); 1553 | | 1554 | | acctPremia = isLong == 1 ? premiumOwed : premiumGross; 1555 | | } 1556 | | } else { 1557 | | // Extract the account liquidity for a given uniswap pool, owner, token type, and ticks 1558 | * | acctPremia = isLong == 1 1559 | * | ? s_accountPremiumOwed[positionKey] 1560 | * | : s_accountPremiumGross[positionKey]; 1561 | | } 1562 | * | return (acctPremia.rightSlot(), acctPremia.leftSlot()); 1563 | | } 1564 | | 1565 | | /// @notice Return the feesBase associated with a given liquidity chunk. 1566 | | /// @dev Computes accountFeesBase[keccak256(abi.encodePacked(univ3pool, owner, tickLower, tickUpper))]. 1567 | | /// @param univ3pool The address of the Uniswap v3 Pool 1568 | | /// @param owner The address of the account that is queried 1569 | | /// @param tokenType The tokenType of the position (the token it started as) 1570 | | /// @param tickLower The lower end of the tick range for the position (int24) 1571 | | /// @param tickUpper The upper end of the tick range for the position (int24) 1572 | | /// @return feesBase0 The feesBase of the position for token0 1573 | | /// @return feesBase1 The feesBase of the position for token1 1574 | * | function getAccountFeesBase( 1575 | | address univ3pool, 1576 | | address owner, 1577 | | uint256 tokenType, 1578 | | int24 tickLower, 1579 | | int24 tickUpper 1580 | * | ) external view returns (int128 feesBase0, int128 feesBase1) { 1581 | | // Get accumulated fees for token0 (rightSlot) and token1 (leftSlot) 1582 | * | LeftRightSigned feesBase = s_accountFeesBase[ 1583 | * | keccak256(abi.encodePacked(univ3pool, owner, tokenType, tickLower, tickUpper)) 1584 | | ]; 1585 | * | feesBase0 = feesBase.rightSlot(); 1586 | * | feesBase1 = feesBase.leftSlot(); 1587 | | } 1588 | | 1589 | | /// @notice Returns the Uniswap v3 pool for a given poolId. 1590 | | /// @param poolId The poolId for a Uni v3 pool 1591 | | /// @return UniswapV3Pool The unique poolId for that Uni v3 pool 1592 | | function getUniswapV3PoolFromId( 1593 | | uint64 poolId 1594 | | ) external view returns (IUniswapV3Pool UniswapV3Pool) { 1595 | | return s_poolContext[poolId].pool; 1596 | | } 1597 | | 1598 | | /// @notice Returns the poolId for a given Uniswap v3 pool. 1599 | | /// @param univ3pool The address of the Uniswap v3 Pool 1600 | | /// @return poolId The unique poolId for that Uni v3 pool 1601 | * | function getPoolId(address univ3pool) external view returns (uint64 poolId) { 1602 | * | poolId = uint64(s_AddrToPoolIdData[univ3pool]); 1603 | | } 1604 | | } 1605 | |
/home/xshadow/panoptic-v1-core-private/contracts/base/FactoryNFT.sol 1 | | // SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1 2 | | pragma solidity ^0.8.18; 3 | | 4 | | // Interfaces 5 | | import {PanopticMath} from "@libraries/PanopticMath.sol"; 6 | | import {PanopticPool} from "@contracts/PanopticPool.sol"; 7 | | // Inherited implementations 8 | | import {ERC721} from "solmate/tokens/ERC721.sol"; 9 | | import {MetadataStore} from "@base/MetadataStore.sol"; 10 | | // Custom types 11 | | import {Pointer} from "@types/Pointer.sol"; 12 | | // Solady libraries 13 | | import {LibString} from "solady/utils/LibString.sol"; 14 | | import {Base64} from "solady/utils/Base64.sol"; 15 | | 16 | | /// @title FactoryNFT: ERC721 contract for Panoptic Factory NFTs. 17 | | /// @notice Constructs dynamic SVG art and metadata for Panoptic Factory NFTs from a set of building blocks. 18 | | /// @dev Pointers to metadata are provided at deployment time. 19 | | contract FactoryNFT is MetadataStore, ERC721 { 20 | | using LibString for string; 21 | | 22 | | /// @notice Initialize metadata pointers and token name/symbol. 23 | | /// @param properties An array of identifiers for different categories of metadata 24 | | /// @param indices A nested array of keys for K-V metadata pairs for each property in `properties` 25 | | /// @param pointers Contains pointers to the metadata values stored in contract data slices for each index in `indices` 26 | | constructor( 27 | | bytes32[] memory properties, 28 | | uint256[][] memory indices, 29 | | Pointer[][] memory pointers 30 | | ) 31 | * | MetadataStore(properties, indices, pointers) 32 | | ERC721("Panoptic Factory Deployer NFTs", "PANOPTIC-NFT") 33 | | {} 34 | | 35 | | /// @notice Returns the metadata URI for a given `tokenId`. 36 | | /// @dev The metadata is dynamically generated from the characteristics of the `PanopticPool` encoded in `tokenId`. 37 | | /// @dev The first 160 bits of `tokenId` are the address of a Panoptic Pool. 38 | | /// @param tokenId The token ID (encoded pool address) to get the metadata URI for 39 | | /// @return The metadata URI for the token ID 40 | * | function tokenURI(uint256 tokenId) public view override returns (string memory) { 41 | * | address panopticPool = address(uint160(tokenId)); 42 | | 43 | | return 44 | | constructMetadata( 45 | * | panopticPool, 46 | | PanopticMath.safeERC20Symbol(PanopticPool(panopticPool).univ3pool().token0()), 47 | | PanopticMath.safeERC20Symbol(PanopticPool(panopticPool).univ3pool().token1()), 48 | | PanopticPool(panopticPool).univ3pool().fee() 49 | | ); 50 | | } 51 | | 52 | | /// @notice Returns the metadata URI for a given set of characteristics. 53 | | /// @param panopticPool The displayed address used to determine the rarity (leading zeros) and lastCharVal (last 4 bits) 54 | | /// @param symbol0 The symbol of `token0` in the Uniswap pool 55 | | /// @param symbol1 The symbol of `token1` in the Uniswap pool 56 | | /// @param fee The fee of the Uniswap pool (in hundredths of basis points) 57 | | /// @return The metadata URI for the given characteristics 58 | | function constructMetadata( 59 | | address panopticPool, 60 | | string memory symbol0, 61 | | string memory symbol1, 62 | | uint256 fee 63 | | ) public view returns (string memory) { 64 | * | uint256 lastCharVal = uint160(panopticPool) & 0xF; 65 | | uint256 rarity = PanopticMath.numberOfLeadingHexZeros(panopticPool); 66 | | 67 | | string memory svgOut = generateSVGArt(lastCharVal, rarity); 68 | | 69 | | svgOut = generateSVGInfo(svgOut, panopticPool, rarity, symbol0, symbol1); 70 | | return 71 | | string( 72 | | abi.encodePacked( 73 | | "data:application/json;base64,", 74 | * | Base64.encode( 75 | | bytes( 76 | | abi.encodePacked( 77 | | '{"name":"', 78 | | abi.encodePacked( 79 | | LibString.toHexString(uint256(uint160(panopticPool)), 20), 80 | | "-", 81 | * | string.concat( 82 | * | metadata[bytes32("strategies")][lastCharVal].dataStr(), 83 | | "-", 84 | | LibString.toString(rarity) 85 | | ) 86 | | ), 87 | | '", "description":"', 88 | | string.concat( 89 | | "Panoptic Pool for the ", 90 | | symbol0, 91 | * | "-", 92 | * | symbol1, 93 | * | "-", 94 | | PanopticMath.uniswapFeeToString(uint24(fee)), 95 | | " market" 96 | | ), 97 | | '", "attributes": [{"trait_type": "Rarity", "value": "', 98 | | string.concat( 99 | | LibString.toString(rarity), 100 | | " - ", 101 | | metadata[bytes32("rarities")][rarity].dataStr() 102 | | ), 103 | * | '"}, {"trait_type": "Strategy", "value": "', 104 | * | metadata[bytes32("strategies")][lastCharVal].dataStr(), 105 | * | '"}, {"trait_type": "ChainId", "value": "', 106 | * | getChainName(), 107 | | '"}], "image": "data:image/svg+xml;base64,', 108 | | Base64.encode(bytes(svgOut)), 109 | | '"}' 110 | | ) 111 | * | ) 112 | | ) 113 | | ) 114 | | ); 115 | | } 116 | | 117 | | /// @notice Generate the artwork component of the SVG for a given rarity and last character value. 118 | * | /// @param lastCharVal The last character of the pool address 119 | * | /// @param rarity The rarity of the NFT 120 | * | /// @return svgOut The SVG artwork for the NFT 121 | * | function generateSVGArt( 122 | * | uint256 lastCharVal, 123 | | uint256 rarity 124 | * | ) internal view returns (string memory svgOut) { 125 | * | svgOut = metadata[bytes32("frames")][ 126 | * | rarity < 18 ? rarity / 3 : rarity < 23 ? 23 - rarity : 0 127 | | ].decompressedDataStr(); 128 | | svgOut = svgOut.replace( 129 | | "<!-- LABEL -->", 130 | | write( 131 | * | metadata[bytes32("strategies")][lastCharVal].dataStr(), 132 | * | maxStrategyLabelWidth(rarity) 133 | * | ) 134 | | ); 135 | | 136 | * | svgOut = svgOut 137 | * | .replace( 138 | * | "<!-- TEXT -->", 139 | | metadata[bytes32("descriptions")][lastCharVal + 16 * (rarity / 8)] 140 | | .decompressedDataStr() 141 | | ) 142 | | .replace("<!-- ART -->", metadata[bytes32("art")][lastCharVal].decompressedDataStr()) 143 | | .replace("<!-- FILTER -->", metadata[bytes32("filters")][rarity].decompressedDataStr()); 144 | | } 145 | | 146 | | /// @notice Fill in the pool/rarity specific text fields on the SVG artwork. 147 | | /// @param svgIn The SVG artwork to complete 148 | | /// @param panopticPool The address of the Panoptic Pool 149 | | /// @param rarity The rarity of the NFT 150 | | /// @param symbol0 The symbol of `token0` in the Uniswap pool 151 | | /// @param symbol1 The symbol of `token1` in the Uniswap pool 152 | | /// @return The final SVG artwork with the pool/rarity specific text fields filled in 153 | | function generateSVGInfo( 154 | | string memory svgIn, 155 | | address panopticPool, 156 | | uint256 rarity, 157 | | string memory symbol0, 158 | | string memory symbol1 159 | | ) internal view returns (string memory) { 160 | | svgIn = svgIn 161 | | .replace("<!-- POOLADDRESS -->", LibString.toHexString(uint160(panopticPool), 20)) 162 | | .replace("<!-- CHAINID -->", getChainName()); 163 | | 164 | | svgIn = svgIn.replace( 165 | | "<!-- RARITY_NAME -->", 166 | | write(metadata[bytes32("rarities")][rarity].dataStr(), maxRarityLabelWidth(rarity)) 167 | | ); 168 | | 169 | | return 170 | | svgIn 171 | | .replace("<!-- RARITY -->", write(LibString.toString(rarity))) 172 | | .replace("<!-- SYMBOL0 -->", write(symbol0, maxSymbolWidth(rarity))) 173 | | .replace("<!-- SYMBOL1 -->", write(symbol1, maxSymbolWidth(rarity))); 174 | | } 175 | | 176 | | /// @notice Get the name of the current chain. 177 | | /// @return The name of the current chain, or the chain ID if not recognized 178 | | function getChainName() internal view returns (string memory) { 179 | | if (block.chainid == 1) { 180 | | return "Ethereum Mainnet"; 181 | | } else if (block.chainid == 56) { 182 | | return "BNB Smart Chain Mainnet"; 183 | | } else if (block.chainid == 42161) { 184 | | return "Arbitrum One"; 185 | | } else if (block.chainid == 8453) { 186 | | return "Base"; 187 | | } else if (block.chainid == 43114) { 188 | | return "Avalanche C-Chain"; 189 | | } else if (block.chainid == 137) { 190 | | return "Polygon Mainnet"; 191 | | } else if (block.chainid == 10) { 192 | | return "OP Mainnet"; 193 | | } else if (block.chainid == 42220) { 194 | | return "Celo Mainnet"; 195 | | } else if (block.chainid == 238) { 196 | | return "Blast Mainnet"; 197 | | } else { 198 | | return LibString.toString(block.chainid); 199 | | } 200 | | } 201 | | 202 | | /// @notice Get a group of paths representing `chars` written in a certain font at a default size. 203 | | /// @param chars The characters to write 204 | | /// @return The group of paths representing the characters written in the font 205 | | function write(string memory chars) internal view returns (string memory) { 206 | | return write(chars, type(uint256).max); 207 | | } 208 | | 209 | | /// @notice Get a group of paths representing `chars` written in a certain font, scaled to a maximum width. 210 | * | /// @param chars The characters to write 211 | | /// @param maxWidth The maximum width (in SVG units) of the group of paths 212 | | /// @return fontGroup The group of paths representing the characters written in the font 213 | | function write( 214 | | string memory chars, 215 | | uint256 maxWidth 216 | | ) internal view returns (string memory fontGroup) { 217 | | // the sum of all character widths in `chars` 218 | | uint256 offset; 219 | | 220 | | for (uint256 i = 0; i < bytes(chars).length; ++i) { 221 | | // character widths are hardcoded in the metadata 222 | | uint256 charOffset = uint256( 223 | | bytes32(metadata[bytes32("charOffsets")][uint256(bytes32(bytes(chars)[i]))].data()) 224 | | ); 225 | | offset += charOffset; 226 | | 227 | | fontGroup = string.concat( 228 | | '<g transform="translate(-', 229 | | LibString.toString(charOffset), 230 | | ', 0)">', 231 | | fontGroup, 232 | | metadata[bytes32("charPaths")][uint256(bytes32(bytes(chars)[i]))].dataStr(), 233 | | "</g>" 234 | | ); 235 | | } 236 | | 237 | | // scale the font to fit within the maximum width, if necessary 238 | | string memory factor; 239 | | if (offset > maxWidth) { 240 | | uint256 _scale = (3400 * maxWidth) / offset; 241 | | if (_scale > 99) { 242 | | factor = LibString.toString(_scale); 243 | | } else { 244 | | factor = string.concat("0", LibString.toString(_scale)); 245 | | } 246 | | } else { 247 | | factor = "34"; 248 | | } 249 | | 250 | | fontGroup = string.concat( 251 | | '<g transform="scale(0.0', 252 | | factor, 253 | | ") translate(", 254 | | LibString.toString(offset / 2), 255 | | ', 0)">', 256 | | fontGroup, 257 | | "</g>" 258 | | ); 259 | | } 260 | | 261 | | /// @notice Get the maximum SVG unit width for the token symbols at the bottom left/right corners for a given rarity. 262 | | /// @dev This is to ensure the text fits within its section on the frame. There are 6 frames, and each rarity is assigned one of the six. 263 | | /// @param rarity The rarity of the NFT 264 | | /// @return width The maximum SVG unit width for the token symbols 265 | | function maxSymbolWidth(uint256 rarity) internal pure returns (uint256 width) { 266 | | if (rarity < 3) { 267 | | width = 1600; 268 | | } else if (rarity < 9) { 269 | | width = 1350; 270 | | } else if (rarity < 12) { 271 | | width = 1450; 272 | | } else if (rarity < 15) { 273 | | width = 1350; 274 | | } else if (rarity < 19) { 275 | | width = 1250; 276 | | } else if (rarity < 20) { 277 | | width = 1350; 278 | | } else if (rarity < 21) { 279 | | width = 1450; 280 | | } else if (rarity < 23) { 281 | | width = 1350; 282 | | } else if (rarity >= 23) { 283 | * | width = 1600; 284 | | } 285 | | } 286 | | 287 | * | /// @notice Get the maximum SVG unit width for the rarity name at the top for a given rarity (frame). 288 | * | /// @dev This is to ensure the text fits within its section on the frame. There are 6 frames, and each rarity is assigned one of the six. 289 | * | /// @param rarity The rarity of the NFT 290 | * | /// @return width The maximum SVG unit width for the rarity name 291 | * | function maxRarityLabelWidth(uint256 rarity) internal pure returns (uint256 width) { 292 | * | if (rarity < 3) { 293 | | width = 210; 294 | * | } else if (rarity < 6) { 295 | * | width = 220; 296 | * | } else if (rarity < 9) { 297 | * | width = 210; 298 | | } else if (rarity < 12) { 299 | | width = 220; 300 | | } else if (rarity < 15) { 301 | | width = 260; 302 | | } else if (rarity < 19) { 303 | | width = 225; 304 | | } else if (rarity < 20) { 305 | | width = 260; 306 | * | } else if (rarity < 21) { 307 | | width = 220; 308 | * | } else if (rarity < 22) { 309 | * | width = 210; 310 | * | } else if (rarity < 23) { 311 | * | width = 220; 312 | * | } else if (rarity >= 23) { 313 | | width = 210; 314 | | } 315 | | } 316 | | 317 | * | /// @notice Get the maximum SVG unit width for the strategy name label in the center for a given rarity (frame). 318 | * | /// @dev This is to ensure the text fits within its section on the frame. There are 6 frames, and each rarity is assigned one of the six. 319 | * | /// @param rarity The rarity of the NFT 320 | * | /// @return width The maximum SVG unit width for the strategy name label 321 | * | function maxStrategyLabelWidth(uint256 rarity) internal pure returns (uint256 width) { 322 | * | if (rarity < 6) { 323 | | width = 9000; 324 | * | } else if (rarity <= 22) { 325 | | width = 3900; 326 | * | } else if (rarity > 22) { 327 | * | width = 9000; 328 | | } 329 | | } 330 | | } 331 | * |
/home/xshadow/panoptic-v1-core-private/contracts/base/MetadataStore.sol 1 | | // SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1 2 | | pragma solidity ^0.8.18; 3 | | 4 | | // Custom types 5 | | import {Pointer} from "@types/Pointer.sol"; 6 | | 7 | | /// @title MetadataStore: Cost-effective structured data storage. 8 | | /// @notice Base contract that can store two-deep objects with large value sizes at deployment time. 9 | | /// @author Axicon Labs Limited 10 | | contract MetadataStore { 11 | | /// @notice Stores metadata pointers for future retrieval. 12 | | /// @dev Can hold 2-deep object structures. 13 | | /// @dev Examples include `{"A": ["B", "C"]}` and `{"A": {"B": "C"}}`. 14 | | /// @dev The first and second keys can be up-to-32-char strings (or array indices. 15 | | /// @dev Values are pointers to a certain section of contract code: [address, start, length]. 16 | | /// @dev The maximum size of a value is theoretically unlimited, but depends on the effective contract size limit for a given chain. 17 | | mapping(bytes32 property => mapping(uint256 index => Pointer pointer)) internal metadata; 18 | | 19 | | /// @notice Stores provided metadata pointers for future retrieval. 20 | | /// @param properties An array of identifiers for different categories of metadata 21 | | /// @param indices A nested array of keys for K-V metadata pairs for each property in `properties` 22 | | /// @param pointers Contains pointers to the metadata values stored in contract data slices for each index in `indices` 23 | | constructor( 24 | | bytes32[] memory properties, 25 | | uint256[][] memory indices, 26 | | Pointer[][] memory pointers 27 | | ) { 28 | | for (uint256 i = 0; i < properties.length; i++) { 29 | | for (uint256 j = 0; j < indices[i].length; j++) { 30 | | metadata[properties[i]][indices[i][j]] = pointers[i][j]; 31 | | } 32 | | } 33 | | } 34 | | } 35 | |
/home/xshadow/panoptic-v1-core-private/contracts/base/Multicall.sol 1 | | // SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-2.0-or-later 2 | | pragma solidity ^0.8.18; 3 | | 4 | | /// @title Multicall 5 | | /// @notice Enables calling multiple methods in a single call to the contract. 6 | | /// @dev Helpful for performing batch operations such as an "emergency exit", or simply creating advanced positions. 7 | | /// @author Axicon Labs Limited 8 | | abstract contract Multicall { 9 | | /// @notice Performs multiple calls on the inheritor in a single transaction, and returns the data from each call. 10 | | /// @param data The calldata for each call 11 | | /// @return results The data returned by each call 12 | * | function multicall(bytes[] calldata data) public payable returns (bytes[] memory results) { 13 | | results = new bytes[](data.length); 14 | * | for (uint256 i = 0; i < data.length; ) { 15 | | (bool success, bytes memory result) = address(this).delegatecall(data[i]); 16 | | 17 | | if (!success) { 18 | | // Bubble up the revert reason 19 | | // The bytes type is ABI encoded as a length-prefixed byte array 20 | | // So we simply need to add 32 to the pointer to get the start of the data 21 | | // And then revert with the size loaded from the first 32 bytes 22 | | // Other solutions will do work to differentiate the revert reasons and provide paranthetical information 23 | | // However, we have chosen to simply replicate the the normal behavior of the call 24 | | // NOTE: memory-safe because it reads from memory already allocated by solidity (the bytes memory result) 25 | | assembly ("memory-safe") { 26 | | revert(add(result, 32), mload(result)) 27 | | } 28 | | } 29 | | 30 | | results[i] = result; 31 | | 32 | | unchecked { 33 | | ++i; 34 | | } 35 | | } 36 | | } 37 | | } 38 | |
/home/xshadow/panoptic-v1-core-private/contracts/fuzz/echidna/CollateralActions.sol 1 | | // SPDX-License-Identifier: UNLICENSED 2 | | pragma solidity ^0.8.12; 3 | | 4 | | import "./FuzzHelpers.sol"; 5 | | import {SFPMActions} from "./SFPMActions.sol"; 6 | | 7 | | contract CollateralActions is SFPMActions { 8 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 9 | | POSITIVE FUNCTIONAL 10 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 11 | | 12 | | /// @custom:property PANO-DEP-001 The Panoptic pool balance must increase by the deposited amount when a deposit is made (or the corresponding amount of assets for a given share value when a mint is made) 13 | | /// @custom:property PANO-DEP-002 The user balance must decrease by the deposited amount when a deposit is made (or the corresponding amount of assets for a given share value when a mint is made) 14 | | /// @custom:property PANO-DEP-003 A user's share balance must increase by the amount of shares previewMint returns 15 | * | function deposit_to_ct(bool token0, uint256 assets, bool viaMint) public { 16 | * | if (token0) { 17 | * | emit LogString("Attempting to deposit/mint token0"); 18 | * | _deposit_and_check(collToken0, viaMint, assets, msg.sender); 19 | | } else { 20 | * | emit LogString("Attempting to deposit/mint token1"); 21 | * | _deposit_and_check(collToken1, viaMint, assets, msg.sender); 22 | | } 23 | | } 24 | | 25 | * | function _deposit_and_check( 26 | | CollateralTracker collToken, 27 | | bool viaMint, 28 | | uint256 assets, 29 | | address depositor 30 | | ) internal { 31 | * | uint256 depositorBalBefore = IERC20(collToken.asset()).balanceOf(depositor); 32 | * | uint256 poolBalBefore = IERC20(collToken.asset()).balanceOf(address(panopticPool)); 33 | * | uint256 sharesBefore = collToken.balanceOf(depositor); 34 | | 35 | * | assets = bound(assets, 1, MAX_DEPOSIT); 36 | | 37 | | // Limit the maximum amount of collateral to deposit 38 | * | if (collToken.convertToAssets(collToken.balanceOf(depositor)) > 10 * MAX_DEPOSIT) { 39 | | return; 40 | | } 41 | * | assets = bound(assets, MIN_DEPOSIT, min(MAX_DEPOSIT, depositorBalBefore / 10)); 42 | * | uint256 shares = collToken.previewDeposit(assets); 43 | | 44 | * | hevm.prank(depositor); 45 | * | if (viaMint) { 46 | * | collToken.mint(shares, depositor); 47 | | } else { 48 | * | collToken.deposit(assets, depositor); 49 | | } 50 | | 51 | * | uint256 poolBalAfter = IERC20(collToken.asset()).balanceOf(address(panopticPool)); 52 | * | assertWithMsg( 53 | * | poolBalAfter - poolBalBefore == assets, 54 | | "Pool token balance incorrect after deposit" 55 | | ); 56 | * | uint256 depositorBalAfter = IERC20(collToken.asset()).balanceOf(depositor); 57 | * | assertWithMsg( 58 | * | depositorBalBefore - depositorBalAfter == assets, 59 | | "User token balance incorrect after deposit" 60 | | ); 61 | * | uint256 sharesAfter = collToken.balanceOf(depositor); 62 | * | assertWithMsg( 63 | * | sharesAfter - sharesBefore == shares, 64 | | "User shares balance incorrect after deposit" 65 | | ); 66 | | } 67 | | 68 | | /// @custom:property PANO-WIT-001 The Panoptic pool balance must decrease by the withdrawn amount when a withdrawal is made 69 | | /// @custom:property PANO-WIT-002 The user balance must increase by the withdrawn amount when a withdrawal is made 70 | * | function withdraw_from_ct( 71 | | bool token0, 72 | | bool viaRedeem, 73 | | uint256 assets, 74 | | address withdrawer 75 | | ) public { 76 | * | uint256 numOfPositions = panopticPool.numberOfPositions(withdrawer); 77 | * | if (numOfPositions > 0) { 78 | | if (token0) { 79 | | emit LogString("Attempting to withdraw token0 with open positions"); 80 | | _withdraw_with_open_positions_and_check(collToken0, assets, withdrawer, true); 81 | | } else { 82 | | emit LogString("Attempting to withdraw token1 with open positions"); 83 | | _withdraw_with_open_positions_and_check(collToken1, assets, withdrawer, false); 84 | | } 85 | | } else { 86 | * | if (token0) { 87 | * | emit LogString("Attempting to withdraw/redeem token0 without open positions"); 88 | * | _regular_withdraw_and_check(collToken0, viaRedeem, assets, withdrawer); 89 | | } else { 90 | * | emit LogString("Attempting to withdraw/redeem token1 without open positions"); 91 | * | _regular_withdraw_and_check(collToken1, viaRedeem, assets, withdrawer); 92 | | } 93 | | } 94 | | } 95 | | 96 | * | function _regular_withdraw_and_check( 97 | | CollateralTracker collToken, 98 | | bool viaRedeem, 99 | | uint256 assetsToWithdraw, 100 | | address withdrawer 101 | | ) internal { 102 | * | uint256 withdrawerAssetsBefore = IERC20(collToken.asset()).balanceOf(withdrawer); 103 | * | uint256 poolAssetsBefore = IERC20(collToken.asset()).balanceOf(address(panopticPool)); 104 | * | uint256 withdrawerSharesBefore = collToken.balanceOf(withdrawer); 105 | | 106 | * | assetsToWithdraw = bound( 107 | * | assetsToWithdraw, 108 | * | 1, 109 | * | _max_assets_withdrawable(collToken, collToken.balanceOf(withdrawer)) 110 | | ); 111 | | 112 | | uint256 sharesToWithdraw = collToken.previewWithdraw(assetsToWithdraw); 113 | | 114 | | hevm.prank(withdrawer); 115 | | if (viaRedeem) { 116 | | try collToken.redeem(sharesToWithdraw, withdrawer, withdrawer) { 117 | | uint256 poolAssetsAfter = IERC20(collToken.asset()).balanceOf( 118 | | address(panopticPool) 119 | | ); 120 | | uint256 withdrawerAssetsAfter = IERC20(collToken.asset()).balanceOf(withdrawer); 121 | | uint256 withdrawerSharesAfter = collToken.balanceOf(withdrawer); 122 | | assertWithMsg( 123 | | poolAssetsBefore - poolAssetsAfter == assetsToWithdraw, 124 | | "Pool asset balance incorrect after redemption" 125 | | ); 126 | | assertWithMsg( 127 | | withdrawerAssetsAfter - withdrawerAssetsBefore == assetsToWithdraw, 128 | | "User balance incorrect after redemption" 129 | | ); 130 | | assertWithMsg( 131 | | withdrawerSharesBefore - withdrawerSharesAfter == sharesToWithdraw, 132 | | "User share balance incorrect after redemption" 133 | | ); 134 | | } catch { 135 | | assertWithMsg(false, "Failed to redeem for unknown reason"); 136 | | } 137 | | } else { 138 | | try collToken.withdraw(assetsToWithdraw, withdrawer, withdrawer) { 139 | | uint256 poolAssetsAfter = IERC20(collToken.asset()).balanceOf( 140 | | address(panopticPool) 141 | | ); 142 | | uint256 withdrawerAssetsAfter = IERC20(collToken.asset()).balanceOf(withdrawer); 143 | | uint256 withdrawerSharesAfter = collToken.balanceOf(withdrawer); 144 | | assertWithMsg( 145 | | poolAssetsBefore - poolAssetsAfter == assetsToWithdraw, 146 | | "Pool asset balance incorrect after withdrawal" 147 | | ); 148 | | assertWithMsg( 149 | | withdrawerAssetsAfter - withdrawerAssetsBefore == assetsToWithdraw, 150 | | "User balance incorrect after withdrawal" 151 | | ); 152 | | assertWithMsg( 153 | | withdrawerSharesBefore - withdrawerSharesAfter == sharesToWithdraw, 154 | | "User share balance incorrect after withdrawal" 155 | | ); 156 | | } catch { 157 | | assertWithMsg(false, "Failed to withdraw for unknown reason"); 158 | | } 159 | | } 160 | | } 161 | | 162 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 163 | | NEGATIVE FUNCTIONAL 164 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 165 | | 166 | | /// @custom:property PANO-SYS-001 The max withdrawal or redemption amount of users with open positions is zero, excluding the overloaded withdraw that takes in a positionId list 167 | | /// @custom:property PANO-SYS-002 Users can't withdraw or redeem collateral with open positions, excluding the overloaded withdraw that takes in a positionId list 168 | | /// @custom:precondition The user has a position open 169 | * | function invariant_collateral_removal_via_withdrawal_or_redemption( 170 | | uint256 fuzzNumerator, 171 | | uint256 fuzzDenominator, 172 | | address recipient, 173 | | uint256 fullOrSelfFuzz 174 | | ) public { 175 | * | uint256 numOfPositions = panopticPool.numberOfPositions(msg.sender); 176 | * | emit LogAddress("Caller", msg.sender); 177 | * | emit LogUint256("Positions opened for user", numOfPositions); 178 | | 179 | * | if (numOfPositions > 0) { 180 | | uint256 shareBal0 = collToken0.balanceOf(msg.sender); 181 | | uint256 shareBal1 = collToken1.balanceOf(msg.sender); 182 | | uint assetBal0 = collToken0.convertToAssets(shareBal0); 183 | | uint assetBal1 = collToken1.convertToAssets(shareBal1); 184 | | 185 | | assertWithMsg( 186 | | collToken0.maxWithdraw(msg.sender) == 0 && collToken1.maxWithdraw(msg.sender) == 0, 187 | | "It is possible to withdraw assets when the user has open positions" 188 | | ); 189 | | assertWithMsg( 190 | | collToken0.maxRedeem(msg.sender) == 0 && collToken1.maxRedeem(msg.sender) == 0, 191 | | "It is possible to redeem assets when the user has open positions" 192 | | ); 193 | | 194 | | // fuzz a fraction of the total balance to try and withdraw 195 | | if (fuzzNumerator > fuzzDenominator) 196 | | (fuzzNumerator, fuzzDenominator) = (fuzzDenominator, fuzzNumerator); 197 | | 198 | | // attempt a full withdrawal every 3rd attempt, and a self-withdrawal every 5th attempt, to ensure we're testing those cases 199 | | if (fullOrSelfFuzz % 3 == 0) fuzzNumerator = fuzzDenominator; 200 | | if (fullOrSelfFuzz % 5 == 0) recipient = msg.sender; 201 | | 202 | | uint256 fuzzedSharesToRedeem0 = (shareBal0 * fuzzNumerator) / fuzzDenominator; 203 | | uint256 fuzzedSharesToRedeem1 = (shareBal1 * fuzzNumerator) / fuzzDenominator; 204 | | 205 | | uint256 fuzzedAssetsToWithdraw0 = (assetBal0 * fuzzNumerator) / fuzzDenominator; 206 | | uint256 fuzzedAssetsToWithdraw1 = (assetBal1 * fuzzNumerator) / fuzzDenominator; 207 | | 208 | | if (fuzzedAssetsToWithdraw0 > 0) { 209 | | hevm.prank(msg.sender); 210 | | try collToken0.redeem(fuzzedSharesToRedeem0, recipient, msg.sender) { 211 | | assertWithMsg(false, "Collateral could be removed with open positions"); 212 | | } catch {} 213 | | 214 | | hevm.prank(msg.sender); 215 | | try collToken0.withdraw(fuzzedAssetsToWithdraw0, recipient, msg.sender) { 216 | | assertWithMsg(false, "Collateral could be removed with open positions"); 217 | | } catch {} 218 | | } 219 | | 220 | | if (fuzzedAssetsToWithdraw1 > 0) { 221 | | hevm.prank(msg.sender); 222 | | try collToken1.redeem(fuzzedSharesToRedeem1, recipient, msg.sender) { 223 | | assertWithMsg(false, "Collateral could be removed with open positions"); 224 | | } catch {} 225 | | 226 | | hevm.prank(msg.sender); 227 | | try collToken1.withdraw(fuzzedAssetsToWithdraw1, recipient, msg.sender) { 228 | | assertWithMsg(false, "Collateral could be removed with open positions"); 229 | | } catch {} 230 | | } 231 | | } 232 | | } 233 | | 234 | | /// @custom:property PANO-SYS-003 The max transfer amount of users with open positions is zero 235 | | /// @custom:property PANO-SYS-004 Users can't transfer collateral with open positions 236 | | /// @custom:precondition The user has a position open 237 | * | function invariant_collateral_removal_via_transfer( 238 | | uint256 fuzzNumerator, 239 | | uint256 fuzzDenominator, 240 | | address recipient, 241 | | uint256 fullOrNotFuzz 242 | * | ) public { 243 | * | uint256 numOfPositions = panopticPool.numberOfPositions(msg.sender); 244 | * | emit LogAddress("Caller", msg.sender); 245 | * | emit LogUint256("Positions opened for user", numOfPositions); 246 | | 247 | * | if (numOfPositions > 0) { 248 | | uint256 bal0 = collToken0.balanceOf(msg.sender); 249 | | uint256 bal1 = collToken1.balanceOf(msg.sender); 250 | | 251 | | if (fuzzNumerator > fuzzDenominator) 252 | | (fuzzNumerator, fuzzDenominator) = (fuzzDenominator, fuzzNumerator); 253 | | 254 | | uint256 fuzzedAmtToTransfer0 = (bal0 * fuzzNumerator) / fuzzDenominator; 255 | | uint256 fuzzedAmtToTransfer1 = (bal1 * fuzzNumerator) / fuzzDenominator; 256 | | 257 | | // attempt a full withdrawal every 4th attempt, to ensure we're testing that case too 258 | | if (fullOrNotFuzz % 4 == 0) (fuzzedAmtToTransfer0, fuzzedAmtToTransfer1) = (bal0, bal1); 259 | | 260 | | if (fuzzedAmtToTransfer0 > 0) { 261 | | hevm.prank(msg.sender); 262 | | try collToken0.transfer(recipient, fuzzedAmtToTransfer0) { 263 | | assertWithMsg( 264 | | false, 265 | | "Collateral could be removed via transfer with open positions" 266 | | ); 267 | | } catch {} 268 | | 269 | | hevm.prank(msg.sender); 270 | | collToken0.approve(recipient, fuzzedAmtToTransfer0); 271 | | 272 | | hevm.prank(recipient); 273 | | try collToken0.transferFrom(msg.sender, recipient, fuzzedAmtToTransfer0) { 274 | | assertWithMsg( 275 | | false, 276 | | "Collateral could be removed via transferFrom with open positions" 277 | | ); 278 | | } catch {} 279 | | } 280 | | if (fuzzedAmtToTransfer1 > 0) { 281 | | hevm.prank(msg.sender); 282 | | try collToken1.transfer(recipient, fuzzedAmtToTransfer1) { 283 | | assertWithMsg( 284 | | false, 285 | | "Collateral could be removed via transfer with open positions" 286 | | ); 287 | | } catch {} 288 | | 289 | | hevm.prank(msg.sender); 290 | | collToken1.approve(recipient, fuzzedAmtToTransfer1); 291 | | 292 | | hevm.prank(recipient); 293 | | try collToken1.transferFrom(msg.sender, recipient, fuzzedAmtToTransfer1) { 294 | | assertWithMsg( 295 | | false, 296 | | "Collateral could be removed via transferFrom with open positions" 297 | | ); 298 | | } catch {} 299 | | } 300 | | } 301 | | } 302 | | 303 | | /// @custom:property PANO-SYS-005 Users can't use the overloaded withdraw to withdraw so much that it makes their open positions insolvent 304 | * | function invariant_collateral_overremoval_with_open_positions( 305 | | CollateralTracker collToken, 306 | | uint256 amountToWithdraw 307 | | ) public { 308 | * | _attempt_collateral_overremoval(collToken0, msg.sender, true, amountToWithdraw); 309 | * | _attempt_collateral_overremoval(collToken1, msg.sender, false, amountToWithdraw); 310 | | } 311 | | 312 | * | function _attempt_collateral_overremoval( 313 | | CollateralTracker collToken, 314 | | address withdrawer, 315 | | bool isToken0, 316 | | uint256 amountToWithdraw 317 | | ) internal { 318 | * | TokenId[] memory withdrawersOpenPositions = userPositions[withdrawer]; 319 | | // return early if user has no open positions 320 | * | if (withdrawersOpenPositions.length == 0) return; 321 | | 322 | | amountToWithdraw = bound( 323 | | amountToWithdraw, 324 | | 1, 325 | * | _max_assets_withdrawable(collToken, collToken.balanceOf(withdrawer)) 326 | | ); 327 | | try panopticPool.validateCollateralWithdrawable(withdrawer, withdrawersOpenPositions) { 328 | | // Do nothing: the user _can_ withdraw their collateral, so there's nothing to test 329 | | } catch { 330 | | // if validateCollateralWithdrawable says we should not be able to withdraw, 331 | | // then we should fail here: 332 | | hevm.prank(withdrawer); 333 | | try 334 | | collToken.withdraw( 335 | | amountToWithdraw, 336 | | withdrawer, 337 | | withdrawer, 338 | | withdrawersOpenPositions 339 | | ) 340 | | { 341 | | assertWithMsg( 342 | | false, 343 | | "User was able to withdraw despite validateCollateralWithdrawable saying they could not" 344 | | ); 345 | | } catch {} 346 | | } 347 | | } 348 | | 349 | | /// @custom:property PANO-SYS-009 No user can ever withdraw greater than the Collateral Tracker's internally-accounted poolAssets 350 | * | function invariant_no_withdrawal_gt_pool_assets( 351 | | address owner, 352 | | address recipient, 353 | | uint256 amountOver, 354 | | bool nonOwnerCall 355 | | ) public { 356 | * | _attempt_withdrawal_gt_pool_assets_via_withdraw( 357 | * | collToken0, 358 | * | owner, 359 | * | recipient, 360 | * | amountOver, 361 | * | nonOwnerCall 362 | | ); 363 | * | _attempt_withdrawal_gt_pool_assets_via_withdraw( 364 | * | collToken1, 365 | * | owner, 366 | * | recipient, 367 | * | amountOver, 368 | * | nonOwnerCall 369 | | ); 370 | | 371 | * | _attempt_withdrawal_gt_pool_assets_via_redeem( 372 | * | collToken0, 373 | * | owner, 374 | * | recipient, 375 | * | amountOver, 376 | * | nonOwnerCall 377 | | ); 378 | * | _attempt_withdrawal_gt_pool_assets_via_redeem( 379 | * | collToken1, 380 | * | owner, 381 | * | recipient, 382 | * | amountOver, 383 | * | nonOwnerCall 384 | | ); 385 | | } 386 | | 387 | * | function _attempt_withdrawal_gt_pool_assets_via_withdraw( 388 | | CollateralTracker collToken, 389 | | address owner, 390 | | address recipient, 391 | | uint256 amountOver, 392 | | bool nonOwnerCall 393 | | ) internal { 394 | * | (uint256 ct_s_poolAssets, , ) = collToken.getPoolData(); 395 | * | amountOver = bound(amountOver, 1, type(uint256).max - ct_s_poolAssets); 396 | * | uint256 numOfPositions = panopticPool.numberOfPositions(owner); 397 | * | TokenId[] memory withdrawersOpenPositions = userPositions[owner]; 398 | | 399 | * | hevm.prank(owner); 400 | * | if (nonOwnerCall) { 401 | *r | collToken.approve(recipient, collToken.convertToShares(ct_s_poolAssets + amountOver)); 402 | * | hevm.prank(recipient); 403 | | } 404 | | 405 | * | if (numOfPositions == 0) { 406 | * | try collToken.withdraw(ct_s_poolAssets + amountOver, recipient, owner) { 407 | * | assertWithMsg(false, "User withdrew > collateralTokens poolAssets"); 408 | | } catch { 409 | * | if ( 410 | *r | collToken.convertToShares(ct_s_poolAssets + amountOver) > 411 | * | collToken.balanceOf(owner) 412 | | ) { 413 | * | emit LogString( 414 | | "invariant_no_withdrawal_gt_pool_assets succeeded because user didnt have enough shares to attempt overwithdrawal" 415 | | ); 416 | | } else { 417 | | // NOTE: we could add a deal of the collToken.asset() if we wanted to ensure we hit this case more often 418 | | emit LogString( 419 | | "invariant_no_withdrawal_gt_pool_assets succeeded, possibly because we correctly enforced a max withdrawal of ct_s_poolAssets" 420 | | ); 421 | | } 422 | | } 423 | | 424 | * | nonOwnerCall ? hevm.prank(recipient) : hevm.prank(owner); 425 | * | try 426 | * | collToken.withdraw(ct_s_poolAssets + amountOver, recipient, owner, new TokenId[](0)) 427 | | { 428 | | assertWithMsg(false, "User withdrew > collateralTokens poolAssets"); 429 | | } catch { 430 | * | if ( 431 | * | collToken.convertToShares(ct_s_poolAssets + amountOver) > 432 | * | collToken.balanceOf(owner) 433 | | ) { 434 | * | emit LogString( 435 | | "invariant_no_withdrawal_gt_pool_assets succeeded because user didnt have enough shares to attempt overwithdrawal" 436 | | ); 437 | | } else { 438 | | // NOTE: we could add a deal of the collToken.asset() if we wanted to ensure we hit this case more often 439 | | emit LogString( 440 | | "invariant_no_withdrawal_gt_pool_assets succeeded, possibly because we correctly enforced a max withdrawal of ct_s_poolAssets" 441 | | ); 442 | | } 443 | | } 444 | | } else { 445 | | try 446 | | collToken.withdraw( 447 | | ct_s_poolAssets + amountOver, 448 | | recipient, 449 | | owner, 450 | | withdrawersOpenPositions 451 | | ) 452 | | { 453 | | assertWithMsg(false, "User withdrew > collateralTokens poolAssets"); 454 | | } catch { 455 | | if ( 456 | | collToken.convertToShares(ct_s_poolAssets + amountOver) > 457 | | collToken.balanceOf(owner) 458 | | ) { 459 | | emit LogString( 460 | | "invariant_no_withdrawal_gt_pool_assets succeeded because user didnt have enough shares to attempt overwithdrawal" 461 | | ); 462 | | } else { 463 | | // NOTE: we could add a deal of the collToken.asset() if we wanted to ensure we hit this case more often 464 | | emit LogString( 465 | | "invariant_no_withdrawal_gt_pool_assets succeeded, possibly because we correctly enforced a max withdrawal of ct_s_poolAssets" 466 | | ); 467 | | } 468 | | } 469 | | } 470 | | } 471 | | 472 | * | function _attempt_withdrawal_gt_pool_assets_via_redeem( 473 | | CollateralTracker collToken, 474 | | address owner, 475 | | address recipient, 476 | | uint256 amountOver, 477 | | bool nonOwnerCall 478 | * | ) internal { 479 | * | (uint256 ct_s_poolAssets, , ) = collToken.getPoolData(); 480 | * | amountOver = bound(amountOver, 1, type(uint256).max - ct_s_poolAssets); 481 | | 482 | * | uint256 numOfPositions = panopticPool.numberOfPositions(owner); 483 | * | if (numOfPositions == 0) { 484 | * | hevm.prank(owner); 485 | * | if (nonOwnerCall) { 486 | * | collToken.approve( 487 | * | recipient, 488 | * | collToken.convertToShares(ct_s_poolAssets) + amountOver 489 | | ); 490 | * | hevm.prank(recipient); 491 | | } 492 | | 493 | * | try 494 | * | collToken.redeem( 495 | * | collToken.convertToShares(ct_s_poolAssets) + amountOver, 496 | * | recipient, 497 | * | owner 498 | | ) 499 | | { 500 | * | assertWithMsg(false, "User redeemed > the poolAssets of collToken"); 501 | | } catch { 502 | * | if ( 503 | * | collToken.convertToShares(ct_s_poolAssets + amountOver) > 504 | * | collToken.balanceOf(owner) 505 | | ) { 506 | * | emit LogString( 507 | | "invariant_no_withdrawal_gt_pool_assets succeeded because user didnt have enough shares to attempt overwithdrawal" 508 | | ); 509 | | } else { 510 | | // NOTE: we could add a deal of the collToken.asset() if we wanted to ensure we hit this case more often 511 | | emit LogString( 512 | | "invariant_no_withdrawal_gt_pool_assets succeeded, possibly because we correctly enforced a max redemption of convertToShares(ct_s_poolAssets)" 513 | | ); 514 | | } 515 | | } 516 | | } 517 | | } 518 | | 519 | | /// @custom:property PANO-SYS-010 No user can ever withdraw, redeem, nor transfer an amount greater than their own balance 520 | * | function invariant_never_allow_overremoval( 521 | | address owner, 522 | | address recipient, 523 | | uint256 amountOver, 524 | | bool nonOwnerCall 525 | | ) public { 526 | * | _attempt_overwithdrawal_via_withdraw( 527 | * | collToken0, 528 | * | owner, 529 | * | recipient, 530 | * | amountOver, 531 | * | nonOwnerCall 532 | | ); 533 | * | _attempt_overwithdrawal_via_withdraw( 534 | * | collToken1, 535 | * | owner, 536 | * | recipient, 537 | * | amountOver, 538 | * | nonOwnerCall 539 | | ); 540 | | 541 | * | uint256 numOfPositions = panopticPool.numberOfPositions(owner); 542 | * | if (numOfPositions == 0) { 543 | * | _attempt_overwithdrawal_via_redeem( 544 | * | collToken0, 545 | * | owner, 546 | * | recipient, 547 | * | amountOver, 548 | * | nonOwnerCall 549 | | ); 550 | * | _attempt_overwithdrawal_via_redeem( 551 | * | collToken1, 552 | * | owner, 553 | * | recipient, 554 | * | amountOver, 555 | * | nonOwnerCall 556 | | ); 557 | | 558 | * | _attempt_overtransfer(collToken0, owner, recipient, amountOver, nonOwnerCall); 559 | * | _attempt_overtransfer(collToken1, owner, recipient, amountOver, nonOwnerCall); 560 | | } 561 | | } 562 | | 563 | * | function _attempt_overwithdrawal_via_withdraw( 564 | | CollateralTracker collToken, 565 | | address owner, 566 | | address recipient, 567 | | uint256 amountOver, 568 | | bool nonOwnerCall 569 | | ) internal { 570 | * | uint256 ownersAssets = collToken.convertToAssets(collToken.balanceOf(owner)); 571 | * | amountOver = bound(amountOver, 1, type(uint256).max - ownersAssets); 572 | * | uint256 numOfPositions = panopticPool.numberOfPositions(owner); 573 | * | TokenId[] memory withdrawersOpenPositions = userPositions[owner]; 574 | | 575 | * | hevm.prank(owner); 576 | | // every other attempt, make it a non-owner call: 577 | * | if (nonOwnerCall) { 578 | * | collToken.approve(recipient, collToken.convertToShares(ownersAssets) + amountOver); 579 | * | hevm.prank(recipient); 580 | | } 581 | | 582 | * | if (numOfPositions == 0) { 583 | * | try collToken.withdraw(ownersAssets + amountOver, recipient, owner) { 584 | * | assertWithMsg(false, "User withdrew > their balance"); 585 | | } catch {} 586 | | 587 | * | nonOwnerCall ? hevm.prank(recipient) : hevm.prank(owner); 588 | * | try collToken.withdraw(ownersAssets + amountOver, recipient, owner, new TokenId[](0)) { 589 | | assertWithMsg(false, "User withdrew > their balance"); 590 | | } catch {} 591 | | } else { 592 | | try 593 | | collToken.withdraw( 594 | | ownersAssets + amountOver, 595 | | recipient, 596 | | owner, 597 | | withdrawersOpenPositions 598 | | ) 599 | | { 600 | | assertWithMsg(false, "User withdrew > their balance"); 601 | | } catch {} 602 | | } 603 | | } 604 | | 605 | * | function _attempt_overwithdrawal_via_redeem( 606 | | CollateralTracker collToken, 607 | | address owner, 608 | | address recipient, 609 | | uint256 amountOver, 610 | | bool nonOwnerCall 611 | | ) internal { 612 | * | uint256 ownersShares = collToken.balanceOf(owner); 613 | * | amountOver = bound(amountOver, 1, type(uint256).max - ownersShares); 614 | | 615 | * | hevm.prank(owner); 616 | * | if (nonOwnerCall) { 617 | * | collToken.approve(recipient, ownersShares + amountOver); 618 | * | hevm.prank(recipient); 619 | | } 620 | | 621 | * | try collToken.redeem(ownersShares + amountOver, recipient, owner) { 622 | | assertWithMsg(false, "User redeemed > their balance"); 623 | | } catch {} 624 | | } 625 | | 626 | * | function _attempt_overtransfer( 627 | | CollateralTracker collToken, 628 | | address owner, 629 | | address recipient, 630 | | uint256 amountOver, 631 | | bool nonOwnerCall 632 | | ) internal { 633 | * | uint256 ownersShares = collToken.balanceOf(owner); 634 | * | amountOver = bound(amountOver, 1, type(uint256).max - ownersShares); 635 | * | hevm.prank(owner); 636 | * | if (nonOwnerCall) { 637 | * | try collToken.transfer(recipient, ownersShares + amountOver) { 638 | | assertWithMsg(false, "User transferred > their balance"); 639 | | } catch {} 640 | | } else { 641 | * | collToken.approve(recipient, ownersShares + amountOver); 642 | * | hevm.prank(recipient); 643 | * | try collToken.transferFrom(owner, recipient, ownersShares + amountOver) { 644 | | assertWithMsg(false, "User transferFromed > their balance");
