a : b; 35 | | } 36 | | 37 | * | /// @notice Compute the min of the incoming `a` and `b`. 38 | * | /// @param a The first number 39 | * | /// @param b The second number 40 | | /// @return The min of `a` and `b`: min(a, b), e.g.: min(4, 1) = 1 41 | * | function min(uint256 a, uint256 b) internal pure returns (uint256) { 42 | * | return a < b ? a : b; 43 | | } 44 | | 45 | * | /// @notice Compute the min of the incoming `a` and `b`. 46 | * | /// @param a The first number 47 | | /// @param b The second number 48 | | /// @return The min of `a` and `b`: min(a, b), e.g.: min(4, 1) = 1 49 | * | function min(int256 a, int256 b) internal pure returns (int256) { 50 | * | return a < b ? a : b; 51 | | } 52 | | 53 | | /// @notice Compute the max of the incoming `a` and `b`. 54 | | /// @param a The first number 55 | | /// @param b The second number 56 | | /// @return The max of `a` and `b`: max(a, b), e.g.: max(4, 1) = 4 57 | * | function max(uint256 a, uint256 b) internal pure returns (uint256) { 58 | * | return a > b ? a : b; 59 | | } 60 | | 61 | * | /// @notice Compute the max of the incoming `a` and `b`. 62 | | /// @param a The first number 63 | | /// @param b The second number 64 | * | /// @return The max of `a` and `b`: max(a, b), e.g.: max(4, 1) = 4 65 | * | function max(int256 a, int256 b) internal pure returns (int256) { 66 | * | return a > b ? a : b; 67 | | } 68 | | 69 | | /// @notice Compute the absolute value of an integer (int256). 70 | * | /// @param x The incoming *signed* integer to take the absolute value of 71 | | /// @dev Does not support `type(int256).min` and will revert (type(int256).max is one less). 72 | * | /// @return The absolute value of `x`, e.g. abs(-4) = 4 73 | * | function abs(int256 x) internal pure returns (int256) { 74 | * | return x > 0 ? x : -x; 75 | | } 76 | | 77 | * | /// @notice Compute the absolute value of an integer (int256). 78 | | /// @param x The incoming *signed* integer to take the absolute value of 79 | | /// @dev Supports `type(int256).min` because the corresponding value can fit in a uint (unlike `type(int256).max`). 80 | * | /// @return The absolute value of `x`, e.g. abs(-4) = 4 81 | * | function absUint(int256 x) internal pure returns (uint256) { 82 | | unchecked { 83 | * | return x > 0 ? uint256(x) : uint256(-x); 84 | | } 85 | | } 86 | | 87 | | /// @notice Returns the index of the most significant nibble of the 160-bit number, 88 | | /// where the least significant nibble is at index 0 and the most significant nibble is at index 40. 89 | * | /// @param x The value for which to compute the most significant nibble 90 | | /// @return r The index of the most significant nibble (default: 0) 91 | | function mostSignificantNibble(uint160 x) internal pure returns (uint256 r) { 92 | | unchecked { 93 | * | if (x >= 0x100000000000000000000000000000000) { 94 | * | x >>= 128; 95 | * | r += 32; 96 | * | } 97 | | if (x >= 0x10000000000000000) { 98 | * | x >>= 64; 99 | | r += 16; 100 | | } 101 | * | if (x >= 0x100000000) { 102 | | x >>= 32; 103 | * | r += 8; 104 | | } 105 | * | if (x >= 0x10000) { 106 | * | x >>= 16; 107 | | r += 4; 108 | | } 109 | | if (x >= 0x100) { 110 | | x >>= 8; 111 | | r += 2; 112 | | } 113 | | if (x >= 0x10) { 114 | | r += 1; 115 | | } 116 | | } 117 | | } 118 | | 119 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 120 | | TICK MATH 121 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 122 | | 123 | | /// @notice Calculates 1.0001^(tick/2) as an X96 number. 124 | * | /// @dev Implemented using Uniswap's "incorrect" constants. Supplying commented-out real values for an accurate calculation. 125 | | /// @dev Will revert if |tick| > max tick. 126 | | /// @param tick Value of the tick for which sqrt(1.0001^tick) is calculated 127 | | /// @return A Q64.96 number representing the sqrt price at the provided tick 128 | * | function getSqrtRatioAtTick(int24 tick) internal pure returns (uint160) { 129 | | unchecked { 130 | * | uint256 absTick = tick < 0 ? uint256(-int256(tick)) : uint256(int256(tick)); 131 | * | if (absTick > uint256(int256(Constants.MAX_V3POOL_TICK))) revert Errors.InvalidTick(); 132 | | 133 | * | // sqrt(1.0001^(-absTick)) = ∏ sqrt(1.0001^(-bit_i)) 134 | | // ex: absTick = 100 = binary 1100100, so sqrt(1.0001^-100) = sqrt(1.0001^-64) * sqrt(1.0001^-32) * sqrt(1.0001^-4) 135 | | // constants are 2^128/(sqrt(1.0001)^bit_i) rounded half-up 136 | | 137 | | // if the first bit is 0, initialize sqrtR to 1 (2^128) 138 | * | uint256 sqrtR = absTick & 0x1 != 0 139 | | ? 0xfffcb933bd6fad37aa2d162d1a594001 140 | | : 0x100000000000000000000000000000000; 141 | | 142 | * | if (absTick & 0x2 != 0) sqrtR = (sqrtR * 0xfff97272373d413259a46990580e213a) >> 128; 143 | | 144 | * | if (absTick & 0x4 != 0) sqrtR = (sqrtR * 0xfff2e50f5f656932ef12357cf3c7fdcc) >> 128; 145 | | 146 | * | if (absTick & 0x8 != 0) sqrtR = (sqrtR * 0xffe5caca7e10e4e61c3624eaa0941cd0) >> 128; 147 | | 148 | * | if (absTick & 0x10 != 0) sqrtR = (sqrtR * 0xffcb9843d60f6159c9db58835c926644) >> 128; 149 | | 150 | * | if (absTick & 0x20 != 0) sqrtR = (sqrtR * 0xff973b41fa98c081472e6896dfb254c0) >> 128; 151 | | 152 | * | if (absTick & 0x40 != 0) sqrtR = (sqrtR * 0xff2ea16466c96a3843ec78b326b52861) >> 128; 153 | | 154 | * | if (absTick & 0x80 != 0) sqrtR = (sqrtR * 0xfe5dee046a99a2a811c461f1969c3053) >> 128; 155 | | 156 | * | if (absTick & 0x100 != 0) sqrtR = (sqrtR * 0xfcbe86c7900a88aedcffc83b479aa3a4) >> 128; 157 | | 158 | * | if (absTick & 0x200 != 0) sqrtR = (sqrtR * 0xf987a7253ac413176f2b074cf7815e54) >> 128; 159 | | 160 | * | if (absTick & 0x400 != 0) sqrtR = (sqrtR * 0xf3392b0822b70005940c7a398e4b70f3) >> 128; 161 | | 162 | * | if (absTick & 0x800 != 0) sqrtR = (sqrtR * 0xe7159475a2c29b7443b29c7fa6e889d9) >> 128; 163 | | 164 | * | if (absTick & 0x1000 != 0) sqrtR = (sqrtR * 0xd097f3bdfd2022b8845ad8f792aa5825) >> 128; 165 | | 166 | * | if (absTick & 0x2000 != 0) sqrtR = (sqrtR * 0xa9f746462d870fdf8a65dc1f90e061e5) >> 128; 167 | | 168 | * | if (absTick & 0x4000 != 0) sqrtR = (sqrtR * 0x70d869a156d2a1b890bb3df62baf32f7) >> 128; 169 | | 170 | * | if (absTick & 0x8000 != 0) sqrtR = (sqrtR * 0x31be135f97d08fd981231505542fcfa6) >> 128; 171 | | 172 | * | if (absTick & 0x10000 != 0) sqrtR = (sqrtR * 0x9aa508b5b7a84e1c677de54f3e99bc9) >> 128; 173 | | 174 | * | if (absTick & 0x20000 != 0) sqrtR = (sqrtR * 0x5d6af8dedb81196699c329225ee604) >> 128; 175 | | 176 | * | if (absTick & 0x40000 != 0) sqrtR = (sqrtR * 0x2216e584f5fa1ea926041bedfe98) >> 128; 177 | | 178 | * | if (absTick & 0x80000 != 0) sqrtR = (sqrtR * 0x48a170391f7dc42444e8fa2) >> 128; 179 | | 180 | | // 2^128 * sqrt(1.0001^x) = 2^128 / sqrt(1.0001^-x) 181 | * | if (tick > 0) sqrtR = type(uint256).max / sqrtR; 182 | | 183 | | // Downcast + rounding up to keep is consistent with Uniswap's 184 | * | return uint160((sqrtR >> 32) + (sqrtR % (1 << 32) == 0 ? 0 : 1)); 185 | | } 186 | | } 187 | | 188 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 189 | | LIQUIDITY AMOUNTS (STRIKE+WIDTH) 190 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 191 | | 192 | | /// @notice Calculates the amount of token0 received for a given LiquidityChunk. 193 | | /// @dev Had to use a less optimal calculation to match Uniswap's implementation. 194 | | /// @param liquidityChunk Variable that efficiently packs the liquidity, tickLower, and tickUpper. 195 | | /// @return The amount of token0 196 | * | function getAmount0ForLiquidity(LiquidityChunk liquidityChunk) internal pure returns (uint256) { 197 | * | uint160 lowPriceX96 = getSqrtRatioAtTick(liquidityChunk.tickLower()); 198 | * | uint160 highPriceX96 = getSqrtRatioAtTick(liquidityChunk.tickUpper()); 199 | | unchecked { 200 | | return 201 | * | mulDiv( 202 | * | uint256(liquidityChunk.liquidity()) << 96, 203 | * | highPriceX96 - lowPriceX96, 204 | * | highPriceX96 205 | | ) / lowPriceX96; 206 | | } 207 | | } 208 | | 209 | | /// @notice Calculates the amount of token1 received for a given LiquidityChunk. 210 | | /// @param liquidityChunk Variable that efficiently packs the liquidity, tickLower, and tickUpper 211 | | /// @return The amount of token1 212 | * | function getAmount1ForLiquidity(LiquidityChunk liquidityChunk) internal pure returns (uint256) { 213 | * | uint160 lowPriceX96 = getSqrtRatioAtTick(liquidityChunk.tickLower()); 214 | * | uint160 highPriceX96 = getSqrtRatioAtTick(liquidityChunk.tickUpper()); 215 | | 216 | | unchecked { 217 | * | return mulDiv96(liquidityChunk.liquidity(), highPriceX96 - lowPriceX96); 218 | | } 219 | | } 220 | | 221 | | /// @notice Calculates the amount of token0 and token1 received for a given LiquidityChunk at the provided currentTick. 222 | | /// @param currentTick The current tick to be evaluated 223 | | /// @param liquidityChunk Variable that efficiently packs the liquidity, tickLower, and tickUpper 224 | | /// @return amount0 The amount of token0 225 | | /// @return amount1 The amount of token1 226 | * | function getAmountsForLiquidity( 227 | | int24 currentTick, 228 | | LiquidityChunk liquidityChunk 229 | * | ) internal pure returns (uint256 amount0, uint256 amount1) { 230 | * | if (currentTick <= liquidityChunk.tickLower()) { 231 | * | amount0 = getAmount0ForLiquidity(liquidityChunk); 232 | * | } else if (currentTick >= liquidityChunk.tickUpper()) { 233 | * | amount1 = getAmount1ForLiquidity(liquidityChunk); 234 | | } else { 235 | * | amount0 = getAmount0ForLiquidity(liquidityChunk.updateTickLower(currentTick)); 236 | * | amount1 = getAmount1ForLiquidity(liquidityChunk.updateTickUpper(currentTick)); 237 | | } 238 | | } 239 | | 240 | | /// @notice Returns a LiquidityChunk with `liquidity` corresponding to `amount0` at the provided ticks. 241 | | /// @dev Had to use a less optimal calculation to match Uniswap's implementation. 242 | | /// @param tickLower The lower tick of the chunk 243 | | /// @param tickUpper The upper tick of the chunk 244 | | /// @param amount0 The amount of token0 245 | | /// @return A LiquidityChunk with `tickLower`, `tickUpper`, and the calculated amount of liquidity 246 | * | function getLiquidityForAmount0( 247 | | int24 tickLower, 248 | | int24 tickUpper, 249 | | uint256 amount0 250 | * | ) internal pure returns (LiquidityChunk) { 251 | * | uint160 lowPriceX96 = getSqrtRatioAtTick(tickLower); 252 | * | uint160 highPriceX96 = getSqrtRatioAtTick(tickUpper); 253 | | 254 | | unchecked { 255 | * | return 256 | * | LiquidityChunkLibrary.createChunk( 257 | * | tickLower, 258 | * | tickUpper, 259 | * | toUint128( 260 | * | mulDiv( 261 | * | amount0, 262 | * | mulDiv96(highPriceX96, lowPriceX96), 263 | * | highPriceX96 - lowPriceX96 264 | | ) 265 | | ) 266 | | ); 267 | | } 268 | | } 269 | | 270 | | /// @notice Returns a LiquidityChunk with `liquidity` corresponding to `amount1` at the provided ticks. 271 | | /// @dev Had to use a less optimal calculation to match Uniswap's implementation. 272 | | /// @param tickLower The lower tick of the chunk 273 | | /// @param tickUpper The upper tick of the chunk 274 | | /// @param amount1 The amount of token1 275 | | /// @return A LiquidityChunk with `tickLower`, `tickUpper`, and the calculated amount of liquidity 276 | * | function getLiquidityForAmount1( 277 | | int24 tickLower, 278 | | int24 tickUpper, 279 | | uint256 amount1 280 | * | ) internal pure returns (LiquidityChunk) { 281 | * | uint160 lowPriceX96 = getSqrtRatioAtTick(tickLower); 282 | * | uint160 highPriceX96 = getSqrtRatioAtTick(tickUpper); 283 | | 284 | | unchecked { 285 | | return 286 | * | LiquidityChunkLibrary.createChunk( 287 | * | tickLower, 288 | * | tickUpper, 289 | * | toUint128(mulDiv(amount1, Constants.FP96, highPriceX96 - lowPriceX96)) 290 | | ); 291 | | } 292 | | } 293 | | 294 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 295 | | CASTING 296 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 297 | | 298 | | /// @notice Downcast uint256 to uint128. Revert on overflow or underflow. 299 | | /// @param toDowncast The uint256 to be downcasted 300 | | /// @return downcastedInt The downcasted uint (uint128 now) 301 | * | function toUint128(uint256 toDowncast) internal pure returns (uint128 downcastedInt) { 302 | * | if ((downcastedInt = uint128(toDowncast)) != toDowncast) revert Errors.CastingError(); 303 | | } 304 | | 305 | | /// @notice Downcast uint256 to uint128, but cap at type(uint128).max on overflow. 306 | | /// @return downcastedInt The downcasted uint (uint128 now) 307 | * | function toUint128Capped(uint256 toDowncast) internal pure returns (uint128 downcastedInt) { 308 | * | if ((downcastedInt = uint128(toDowncast)) != toDowncast) { 309 | | downcastedInt = type(uint128).max; 310 | | } 311 | | } 312 | | 313 | | /// @notice Recast uint128 to int128. 314 | | /// @param toCast The uint256 to be downcasted 315 | | /// @return downcastedInt The downcasted int (int128 now) 316 | * | function toInt128(uint128 toCast) internal pure returns (int128 downcastedInt) { 317 | * | if ((downcastedInt = int128(toCast)) < 0) revert Errors.CastingError(); 318 | | } 319 | | 320 | | /// @notice Cast an int256 to an int128, revert on overflow or underflow. 321 | | /// @param toCast the int256 to be downcasted to int128 322 | | /// @return downcastedInt the downcasted integer, now of type int128 323 | * | function toInt128(int256 toCast) internal pure returns (int128 downcastedInt) { 324 | * | if (!((downcastedInt = int128(toCast)) == toCast)) revert Errors.CastingError(); 325 | | } 326 | | 327 | | /// @notice Cast a uint256 to an int256, revert on overflow. 328 | | /// @param toCast The value to be downcasted to uint128 329 | | /// @return The incoming uint256 but now of type int256 330 | * | function toInt256(uint256 toCast) internal pure returns (int256) { 331 | * | if (toCast > uint256(type(int256).max)) revert Errors.CastingError(); 332 | | return int256(toCast); 333 | | } 334 | | 335 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 336 | | MULDIV ALGORITHMS 337 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 338 | | 339 | | /// @notice Calculates floor(a×b÷denominator) with full precision. Throws if result overflows a uint256 or denominator == 0. 340 | | /// @param a The multiplicand 341 | | /// @param b The multiplier 342 | | /// @param denominator The divisor 343 | | /// @return result The 256-bit result 344 | | /// @dev Credit to Remco Bloemen under MIT license https://xn--2-umb.com/21/muldiv 345 | * | function mulDiv( 346 | | uint256 a, 347 | | uint256 b, 348 | | uint256 denominator 349 | * | ) internal pure returns (uint256 result) { 350 | | unchecked { 351 | | // 512-bit multiply [prod1 prod0] = a * b 352 | | // Compute the product mod 2**256 and mod 2**256 - 1 353 | | // then use the Chinese Remainder Theorem to reconstruct 354 | | // the 512 bit result. The result is stored in two 256 355 | | // variables such that product = prod1 * 2**256 + prod0 356 | | uint256 prod0; // Least significant 256 bits of the product 357 | | uint256 prod1; // Most significant 256 bits of the product 358 | | assembly ("memory-safe") { 359 | * | let mm := mulmod(a, b, not(0)) 360 | * | prod0 := mul(a, b) 361 | * | prod1 := sub(sub(mm, prod0), lt(mm, prod0)) 362 | | } 363 | | 364 | | // Handle non-overflow cases, 256 by 256 division 365 | * | if (prod1 == 0) { 366 | * | require(denominator > 0); 367 | | assembly ("memory-safe") { 368 | * | result := div(prod0, denominator) 369 | | } 370 | * | return result; 371 | | } 372 | | 373 | | // Make sure the result is less than 2**256. 374 | | // Also prevents denominator == 0 375 | * | require(denominator > prod1); 376 | | 377 | | /////////////////////////////////////////////// 378 | | // 512 by 256 division. 379 | | /////////////////////////////////////////////// 380 | | 381 | | // Make division exact by subtracting the remainder from [prod1 prod0] 382 | | // Compute remainder using mulmod 383 | * | uint256 remainder; 384 | | assembly ("memory-safe") { 385 | * | remainder := mulmod(a, b, denominator) 386 | | } 387 | | // Subtract 256 bit number from 512 bit number 388 | | assembly ("memory-safe") { 389 | * | prod1 := sub(prod1, gt(remainder, prod0)) 390 | * | prod0 := sub(prod0, remainder) 391 | | } 392 | | 393 | | // Factor powers of two out of denominator 394 | | // Compute largest power of two divisor of denominator. 395 | | // Always >= 1. 396 | * | uint256 twos = (0 - denominator) & denominator; 397 | | // Divide denominator by power of two 398 | | assembly ("memory-safe") { 399 | * | denominator := div(denominator, twos) 400 | | } 401 | | 402 | | // Divide [prod1 prod0] by the factors of two 403 | | assembly ("memory-safe") { 404 | * | prod0 := div(prod0, twos) 405 | | } 406 | | // Shift in bits from prod1 into prod0. For this we need 407 | | // to flip `twos` such that it is 2**256 / twos. 408 | | // If twos is zero, then it becomes one 409 | | assembly ("memory-safe") { 410 | * | twos := add(div(sub(0, twos), twos), 1) 411 | | } 412 | * | prod0 |= prod1 * twos; 413 | | 414 | | // Invert denominator mod 2**256 415 | | // Now that denominator is an odd number, it has an inverse 416 | | // modulo 2**256 such that denominator * inv = 1 mod 2**256. 417 | | // Compute the inverse by starting with a seed that is correct 418 | | // correct for four bits. That is, denominator * inv = 1 mod 2**4 419 | * | uint256 inv = (3 * denominator) ^ 2; 420 | | // Now use Newton-Raphson iteration to improve the precision. 421 | | // Thanks to Hensel's lifting lemma, this also works in modular 422 | | // arithmetic, doubling the correct bits in each step. 423 | * | inv *= 2 - denominator * inv; // inverse mod 2**8 424 | * | inv *= 2 - denominator * inv; // inverse mod 2**16 425 | * | inv *= 2 - denominator * inv; // inverse mod 2**32 426 | * | inv *= 2 - denominator * inv; // inverse mod 2**64 427 | * | inv *= 2 - denominator * inv; // inverse mod 2**128 428 | * | inv *= 2 - denominator * inv; // inverse mod 2**256 429 | | 430 | | // Because the division is now exact we can divide by multiplying 431 | | // with the modular inverse of denominator. This will give us the 432 | | // correct result modulo 2**256. Since the preconditions guarantee 433 | | // that the outcome is less than 2**256, this is the final result. 434 | | // We don't need to compute the high bits of the result and prod1 435 | | // is no longer required. 436 | * | result = prod0 * inv; 437 | | } 438 | | } 439 | | 440 | | /// @notice Calculates ceil(a×b÷denominator) with full precision. Throws if result overflows a uint256 or denominator == 0. 441 | | /// @param a The multiplicand 442 | | /// @param b The multiplier 443 | | /// @param denominator The divisor 444 | | /// @return result The 256-bit result 445 | * | function mulDivRoundingUp( 446 | | uint256 a, 447 | | uint256 b, 448 | | uint256 denominator 449 | * | ) internal pure returns (uint256 result) { 450 | | unchecked { 451 | * | result = mulDiv(a, b, denominator); 452 | * | if (mulmod(a, b, denominator) > 0) { 453 | * | require(result < type(uint256).max); 454 | * | result++; 455 | | } 456 | | } 457 | | } 458 | | 459 | | /// @notice Calculates floor(a×b÷2^64) with full precision. Throws if result overflows a uint256. 460 | | /// @param a The multiplicand 461 | | /// @param b The multiplier 462 | | /// @return The 256-bit result 463 | * | function mulDiv64(uint256 a, uint256 b) internal pure returns (uint256) { 464 | | unchecked { 465 | | // 512-bit multiply [prod1 prod0] = a * b 466 | | // Compute the product mod 2**256 and mod 2**256 - 1 467 | | // then use the Chinese Remainder Theorem to reconstruct 468 | | // the 512 bit result. The result is stored in two 256 469 | | // variables such that product = prod1 * 2**256 + prod0 470 | | uint256 prod0; // Least significant 256 bits of the product 471 | | uint256 prod1; // Most significant 256 bits of the product 472 | | assembly ("memory-safe") { 473 | * | let mm := mulmod(a, b, not(0)) 474 | * | prod0 := mul(a, b) 475 | * | prod1 := sub(sub(mm, prod0), lt(mm, prod0)) 476 | | } 477 | | 478 | | // Handle non-overflow cases, 256 by 256 division 479 | * | if (prod1 == 0) { 480 | | uint256 res; 481 | | assembly ("memory-safe") { 482 | | // Right shift by n is equivalent and 2 gas cheaper than division by 2^n 483 | * | res := shr(64, prod0) 484 | | } 485 | * | return res; 486 | | } 487 | | 488 | | // Make sure the result is less than 2**256. 489 | *r | require(2 ** 64 > prod1); 490 | | 491 | | /////////////////////////////////////////////// 492 | | // 512 by 256 division. 493 | | /////////////////////////////////////////////// 494 | | 495 | | // Make division exact by subtracting the remainder from [prod1 prod0] 496 | | // Compute remainder using mulmod 497 | * | uint256 remainder; 498 | | assembly ("memory-safe") { 499 | * | remainder := mulmod(a, b, 0x10000000000000000) 500 | | } 501 | | // Subtract 256 bit number from 512 bit number 502 | | assembly ("memory-safe") { 503 | * | prod1 := sub(prod1, gt(remainder, prod0)) 504 | * | prod0 := sub(prod0, remainder) 505 | | } 506 | | 507 | | // Divide [prod1 prod0] by the factors of two (note that this is just 2**96 since the denominator is a power of 2 itself) 508 | | assembly ("memory-safe") { 509 | | // Right shift by n is equivalent and 2 gas cheaper than division by 2^n 510 | * | prod0 := shr(64, prod0) 511 | | } 512 | | // Shift in bits from prod1 into prod0. For this we need 513 | | // to flip `twos` such that it is 2**256 / twos. 514 | | // If twos is zero, then it becomes one 515 | | // Note that this is just 2**192 since 2**256 over the fixed denominator (2**64) equals 2**192 516 | * | prod0 |= prod1 * 2 ** 192; 517 | | 518 | | return prod0; 519 | | } 520 | | } 521 | | 522 | | /// @notice Calculates floor(a×b÷2^96) with full precision. Throws if result overflows a uint256. 523 | | /// @param a The multiplicand 524 | | /// @param b The multiplier 525 | | /// @return The 256-bit result 526 | * | function mulDiv96(uint256 a, uint256 b) internal pure returns (uint256) { 527 | | unchecked { 528 | | // 512-bit multiply [prod1 prod0] = a * b 529 | | // Compute the product mod 2**256 and mod 2**256 - 1 530 | | // then use the Chinese Remainder Theorem to reconstruct 531 | | // the 512 bit result. The result is stored in two 256 532 | | // variables such that product = prod1 * 2**256 + prod0 533 | | uint256 prod0; // Least significant 256 bits of the product 534 | | uint256 prod1; // Most significant 256 bits of the product 535 | | assembly ("memory-safe") { 536 | * | let mm := mulmod(a, b, not(0)) 537 | * | prod0 := mul(a, b) 538 | * | prod1 := sub(sub(mm, prod0), lt(mm, prod0)) 539 | | } 540 | | 541 | | // Handle non-overflow cases, 256 by 256 division 542 | * | if (prod1 == 0) { 543 | | uint256 res; 544 | | assembly ("memory-safe") { 545 | | // Right shift by n is equivalent and 2 gas cheaper than division by 2^n 546 | * | res := shr(96, prod0) 547 | | } 548 | * | return res; 549 | | } 550 | | 551 | | // Make sure the result is less than 2**256. 552 | * | require(2 ** 96 > prod1); 553 | | 554 | | /////////////////////////////////////////////// 555 | | // 512 by 256 division. 556 | | /////////////////////////////////////////////// 557 | | 558 | | // Make division exact by subtracting the remainder from [prod1 prod0] 559 | | // Compute remainder using mulmod 560 | * | uint256 remainder; 561 | | assembly ("memory-safe") { 562 | * | remainder := mulmod(a, b, 0x1000000000000000000000000) 563 | | } 564 | | // Subtract 256 bit number from 512 bit number 565 | | assembly ("memory-safe") { 566 | * | prod1 := sub(prod1, gt(remainder, prod0)) 567 | * | prod0 := sub(prod0, remainder) 568 | | } 569 | | 570 | | // Divide [prod1 prod0] by the factors of two (note that this is just 2**96 since the denominator is a power of 2 itself) 571 | | assembly ("memory-safe") { 572 | | // Right shift by n is equivalent and 2 gas cheaper than division by 2^n 573 | * | prod0 := shr(96, prod0) 574 | | } 575 | | // Shift in bits from prod1 into prod0. For this we need 576 | | // to flip `twos` such that it is 2**256 / twos. 577 | | // If twos is zero, then it becomes one 578 | | // Note that this is just 2**160 since 2**256 over the fixed denominator (2**96) equals 2**160 579 | * | prod0 |= prod1 * 2 ** 160; 580 | | 581 | | return prod0; 582 | | } 583 | | } 584 | | 585 | | /// @notice Calculates ceil(a×b÷2^96) with full precision. Throws if result overflows a uint256. 586 | | /// @param a The multiplicand 587 | | /// @param b The multiplier 588 | | /// @return result The 256-bit result 589 | * | function mulDiv96RoundingUp(uint256 a, uint256 b) internal pure returns (uint256 result) { 590 | | unchecked { 591 | * | result = mulDiv96(a, b); 592 | * | if (mulmod(a, b, 2 ** 96) > 0) { 593 | | require(result < type(uint256).max); 594 | | result++; 595 | | } 596 | | } 597 | | } 598 | | 599 | | /// @notice Calculates floor(a×b÷2^128) with full precision. Throws if result overflows a uint256. 600 | | /// @param a The multiplicand 601 | | /// @param b The multiplier 602 | | /// @return The 256-bit result 603 | * | function mulDiv128(uint256 a, uint256 b) internal pure returns (uint256) { 604 | | unchecked { 605 | | // 512-bit multiply [prod1 prod0] = a * b 606 | | // Compute the product mod 2**256 and mod 2**256 - 1 607 | | // then use the Chinese Remainder Theorem to reconstruct 608 | | // the 512 bit result. The result is stored in two 256 609 | | // variables such that product = prod1 * 2**256 + prod0 610 | | uint256 prod0; // Least significant 256 bits of the product 611 | | uint256 prod1; // Most significant 256 bits of the product 612 | | assembly ("memory-safe") { 613 | * | let mm := mulmod(a, b, not(0)) 614 | * | prod0 := mul(a, b) 615 | * | prod1 := sub(sub(mm, prod0), lt(mm, prod0)) 616 | | } 617 | | 618 | | // Handle non-overflow cases, 256 by 256 division 619 | * | if (prod1 == 0) { 620 | | uint256 res; 621 | | assembly ("memory-safe") { 622 | | // Right shift by n is equivalent and 2 gas cheaper than division by 2^n 623 | * | res := shr(128, prod0) 624 | | } 625 | * | return res; 626 | | } 627 | | 628 | | // Make sure the result is less than 2**256. 629 | | require(2 ** 128 > prod1); 630 | | 631 | | /////////////////////////////////////////////// 632 | | // 512 by 256 division. 633 | | /////////////////////////////////////////////// 634 | | 635 | | // Make division exact by subtracting the remainder from [prod1 prod0] 636 | | // Compute remainder using mulmod 637 | | uint256 remainder; 638 | | assembly ("memory-safe") { 639 | | remainder := mulmod(a, b, 0x100000000000000000000000000000000) 640 | | } 641 | | // Subtract 256 bit number from 512 bit number 642 | | assembly ("memory-safe") { 643 | | prod1 := sub(prod1, gt(remainder, prod0)) 644 | | prod0 := sub(prod0, remainder) 645 | | } 646 | | 647 | | // Divide [prod1 prod0] by the factors of two (note that this is just 2**128 since the denominator is a power of 2 itself) 648 | | assembly ("memory-safe") { 649 | | // Right shift by n is equivalent and 2 gas cheaper than division by 2^n 650 | | prod0 := shr(128, prod0) 651 | | } 652 | | // Shift in bits from prod1 into prod0. For this we need 653 | | // to flip `twos` such that it is 2**256 / twos. 654 | | // If twos is zero, then it becomes one 655 | | // Note that this is just 2**160 since 2**256 over the fixed denominator (2**128) equals 2**128 656 | | prod0 |= prod1 * 2 ** 128; 657 | | 658 | | return prod0; 659 | | } 660 | | } 661 | | 662 | | /// @notice Calculates ceil(a×b÷2^128) with full precision. Throws if result overflows a uint256. 663 | | /// @param a The multiplicand 664 | | /// @param b The multiplier 665 | | /// @return result The 256-bit result 666 | * | function mulDiv128RoundingUp(uint256 a, uint256 b) internal pure returns (uint256 result) { 667 | | unchecked { 668 | * | result = mulDiv128(a, b); 669 | * | if (mulmod(a, b, 2 ** 128) > 0) { 670 | * | require(result < type(uint256).max); 671 | * | result++; 672 | | } 673 | | } 674 | | } 675 | | 676 | | /// @notice Calculates floor(a×b÷2^192) with full precision. Throws if result overflows a uint256. 677 | | /// @param a The multiplicand 678 | | /// @param b The multiplier 679 | | /// @return The 256-bit result 680 | * | function mulDiv192(uint256 a, uint256 b) internal pure returns (uint256) { 681 | | unchecked { 682 | | // 512-bit multiply [prod1 prod0] = a * b 683 | | // Compute the product mod 2**256 and mod 2**256 - 1 684 | | // then use the Chinese Remainder Theorem to reconstruct 685 | | // the 512 bit result. The result is stored in two 256 686 | | // variables such that product = prod1 * 2**256 + prod0 687 | | uint256 prod0; // Least significant 256 bits of the product 688 | | uint256 prod1; // Most significant 256 bits of the product 689 | | assembly ("memory-safe") { 690 | * | let mm := mulmod(a, b, not(0)) 691 | * | prod0 := mul(a, b) 692 | * | prod1 := sub(sub(mm, prod0), lt(mm, prod0)) 693 | | } 694 | | 695 | | // Handle non-overflow cases, 256 by 256 division 696 | * | if (prod1 == 0) { 697 | | uint256 res; 698 | | assembly ("memory-safe") { 699 | | // Right shift by n is equivalent and 2 gas cheaper than division by 2^n 700 | * | res := shr(192, prod0) 701 | | } 702 | * | return res; 703 | | } 704 | | 705 | | // Make sure the result is less than 2**256. 706 | * | require(2 ** 192 > prod1); 707 | | 708 | | /////////////////////////////////////////////// 709 | | // 512 by 256 division. 710 | | /////////////////////////////////////////////// 711 | | 712 | | // Make division exact by subtracting the remainder from [prod1 prod0] 713 | | // Compute remainder using mulmod 714 | * | uint256 remainder; 715 | | assembly ("memory-safe") { 716 | * | remainder := mulmod(a, b, 0x1000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000) 717 | | } 718 | | // Subtract 256 bit number from 512 bit number 719 | | assembly ("memory-safe") { 720 | * | prod1 := sub(prod1, gt(remainder, prod0)) 721 | * | prod0 := sub(prod0, remainder) 722 | | } 723 | | 724 | | // Divide [prod1 prod0] by the factors of two (note that this is just 2**96 since the denominator is a power of 2 itself) 725 | | assembly ("memory-safe") { 726 | | // Right shift by n is equivalent and 2 gas cheaper than division by 2^n 727 | * | prod0 := shr(192, prod0) 728 | | } 729 | | // Shift in bits from prod1 into prod0. For this we need 730 | | // to flip `twos` such that it is 2**256 / twos. 731 | | // If twos is zero, then it becomes one 732 | | // Note that this is just 2**64 since 2**256 over the fixed denominator (2**192) equals 2**64 733 | * | prod0 |= prod1 * 2 ** 64; 734 | | 735 | | return prod0; 736 | | } 737 | | } 738 | | 739 | | /// @notice Calculates ceil(a÷b), returning 0 if b == 0. 740 | | /// @param a The numerator 741 | | /// @param b The denominator 742 | | /// @return result The 256-bit result 743 | * | function unsafeDivRoundingUp(uint256 a, uint256 b) internal pure returns (uint256 result) { 744 | | assembly ("memory-safe") { 745 | * | result := add(div(a, b), gt(mod(a, b), 0)) 746 | | } 747 | | } 748 | | 749 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 750 | | SORTING 751 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 752 | | 753 | | /// @notice QuickSort is a sorting algorithm that employs the Divide and Conquer strategy. It selects a pivot element and arranges the given array around 754 | | /// this pivot by correctly positioning it within the sorted array. 755 | | /// @param arr The elements that must be sorted 756 | | /// @param left The starting index 757 | | /// @param right The ending index 758 | * | function quickSort(int256[] memory arr, int256 left, int256 right) internal pure { 759 | * | unchecked { 760 | * | int256 i = left; 761 | * | int256 j = right; 762 | * | if (i == j) return; 763 | * | int256 pivot = arr[uint256(left + (right - left) / 2)]; 764 | * | while (i < j) { 765 | * | while (arr[uint256(i)] < pivot) i++; 766 | * | while (pivot < arr[uint256(j)]) j--; 767 | * | if (i <= j) { 768 | * | (arr[uint256(i)], arr[uint256(j)]) = (arr[uint256(j)], arr[uint256(i)]); 769 | * | i++; 770 | * | j--; 771 | | } 772 | | } 773 | * | if (left < j) quickSort(arr, left, j); 774 | * | if (i < right) quickSort(arr, i, right); 775 | | } 776 | | } 777 | | 778 | | /// @notice Calls `quickSort` with default starting index of 0 and ending index of the last element in the array. 779 | | /// @param data The elements that must be sorted 780 | | /// @return The sorted array 781 | * | function sort(int256[] memory data) internal pure returns (int256[] memory) { 782 | | unchecked { 783 | * | quickSort(data, int256(0), int256(data.length - 1)); 784 | | } 785 | * | return data; 786 | | } 787 | | } 788 | |
/home/xshadow/panoptic-v1-core-private/contracts/libraries/PanopticMath.sol 1 | | // SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1 2 | | pragma solidity ^0.8.0; 3 | | 4 | | // Interfaces 5 | | import {CollateralTracker} from "@contracts/CollateralTracker.sol"; 6 | | import {IERC20Metadata} from "@openzeppelin/contracts/token/ERC20/extensions/IERC20Metadata.sol"; 7 | | import {IUniswapV3Pool} from "univ3-core/interfaces/IUniswapV3Pool.sol"; 8 | | // Libraries 9 | | import {Constants} from "@libraries/Constants.sol"; 10 | | import {Errors} from "@libraries/Errors.sol"; 11 | | import {Math} from "@libraries/Math.sol"; 12 | | // OpenZeppelin libraries 13 | | import {Strings} from "@openzeppelin/contracts/utils/Strings.sol"; 14 | | // Custom types 15 | | import {LeftRightUnsigned, LeftRightSigned} from "@types/LeftRight.sol"; 16 | | import {LiquidityChunk} from "@types/LiquidityChunk.sol"; 17 | | import {TokenId} from "@types/TokenId.sol"; 18 | | 19 | | /// @title Compute general math quantities relevant to Panoptic and AMM pool management. 20 | | /// @author Axicon Labs Limited 21 | * | library PanopticMath { 22 | | using Math for uint256; 23 | | 24 | | /// @notice This is equivalent to type(uint256).max — used in assembly blocks as a replacement. 25 | | uint256 internal constant MAX_UINT256 = 2 ** 256 - 1; 26 | | 27 | | /// @notice Masks 16-bit tickSpacing out of 64-bit [16-bit tickspacing][48-bit poolPattern] format poolId 28 | | uint64 internal constant TICKSPACING_MASK = 0xFFFF000000000000; 29 | | 30 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 31 | | MATH HELPERS 32 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 33 | | 34 | | /// @notice Given an address to a Uniswap v3 pool, return its 64-bit ID as used in the `TokenId` of Panoptic. 35 | | // Example: 36 | | // the 64 bits are the 48 *last* (most significant) bits - and thus corresponds to the *first* 12 hex characters (reading left to right) 37 | * | // of the Uniswap v3 pool address, with the tickSpacing written in the highest 16 bits (i.e, max tickSpacing is 32768) 38 | | // e.g.: 39 | | // univ3pool = 0x8ad599c3A0ff1De082011EFDDc58f1908eb6e6D8 40 | | // tickSpacing = 60 41 | * | // the returned id is then: 42 | * | // poolPattern = 0x00008ad599c3A0ff 43 | * | // tickSpacing = 0x003c000000000000 + 44 | * | // -------------------------------------------- 45 | * | // poolId = 0x003c8ad599c3A0ff 46 | | // 47 | * | /// @param univ3pool The address of the Uniswap v3 pool to get the ID of 48 | * | /// @return A uint64 representing a fingerprint of the uniswap v3 pool address 49 | * | function getPoolId(address univ3pool) internal view returns (uint64) { 50 | * | unchecked { 51 | * | int24 tickSpacing = IUniswapV3Pool(univ3pool).tickSpacing(); 52 | | uint64 poolId = uint64(uint160(univ3pool) >> 112); 53 | | poolId += uint64(uint24(tickSpacing)) << 48; 54 | | return poolId; 55 | | } 56 | | } 57 | | 58 | | /// @notice Increments the pool pattern (first 48 bits) of a poolId by 1. 59 | | /// @param poolId The 64-bit pool ID 60 | | /// @return The provided `poolId` with its pool pattern slot incremented by 1 61 | | function incrementPoolPattern(uint64 poolId) internal pure returns (uint64) { 62 | | unchecked { 63 | | return (poolId & TICKSPACING_MASK) + (uint48(poolId) + 1); 64 | | } 65 | | } 66 | | 67 | | /// @notice Get the number of leading hex characters in an address. 68 | | // 0x0000bababaab... 0xababababab... 69 | | // ▲ ▲ 70 | | // │ │ 71 | | // 4 leading hex 0 leading hex 72 | | // character zeros character zeros 73 | | // 74 | | /// @param addr The address to get the number of leading zero hex characters for 75 | | /// @return The number of leading zero hex characters in the address 76 | | function numberOfLeadingHexZeros(address addr) external pure returns (uint256) { 77 | | unchecked { 78 | | return addr == address(0) ? 40 : 39 - Math.mostSignificantNibble(uint160(addr)); 79 | | } 80 | | } 81 | | 82 | | /// @notice Returns ERC20 symbol of `token`. 83 | | /// @param token The address of the token to get the symbol of 84 | | /// @return The symbol of `token` or "???" if not supported 85 | * | function safeERC20Symbol(address token) external view returns (string memory) { 86 | | // not guaranteed that token supports metadata extension 87 | | // so we need to let call fail and return placeholder if not 88 | * | try IERC20Metadata(token).symbol() returns (string memory symbol) { 89 | | return symbol; 90 | | } catch { 91 | | return "???"; 92 | | } 93 | | } 94 | | 95 | | /// @notice Converts `fee` to a string with "bps" appended. 96 | | /// @dev The lowest supported value of `fee` is 1 (`="0.01bps"`). 97 | * | /// @param fee The fee to convert to a string (in hundredths of basis points) 98 | | /// @return Stringified version of `fee` with "bps" appended 99 | | function uniswapFeeToString(uint24 fee) internal pure returns (string memory) { 100 | | return 101 | | string.concat( 102 | * | Strings.toString(fee / 100), 103 | * | fee % 100 == 0 104 | | ? "" 105 | * | : string.concat( 106 | * | ".", 107 | | Strings.toString((fee / 10) % 10), 108 | * | Strings.toString(fee % 10) 109 | * | ), 110 | * | "bps" 111 | | ); 112 | | } 113 | | 114 | * | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 115 | * | UTILITIES 116 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 117 | | 118 | * | /// @notice Update an existing account's "positions hash" with a new single position `tokenId`. 119 | * | /// @notice The positions hash contains a single fingerprint of all positions created by an account/user as well as a tally of the positions. 120 | | /// @dev The combined hash is the XOR of all individual position hashes. 121 | | /// @param existingHash The existing position hash containing all historical N positions created and the count of the positions 122 | | /// @param tokenId The new position to add to the existing hash: existingHash = uint248(existingHash) ^ hashOf(tokenId) 123 | | /// @param addFlag Whether to mint (add) the tokenId to the count of positions or burn (subtract) it from the count (existingHash >> 248) +/- 1 124 | | /// @return newHash The new positionHash with the updated hash 125 | | function updatePositionsHash( 126 | | uint256 existingHash, 127 | | TokenId tokenId, 128 | | bool addFlag 129 | | ) internal pure returns (uint256) { 130 | | // add the XOR`ed hash of the single option position `tokenId` to the `existingHash` 131 | | // @dev 0 ^ x = x 132 | | 133 | | // update hash by taking the XOR of the new tokenId 134 | | uint256 updatedHash = uint248(existingHash) ^ 135 | | (uint248(uint256(keccak256(abi.encode(tokenId))))); 136 | | 137 | | // increment the upper 8 bits (position counter) if addflag=true, decrement otherwise 138 | | uint256 newPositionCount = addFlag 139 | | ? uint8(existingHash >> 248) + 1 140 | | : uint8(existingHash >> 248) - 1; 141 | | 142 | | unchecked { 143 | | return uint256(updatedHash) + (newPositionCount << 248); 144 | | } 145 | | } 146 | | 147 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 148 | | ORACLE CALCULATIONS 149 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 150 | | 151 | | /// @notice Gets several ticks from Uniswap regarding the underlying pair. 152 | | /// @param univ3pool The Uniswap pool to get the observations from 153 | | /// @param miniMedian The packed structure representing the sorted 8-slot queue of ticks 154 | | /// @return currentTick The current tick in the Uniswap pool (as returned in slot0) 155 | | /// @return fastOracleTick The fast oracle tick computed as the median of the past N observations in the Uniswap Pool 156 | | /// @return slowOracleTick The slow oracle tick as tracked by `s_miniMedian` 157 | | /// @return latestObservation The latest observation from the Uniswap pool (price at the end of the last block) 158 | | /// @return medianData the updated value for `s_miniMedian` (returns 0 if not enough time has passed since last observation) 159 | * | function getOracleTicks( 160 | | IUniswapV3Pool univ3pool, 161 | | uint256 miniMedian 162 | | ) 163 | | external 164 | | view 165 | | returns ( 166 | * | int24 currentTick, 167 | * | int24 fastOracleTick, 168 | * | int24 slowOracleTick, 169 | * | int24 latestObservation, 170 | * | uint256 medianData 171 | | ) 172 | * | { 173 | * | uint16 observationIndex; 174 | * | uint16 observationCardinality; 175 | | 176 | * | IUniswapV3Pool _univ3pool = univ3pool; 177 | * | (, currentTick, observationIndex, observationCardinality, , , ) = univ3pool.slot0(); 178 | | 179 | * | (fastOracleTick, latestObservation) = computeMedianObservedPrice( 180 | * | _univ3pool, 181 | | observationIndex, 182 | | observationCardinality, 183 | | Constants.FAST_ORACLE_CARDINALITY, 184 | | Constants.FAST_ORACLE_PERIOD 185 | | ); 186 | | 187 | | if (Constants.SLOW_ORACLE_UNISWAP_MODE) { 188 | | (slowOracleTick, ) = computeMedianObservedPrice( 189 | | _univ3pool, 190 | | observationIndex, 191 | | observationCardinality, 192 | | Constants.SLOW_ORACLE_CARDINALITY, 193 | | Constants.SLOW_ORACLE_PERIOD 194 | | ); 195 | | } else { 196 | * | (slowOracleTick, medianData) = computeInternalMedian( 197 | * | observationIndex, 198 | * | observationCardinality, 199 | | Constants.MEDIAN_PERIOD, 200 | * | miniMedian, 201 | * | _univ3pool 202 | | ); 203 | | } 204 | | } 205 | | 206 | | /// @notice Returns the median of the last `cardinality` average prices over `period` observations from `univ3pool`. 207 | | /// @dev Used when we need a manipulation-resistant TWAP price. 208 | | /// @dev Uniswap observations snapshot the closing price of the last block before the first interaction of a given block. 209 | | /// @dev The maximum frequency of observations is 1 per block, but there is no guarantee that the pool will be observed at every block. 210 | | /// @dev Each period has a minimum length of blocktime * period, but may be longer if the Uniswap pool is relatively inactive. 211 | | /// @dev The final price used in the array (of length `cardinality`) is the average of all observations comprising `period` (which is itself a number of observations). 212 | | /// @dev Thus, the minimum total time window is `cardinality` * `period` * `blocktime`. 213 | | /// @param univ3pool The Uniswap pool to get the median observation from 214 | | /// @param observationIndex The index of the last observation in the pool 215 | | /// @param observationCardinality The number of observations in the pool 216 | | /// @param cardinality The number of `periods` to in the median price array, should be odd 217 | | /// @param period The number of observations to average to compute one entry in the median price array 218 | | /// @return The median of `cardinality` observations spaced by `period` in the Uniswap pool 219 | | /// @return The latest observation in the Uniswap pool 220 | * | function computeMedianObservedPrice( 221 | | IUniswapV3Pool univ3pool, 222 | | uint256 observationIndex, 223 | | uint256 observationCardinality, 224 | | uint256 cardinality, 225 | | uint256 period 226 | * | ) internal view returns (int24, int24) { 227 | | unchecked { 228 | * | int256[] memory tickCumulatives = new int256[](cardinality + 1); 229 | | 230 | * | uint256[] memory timestamps = new uint256[](cardinality + 1); 231 | | // get the last "cardinality" timestamps/tickCumulatives (if observationIndex < cardinality, the index will wrap back from observationCardinality) 232 | * | for (uint256 i = 0; i < cardinality + 1; ++i) { 233 | * | (timestamps[i], tickCumulatives[i], , ) = univ3pool.observations( 234 | * | uint256( 235 | * | (int256(observationIndex) - int256(i * period)) + 236 | * | int256(observationCardinality) 237 | * | ) % observationCardinality 238 | | ); 239 | | } 240 | | 241 | * | int256[] memory ticks = new int256[](cardinality); 242 | | // use cardinality periods given by cardinality + 1 accumulator observations to compute the last cardinality observed ticks spaced by period 243 | * | for (uint256 i = 0; i < cardinality; ++i) { 244 | * | ticks[i] = 245 | * | (tickCumulatives[i] - tickCumulatives[i + 1]) / 246 | * | int256(timestamps[i] - timestamps[i + 1]); 247 | | } 248 | | 249 | | // get the median of the `ticks` array (assuming `cardinality` is odd) 250 | * | return (int24(Math.sort(ticks)[cardinality / 2]), int24(ticks[0])); 251 | | } 252 | | } 253 | | 254 | | /// @notice Takes a packed structure representing a sorted 8-slot queue of ticks and returns the median of those values. 255 | | /// @dev Also inserts the latest Uniswap observation into the buffer, resorts, and returns if the last entry is at least `period` seconds old. 256 | | /// @param observationIndex The index of the last observation in the Uniswap pool 257 | | /// @param observationCardinality The number of observations in the Uniswap pool 258 | | /// @param period The minimum time in seconds that must have passed since the last observation was inserted into the buffer 259 | | /// @param medianData The packed structure representing the sorted 8-slot queue of ticks 260 | | /// @param univ3pool The Uniswap pool to retrieve observations from 261 | | /// @return medianTick The median of the provided 8-slot queue of ticks in `medianData` 262 | * | /// @return updatedMedianData The updated 8-slot queue of ticks with the latest observation inserted if the last entry is at least `period` seconds old (returns 0 otherwise) 263 | * | function computeInternalMedian( 264 | | uint256 observationIndex, 265 | | uint256 observationCardinality, 266 | | uint256 period, 267 | * | uint256 medianData, 268 | | IUniswapV3Pool univ3pool 269 | * | ) public view returns (int24 medianTick, uint256 updatedMedianData) { 270 | * | unchecked { 271 | * | // return the average of the rank 3 and 4 values 272 | * | medianTick = 273 | * | (int24(uint24(medianData >> ((uint24(medianData >> (192 + 3 * 3)) % 8) * 24))) + 274 | * | int24(uint24(medianData >> ((uint24(medianData >> (192 + 3 * 4)) % 8) * 24)))) / 275 | * | 2; 276 | | 277 | | // only proceed if last entry is at least MEDIAN_PERIOD seconds old 278 | * | if (block.timestamp >= uint256(uint40(medianData >> 216)) + period) { 279 | * | int24 lastObservedTick; 280 | | { 281 | * | (uint256 timestamp_old, int56 tickCumulative_old, , ) = univ3pool.observations( 282 | * | uint256( 283 | * | int256(observationIndex) - int256(1) + int256(observationCardinality) 284 | * | ) % observationCardinality 285 | | ); 286 | | 287 | * | (uint256 timestamp_last, int56 tickCumulative_last, , ) = univ3pool 288 | * | .observations(observationIndex); 289 | | lastObservedTick = int24( 290 | * | (tickCumulative_last - tickCumulative_old) / 291 | * | int256(timestamp_last - timestamp_old) 292 | | ); 293 | | } 294 | | 295 | * | uint24 orderMap = uint24(medianData >> 192); 296 | | 297 | | uint24 newOrderMap; 298 | * | uint24 shift = 1; 299 | | bool below = true; 300 | | uint24 rank; 301 | | int24 entry; 302 | * | for (uint8 i; i < 8; ++i) { 303 | | // read the rank from the existing ordering 304 | * | rank = (orderMap >> (3 * i)) % 8; 305 | | 306 | * | if (rank == 7) { 307 | * | shift -= 1; 308 | * | continue; 309 | | } 310 | | 311 | | // read the corresponding entry 312 | * | entry = int24(uint24(medianData >> (rank * 24))); 313 | * | if ((below) && (lastObservedTick > entry)) { 314 | * | shift += 1; 315 | * | below = false; 316 | | } 317 | | 318 | * | newOrderMap = newOrderMap + ((rank + 1) << (3 * (i + shift - 1))); 319 | | } 320 | | 321 | * | updatedMedianData = 322 | * | (block.timestamp << 216) + 323 | * | (uint256(newOrderMap) << 192) + 324 | * | uint256(uint192(medianData << 24)) + 325 | * | uint256(uint24(lastObservedTick)); 326 | | } 327 | | } 328 | | } 329 | | 330 | | /// @notice Computes the twap of a Uniswap V3 pool using data from its oracle. 331 | | /// @dev Note that our definition of TWAP differs from a typical mean of prices over a time window. 332 | | /// @dev We instead observe the average price over a series of time intervals, and define the TWAP as the median of those averages. 333 | | /// @param univ3pool The Uniswap pool from which to compute the TWAP 334 | | /// @param twapWindow The time window to compute the TWAP over 335 | | /// @return The final calculated TWAP tick 336 | | function twapFilter(IUniswapV3Pool univ3pool, uint32 twapWindow) external view returns (int24) { 337 | | uint32[] memory secondsAgos = new uint32[](20); 338 | | 339 | | int256[] memory twapMeasurement = new int256[](19); 340 | | 341 | | unchecked { 342 | | // construct the time stots 343 | | for (uint256 i = 0; i < 20; ++i) { 344 | | secondsAgos[i] = uint32(((i + 1) * twapWindow) / 20); 345 | | } 346 | | 347 | | // observe the tickCumulative at the 20 pre-defined time slots 348 | | (int56[] memory tickCumulatives, ) = univ3pool.observe(secondsAgos); 349 | | 350 | | // compute the average tick per 30s window 351 | | for (uint256 i = 0; i < 19; ++i) { 352 | | twapMeasurement[i] = int24( 353 | | (tickCumulatives[i] - tickCumulatives[i + 1]) / int56(uint56(twapWindow / 20)) 354 | | ); 355 | | } 356 | | 357 | | // sort the tick measurements 358 | | int256[] memory sortedTicks = Math.sort(twapMeasurement); 359 | | 360 | | // Get the median value 361 | | return int24(sortedTicks[9]); 362 | | } 363 | | } 364 | | 365 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 366 | | LIQUIDITY CALCULATION 367 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 368 | | 369 | | /// @notice For a given option position (`tokenId`), leg index within that position (`legIndex`), and `positionSize` get the tick range spanned and its 370 | | /// liquidity (share ownership) in the Univ3 pool; this is a liquidity chunk. 371 | | // Liquidity chunk (defined by tick upper, tick lower, and its size/amount: the liquidity) 372 | | // liquidity │ 373 | | // ▲ │ 374 | | // │ ┌▼┐ 375 | | // │ ┌──┴─┴──┐ 376 | | // │ │ │ 377 | | // │ │ │ 378 | | // └──┴───────┴────► price 379 | | // Uniswap v3 Pool 380 | | /// @param tokenId The option position id 381 | | /// @param legIndex The leg index of the option position, can be {0,1,2,3} 382 | | /// @param positionSize The number of contracts held by this leg 383 | | /// @return A LiquidityChunk with `tickLower`, `tickUpper`, and `liquidity` 384 | * | function getLiquidityChunk( 385 | | TokenId tokenId, 386 | | uint256 legIndex, 387 | | uint128 positionSize 388 | * | ) internal pure returns (LiquidityChunk) { 389 | | // get the tick range for this leg 390 | * | (int24 tickLower, int24 tickUpper) = tokenId.asTicks(legIndex); 391 | | 392 | | // Get the amount of liquidity owned by this leg in the univ3 pool in the above tick range 393 | | // Background: 394 | | // 395 | | // In Uniswap v3, the amount of liquidity received for a given amount of token0 when the price is 396 | | // not in range is given by: 397 | | // Liquidity = amount0 * (sqrt(upper) * sqrt(lower)) / (sqrt(upper) - sqrt(lower)) 398 | | // For token1, it is given by: 399 | | // Liquidity = amount1 / (sqrt(upper) - sqrt(lower)) 400 | | // 401 | | // However, in Panoptic, each position has a asset parameter. The asset is the "basis" of the position. 402 | | // In TradFi, the asset is always cash and selling a $1000 put requires the user to lock $1000, and selling 403 | | // a call requires the user to lock 1 unit of asset. 404 | | // 405 | | // Because Uni v3 chooses token0 and token1 from the alphanumeric order, there is no consistency as to whether token0 is 406 | | // stablecoin, ETH, or an ERC20. Some pools may want ETH to be the asset (e.g. ETH-DAI) and some may wish the stablecoin to 407 | | // be the asset (e.g. DAI-ETH) so that K asset is moved for puts and 1 asset is moved for calls. 408 | | // But since the convention is to force the order always we have no say in this. 409 | | // 410 | | // To solve this, we encode the asset value in tokenId. This parameter specifies which of token0 or token1 is the 411 | | // asset, such that: 412 | | // when asset=0, then amount0 moved at strike K =1.0001**currentTick is 1, amount1 moved to strike K is 1/K 413 | | // when asset=1, then amount1 moved at strike K =1.0001**currentTick is K, amount0 moved to strike K is 1 414 | | // 415 | | // The following function takes this into account when computing the liquidity of the leg and switches between 416 | | // the definition for getLiquidityForAmount0 or getLiquidityForAmount1 when relevant. 417 | | // 418 | | // 419 | * | uint256 amount = uint256(positionSize) * tokenId.optionRatio(legIndex); 420 | * | if (tokenId.asset(legIndex) == 0) { 421 | * | return Math.getLiquidityForAmount0(tickLower, tickUpper, amount); 422 | | } else { 423 | * | return Math.getLiquidityForAmount1(tickLower, tickUpper, amount); 424 | | } 425 | | } 426 | | 427 | | /// @notice Extract the tick range specified by `strike` and `width` for the given `tickSpacing`, if valid. 428 | | /// @param strike The strike price of the option 429 | | /// @param width The width of the option 430 | | /// @param tickSpacing The tick spacing of the underlying Uniswap v3 pool 431 | | /// @return tickLower The lower tick of the liquidity chunk 432 | | /// @return tickUpper The upper tick of the liquidity chunk 433 | * | function getTicks( 434 | | int24 strike, 435 | | int24 width, 436 | | int24 tickSpacing 437 | * | ) internal pure returns (int24 tickLower, int24 tickUpper) { 438 | * | unchecked { 439 | | // The max/min ticks that can be initialized are the closest multiple of tickSpacing to the actual max/min tick abs()=887272 440 | | // Dividing and multiplying by tickSpacing rounds down and forces the tick to be a multiple of tickSpacing 441 | * | int24 minTick = (Constants.MIN_V3POOL_TICK / tickSpacing) * tickSpacing; 442 | * | int24 maxTick = (Constants.MAX_V3POOL_TICK / tickSpacing) * tickSpacing; 443 | | 444 | * | (int24 rangeDown, int24 rangeUp) = PanopticMath.getRangesFromStrike(width, tickSpacing); 445 | | 446 | * | (tickLower, tickUpper) = (strike - rangeDown, strike + rangeUp); 447 | | 448 | | // Revert if the upper/lower ticks are not multiples of tickSpacing 449 | | // Revert if the tick range extends from the strike outside of the valid tick range 450 | | // These are invalid states, and would revert silently later in `univ3Pool.mint` 451 | * | if ( 452 | * | tickLower % tickSpacing != 0 || 453 | * | tickUpper % tickSpacing != 0 || 454 | * | tickLower < minTick || 455 | * | tickUpper > maxTick 456 | | ) revert Errors.TicksNotInitializable(); 457 | | } 458 | | } 459 | | 460 | | /// @notice Returns the distances of the upper and lower ticks from the strike for a position with the given width and tickSpacing. 461 | | /// @dev Given `r = (width * tickSpacing) / 2`, `tickLower = strike - floor(r)` and `tickUpper = strike + ceil(r)`. 462 | | /// @param width The width of the leg 463 | | /// @param tickSpacing The tick spacing of the underlying pool 464 | | /// @return The lower tick of the range 465 | | /// @return The upper tick of the range 466 | * | function getRangesFromStrike( 467 | | int24 width, 468 | | int24 tickSpacing 469 | * | ) internal pure returns (int24, int24) { 470 | * | return ( 471 | * | (width * tickSpacing) / 2, 472 | * | int24(int256(Math.unsafeDivRoundingUp(uint24(width) * uint24(tickSpacing), 2))) 473 | | ); 474 | | } 475 | | 476 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 477 | | TOKEN CONVERSION LOGIC 478 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 479 | | 480 | | /// @notice Compute the amount of funds that are underlying this option position. This is useful when exercising a position. 481 | | /// @param tokenId The option position id 482 | | /// @param positionSize The number of contracts of this option 483 | | /// @return longAmounts Left-right packed word where rightSlot = token0 and leftSlot = token1 held against borrowed Uniswap liquidity for long legs 484 | | /// @return shortAmounts Left-right packed word where where rightSlot = token0 and leftSlot = token1 borrowed to create short legs 485 | * | function computeExercisedAmounts( 486 | | TokenId tokenId, 487 | | uint128 positionSize 488 | * | ) internal pure returns (LeftRightSigned longAmounts, LeftRightSigned shortAmounts) { 489 | * | uint256 numLegs = tokenId.countLegs(); 490 | * | for (uint256 leg = 0; leg < numLegs; ) { 491 | | // Compute the amount of funds that have been removed from the Panoptic Pool 492 | * | (LeftRightSigned longs, LeftRightSigned shorts) = _calculateIOAmounts( 493 | * | tokenId, 494 | * | positionSize, 495 | * | leg 496 | | ); 497 | | 498 | * | longAmounts = longAmounts.add(longs); 499 | * | shortAmounts = shortAmounts.add(shorts); 500 | | 501 | | unchecked { 502 | * | ++leg; 503 | | } 504 | | } 505 | | } 506 | | 507 | | /// @notice Adds required collateral and collateral balance from collateralTracker0 and collateralTracker1 and converts to single values in terms of `tokenType`. 508 | | /// @param tokenData0 LeftRight type container holding the collateralBalance (right slot) and requiredCollateral (left slot) for a user in CollateralTracker0 (expressed in terms of token0) 509 | | /// @param tokenData1 LeftRight type container holding the collateralBalance (right slot) and requiredCollateral (left slot) for a user in CollateralTracker1 (expressed in terms of token1) 510 | | /// @param tokenType The type of token (token0 or token1) to express collateralBalance and requiredCollateral in 511 | | /// @param sqrtPriceX96 The sqrt price at which to convert between token0/token1 512 | | /// @return The total combined balance of token0 and token1 for a user in terms of tokenType 513 | | /// @return The combined collateral requirement for a user in terms of tokenType 514 | | function convertCollateralData( 515 | | LeftRightUnsigned tokenData0, 516 | | LeftRightUnsigned tokenData1, 517 | | uint256 tokenType, 518 | | uint160 sqrtPriceX96 519 | | ) internal pure returns (uint256, uint256) { 520 | | if (tokenType == 0) { 521 | | return ( 522 | | tokenData0.rightSlot() + convert1to0(tokenData1.rightSlot(), sqrtPriceX96), 523 | | tokenData0.leftSlot() + convert1to0(tokenData1.leftSlot(), sqrtPriceX96) 524 | | ); 525 | | } else { 526 | | return ( 527 | | tokenData1.rightSlot() + convert0to1(tokenData0.rightSlot(), sqrtPriceX96), 528 | | tokenData1.leftSlot() + convert0to1(tokenData0.leftSlot(), sqrtPriceX96) 529 | | ); 530 | | } 531 | | } 532 | | 533 | | /// @notice Adds required collateral and collateral balance from collateralTracker0 and collateralTracker1 and converts to single values in terms of `tokenType`. 534 | | /// @param tokenData0 LeftRight type container holding the collateralBalance (right slot) and requiredCollateral (left slot) for a user in CollateralTracker0 (expressed in terms of token0) 535 | | /// @param tokenData1 LeftRight type container holding the collateralBalance (right slot) and requiredCollateral (left slot) for a user in CollateralTracker1 (expressed in terms of token1) 536 | | /// @param tokenType The type of token (token0 or token1) to express collateralBalance and requiredCollateral in 537 | | /// @param tick The tick at which to convert between token0/token1 538 | | /// @return The total combined balance of token0 and token1 for a user in terms of tokenType 539 | | /// @return The combined collateral requirement for a user in terms of tokenType 540 | | function convertCollateralData( 541 | | LeftRightUnsigned tokenData0, 542 | | LeftRightUnsigned tokenData1, 543 | | uint256 tokenType, 544 | | int24 tick 545 | | ) internal pure returns (uint256, uint256) { 546 | | return 547 | | convertCollateralData(tokenData0, tokenData1, tokenType, Math.getSqrtRatioAtTick(tick)); 548 | | } 549 | | 550 | | /// @notice Convert an amount of token0 into an amount of token1 given the sqrtPriceX96 in a Uniswap pool defined as sqrt(1/0)*2^96. 551 | | /// @dev Uses reduced precision after tick 443636 in order to accommodate the full range of ticks 552 | | /// @param amount The amount of token0 to convert into token1 553 | | /// @param sqrtPriceX96 The square root of the price at which to convert `amount` of token0 into token1 554 | | /// @return The converted `amount` of token0 represented in terms of token1 555 | * | function convert0to1(uint256 amount, uint160 sqrtPriceX96) internal pure returns (uint256) { 556 | | unchecked { 557 | | // the tick 443636 is the maximum price where (price) * 2**192 fits into a uint256 (< 2**256-1) 558 | | // above that tick, we are forced to reduce the amount of decimals in the final price by 2**64 to 2**128 559 | * | if (sqrtPriceX96 < type(uint128).max) { 560 | * | return Math.mulDiv192(amount, uint256(sqrtPriceX96) ** 2); 561 | | } else { 562 | | return Math.mulDiv128(amount, Math.mulDiv64(sqrtPriceX96, sqrtPriceX96)); 563 | | } 564 | | } 565 | | } 566 | | 567 | | /// @notice Convert an amount of token1 into an amount of token0 given the sqrtPriceX96 in a Uniswap pool defined as sqrt(1/0)*2^96. 568 | | /// @dev Uses reduced precision after tick 443636 in order to accommodate the full range of ticks. 569 | | /// @param amount The amount of token1 to convert into token0 570 | | /// @param sqrtPriceX96 The square root of the price at which to convert `amount` of token1 into token0 571 | | /// @return The converted `amount` of token1 represented in terms of token0 572 | * | function convert1to0(uint256 amount, uint160 sqrtPriceX96) internal pure returns (uint256) { 573 | | unchecked { 574 | | // the tick 443636 is the maximum price where (price) * 2**192 fits into a uint256 (< 2**256-1) 575 | | // above that tick, we are forced to reduce the amount of decimals in the final price by 2**64 to 2**128 576 | * | if (sqrtPriceX96 < type(uint128).max) { 577 | * | return Math.mulDiv(amount, 2 ** 192, uint256(sqrtPriceX96) ** 2); 578 | | } else { 579 | | return Math.mulDiv(amount, 2 ** 128, Math.mulDiv64(sqrtPriceX96, sqrtPriceX96)); 580 | | } 581 | | } 582 | | } 583 | | 584 | | /// @notice Convert an amount of token0 into an amount of token1 given the sqrtPriceX96 in a Uniswap pool defined as sqrt(1/0)*2^96. 585 | | /// @dev Uses reduced precision after tick 443636 in order to accommodate the full range of ticks. 586 | | /// @param amount The amount of token0 to convert into token1 587 | | /// @param sqrtPriceX96 The square root of the price at which to convert `amount` of token0 into token1 588 | | /// @return The converted `amount` of token0 represented in terms of token1 589 | | function convert0to1(int256 amount, uint160 sqrtPriceX96) internal pure returns (int256) { 590 | | unchecked { 591 | | // the tick 443636 is the maximum price where (price) * 2**192 fits into a uint256 (< 2**256-1) 592 | | // above that tick, we are forced to reduce the amount of decimals in the final price by 2**64 to 2**128 593 | | if (sqrtPriceX96 < type(uint128).max) { 594 | | int256 absResult = Math 595 | | .mulDiv192(Math.absUint(amount), uint256(sqrtPriceX96) ** 2) 596 | | .toInt256(); 597 | | return amount < 0 ? -absResult : absResult; 598 | | } else { 599 | | int256 absResult = Math 600 | | .mulDiv128(Math.absUint(amount), Math.mulDiv64(sqrtPriceX96, sqrtPriceX96)) 601 | | .toInt256(); 602 | | return amount < 0 ? -absResult : absResult; 603 | | } 604 | | } 605 | | } 606 | | 607 | | /// @notice Convert an amount of token1 into an amount of token0 given the sqrtPriceX96 in a Uniswap pool defined as sqrt(1/0)*2^96. 608 | | /// @dev Uses reduced precision after tick 443636 in order to accommodate the full range of ticks. 609 | | /// @param amount The amount of token1 to convert into token0 610 | | /// @param sqrtPriceX96 The square root of the price at which to convert `amount` of token1 into token0 611 | | /// @return The converted `amount` of token1 represented in terms of token0 612 | * | function convert1to0(int256 amount, uint160 sqrtPriceX96) internal pure returns (int256) { 613 | | unchecked { 614 | | // the tick 443636 is the maximum price where (price) * 2**192 fits into a uint256 (< 2**256-1) 615 | | // above that tick, we are forced to reduce the amount of decimals in the final price by 2**64 to 2**128 616 | * | if (sqrtPriceX96 < type(uint128).max) { 617 | * | int256 absResult = Math 618 | * | .mulDiv(Math.absUint(amount), 2 ** 192, uint256(sqrtPriceX96) ** 2) 619 | | .toInt256(); 620 | * | return amount < 0 ? -absResult : absResult; 621 | | } else { 622 | | int256 absResult = Math 623 | | .mulDiv( 624 | | Math.absUint(amount), 625 | | 2 ** 128, 626 | | Math.mulDiv64(sqrtPriceX96, sqrtPriceX96) 627 | | ) 628 | | .toInt256(); 629 | | return amount < 0 ? -absResult : absResult; 630 | | } 631 | | } 632 | | } 633 | | 634 | | /// @notice Compute the amount of token0 and token1 moved. Given an option position `tokenId`, leg index `legIndex`, and how many contracts are in the leg `positionSize`. 635 | | /// @param tokenId The option position identifier 636 | | /// @param positionSize The number of option contracts held in this position (each contract can control multiple tokens) 637 | | /// @param legIndex The leg index of the option contract, can be {0,1,2,3} 638 | | /// @return A LeftRight encoded variable containing the amount0 and the amount1 value controlled by this option position's leg 639 | * | function getAmountsMoved( 640 | | TokenId tokenId, 641 | | uint128 positionSize, 642 | | uint256 legIndex 643 | * | ) internal pure returns (LeftRightUnsigned) { 644 | | uint128 amount0; 645 | | uint128 amount1; 646 | | 647 | * | (int24 tickLower, int24 tickUpper) = tokenId.asTicks(legIndex); 648 | | 649 | | // effective strike price of the option (avg. price over LP range) 650 | | // geometric mean of two numbers = √(x1 * x2) = √x1 * √x2 651 | * | uint256 geometricMeanPriceX96 = Math.mulDiv96( 652 | * | Math.getSqrtRatioAtTick(tickLower), 653 | * | Math.getSqrtRatioAtTick(tickUpper) 654 | | ); 655 | | 656 | * | if (tokenId.asset(legIndex) == 0) { 657 | * | amount0 = positionSize * uint128(tokenId.optionRatio(legIndex)); 658 | | 659 | * | amount1 = Math.mulDiv96RoundingUp(amount0, geometricMeanPriceX96).toUint128(); 660 | | } else { 661 | * | amount1 = positionSize * uint128(tokenId.optionRatio(legIndex)); 662 | | 663 | * | amount0 = Math.mulDivRoundingUp(amount1, 2 ** 96, geometricMeanPriceX96).toUint128(); 664 | | } 665 | | 666 | * | return LeftRightUnsigned.wrap(0).toRightSlot(amount0).toLeftSlot(amount1); 667 | | } 668 | | 669 | | /// @notice Compute the amount of funds that are moved to and removed from the Panoptic Pool. 670 | | /// @param tokenId The option position identifier 671 | | /// @param positionSize The number of positions minted 672 | | /// @param legIndex The leg index minted in this position, can be {0,1,2,3} 673 | | /// @return longs A LeftRight-packed word containing the total amount of long positions 674 | | /// @return shorts A LeftRight-packed word containing the amount of short positions 675 | * | function _calculateIOAmounts( 676 | | TokenId tokenId, 677 | | uint128 positionSize, 678 | | uint256 legIndex 679 | * | ) internal pure returns (LeftRightSigned longs, LeftRightSigned shorts) { 680 | | // compute amounts moved 681 | * | LeftRightUnsigned amountsMoved = getAmountsMoved(tokenId, positionSize, legIndex); 682 | | 683 | * | bool isShort = tokenId.isLong(legIndex) == 0; 684 | | 685 | | // if token0 686 | * | if (tokenId.tokenType(legIndex) == 0) { 687 | * | if (isShort) { 688 | | // if option is short, increment shorts by contracts 689 | * | shorts = shorts.toRightSlot(Math.toInt128(amountsMoved.rightSlot())); 690 | | } else { 691 | | // is option is long, increment longs by contracts 692 | * | longs = longs.toRightSlot(Math.toInt128(amountsMoved.rightSlot())); 693 | | } 694 | | } else { 695 | * | if (isShort) { 696 | | // if option is short, increment shorts by notional 697 | * | shorts = shorts.toLeftSlot(Math.toInt128(amountsMoved.leftSlot())); 698 | | } else { 699 | | // if option is long, increment longs by notional 700 | * | longs = longs.toLeftSlot(Math.toInt128(amountsMoved.leftSlot())); 701 | | } 702 | | } 703 | | } 704 | | 705 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 706 | | REVOKE/REFUND COMPUTATIONS 707 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 708 | | 709 | | /// @notice Check that the account is liquidatable, get the split of bonus0 and bonus1 amounts. 710 | | /// @param tokenData0 Leftright encoded word with balance of token0 in the right slot, and required balance in left slot 711 | | /// @param tokenData1 Leftright encoded word with balance of token1 in the right slot, and required balance in left slot 712 | | /// @param sqrtPriceX96Twap The sqrt(price) of the TWAP tick before liquidation used to evaluate solvency 713 | | /// @param sqrtPriceX96Final The current sqrt(price) of the AMM after liquidating a user 714 | | /// @param netExchanged The net exchanged value of the closed portfolio 715 | | /// @param premia Premium across all positions being liquidated present in tokenData 716 | | /// @return bonus0 Bonus amount for token0 717 | | /// @return bonus1 Bonus amount for token1 718 | | /// @return The LeftRight-packed protocol loss for both tokens, i.e., the delta between the user's balance and expended tokens 719 | | function getLiquidationBonus( 720 | | LeftRightUnsigned tokenData0, 721 | | LeftRightUnsigned tokenData1, 722 | | uint160 sqrtPriceX96Twap, 723 | | uint160 sqrtPriceX96Final, 724 | | LeftRightSigned netExchanged, 725 | | LeftRightSigned premia 726 | | ) external pure returns (int256 bonus0, int256 bonus1, LeftRightSigned) { 727 | | unchecked { 728 | | // compute bonus as min(collateralBalance/2, required-collateralBalance) 729 | | { 730 | | // compute the ratio of token0 to total collateral requirements 731 | | // evaluate at TWAP price to keep consistentcy with solvency calculations 732 | | uint256 required0 = PanopticMath.convert0to1( 733 | | tokenData0.leftSlot(), 734 | | sqrtPriceX96Twap 735 | | ); 736 | | uint256 required1 = tokenData1.leftSlot(); 737 | | 738 | | uint256 requiredRatioX128 = Math.mulDiv(required0, 2 ** 128, required0 + required1); 739 | | 740 | | (uint256 balanceCross, uint256 thresholdCross) = PanopticMath.convertCollateralData( 741 | | tokenData0, 742 | | tokenData1, 743 | | 0, 744 | | sqrtPriceX96Twap 745 | | ); 746 | | 747 | | uint256 bonusCross = Math.min(balanceCross / 2, thresholdCross - balanceCross); 748 | | 749 | | // convert that bonus to tokens 0 and 1 750 | | bonus0 = int256(Math.mulDiv128(bonusCross, requiredRatioX128)); 751 | | 752 | | bonus1 = int256( 753 | | PanopticMath.convert0to1( 754 | | Math.mulDiv128(bonusCross, 2 ** 128 - requiredRatioX128), 755 | | sqrtPriceX96Final 756 | | ) 757 | | ); 758 | | } 759 | | 760 | | // negative premium (owed to the liquidatee) is credited to the collateral balance 761 | | // this is already present in the netExchanged amount, so to avoid double-counting we remove it from the balance 762 | | int256 balance0 = int256(uint256(tokenData0.rightSlot())) - 763 | | Math.max(premia.rightSlot(), 0); 764 | | int256 balance1 = int256(uint256(tokenData1.rightSlot())) - 765 | | Math.max(premia.leftSlot(), 0); 766 | | 767 | | int256 paid0 = bonus0 + int256(netExchanged.rightSlot()); 768 | | int256 paid1 = bonus1 + int256(netExchanged.leftSlot()); 769 | | 770 | | // note that "balance0" and "balance1" are the liquidatee's original balances before token delegation by a liquidator 771 | | // their actual balances at the time of computation may be higher, but these are a buffer representing the amount of tokens we 772 | | // have to work with before cutting into the liquidator's funds 773 | | if (!(paid0 > balance0 && paid1 > balance1)) { 774 | | // liquidatee cannot pay back the liquidator fully in either token, so no protocol loss can be avoided 775 | | if ((paid0 > balance0)) { 776 | | // liquidatee has insufficient token0 but some token1 left over, so we use what they have left to mitigate token0 losses 777 | | // we do this by substituting an equivalent value of token1 in our refund to the liquidator, plus a bonus, for the token0 we convert 778 | | // we want to convert the minimum amount of tokens required to achieve the lowest possible protocol loss (to avoid overpaying on the conversion bonus) 779 | | // the maximum level of protocol loss mitigation that can be achieved is the liquidatee's excess token1 balance: balance1 - paid1 780 | | // and paid0 - balance0 is the amount of token0 that the liquidatee is missing, i.e the protocol loss 781 | | // if the protocol loss is lower than the excess token1 balance, then we can fully mitigate the loss and we should only convert the loss amount 782 | | // if the protocol loss is higher than the excess token1 balance, we can only mitigate part of the loss, so we should convert only the excess token1 balance 783 | | // thus, the value converted should be min(balance1 - paid1, paid0 - balance0) 784 | | bonus1 += Math.min( 785 | | balance1 - paid1, 786 | | PanopticMath.convert0to1(paid0 - balance0, sqrtPriceX96Final) 787 | | ); 788 | | bonus0 -= Math.min( 789 | | PanopticMath.convert1to0(balance1 - paid1, sqrtPriceX96Final), 790 | | paid0 - balance0 791 | | ); 792 | | } 793 | | if ((paid1 > balance1)) { 794 | | // liquidatee has insufficient token1 but some token0 left over, so we use what they have left to mitigate token1 losses 795 | | // we do this by substituting an equivalent value of token0 in our refund to the liquidator, plus a bonus, for the token1 we convert 796 | | // we want to convert the minimum amount of tokens required to achieve the lowest possible protocol loss (to avoid overpaying on the conversion bonus) 797 | | // the maximum level of protocol loss mitigation that can be achieved is the liquidatee's excess token0 balance: balance0 - paid0 798 | | // and paid1 - balance1 is the amount of token1 that the liquidatee is missing, i.e the protocol loss 799 | | // if the protocol loss is lower than the excess token0 balance, then we can fully mitigate the loss and we should only convert the loss amount 800 | | // if the protocol loss is higher than the excess token0 balance, we can only mitigate part of the loss, so we should convert only the excess token0 balance 801 | | // thus, the value converted should be min(balance0 - paid0, paid1 - balance1) 802 | | bonus0 += Math.min( 803 | | balance0 - paid0, 804 | | PanopticMath.convert1to0(paid1 - balance1, sqrtPriceX96Final) 805 | | ); 806 | | bonus1 -= Math.min( 807 | | PanopticMath.convert0to1(balance0 - paid0, sqrtPriceX96Final), 808 | | paid1 - balance1 809 | | ); 810 | | } 811 | | } 812 | | 813 | | paid0 = bonus0 + int256(netExchanged.rightSlot()); 814 | | paid1 = bonus1 + int256(netExchanged.leftSlot()); 815 | | return ( 816 | | bonus0, 817 | | bonus1, 818 | | LeftRightSigned.wrap(0).toRightSlot(int128(balance0 - paid0)).toLeftSlot( 819 | | int128(balance1 - paid1) 820 | | ) 821 | | ); 822 | | } 823 | | } 824 | | 825 | | /// @notice Haircut/clawback any premium paid by `liquidatee` on `positionIdList` over the protocol loss threshold during a liquidation. 826 | | /// @dev Note that the storage mapping provided as the `settledTokens` parameter WILL be modified on the caller by this function. 827 | | /// @param liquidatee The address of the user being liquidated 828 | | /// @param positionIdList The list of position ids being liquidated 829 | | /// @param premiasByLeg The premium paid (or received) by the liquidatee for each leg of each position 830 | | /// @param collateralRemaining The remaining collateral after the liquidation (negative if protocol loss) 831 | | /// @param sqrtPriceX96Final The sqrt price at which to convert between token0/token1 when awarding the bonus 832 | | /// @param collateral0 The collateral tracker for token0 833 | | /// @param collateral1 The collateral tracker for token1 834 | | /// @param settledTokens The per-chunk accumulator of settled tokens in storage from which to subtract the haircut premium 835 | | /// @return The delta in bonus0 for the liquidator post-haircut 836 | | /// @return The delta in bonus1 for the liquidator post-haircut 837 | | function haircutPremia( 838 | | address liquidatee, 839 | | TokenId[] memory positionIdList, 840 | | LeftRightSigned[4][] memory premiasByLeg, 841 | | LeftRightSigned collateralRemaining, 842 | | CollateralTracker collateral0, 843 | | CollateralTracker collateral1, 844 | | uint160 sqrtPriceX96Final, 845 | | mapping(bytes32 chunkKey => LeftRightUnsigned settledTokens) storage settledTokens 846 | | ) external returns (int256, int256) { 847 | | unchecked { 848 | | // get the amount of premium paid by the liquidatee 849 | | LeftRightSigned longPremium; 850 | | for (uint256 i = 0; i < positionIdList.length; ++i) { 851 | | TokenId tokenId = positionIdList[i]; 852 | | uint256 numLegs = tokenId.countLegs(); 853 | | for (uint256 leg = 0; leg < numLegs; ++leg) { 854 | | if (tokenId.isLong(leg) == 1) { 855 | | longPremium = longPremium.sub(premiasByLeg[i][leg]); 856 | | } 857 | | } 858 | | } 859 | | // Ignore any surplus collateral - the liquidatee is either solvent or it converts to <1 unit of the other token 860 | | int256 collateralDelta0 = -Math.min(collateralRemaining.rightSlot(), 0); 861 | | int256 collateralDelta1 = -Math.min(collateralRemaining.leftSlot(), 0); 862 | | int256 haircut0; 863 | | int256 haircut1; 864 | | 865 | | // if the premium in the same token is not enough to cover the loss and there is a surplus of the other token, 866 | | // the liquidator will provide the tokens (reflected in the bonus amount) & receive compensation in the other token 867 | | if ( 868 | | longPremium.rightSlot() < collateralDelta0 && 869 | | longPremium.leftSlot() > collateralDelta1 870 | | ) { 871 | | int256 protocolLoss1 = collateralDelta1; 872 | | (collateralDelta0, collateralDelta1) = ( 873 | | -Math.min( 874 | | collateralDelta0 - longPremium.rightSlot(), 875 | | PanopticMath.convert1to0( 876 | | longPremium.leftSlot() - collateralDelta1, 877 | | sqrtPriceX96Final 878 | | ) 879 | | ), 880 | | Math.min( 881 | | longPremium.leftSlot() - collateralDelta1, 882 | | PanopticMath.convert0to1( 883 | | collateralDelta0 - longPremium.rightSlot(), 884 | | sqrtPriceX96Final 885 | | ) 886 | | ) 887 | | ); 888 | | 889 | | haircut0 = longPremium.rightSlot(); 890 | | haircut1 = protocolLoss1 + collateralDelta1; 891 | | } else if ( 892 | | longPremium.leftSlot() < collateralDelta1 && 893 | | longPremium.rightSlot() > collateralDelta0 894 | | ) { 895 | | int256 protocolLoss0 = collateralDelta0; 896 | | (collateralDelta0, collateralDelta1) = ( 897 | | Math.min( 898 | | longPremium.rightSlot() - collateralDelta0, 899 | | PanopticMath.convert1to0( 900 | | collateralDelta1 - longPremium.leftSlot(), 901 | | sqrtPriceX96Final 902 | | ) 903 | | ), 904 | | -Math.min( 905 | | collateralDelta1 - longPremium.leftSlot(), 906 | | PanopticMath.convert0to1( 907 | | longPremium.rightSlot() - collateralDelta0, 908 | | sqrtPriceX96Final 909 | | ) 910 | | ) 911 | | ); 912 | | 913 | | haircut0 = collateralDelta0 + protocolLoss0; 914 | | haircut1 = longPremium.leftSlot(); 915 | | } else { 916 | | // for each token, haircut until the protocol loss is mitigated or the premium paid is exhausted 917 | | haircut0 = Math.min(collateralDelta0,
