longPremium.rightSlot()); 918 | | haircut1 = Math.min(collateralDelta1, longPremium.leftSlot()); 919 | | 920 | | collateralDelta0 = 0; 921 | | collateralDelta1 = 0; 922 | | } 923 | | 924 | | { 925 | | address _liquidatee = liquidatee; 926 | | if (haircut0 != 0) collateral0.exercise(_liquidatee, 0, 0, 0, int128(haircut0)); 927 | | if (haircut1 != 0) collateral1.exercise(_liquidatee, 0, 0, 0, int128(haircut1)); 928 | | } 929 | | 930 | | for (uint256 i = 0; i < positionIdList.length; i++) { 931 | | TokenId tokenId = positionIdList[i]; 932 | | LeftRightSigned[4][] memory _premiasByLeg = premiasByLeg; 933 | | for (uint256 leg = 0; leg < tokenId.countLegs(); ++leg) { 934 | | if (tokenId.isLong(leg) == 1) { 935 | | mapping(bytes32 chunkKey => LeftRightUnsigned settledTokens) 936 | | storage _settledTokens = settledTokens; 937 | | 938 | | // calculate amounts to revoke from settled and subtract from haircut req 939 | | uint256 settled0 = Math.unsafeDivRoundingUp( 940 | | uint128(-_premiasByLeg[i][leg].rightSlot()) * uint256(haircut0), 941 | | uint128(longPremium.rightSlot()) 942 | | ); 943 | | uint256 settled1 = Math.unsafeDivRoundingUp( 944 | | uint128(-_premiasByLeg[i][leg].leftSlot()) * uint256(haircut1), 945 | | uint128(longPremium.leftSlot()) 946 | | ); 947 | | 948 | | bytes32 chunkKey = keccak256( 949 | | abi.encodePacked( 950 | | tokenId.strike(leg), 951 | | tokenId.width(leg), 952 | | tokenId.tokenType(leg) 953 | | ) 954 | | ); 955 | | 956 | | // The long premium is not commited to storage during the liquidation, so we add the entire adjusted amount 957 | | // for the haircut directly to the accumulator 958 | | settled0 = Math.max( 959 | | 0, 960 | | uint128(-_premiasByLeg[i][leg].rightSlot()) - settled0 961 | | ); 962 | | settled1 = Math.max( 963 | | 0, 964 | | uint128(-_premiasByLeg[i][leg].leftSlot()) - settled1 965 | | ); 966 | | 967 | | _settledTokens[chunkKey] = _settledTokens[chunkKey].add( 968 | | LeftRightUnsigned.wrap(0).toRightSlot(uint128(settled0)).toLeftSlot( 969 | | uint128(settled1) 970 | | ) 971 | | ); 972 | | } 973 | | } 974 | | } 975 | | 976 | | return (collateralDelta0, collateralDelta1); 977 | | } 978 | | } 979 | | 980 | | /// @notice Redistribute the final exercise fee deltas between tokens if necessary according to the available collateral from the exercised user. 981 | | /// @param exercisee Address of the force exercisee 982 | | /// @param delegated0 The amount of virtual token0 shares delegated to the exercisor that must be returned to the protocol 983 | | /// @param delegated1 The amount of virtual token1 shares delegated to the exercisor that must be returned to the protocol 984 | | /// @param exerciseFees Exercise fees to debit from exercisor at tick(atTick) rightSlot = token0 left = token1 985 | | /// @param atTick Tick to convert values at. This can be the current tick or some TWAP/median tick 986 | | /// @param collateral0 CollateralTracker for token0 987 | | /// @param collateral1 CollateralTracker for token1 988 | | /// @return The LeftRight-packed deltas for token0/token1 to move from the exercisor to the exercisee 989 | | function getExerciseDeltas( 990 | | address exercisee, 991 | | uint256 delegated0, 992 | | uint256 delegated1, 993 | | LeftRightSigned exerciseFees, 994 | | int24 atTick, 995 | | CollateralTracker collateral0, 996 | | CollateralTracker collateral1 997 | | ) external view returns (LeftRightSigned) { 998 | | uint160 sqrtPriceX96 = Math.getSqrtRatioAtTick(atTick); 999 | | unchecked { 1000 | | // if the refunder lacks sufficient token0 to pay back the virtual shares, have the exercisor cover the difference in exchange for token1 (and vice versa) 1001 | | int256 balanceShortage = int256( 1002 | | Math.mulDivRoundingUp( 1003 | | delegated0, 1004 | | collateral0.totalAssets(), 1005 | | collateral0.totalSupply() 1006 | | ) 1007 | | ) - 1008 | | int256(collateral0.convertToAssets(collateral0.balanceOf(exercisee))) + 1009 | | exerciseFees.rightSlot(); 1010 | | 1011 | | if (balanceShortage > 0) { 1012 | | return 1013 | | LeftRightSigned 1014 | | .wrap(0) 1015 | | .toRightSlot(int128(exerciseFees.rightSlot() - balanceShortage)) 1016 | | .toLeftSlot( 1017 | | int128( 1018 | | int256( 1019 | | PanopticMath.convert0to1(uint256(balanceShortage), sqrtPriceX96) 1020 | | ) + exerciseFees.leftSlot() 1021 | | ) 1022 | | ); 1023 | | } 1024 | | 1025 | | balanceShortage = 1026 | | int256( 1027 | | Math.mulDivRoundingUp( 1028 | | delegated1, 1029 | | collateral1.totalAssets(), 1030 | | collateral1.totalSupply() 1031 | | ) 1032 | | ) - 1033 | | int256(collateral1.convertToAssets(collateral1.balanceOf(exercisee))) + 1034 | | exerciseFees.leftSlot(); 1035 | | 1036 | | if (balanceShortage > 0) { 1037 | | return 1038 | | LeftRightSigned 1039 | | .wrap(0) 1040 | | .toLeftSlot(int128(exerciseFees.leftSlot() - balanceShortage)) 1041 | | .toRightSlot( 1042 | | int128( 1043 | | int256( 1044 | | PanopticMath.convert1to0(uint256(balanceShortage), sqrtPriceX96) 1045 | | ) + exerciseFees.rightSlot() 1046 | | ) 1047 | | ); 1048 | | } 1049 | | } 1050 | | 1051 | | // otherwise, no need to deviate from the original exercise fee deltas 1052 | | return exerciseFees; 1053 | | } 1054 | | } 1055 | |
/home/xshadow/panoptic-v1-core-private/contracts/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol 1 | | // SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-2.0-or-later 2 | | pragma solidity ^0.8.0; 3 | | 4 | | // Libraries 5 | | import {Errors} from "@libraries/Errors.sol"; 6 | | 7 | | /// @notice Safe ERC20 transfer library that gracefully handles missing return values. 8 | | /// @author Axicon Labs Limited 9 | | /// @author Modified from Solmate (https://github.com/Rari-Capital/solmate/blob/main/src/utils/SafeTransferLib.sol) 10 | | /// @dev Caution! This library won't check that a token has code, responsibility is delegated to the caller. 11 | | library SafeTransferLib { 12 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 13 | | ERC20 OPERATIONS 14 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 15 | | 16 | | /// @notice Safely transfers ERC20 tokens from one address to another. 17 | | /// @param token The address of the ERC20 token 18 | | /// @param from The address to transfer tokens from 19 | | /// @param to The address to transfer tokens to 20 | | /// @param amount The amount of tokens to transfer 21 | * | function safeTransferFrom(address token, address from, address to, uint256 amount) internal { 22 | * | bool success; 23 | | 24 | | assembly ("memory-safe") { 25 | | // Get free memory pointer - we will store our calldata in scratch space starting at the offset specified here. 26 | * | let p := mload(0x40) 27 | | 28 | | // Write the abi-encoded calldata into memory, beginning with the function selector. 29 | * | mstore(p, 0x23b872dd00000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000) 30 | * | mstore(add(4, p), from) // Append the "from" argument. 31 | * | mstore(add(36, p), to) // Append the "to" argument. 32 | * | mstore(add(68, p), amount) // Append the "amount" argument. 33 | | 34 | * | success := and( 35 | | // Set success to whether the call reverted, if not we check it either 36 | | // returned exactly 1 (can't just be non-zero data), or had no return data. 37 | * | or(and(eq(mload(0), 1), gt(returndatasize(), 31)), iszero(returndatasize())), 38 | * | // We use 100 because that's the total length of our calldata (4 + 32 * 3) 39 | | // Counterintuitively, this call() must be positioned after the or() in the 40 | * | // surrounding and() because and() evaluates its arguments from right to left. 41 | * | call(gas(), token, 0, p, 100, 0, 32) 42 | * | ) 43 | | } 44 | | 45 | * | if (!success) revert Errors.TransferFailed(); 46 | | } 47 | | 48 | | /// @notice Safely transfers ERC20 tokens to a specified address. 49 | * | /// @param token The address of the ERC20 token 50 | * | /// @param to The address to transfer tokens to 51 | * | /// @param amount The amount of tokens to transfer 52 | | function safeTransfer(address token, address to, uint256 amount) internal { 53 | | bool success; 54 | | 55 | * | assembly ("memory-safe") { 56 | * | // Get free memory pointer - we will store our calldata in scratch space starting at the offset specified here. 57 | * | let p := mload(0x40) 58 | | 59 | * | // Write the abi-encoded calldata into memory, beginning with the function selector. 60 | * | mstore(p, 0xa9059cbb00000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000) 61 | * | mstore(add(4, p), to) // Append the "to" argument. 62 | * | mstore(add(36, p), amount) // Append the "amount" argument. 63 | | 64 | | success := and( 65 | | // Set success to whether the call reverted, if not we check it either 66 | * | // returned exactly 1 (can't just be non-zero data), or had no return data. 67 | * | or(and(eq(mload(0), 1), gt(returndatasize(), 31)), iszero(returndatasize())), 68 | * | // We use 68 because that's the total length of our calldata (4 + 32 * 2) 69 | | // Counterintuitively, this call() must be positioned after the or() in the 70 | | // surrounding and() because and() evaluates its arguments from right to left. 71 | | call(gas(), token, 0, p, 68, 0, 32) 72 | | ) 73 | | } 74 | | 75 | | if (!success) revert Errors.TransferFailed(); 76 | | } 77 | | } 78 | |
/home/xshadow/panoptic-v1-core-private/contracts/tokens/ERC1155Minimal.sol 1 | | // SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-2.0-or-later 2 | | pragma solidity ^0.8.0; 3 | | 4 | | // OpenZeppelin libraries 5 | | import {ERC1155Holder} from "@openzeppelin/contracts/token/ERC1155/utils/ERC1155Holder.sol"; 6 | | 7 | | /// @title Minimalist ERC1155 implementation without metadata. 8 | | /// @author Axicon Labs Limited 9 | * | /// @author Modified from Solmate (https://github.com/transmissions11/solmate/blob/v7/src/tokens/ERC1155.sol) 10 | * | /// @dev Not compliant to the letter, does not include any metadata functionality. 11 | | abstract contract ERC1155 { 12 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 13 | | EVENTS 14 | * | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 15 | | 16 | * | /// @notice Emitted when only a single token is transferred. 17 | * | /// @param operator The user who initiated the transfer 18 | | /// @param from The user who sent the tokens 19 | | /// @param to The user who received the tokens 20 | * | /// @param id The ERC1155 token id 21 | * | /// @param amount The amount of tokens transferred 22 | * | event TransferSingle( 23 | * | address indexed operator, 24 | | address indexed from, 25 | | address indexed to, 26 | | uint256 id, 27 | | uint256 amount 28 | | ); 29 | | 30 | | /// @notice Emitted when multiple tokens are transferred from one user to another. 31 | | /// @param operator The user who initiated the transfer 32 | | /// @param from The user who sent the tokens 33 | | /// @param to The user who received the tokens 34 | | /// @param ids The ERC1155 token ids 35 | | /// @param amounts The amounts of tokens transferred 36 | | event TransferBatch( 37 | | address indexed operator, 38 | | address indexed from, 39 | | address indexed to, 40 | | uint256[] ids, 41 | | uint256[] amounts 42 | | ); 43 | | 44 | | /// @notice Emitted when the approval status of an operator to transfer all tokens on behalf of a user is modified. 45 | | /// @param owner The user who approved or disapproved `operator` to transfer their tokens 46 | | /// @param operator The user who was approved or disapproved to transfer all tokens on behalf of `owner` 47 | | /// @param approved Whether `operator` is approved or disapproved to transfer all tokens on behalf of `owner` 48 | | event ApprovalForAll(address indexed owner, address indexed operator, bool approved); 49 | | 50 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 51 | | ERRORS 52 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 53 | | 54 | | /// @notice Emitted when a user attempts to transfer tokens they do not own nor are approved to transfer. 55 | | error NotAuthorized(); 56 | | 57 | | /// @notice Emitted when an attempt is made to initiate a transfer to a contract recipient that fails to signal support for ERC1155. 58 | | error UnsafeRecipient(); 59 | | 60 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 61 | | ERC1155 STORAGE 62 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 63 | | 64 | | /// @notice Token balances for each user. 65 | | /// @dev Indexed by user, then by token id. 66 | * | mapping(address account => mapping(uint256 tokenId => uint256 balance)) public balanceOf; 67 | | 68 | | /// @notice Approved addresses for each user. 69 | | /// @dev Indexed by user, then by operator. 70 | | /// @dev Operator is approved to transfer all tokens on behalf of user. 71 | | mapping(address owner => mapping(address operator => bool approvedForAll)) 72 | | public isApprovedForAll; 73 | | 74 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 75 | | ERC1155 LOGIC 76 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 77 | | 78 | | /// @notice Approve or revoke approval for `operator` to transfer all tokens on behalf of the caller. 79 | | /// @param operator The address to approve or revoke approval for 80 | | /// @param approved True to approve, false to revoke approval 81 | | function setApprovalForAll(address operator, bool approved) public { 82 | | isApprovedForAll[msg.sender][operator] = approved; 83 | | 84 | | emit ApprovalForAll(msg.sender, operator, approved); 85 | | } 86 | | 87 | | /// @notice Transfer a single token from one user to another. 88 | | /// @dev Supports approved token transfers. 89 | | /// @param from The user to transfer tokens from 90 | | /// @param to The user to transfer tokens to 91 | | /// @param id The ERC1155 token id to transfer 92 | | /// @param amount The amount of tokens to transfer 93 | | /// @param data Optional data to include in the `onERC1155Received` hook 94 | | function safeTransferFrom( 95 | | address from, 96 | | address to, 97 | | uint256 id, 98 | | uint256 amount, 99 | | bytes calldata data 100 | | ) public virtual { 101 | | if (!(msg.sender == from || isApprovedForAll[from][msg.sender])) revert NotAuthorized(); 102 | | 103 | | balanceOf[from][id] -= amount; 104 | | 105 | | // balance will never overflow 106 | | unchecked { 107 | | balanceOf[to][id] += amount; 108 | | } 109 | | 110 | | emit TransferSingle(msg.sender, from, to, id, amount); 111 | | 112 | | if (to.code.length != 0) { 113 | | if ( 114 | | ERC1155Holder(to).onERC1155Received(msg.sender, from, id, amount, data) != 115 | | ERC1155Holder.onERC1155Received.selector 116 | | ) { 117 | | revert UnsafeRecipient(); 118 | | } 119 | | } 120 | | } 121 | | 122 | | /// @notice Transfer multiple tokens from one user to another. 123 | | /// @dev Supports approved token transfers. 124 | | /// @dev `ids` and `amounts` must be of equal length. 125 | | /// @param from The user to transfer tokens from 126 | | /// @param to The user to transfer tokens to 127 | | /// @param ids The ERC1155 token ids to transfer 128 | | /// @param amounts The amounts of tokens to transfer 129 | | /// @param data Optional data to include in the `onERC1155Received` hook 130 | | function safeBatchTransferFrom( 131 | | address from, 132 | | address to, 133 | | uint256[] calldata ids, 134 | | uint256[] calldata amounts, 135 | | bytes calldata data 136 | | ) public virtual { 137 | | if (!(msg.sender == from || isApprovedForAll[from][msg.sender])) revert NotAuthorized(); 138 | | 139 | | // Storing these outside the loop saves ~15 gas per iteration. 140 | | uint256 id; 141 | | uint256 amount; 142 | | 143 | | for (uint256 i = 0; i < ids.length; ) { 144 | | id = ids[i]; 145 | | amount = amounts[i]; 146 | | 147 | | balanceOf[from][id] -= amount; 148 | | 149 | | // balance will never overflow 150 | | unchecked { 151 | | balanceOf[to][id] += amount; 152 | | } 153 | | 154 | | // An array can't have a total length 155 | | // larger than the max uint256 value. 156 | | unchecked { 157 | | ++i; 158 | | } 159 | | } 160 | | 161 | | emit TransferBatch(msg.sender, from, to, ids, amounts); 162 | | 163 | | if (to.code.length != 0) { 164 | | if ( 165 | | ERC1155Holder(to).onERC1155BatchReceived(msg.sender, from, ids, amounts, data) != 166 | | ERC1155Holder.onERC1155BatchReceived.selector 167 | | ) { 168 | | revert UnsafeRecipient(); 169 | | } 170 | | } 171 | | } 172 | | 173 | | /// @notice Query balances for multiple users and tokens at once. 174 | | /// @dev `owners` and `ids` should be of equal length. 175 | | /// @param owners The list of users to query balances for 176 | | /// @param ids The list of ERC1155 token ids to query 177 | | /// @return balances The balances for each owner-id pair in the same order as the input arrays 178 | | function balanceOfBatch( 179 | | address[] calldata owners, 180 | | uint256[] calldata ids 181 | | ) public view returns (uint256[] memory balances) { 182 | | balances = new uint256[](owners.length); 183 | | 184 | | // Unchecked because the only math done is incrementing 185 | | // the array index counter which cannot possibly overflow. 186 | | unchecked { 187 | | for (uint256 i = 0; i < owners.length; ++i) { 188 | | balances[i] = balanceOf[owners[i]][ids[i]]; 189 | | } 190 | | } 191 | | } 192 | | 193 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 194 | | ERC165 LOGIC 195 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 196 | | 197 | | /// @notice Signal support for ERC165 and ERC1155. 198 | | /// @param interfaceId The interface to check for support 199 | | /// @return Whether the interface is supported 200 | * | function supportsInterface(bytes4 interfaceId) public pure returns (bool) { 201 | * | return 202 | * | interfaceId == 0x01ffc9a7 || // ERC165 Interface ID for ERC165 203 | | interfaceId == 0xd9b67a26; // ERC165 Interface ID for ERC1155 204 | | } 205 | | 206 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 207 | | INTERNAL MINT/BURN LOGIC 208 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 209 | | 210 | | /// @notice Internal utility to mint tokens to a user's account. 211 | | /// @param to The user to mint tokens to 212 | | /// @param id The ERC1155 token id to mint 213 | | /// @param amount The amount of tokens to mint 214 | * | function _mint(address to, uint256 id, uint256 amount) internal { 215 | | // balance will never overflow 216 | | unchecked { 217 | * | balanceOf[to][id] += amount; 218 | | } 219 | | 220 | * | emit TransferSingle(msg.sender, address(0), to, id, amount); 221 | | 222 | * | if (to.code.length != 0) { 223 | * | if ( 224 | * | ERC1155Holder(to).onERC1155Received(msg.sender, address(0), id, amount, "") != 225 | * | ERC1155Holder.onERC1155Received.selector 226 | | ) { 227 | | revert UnsafeRecipient(); 228 | | } 229 | | } 230 | | } 231 | | 232 | | /// @notice Internal utility to burn tokens from a user's account. 233 | | /// @param from The user to burn tokens from 234 | | /// @param id The ERC1155 token id to mint 235 | | /// @param amount The amount of tokens to burn 236 | * | function _burn(address from, uint256 id, uint256 amount) internal { 237 | * | balanceOf[from][id] -= amount; 238 | | 239 | * | emit TransferSingle(msg.sender, from, address(0), id, amount); 240 | | } 241 | | } 242 | |
/home/xshadow/panoptic-v1-core-private/contracts/tokens/ERC20Minimal.sol 1 | | // SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-2.0-or-later 2 | | pragma solidity ^0.8.0; 3 | | 4 | | /// @title Minimal efficient ERC20 implementation without metadata 5 | | /// @author Axicon Labs Limited 6 | | /// @author Modified from Solmate (https://github.com/transmissions11/solmate/blob/v7/src/tokens/ERC20.sol) 7 | | /// @dev The metadata must be set in the inheriting contract. 8 | | /// @dev Do not manually set balances without updating totalSupply, as the sum of all user balances must not exceed it. 9 | | abstract contract ERC20Minimal { 10 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 11 | | EVENTS 12 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 13 | | 14 | | /// @notice Emitted when tokens are transferred 15 | | /// @param from The sender of the tokens 16 | | /// @param to The recipient of the tokens 17 | | /// @param amount The amount of tokens transferred 18 | | event Transfer(address indexed from, address indexed to, uint256 amount); 19 | | 20 | | /// @notice Emitted when a user approves another user to spend tokens on their behalf 21 | | /// @param owner The user who approved the spender 22 | | /// @param spender The user who was approved to spend tokens 23 | | /// @param amount The amount of tokens approved to spend 24 | | event Approval(address indexed owner, address indexed spender, uint256 amount); 25 | | 26 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 27 | * | ERC20 STORAGE 28 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 29 | | 30 | | /// @notice The total supply of tokens. 31 | * | /// @dev This cannot exceed the max uint256 value. 32 | * | uint256 public totalSupply; 33 | | 34 | | /// @notice Token balances for each user 35 | * | mapping(address account => uint256 balance) public balanceOf; 36 | | 37 | * | /// @notice Stored allowances for each user. 38 | * | /// @dev Indexed by owner, then by spender. 39 | * | mapping(address owner => mapping(address spender => uint256 allowance)) public allowance; 40 | | 41 | * | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 42 | * | ERC20 LOGIC 43 | * | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 44 | | 45 | * | /// @notice Approves a user to spend tokens on the caller's behalf. 46 | * | /// @param spender The user to approve 47 | * | /// @param amount The amount of tokens to approve 48 | | /// @return Whether the approval succeeded 49 | * | function approve(address spender, uint256 amount) public returns (bool) { 50 | * | allowance[msg.sender][spender] = amount; 51 | | 52 | * | emit Approval(msg.sender, spender, amount); 53 | | 54 | * | return true; 55 | | } 56 | | 57 | | /// @notice Transfers tokens from the caller to another user. 58 | | /// @param to The user to transfer tokens to 59 | * | /// @param amount The amount of tokens to transfer 60 | * | /// @return Whether the transfer succeeded 61 | * | function transfer(address to, uint256 amount) public virtual returns (bool) { 62 | * | balanceOf[msg.sender] -= amount; 63 | | 64 | | // Cannot overflow because the sum of all user 65 | | // balances can't exceed the max uint256 value. 66 | | unchecked { 67 | | balanceOf[to] += amount; 68 | | } 69 | | 70 | | emit Transfer(msg.sender, to, amount); 71 | | 72 | | return true; 73 | | } 74 | | 75 | | /// @notice Transfers tokens from one user to another. 76 | | /// @dev Supports token approvals. 77 | | /// @param from The user to transfer tokens from 78 | | /// @param to The user to transfer tokens to 79 | | /// @param amount The amount of tokens to transfer 80 | | /// @return Whether the transfer succeeded 81 | * | function transferFrom(address from, address to, uint256 amount) public virtual returns (bool) { 82 | * | uint256 allowed = allowance[from][msg.sender]; // Saves gas for limited approvals. 83 | | 84 | * | if (allowed != type(uint256).max) allowance[from][msg.sender] = allowed - amount; 85 | | 86 | * | balanceOf[from] -= amount; 87 | | 88 | * | // Cannot overflow because the sum of all user 89 | | // balances can't exceed the max uint256 value. 90 | | unchecked { 91 | | balanceOf[to] += amount; 92 | | } 93 | | 94 | * | emit Transfer(from, to, amount); 95 | | 96 | | return true; 97 | | } 98 | | 99 | | /// @notice Internal utility to transfer tokens from one user to another. 100 | * | /// @param from The user to transfer tokens from 101 | * | /// @param to The user to transfer tokens to 102 | * | /// @param amount The amount of tokens to transfer 103 | | function _transferFrom(address from, address to, uint256 amount) internal { 104 | | balanceOf[from] -= amount; 105 | | 106 | * | // Cannot overflow because the sum of all user 107 | | // balances can't exceed the max uint256 value. 108 | * | unchecked { 109 | * | balanceOf[to] += amount; 110 | | } 111 | | 112 | * | emit Transfer(from, to, amount); 113 | * | } 114 | | 115 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 116 | | INTERNAL MINT/BURN LOGIC 117 | * | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 118 | | 119 | | /// @notice Internal utility to mint tokens to a user's account. 120 | * | /// @param to The user to mint tokens to 121 | | /// @param amount The amount of tokens to mint 122 | * | function _mint(address to, uint256 amount) internal { 123 | | // Cannot overflow because the sum of all user 124 | | // balances can't exceed the max uint256 value. 125 | | unchecked { 126 | * | balanceOf[to] += amount; 127 | | } 128 | * | totalSupply += amount; 129 | | 130 | * | emit Transfer(address(0), to, amount); 131 | | } 132 | | 133 | | /// @notice Internal utility to burn tokens from a user's account. 134 | | /// @param from The user to burn tokens from 135 | | /// @param amount The amount of tokens to burn 136 | * | function _burn(address from, uint256 amount) internal { 137 | * | balanceOf[from] -= amount; 138 | | 139 | | // Cannot underflow because a user's balance 140 | * | // will never be larger than the total supply. 141 | | unchecked { 142 | * | totalSupply -= amount; 143 | * | } 144 | | 145 | * | emit Transfer(from, address(0), amount); 146 | | } 147 | | } 148 | * |
/home/xshadow/panoptic-v1-core-private/contracts/tokens/interfaces/IERC20Partial.sol 1 | | // SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-2.0-or-later 2 | | pragma solidity ^0.8.0; 3 | | 4 | | /// @title Partial definition of the ERC20 interface as defined in the EIP 5 | | /// @dev Does not include return values as certain tokens such as USDT fail to implement them. 6 | | /// @dev Since the return value is not expected, this interface works with both compliant and non-compliant tokens. 7 | | /// @dev Clients are recommended to consume and handle the return of negative success values. 8 | | /// @dev However, we cannot productively handle a failed approval and such a situation would surely cause a revert later in execution. 9 | | /// @dev In addition, no notable instances exist of tokens that both i) contain a failure case for `approve` and ii) return `false` instead of reverting. 10 | | /// @author Axicon Labs Limited 11 | | interface IERC20Partial { 12 | | /// @notice Returns the amount of tokens owned by `account`. 13 | | /// @dev This function is unchanged from the EIP 14 | | /// @param account The address to query the balance of 15 | | /// @return The amount of tokens owned by `account` 16 | | function balanceOf(address account) external view returns (uint256); 17 | | 18 | | /// @notice Sets `amount` as the allowance of `spender` over the caller's tokens. 19 | | /// @dev While this function is specified to return a boolean value in the EIP, this interface does not expect one 20 | | /// @param spender The address which will spend the funds 21 | * | /// @param amount The amount of tokens allowed to be spent 22 | | function approve(address spender, uint256 amount) external; 23 | | 24 | | /// @notice Transfers tokens from the caller to another user. 25 | | /// @param to The user to transfer tokens to 26 | | /// @param amount The amount of tokens to transfer 27 | * | function transfer(address to, uint256 amount) external; 28 | | } 29 | * |
/home/xshadow/panoptic-v1-core-private/contracts/types/LeftRight.sol 1 | | // SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-2.0-or-later 2 | | pragma solidity ^0.8.0; 3 | | 4 | | // Libraries 5 | | import {Errors} from "@libraries/Errors.sol"; 6 | | import {Math} from "@libraries/Math.sol"; 7 | | 8 | | type LeftRightUnsigned is uint256; 9 | | using LeftRightLibrary for LeftRightUnsigned global; 10 | | 11 | | type LeftRightSigned is int256; 12 | | using LeftRightLibrary for LeftRightSigned global; 13 | | 14 | | /// @title Pack two separate data (each of 128bit) into a single 256-bit slot; 256bit-to-128bit packing methods. 15 | | /// @author Axicon Labs Limited 16 | | /// @notice Simple data type that divides a 256-bit word into two 128-bit slots. 17 | | library LeftRightLibrary { 18 | | using Math for uint256; 19 | | 20 | | /// @notice AND bitmask to isolate the left half of a uint256. 21 | | uint256 internal constant LEFT_HALF_BIT_MASK = 22 | | 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF00000000000000000000000000000000; 23 | | 24 | | /// @notice AND bitmask to isolate the left half of an int256. 25 | | int256 internal constant LEFT_HALF_BIT_MASK_INT = 26 | | int256(uint256(0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF00000000000000000000000000000000)); 27 | | 28 | | /// @notice AND bitmask to isolate the right half of an int256. 29 | | int256 internal constant RIGHT_HALF_BIT_MASK = 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF; 30 | | 31 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 32 | | RIGHT SLOT 33 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 34 | | 35 | | /// @notice Get the "right" slot from a bit pattern. 36 | | /// @param self The 256 bit value to extract the right half from 37 | | /// @return The right half of `self` 38 | * | function rightSlot(LeftRightUnsigned self) internal pure returns (uint128) { 39 | * | return uint128(LeftRightUnsigned.unwrap(self)); 40 | | } 41 | | 42 | | /// @notice Get the "right" slot from a bit pattern. 43 | | /// @param self The 256 bit value to extract the right half from 44 | | /// @return The right half of `self` 45 | * | function rightSlot(LeftRightSigned self) internal pure returns (int128) { 46 | * | return int128(LeftRightSigned.unwrap(self)); 47 | | } 48 | | 49 | | // All toRightSlot functions add bits to the right slot without clearing it first 50 | | // Typically, the slot is already clear when writing to it, but if it is not, the bits will be added to the existing bits 51 | | // Therefore, the assumption must not be made that the bits will be cleared while using these helpers 52 | | // Note that the values *within* the slots are allowed to overflow, but overflows are contained and will not leak into the other slot 53 | | 54 | | /// @notice Add to the "right" slot in a 256-bit pattern. 55 | | /// @param self The 256-bit pattern to be written to 56 | | /// @param right The value to be added to the right slot 57 | | /// @return `self` with `right` added (not overwritten, but added) to the value in its right 128 bits 58 | * | function toRightSlot( 59 | | LeftRightUnsigned self, 60 | | uint128 right 61 | | ) internal pure returns (LeftRightUnsigned) { 62 | | unchecked { 63 | | // prevent the right slot from leaking into the left one in the case of an overflow 64 | | // ff + 1 = (1)00, but we want just ff + 1 = 00 65 | | return 66 | | LeftRightUnsigned.wrap( 67 | * | (LeftRightUnsigned.unwrap(self) & LEFT_HALF_BIT_MASK) + 68 | * | uint256(uint128(LeftRightUnsigned.unwrap(self)) + right) 69 | | ); 70 | | } 71 | | } 72 | | 73 | * | /// @notice Add to the "right" slot in a 256-bit pattern. 74 | | /// @param self The 256-bit pattern to be written to 75 | | /// @param right The value to be added to the right slot 76 | | /// @return `self` with `right` added (not overwritten, but added) to the value in its right 128 bits 77 | * | function toRightSlot( 78 | | LeftRightSigned self, 79 | | int128 right 80 | | ) internal pure returns (LeftRightSigned) { 81 | * | // bit mask needed in case rightHalfBitPattern < 0 due to 2's complement 82 | | unchecked { 83 | | // prevent the right slot from leaking into the left one in the case of a positive sign change 84 | * | // ff + 1 = (1)00, but we want just ff + 1 = 00 85 | * | return 86 | | LeftRightSigned.wrap( 87 | * | (LeftRightSigned.unwrap(self) & LEFT_HALF_BIT_MASK_INT) + 88 | * | (int256(int128(LeftRightSigned.unwrap(self)) + right) & RIGHT_HALF_BIT_MASK) 89 | | ); 90 | * | } 91 | | } 92 | | 93 | * | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 94 | * | LEFT SLOT 95 | * | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 96 | | 97 | * | /// @notice Get the "left" slot from a bit pattern. 98 | * | /// @param self The 256 bit value to extract the left half from 99 | * | /// @return The left half of `self` 100 | * | function leftSlot(LeftRightUnsigned self) internal pure returns (uint128) { 101 | * | return uint128(LeftRightUnsigned.unwrap(self) >> 128); 102 | | } 103 | | 104 | * | /// @notice Get the "left" slot from a bit pattern. 105 | * | /// @param self The 256 bit value to extract the left half from 106 | | /// @return The left half of `self` 107 | * | function leftSlot(LeftRightSigned self) internal pure returns (int128) { 108 | * | return int128(LeftRightSigned.unwrap(self) >> 128); 109 | | } 110 | | 111 | | /// All toLeftSlot functions add bits to the left slot without clearing it first 112 | | // Typically, the slot is already clear when writing to it, but if it is not, the bits will be added to the existing bits 113 | | // Therefore, the assumption must not be made that the bits will be cleared while using these helpers 114 | | // Note that the values *within* the slots are allowed to overflow, but overflows are contained and will not leak into the other slot 115 | | 116 | | /// @notice Add to the "left" slot in a 256-bit pattern. 117 | | /// @param self The 256-bit pattern to be written to 118 | | /// @param left The value to be added to the left slot 119 | | /// @return `self` with `left` added (not overwritten, but added) to the value in its left 128 bits 120 | * | function toLeftSlot( 121 | | LeftRightUnsigned self, 122 | | uint128 left 123 | | ) internal pure returns (LeftRightUnsigned) { 124 | | unchecked { 125 | * | return LeftRightUnsigned.wrap(LeftRightUnsigned.unwrap(self) + (uint256(left) << 128)); 126 | | } 127 | | } 128 | | 129 | | /// @notice Add to the "left" slot in a 256-bit pattern. 130 | | /// @param self The 256-bit pattern to be written to 131 | | /// @param left The value to be added to the left slot 132 | * | /// @return `self` with `left` added (not overwritten, but added) to the value in its left 128 bits 133 | * | function toLeftSlot(LeftRightSigned self, int128 left) internal pure returns (LeftRightSigned) { 134 | | unchecked { 135 | * | return LeftRightSigned.wrap(LeftRightSigned.unwrap(self) + (int256(left) << 128)); 136 | | } 137 | | } 138 | | 139 | * | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 140 | * | MATH HELPERS 141 | * | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 142 | | 143 | * | /// @notice Add two LeftRight-encoded words; revert on overflow or underflow. 144 | | /// @param x The augend 145 | * | /// @param y The addend 146 | * | /// @return z The sum `x + y` 147 | * | function add( 148 | * | LeftRightUnsigned x, 149 | * | LeftRightUnsigned y 150 | * | ) internal pure returns (LeftRightUnsigned z) { 151 | | unchecked { 152 | * | // adding leftRight packed uint128's is same as just adding the values explictily 153 | * | // given that we check for overflows of the left and right values 154 | * | z = LeftRightUnsigned.wrap(LeftRightUnsigned.unwrap(x) + LeftRightUnsigned.unwrap(y)); 155 | | 156 | * | // on overflow z will be less than either x or y 157 | * | // type cast z to uint128 to isolate the right slot and if it's lower than a value it's comprised of (x) 158 | | // then an overflow has occured 159 | * | if ( 160 | * | LeftRightUnsigned.unwrap(z) < LeftRightUnsigned.unwrap(x) || 161 | * | (uint128(LeftRightUnsigned.unwrap(z)) < uint128(LeftRightUnsigned.unwrap(x))) 162 | | ) revert Errors.UnderOverFlow(); 163 | | } 164 | | } 165 | | 166 | * | /// @notice Subtract two LeftRight-encoded words; revert on overflow or underflow. 167 | * | /// @param x The minuend 168 | | /// @param y The subtrahend 169 | | /// @return z The difference `x - y` 170 | | function sub( 171 | | LeftRightUnsigned x, 172 | | LeftRightUnsigned y 173 | | ) internal pure returns (LeftRightUnsigned z) { 174 | | unchecked { 175 | | // subtracting leftRight packed uint128's is same as just subtracting the values explictily 176 | | // given that we check for underflows of the left and right values 177 | | z = LeftRightUnsigned.wrap(LeftRightUnsigned.unwrap(x) - LeftRightUnsigned.unwrap(y)); 178 | | 179 | | // on underflow z will be greater than either x or y 180 | | // type cast z to uint128 to isolate the right slot and if it's higher than a value that was subtracted from (x) 181 | | // then an underflow has occured 182 | | if ( 183 | | LeftRightUnsigned.unwrap(z) > LeftRightUnsigned.unwrap(x) || 184 | | (uint128(LeftRightUnsigned.unwrap(z)) > uint128(LeftRightUnsigned.unwrap(x))) 185 | | ) revert Errors.UnderOverFlow(); 186 | | } 187 | | } 188 | | 189 | | /// @notice Add two LeftRight-encoded words; revert on overflow or underflow. 190 | * | /// @param x The augend 191 | | /// @param y The addend 192 | | /// @return z The sum `x + y` 193 | | function add(LeftRightUnsigned x, LeftRightSigned y) internal pure returns (LeftRightSigned z) { 194 | | unchecked { 195 | | int256 left = int256(uint256(x.leftSlot())) + y.leftSlot(); 196 | * | int128 left128 = int128(left); 197 | | 198 | * | if (left128 != left) revert Errors.UnderOverFlow(); 199 | | 200 | * | int256 right = int256(uint256(x.rightSlot())) + y.rightSlot(); 201 | * | int128 right128 = int128(right); 202 | | 203 | * | if (right128 != right) revert Errors.UnderOverFlow(); 204 | | 205 | * | return z.toRightSlot(right128).toLeftSlot(left128); 206 | | } 207 | | } 208 | | 209 | * | /// @notice Add two LeftRight-encoded words; revert on overflow or underflow. 210 | * | /// @param x The augend 211 | * | /// @param y The addend 212 | * | /// @return z The sum `x + y` 213 | * | function add(LeftRightSigned x, LeftRightSigned y) internal pure returns (LeftRightSigned z) { 214 | | unchecked { 215 | * | int256 left256 = int256(x.leftSlot()) + y.leftSlot(); 216 | * | int128 left128 = int128(left256); 217 | | 218 | * | int256 right256 = int256(x.rightSlot()) + y.rightSlot(); 219 | * | int128 right128 = int128(right256); 220 | | 221 | * | if (left128 != left256 || right128 != right256) revert Errors.UnderOverFlow(); 222 | | 223 | * | return z.toRightSlot(right128).toLeftSlot(left128); 224 | | } 225 | | } 226 | | 227 | | /// @notice Subtract two LeftRight-encoded words; revert on overflow or underflow. 228 | | /// @param x The minuend 229 | | /// @param y The subtrahend 230 | | /// @return z The difference `x - y` 231 | | function sub(LeftRightSigned x, LeftRightSigned y) internal pure returns (LeftRightSigned z) { 232 | | unchecked { 233 | | int256 left256 = int256(x.leftSlot()) - y.leftSlot(); 234 | | int128 left128 = int128(left256); 235 | | 236 | | int256 right256 = int256(x.rightSlot()) - y.rightSlot(); 237 | | int128 right128 = int128(right256); 238 | | 239 | | if (left128 != left256 || right128 != right256) revert Errors.UnderOverFlow(); 240 | | 241 | | return z.toRightSlot(right128).toLeftSlot(left128); 242 | | } 243 | | } 244 | | 245 | | /// @notice Subtract two LeftRight-encoded words; revert on overflow or underflow. 246 | | /// @notice For each slot, rectify difference `x - y` to 0 if negative. 247 | | /// @param x The minuend 248 | | /// @param y The subtrahend 249 | | /// @return z The difference `x - y` 250 | * | function subRect( 251 | | LeftRightSigned x, 252 | | LeftRightSigned y 253 | | ) internal pure returns (LeftRightSigned z) { 254 | | unchecked { 255 | | int256 left256 = int256(x.leftSlot()) - y.leftSlot(); 256 | | int128 left128 = int128(left256); 257 | | 258 | | int256 right256 = int256(x.rightSlot()) - y.rightSlot(); 259 | | int128 right128 = int128(right256); 260 | | 261 | | if (left128 != left256 || right128 != right256) revert Errors.UnderOverFlow(); 262 | | 263 | * | return 264 | * | z.toRightSlot(int128(Math.max(right128, 0))).toLeftSlot( 265 | | int128(Math.max(left128, 0)) 266 | | ); 267 | | } 268 | | } 269 | | 270 | | /// @notice Adds two sets of LeftRight-encoded words, freezing both right slots if either overflows, and vice versa. 271 | | /// @dev Used for linked accumulators, so if the accumulator for one side overflows for a token, both cease to accumulate. 272 | | /// @param x The first augend 273 | | /// @param dx The addend for `x` 274 | | /// @param y The second augend 275 | | /// @param dy The addend for `y` 276 | | /// @return The sum `x + dx` 277 | | /// @return The sum `y + dy` 278 | | function addCapped( 279 | | LeftRightUnsigned x, 280 | | LeftRightUnsigned dx, 281 | | LeftRightUnsigned y, 282 | | LeftRightUnsigned dy 283 | | ) internal pure returns (LeftRightUnsigned, LeftRightUnsigned) { 284 | | uint128 z_xR = (uint256(x.rightSlot()) + dx.rightSlot()).toUint128Capped(); 285 | | uint128 z_xL = (uint256(x.leftSlot()) + dx.leftSlot()).toUint128Capped(); 286 | | uint128 z_yR = (uint256(y.rightSlot()) + dy.rightSlot()).toUint128Capped(); 287 | | uint128 z_yL = (uint256(y.leftSlot()) + dy.leftSlot()).toUint128Capped(); 288 | | 289 | | bool r_Enabled = !(z_xR == type(uint128).max || z_yR == type(uint128).max); 290 | | bool l_Enabled = !(z_xL == type(uint128).max || z_yL == type(uint128).max); 291 | | 292 | | return ( 293 | | LeftRightUnsigned.wrap(0).toRightSlot(r_Enabled ? z_xR : x.rightSlot()).toLeftSlot( 294 | | l_Enabled ? z_xL : x.leftSlot() 295 | | ), 296 | | LeftRightUnsigned.wrap(0).toRightSlot(r_Enabled ? z_yR : y.rightSlot()).toLeftSlot( 297 | | l_Enabled ? z_yL : y.leftSlot() 298 | | ) 299 | | ); 300 | | } 301 | | } 302 | |
/home/xshadow/panoptic-v1-core-private/contracts/types/LiquidityChunk.sol 1 | | // SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-2.0-or-later 2 | | pragma solidity ^0.8.0; 3 | | 4 | | // Custom types 5 | | import {TokenId} from "@types/TokenId.sol"; 6 | | 7 | | type LiquidityChunk is uint256; 8 | | using LiquidityChunkLibrary for LiquidityChunk global; 9 | | 10 | | /// @title A Panoptic Liquidity Chunk. Tracks Tick Range and Liquidity Information for a "chunk." Used to track movement of chunks. 11 | | /// @author Axicon Labs Limited 12 | | /// 13 | | /// @notice A liquidity chunk is an amount of `liquidity` (an amount of WETH, e.g.) deployed between two ticks: `tickLower` and `tickUpper` 14 | | /// into a concentrated liquidity AMM. 15 | | // 16 | | // liquidity 17 | | // ▲ liquidity chunk 18 | | // │ │ 19 | * | // │ ┌───▼────┐ ▲ 20 | | // │ │ │ │ liquidity/size 21 | * | // Other AMM │ ┌─┴────────┴─┐ ▼ of chunk 22 | | // liquidity ───┼──┼─► │ 23 | | // │ │ │ 24 | | // └──┴─▲────────▲─┴──► price ticks 25 | | // │ │ 26 | * | // │ │ 27 | | // tickLower │ 28 | | // tickUpper 29 | | // 30 | * | /// @notice Track Tick Range Information. Lower and Upper ticks including the liquidity deployed within that range. 31 | * | /// @notice This is used to track information about a leg in the Option Position identified by `TokenId.sol`. 32 | * | /// @notice We pack this tick range info into a uint256. 33 | | // 34 | | // PACKING RULES FOR A LIQUIDITYCHUNK: 35 | | // ================================================================================================= 36 | | // From the LSB to the MSB: 37 | | // (1) Liquidity 128bits : The liquidity within the chunk (uint128). 38 | | // ( ) (Zero-bits) 80bits : Zero-bits to match a total uint256. 39 | | // (2) tick Upper 24bits : The upper tick of the chunk (int24). 40 | | // (3) tick Lower 24bits : The lower tick of the chunk (int24). 41 | | // Total 256bits : Total bits used by a chunk. 42 | | // =============================================================================================== 43 | | // 44 | | // The bit pattern is therefore: 45 | | // 46 | | // (3) (2) ( ) (1) 47 | * | // <-- 24 bits --> <-- 24 bits --> <-- 80 bits --> <-- 128 bits --> 48 | | // tickLower tickUpper Zeros Liquidity 49 | | // 50 | * | // <--- most significant bit least significant bit ---> 51 | | // 52 | * | library LiquidityChunkLibrary { 53 | * | /// @notice AND mask to strip the `tickLower` value from a packed LiquidityChunk. 54 | * | uint256 internal constant CLEAR_TL_MASK = 55 | * | 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF; 56 | | 57 | * | /// @notice AND mask to strip the `tickUpper` value from a packed LiquidityChunk. 58 | * | uint256 internal constant CLEAR_TU_MASK = 59 | * | 0xFFFFFF000000FFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF; 60 | | 61 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 62 | | ENCODING 63 | * | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 64 | | 65 | | /// @notice Create a new `LiquidityChunk` given by its bounding ticks and its liquidity. 66 | * | /// @param _tickLower The lower tick of the chunk 67 | * | /// @param _tickUpper The upper tick of the chunk 68 | * | /// @param amount The amount of liquidity to add to the chunk 69 | * | /// @return The new chunk with the given liquidity and tick range 70 | * | function createChunk( 71 | | int24 _tickLower, 72 | | int24 _tickUpper, 73 | | uint128 amount 74 | * | ) internal pure returns (LiquidityChunk) { 75 | * | unchecked { 76 | * | return 77 | * | LiquidityChunk.wrap( 78 | * | (uint256(uint24(_tickLower)) << 232) + 79 | * | (uint256(uint24(_tickUpper)) << 208) + 80 | * | uint256(amount) 81 | | ); 82 | | } 83 | | } 84 | | 85 | | /// @notice Add liquidity to `self`. 86 | * | /// @param self The LiquidityChunk to add liquidity to 87 | | /// @param amount The amount of liquidity to add to `self` 88 | | /// @return `self` with added liquidity `amount` 89 | * | function addLiquidity( 90 | | LiquidityChunk self, 91 | * | uint128 amount 92 | * | ) internal pure returns (LiquidityChunk) { 93 | * | unchecked { 94 | * | return LiquidityChunk.wrap(LiquidityChunk.unwrap(self) + amount); 95 | | } 96 | * | } 97 | | 98 | | /// @notice Add the lower tick to `self`. 99 | | /// @param self The LiquidityChunk to add the lower tick to 100 | | /// @param _tickLower The lower tick to add to `self` 101 | | /// @return `self` with added lower tick `_tickLower` 102 | * | function addTickLower( 103 | | LiquidityChunk self, 104 | | int24 _tickLower 105 | | ) internal pure returns (LiquidityChunk) { 106 | | unchecked { 107 | | return 108 | | LiquidityChunk.wrap( 109 | * | LiquidityChunk.unwrap(self) + (uint256(uint24(_tickLower)) << 232) 110 | | ); 111 | | } 112 | | } 113 | | 114 | | /// @notice Add the upper tick to `self`. 115 | | /// @param self The LiquidityChunk to add the upper tick to 116 | | /// @param _tickUpper The upper tick to add to `self` 117 | | /// @return `self` with added upper tick `_tickUpper` 118 | * | function addTickUpper( 119 | | LiquidityChunk self, 120 | | int24 _tickUpper 121 | | ) internal pure returns (LiquidityChunk) { 122 | | unchecked { 123 | | // convert tick upper to uint24 as explicit conversion from int24 to uint256 is not allowed 124 | | return 125 | | LiquidityChunk.wrap( 126 | * | LiquidityChunk.unwrap(self) + ((uint256(uint24(_tickUpper))) << 208) 127 | | ); 128 | | } 129 | | } 130 | | 131 | | /// @notice Overwrites the lower tick on `self`. 132 | | /// @param self The LiquidityChunk to overwrite the lower tick on 133 | | /// @param _tickLower The lower tick to overwrite `self` with 134 | | /// @return `self` with `_tickLower` as the new lower tick 135 | * | function updateTickLower( 136 | | LiquidityChunk self, 137 | | int24 _tickLower 138 | * | ) internal pure returns (LiquidityChunk) { 139 | | unchecked { 140 | | return 141 | * | LiquidityChunk.wrap(LiquidityChunk.unwrap(self) & CLEAR_TL_MASK).addTickLower( 142 | * | _tickLower 143 | | ); 144 | | } 145 | | } 146 | | 147 | | /// @notice Overwrites the upper tick on `self`. 148 | | /// @param self The LiquidityChunk to overwrite the upper tick on 149 | | /// @param _tickUpper The upper tick to overwrite `self` with 150 | | /// @return `self` with `_tickUpper` as the new upper tick 151 | * | function updateTickUpper( 152 | | LiquidityChunk self, 153 | | int24 _tickUpper 154 | * | ) internal pure returns (LiquidityChunk) { 155 | | unchecked { 156 | | // convert tick upper to uint24 as explicit conversion from int24 to uint256 is not allowed 157 | | return 158 | * | LiquidityChunk.wrap(LiquidityChunk.unwrap(self) & CLEAR_TU_MASK).addTickUpper( 159 | * | _tickUpper 160 | | ); 161 | | } 162 | | } 163 | | 164 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 165 | | DECODING 166 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 167 | | 168 | | /// @notice Get the lower tick of `self`. 169 | | /// @param self The LiquidityChunk to get the lower tick from 170 | | /// @return The lower tick of `self` 171 | * | function tickLower(LiquidityChunk self) internal pure returns (int24) { 172 | | unchecked { 173 | * | return int24(int256(LiquidityChunk.unwrap(self) >> 232)); 174 | | } 175 | | } 176 | | 177 | | /// @notice Get the upper tick of `self`. 178 | | /// @param self The LiquidityChunk to get the upper tick from 179 | | /// @return The upper tick of `self` 180 | * | function tickUpper(LiquidityChunk self) internal pure returns (int24) { 181 | | unchecked { 182 | * | return int24(int256(LiquidityChunk.unwrap(self) >> 208)); 183 | | } 184 | | } 185 | | 186 | | /// @notice Get the amount of liquidity/size of `self`. 187 | | /// @param self The LiquidityChunk to get the liquidity from 188 | | /// @return The liquidity of `self` 189 | | function liquidity(LiquidityChunk self) internal pure returns (uint128) { 190 | | unchecked { 191 | | return uint128(LiquidityChunk.unwrap(self)); 192 | | } 193 | | } 194 | | } 195 | |
/home/xshadow/panoptic-v1-core-private/contracts/types/Pointer.sol 1 | | // SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-2.0-or-later 2 | | pragma solidity ^0.8.0; 3 | | 4 | | // Solady libraries 5 | | import {LibZip} from "solady/utils/LibZip.sol"; 6 | | 7 | | type Pointer is uint256; 8 | | using PointerLibrary for Pointer global; 9 | | 10 | | /// @notice Data type that represents a pointer to a slice of contract code at an address. 11 | | /// @author Axicon Labs Limited 12 | | library PointerLibrary { 13 | | /// @notice Create a pointer to a slice of contract code at an address. 14 | | /// @param _location The address of the contract code 15 | | /// @param _start The starting position of the slice 16 | | /// @param _size The size of the slice 17 | | function createPointer( 18 | | address _location, 19 | | uint48 _start, 20 | * | uint48 _size 21 | | ) internal pure returns (Pointer) { 22 | | return 23 | | Pointer.wrap((uint256(_size) << 208) + (uint256(_start) << 160) + uint160(_location)); 24 | | } 25 | * | 26 | | /// @notice Get the address of the pointed contract code from a pointer. 27 | | /// @param self The pointer to the slice of contract code 28 | * | /// @return The address of the contract code 29 | | function location(Pointer self) internal pure returns (address) { 30 | | return address(uint160(Pointer.unwrap(self))); 31 | | } 32 | | 33 | | /// @notice Get the starting position of the pointed slice from a pointer. 34 | | /// @param self The pointer to the section of contract code 35 | | /// @return The starting position of the slice 36 | * | function start(Pointer self) internal pure returns (uint48) { 37 | * | return uint48(Pointer.unwrap(self) >> 160); 38 | * | } 39 | | 40 | * | /// @notice Get the size of the pointed slice from a pointer. 41 | * | /// @param self The pointer to the slice of contract code 42 | | /// @return The size of the slice 43 | * | function size(Pointer self) internal pure returns (uint48) { 44 | | return uint48(Pointer.unwrap(self) >> 208); 45 | | } 46 | | 47 | * | /// @notice Get the data of the pointed slice from a pointer as a bytearray. 48 | * | /// @param self The pointer to the slice of contract code 49 | * | /// @return The pointed slice as a bytearray 50 | * | function data(Pointer self) internal view returns (bytes memory) { 51 | * | address _location = self.location(); 52 | * | uint256 _start = self.start(); 53 | * | uint256 _size = self.size(); 54 | | 55 | * | bytes memory pointerData = new bytes(_size); 56 | | 57 | * | assembly ("memory-safe") { 58 | * | extcodecopy(_location, add(pointerData, 0x20), _start, _size) 59 | * | } 60 | * | 61 | * | return pointerData; 62 | | } 63 | | 64 | * | /// @notice Get the data of the pointed slice from a pointer as a string. 65 | * | /// @param self The pointer to the slice of contract code 66 | * | /// @return The pointed slice as a string 67 | * | function dataStr(Pointer self) internal view returns (string memory) { 68 | * | return string(data(self)); 69 | | } 70 | | 71 | * | /// @notice Returns the result of decompressing the pointed slice of data with LZ-77 interpreted as a UTF-8-encoded string. 72 | * | /// @param self The pointer to the slice of contract code 73 | * | /// @return The LZ-77 decompressed data as a string 74 | * | function decompressedDataStr(Pointer self) internal view returns (string memory) { 75 | * | return string(LibZip.flzDecompress(data(self))); 76 | | } 77 | | } 78 | * |
/home/xshadow/panoptic-v1-core-private/contracts/types/TokenId.sol 1 | | // SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-2.0-or-later 2 | | pragma solidity ^0.8.0; 3 | | 4 | | // Libraries 5 | | import {Constants} from "@libraries/Constants.sol"; 6 | | import {Errors} from "@libraries/Errors.sol"; 7 | | import {PanopticMath} from "@libraries/PanopticMath.sol"; 8 | | 9 | | type TokenId is uint256; 10 | | using TokenIdLibrary for TokenId global; 11 | | 12 | | /// @title Panoptic's tokenId: the fundamental options position. 13 | | /// @author Axicon Labs Limited 14 | | /// @notice This is the token ID used in the ERC1155 representation of the option position in the SFPM. 15 | | /// @notice The SFPM "overloads" the ERC1155 `id` by storing all option information in said `id`. 16 | * | /// @notice Contains methods for packing and unpacking a Panoptic options position into a uint256 bit pattern. 17 | * | // PACKING RULES FOR A TOKENID: 18 | * | // this is how the token Id is packed into its bit-constituents containing position information. 19 | * | // the following is a diagram to be read top-down in a little endian format 20 | * | // (so (1) below occupies the first 64 least significant bits, e.g.): 21 | | // From the LSB to the MSB: 22 | | // ===== 1 time (same for all legs) ============================================================== 23 | | // Property Size Offset Comment 24 | | // (0) univ3pool 48bits 0bits : first 6 bytes of the Uniswap v3 pool address (first 48 bits; little-endian), plus a pseudorandom number in the event of a collision 25 | | // (1) tickSpacing 16bits 48bits : tickSpacing for the univ3pool. Up to 16 bits 26 | | // ===== 4 times (one for each leg) ============================================================== 27 | | // (2) asset 1bit 0bits : Specifies the asset (0: token0, 1: token1) 28 | | // (3) optionRatio 7bits 1bits : number of contracts per leg 29 | | // (4) isLong 1bit 8bits : long==1 means liquidity is removed, long==0 -> liquidity is added 30 | | // (5) tokenType 1bit 9bits : put/call: which token is moved when deployed (0 -> token0, 1 -> token1) 31 | | // (6) riskPartner 2bits 10bits : normally its own index. Partner in defined risk position otherwise 32 | | // (7) strike 24bits 12bits : strike price; defined as (tickUpper + tickLower) / 2 33 | | // (8) width 12bits 36bits : width; defined as (tickUpper - tickLower) / tickSpacing 34 | | // Total 48bits : Each leg takes up this many bits 35 | | // =============================================================================================== 36 | | // 37 | | // The bit pattern is therefore, in general: 38 | | // 39 | | // (strike price tick of the 3rd leg) 40 | | // | (width of the 2nd leg) 41 | | // | | 42 | | // (8)(7)(6)(5)(4)(3)(2) (8)(7)(6)(5)(4)(3)(2) (8)(7)(6)(5)(4)(3)(2) (8)(7)(6)(5)(4)(3)(2) (1) (0) 43 | | // <---- 48 bits ----> <---- 48 bits ----> <---- 48 bits ----> <---- 48 bits ----> <- 16 bits -> <- 48 bits -> 44 | | // Leg 4 Leg 3 Leg 2 Leg 1 tickSpacing Univ3 Pool Address 45 | | // 46 | | // <--- most significant bit least significant bit ---> 47 | | // 48 | | // Some rules of how legs behave (we enforce these in a `validate()` function): 49 | | // - a leg is inactive if it's not part of the position. Technically it means that all bits are zero. 50 | | // - a leg is active if it has an optionRatio > 0 since this must always be set for an active leg. 51 | | // - if a leg is active (e.g. leg 1) there can be no gaps in other legs meaning: if leg 1 is active then leg 3 cannot be active if leg 2 is inactive. 52 | | // 53 | | // Examples: 54 | | // We can think of the bit pattern as an array starting at bit index 0 going to bit index 255 (so 256 total bits) 55 | | // We also refer to the legs via their index, so leg number 2 has leg index 1 (legIndex) (counting from zero), and in general leg number N has leg index N-1. 56 | | // - the underlying strike price of the 2nd leg (leg index = 1) in this option position starts at bit index (64 + 12 + 48 * (leg index=1))=123 57 | | // - the tokenType of the 4th leg in this option position starts at bit index 64+9+48*3=217 58 | | // - the Uniswap v3 pool id starts at bit index 0 and ends at bit index 63 (and thus takes up 64 bits). 59 | | // - the width of the 3rd leg in this option position starts at bit index 64+36+48*2=196 60 | | library TokenIdLibrary { 61 | | /// @notice AND mask to extract all `isLong` bits for each leg from a TokenId. 62 | | uint256 internal constant LONG_MASK = 63 | | 0x100_000000000100_000000000100_000000000100_0000000000000000; 64 | | 65 | | /// @notice AND mask to clear `poolId` from a TokenId. 66 | | uint256 internal constant CLEAR_POOLID_MASK = 67 | | 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF_0000000000000000; 68 | | 69 | | /// @notice AND mask to clear all bits except for the option ratios of the legs. 70 | | uint256 internal constant OPTION_RATIO_MASK = 71 | | 0x0000000000FE_0000000000FE_0000000000FE_0000000000FE_0000000000000000; 72 | | 73 | | /// @notice AND mask to clear all bits except for the components of the chunk key (strike, width, tokenType) for each leg. 74 | | uint256 internal constant CHUNK_MASK = 75 | | 0xFFFFFFFFF200_FFFFFFFFF200_FFFFFFFFF200_FFFFFFFFF200_0000000000000000; 76 | | 77 | | /// @notice AND mask to cut a sign-extended int256 back to an int24. 78 | * | int256 internal constant BITMASK_INT24 = 0xFFFFFF; 79 | | 80 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 81 | | DECODING 82 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 83 | | 84 | | /// @notice The full poolId (Uniswap pool identifier + pool pattern) of this option position. 85 | | /// @param self The TokenId to extract `poolId` from 86 | | /// @return The `poolId` (Panoptic's pool fingerprint, contains the whole 64 bit sequence with the tickSpacing) of the Uniswap V3 pool 87 | * | function poolId(TokenId self) internal pure returns (uint64) { 88 | | unchecked { 89 | * | return uint64(TokenId.unwrap(self)); 90 | | } 91 | | } 92 | | 93 | | /// @notice The tickSpacing of this option position. 94 | | /// @param self The TokenId to extract `tickSpacing` from 95 | | /// @return The `tickSpacing` of the Uniswap v3 pool 96 | | function tickSpacing(TokenId self) internal pure returns (int24) { 97 | | unchecked { 98 | * | return int24(uint24((TokenId.unwrap(self) >> 48) % 2 ** 16)); 99 | | } 100 | | } 101 | | 102 | | /// @notice Get the asset basis for this TokenId. 103 | | /// @dev Which token is the asset - can be token0 (return 0) or token1 (return 1). 104 | | /// @param self The TokenId to extract `asset` from 105 | | /// @param legIndex The leg index of this position (in {0,1,2,3}) to extract `asset` from 106 | | /// @dev Occupies the leftmost bit of the optionRatio 4 bits slot. 107 | | /// @return 0 if asset is token0, 1 if asset is token1 108 | * | function asset(TokenId self, uint256 legIndex) internal pure returns (uint256) { 109 | | unchecked { 110 | * | return uint256((TokenId.unwrap(self) >> (64 + legIndex * 48)) % 2); 111 | | } 112 | | } 113 | | 114 | | /// @notice Get the number of contracts multiplier for leg `legIndex`. 115 | | /// @param self The TokenId to extract `optionRatio` at `legIndex` from 116 | | /// @param legIndex The leg index of this position (in {0,1,2,3}) 117 | | /// @return The number of contracts multiplier for leg `legIndex` 118 | * | function optionRatio(TokenId self, uint256 legIndex) internal pure returns (uint256) { 119 | | unchecked { 120 | * | return uint256((TokenId.unwrap(self) >> (64 + legIndex * 48 + 1)) % 128); 121 | | } 122 | | } 123 | | 124 | | /// @notice Return 1 if the nth leg (leg index `legIndex`) is a long position. 125 | | /// @param self The TokenId to extract `isLong` at `legIndex` from 126 | | /// @param legIndex The leg index of this position (in {0,1,2,3}) 127 | | /// @return 1 if long; 0 if not long 128 | * | function isLong(TokenId self, uint256 legIndex) internal pure returns (uint256) { 129 | | unchecked { 130 | * | return uint256((TokenId.unwrap(self) >> (64 + legIndex * 48 + 8)) % 2); 131 | | } 132 | | } 133 | | 134 | | /// @notice Get the type of token moved for a given leg (implies a call or put). Either Token0 or Token1. 135 | | /// @param self The TokenId to extract `tokenType` at `legIndex` from 136 | | /// @param legIndex The leg index of this position (in {0,1,2,3}) 137 | | /// @return 1 if the token moved is token1 or 0 if the token moved is token0 138 | * | function tokenType(TokenId self, uint256 legIndex) internal pure returns (uint256) { 139 | | unchecked { 140 | * | return uint256((TokenId.unwrap(self) >> (64 + legIndex * 48 + 9)) % 2); 141 | | } 142 | | } 143 | | 144 | | /// @notice Get the associated risk partner of the leg index (generally another leg index in the position if enabled or the same leg index if no partner). 145 | | /// @param self The TokenId to extract `riskPartner` at `legIndex` from 146 | | /// @param legIndex The leg index of this position (in {0,1,2,3}) 147 | | /// @return The leg index of `legIndex`'s risk partner 148 | * | function riskPartner(TokenId self, uint256 legIndex) internal pure returns (uint256) { 149 | | unchecked { 150 | * | return uint256((TokenId.unwrap(self) >> (64 + legIndex * 48 + 10)) % 4); 151 | | } 152 | | } 153 | | 154 | | /// @notice Get the strike price tick of the nth leg (with index `legIndex`). 155 | | /// @param self The TokenId to extract `strike` at `legIndex` from 156 | | /// @param legIndex the leg index of this position (in {0,1,2,3}) 157 | | /// @return The strike price (the underlying price of the leg) 158 | * | function strike(TokenId self, uint256 legIndex) internal pure returns (int24) { 159 | | unchecked { 160 | * | return int24(int256(TokenId.unwrap(self) >> (64 + legIndex * 48 + 12))); 161 | | } 162 | | } 163 | | 164 | | /// @notice Get the width of the nth leg (index `legIndex`). This is half the tick-range covered by the leg (tickUpper - tickLower)/2. 165 | | /// @dev Return as int24 to be compatible with the strike tick format (they naturally go together). 166 | | /// @param self The TokenId to extract `width` at `legIndex` from 167 | | /// @param legIndex the leg index of this position (in {0,1,2,3}) 168 | | /// @return The width of the position 169 | * | function width(TokenId self, uint256 legIndex) internal pure returns (int24) { 170 | | unchecked { 171 | * | return int24(int256((TokenId.unwrap(self) >> (64 + legIndex * 48 + 36)) % 4096)); 172 | | } // "% 4096" = take last (2 ** 12 = 4096) 12 bits 173 | | } 174 | | 175 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 176 | | ENCODING 177 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 178 | | 179 | | /// @notice Add the Uniswap V3 Pool pointed to by this option position (contains the entropy and tickSpacing). 180 | | /// @param self The TokenId to add `_poolId` to 181 | | /// @param _poolId The PoolID to add to `self` 182 | | /// @return `self` with `_poolId` added to the PoolID slot 183 | | function addPoolId(TokenId self, uint64 _poolId) internal pure returns (TokenId) { 184 | | unchecked { 185 | | return TokenId.wrap(TokenId.unwrap(self) + _poolId); 186 | | } 187 | | } 188 | | 189 | | /// @notice Add the `tickSpacing` to the PoolID for `self`. 190 | | /// @param self The TokenId to add `_tickSpacing` to 191 | | /// @param _tickSpacing The tickSpacing to add to `self` 192 | | /// @return `self` with `_tickSpacing` added to the TickSpacing slot in the PoolID 193 | | function addTickSpacing(TokenId self, int24 _tickSpacing) internal pure returns (TokenId) { 194 | | unchecked { 195 | | return TokenId.wrap(TokenId.unwrap(self) + (uint256(uint24(_tickSpacing)) << 48)); 196 | | } 197 | | } 198 | | 199 | | /// @notice Add the asset basis for this position. 200 | | /// @param self The TokenId to add `_asset` to 201 | | /// @param _asset The asset to add to the Asset slot in `self` for `legIndex` 202 | | /// @param legIndex The leg index of this position (in {0,1,2,3}) 203 | | /// @dev Occupies the leftmost bit of the optionRatio 4 bits slot 204 | | /// @return `self` with `_asset` added to the Asset slot 205 | * | function addAsset( 206 | | TokenId self, 207 | | uint256 _asset, 208 | | uint256 legIndex 209 | * | ) internal pure returns (TokenId) { 210 | | unchecked { 211 | | return 212 | * | TokenId.wrap(TokenId.unwrap(self) + (uint256(_asset % 2) << (64 + legIndex * 48))); 213 | | } 214 | | } 215 | | 216 | | /// @notice Add the number of contracts multiplier to leg index `legIndex`. 217 | | /// @param self The TokenId to add `_optionRatio` to 218 | | /// @param _optionRatio The number of contracts multiplier to add to the OptionRatio slot in `self` for LegIndex 219 | | /// @param legIndex The leg index of the position (in {0,1,2,3}) 220 | | /// @return `self` with `_optionRatio` added to the OptionRatio slot for `legIndex` 221 | * | function addOptionRatio( 222 | | TokenId self, 223 | | uint256 _optionRatio, 224 | | uint256 legIndex 225 | * | ) internal pure returns (TokenId) { 226 | | unchecked { 227 | * | return 228 | | TokenId.wrap( 229 | * | TokenId.unwrap(self) + (uint256(_optionRatio % 128) << (64 + legIndex * 48 + 1)) 230 | | ); 231 | | } 232 | | } 233 | | 234 | | /// @notice Add "isLong" parameter indicating whether a leg is long (isLong=1) or short (isLong=0). 235 | | /// @notice returns 1 if the nth leg (leg index n-1) is a long position. 236 | | /// @param self The TokenId to add `_isLong` to 237 | | /// @param _isLong The isLong parameter to add to the IsLong slot in `self` for `legIndex` 238 | | /// @param legIndex the leg index of this position (in {0,1,2,3}) 239 | | /// @return `self` with `_isLong` added to the IsLong slot for `legIndex` 240 | * | function addIsLong( 241 | | TokenId self, 242 | | uint256 _isLong, 243 | | uint256 legIndex 244 | * | ) internal pure returns (TokenId) { 245 | | unchecked { 246 | * | return TokenId.wrap(TokenId.unwrap(self) + ((_isLong % 2) << (64 + legIndex * 48 + 8))); 247 | | } 248 | | } 249 | | 250 | | /// @notice Add the type of token moved for a given leg (implies a call or put). Either Token0 or Token1. 251 | | /// @param self The TokenId to add `_tokenType` to 252 | | /// @param _tokenType The tokenType to add to the TokenType slot in `self` for `legIndex` 253 | | /// @param legIndex the leg index of this position (in {0,1,2,3}) 254 | | /// @return `self` with `_tokenType` added to the TokenType slot for `legIndex` 255 | * | function addTokenType( 256 | | TokenId self, 257 | | uint256 _tokenType, 258 | | uint256 legIndex 259 | * | ) internal pure returns (TokenId) { 260 | | unchecked { 261 | | return 262 | | TokenId.wrap( 263 | * | TokenId.unwrap(self) + (uint256(_tokenType % 2) << (64 + legIndex * 48 + 9)) 264 | | ); 265 | | } 266 | | } 267 | | 268 | | /// @notice Add the associated risk partner of the leg index (generally another leg in the overall position). 269 | | /// @param self The TokenId to add `_riskPartner` to 270 | | /// @param _riskPartner The riskPartner to add to the RiskPartner slot in `self` for `legIndex` 271 | | /// @param legIndex the leg index of this position (in {0,1,2,3}) 272 | | /// @return `self` with `_riskPartner` added to the RiskPartner slot for `legIndex` 273 | * | function addRiskPartner( 274 | | TokenId self, 275 | | uint256 _riskPartner, 276 | | uint256 legIndex 277 | * | ) internal pure returns (TokenId) { 278 | | unchecked { 279 | | return 280 | | TokenId.wrap( 281 | * | TokenId.unwrap(self) + (uint256(_riskPartner % 4) << (64 + legIndex * 48 + 10)) 282 | | ); 283 | | } 284 | | } 285 | | 286 | | /// @notice Add the strike price tick of the nth leg (index `legIndex`). 287 | | /// @param self The TokenId to add `_strike` to 288 | | /// @param _strike The strike price tick to add to the Strike slot in `self` for `legIndex` 289 | | /// @param legIndex the leg index of this position (in {0,1,2,3}) 290 | | /// @return `self` with `_strike` added to the Strike slot for `legIndex` 291 | | function addStrike( 292 | | TokenId self, 293 | | int24 _strike, 294 | | uint256 legIndex 295 | | ) internal pure returns (TokenId) { 296 | | unchecked { 297 | | return 298 | | TokenId.wrap( 299 | * | TokenId.unwrap(self) + 300 | * | uint256((int256(_strike) & BITMASK_INT24) << (64 + legIndex * 48 + 12)) 301 | | ); 302 | | } 303 | | } 304 | | 305 | | /// @notice Add the width of the nth leg (index `legIndex`). 306 | | /// @param self The TokenId to add `_width` to 307 | | /// @param _width The width to add to the Width slot in `self` for `legIndex` 308 | | /// @param legIndex the leg index of this position (in {0,1,2,3}) 309 | | /// @return `self` with `_width` added to the Width slot for `legIndex` 310 | | function addWidth( 311 | | TokenId self, 312 | | int24 _width, 313 | | uint256 legIndex 314 | | ) internal pure returns (TokenId) { 315 | | // % 4096 -> take 12 bits from the incoming 24 bits (there's no uint12) 316 | | unchecked { 317 | | return 318 | | TokenId.wrap( 319 | * | TokenId.unwrap(self) + 320 | * | (uint256(uint24(_width) % 4096) << (64 + legIndex * 48 + 36)) 321 | | ); 322 | | } 323 | | } 324 | | 325 | | /// @notice Add a leg to a TokenId. 326 | | /// @param self The tokenId in the SFPM representing an option position 327 | | /// @param legIndex The leg index of this position (in {0,1,2,3}) to add 328 | | /// @param _optionRatio The relative size of the leg 329 | | /// @param _asset The asset of the leg 330 | | /// @param _isLong Whether the leg is long 331 | | /// @param _tokenType The type of token moved for the leg 332 | | /// @param _riskPartner The associated risk partner of the leg 333 | | /// @param _strike The strike price tick of the leg 334 | | /// @param _width The width of the leg 335 | | /// @return tokenId The tokenId with the leg added 336 | * | function addLeg( 337 | | TokenId self, 338 | | uint256 legIndex, 339 | | uint256 _optionRatio, 340 | | uint256 _asset, 341 | | uint256 _isLong, 342 | | uint256 _tokenType, 343 | | uint256 _riskPartner, 344 | | int24 _strike, 345 | | int24 _width 346 | * | ) internal pure returns (TokenId tokenId) { 347 | * | tokenId = addOptionRatio(self, _optionRatio, legIndex); 348 | * | tokenId = addAsset(tokenId, _asset, legIndex); 349 | * | tokenId = addIsLong(tokenId, _isLong, legIndex); 350 | * | tokenId = addTokenType(tokenId, _tokenType, legIndex); 351 | * | tokenId = addRiskPartner(tokenId, _riskPartner, legIndex); 352 | * | tokenId = addStrike(tokenId, _strike, legIndex); 353 | * | tokenId = addWidth(tokenId, _width, legIndex); 354 | | } 355 | | 356 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 357 | | HELPERS 358 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 359 | | 360 | | /// @notice Flip all the `isLong` positions in the legs in the `tokenId` option position. 361 | | /// @dev Uses XOR on existing isLong bits. 362 | | /// @dev Useful when we need to take an existing tokenId but now burn it. 363 | | /// @dev The way to do this is to simply flip it to a short instead. 364 | | /// @param self The TokenId to flip isLong for on all active legs 365 | | /// @return tokenId with all `isLong` bits flipped 366 | | function flipToBurnToken(TokenId self) internal pure returns (TokenId) { 367 | | unchecked { 368 | | // NOTE: This is a hack to avoid blowing up the contract size. 369 | | // We copy the logic from the countLegs function, using it here adds 5K to the contract size with IR for some reason 370 | | // Strip all bits except for the option ratios 371 | | uint256 optionRatios = TokenId.unwrap(self) & OPTION_RATIO_MASK; 372 | | 373 | | // The legs are filled in from least to most significant 374 | | // Each comparison here is to the start of the next leg's option ratio 375 | | // Since only the option ratios remain, we can be sure that no bits above the start of the inactive legs will be 1 376 | | if (optionRatios < 2 ** 64) { 377 | | optionRatios = 0; 378 | | } else if (optionRatios < 2 ** 112) { 379 | | optionRatios = 1; 380 | | } else if (optionRatios < 2 ** 160) { 381 | | optionRatios = 2; 382 | | } else if (optionRatios < 2 ** 208) { 383 | | optionRatios = 3; 384 | | } else { 385 | | optionRatios = 4; 386 | | } 387 | | 388 | | // We need to ensure that only active legs are flipped 389 | | // In order to achieve this, we shift our long bit mask to the right by (4-# active legs) 390 | | // i.e the whole mask is used to flip all legs with 4 legs, but only the first leg is flipped with 1 leg so we shift by 3 legs 391 | | // We also clear the poolId area of the mask to ensure the bits that are shifted right into the area don't flip and cause issues 392 | | return 393 | | TokenId.wrap( 394 | | TokenId.unwrap(self) ^ 395 | | ((LONG_MASK >> (48 * (4 - optionRatios))) & CLEAR_POOLID_MASK) 396 | | ); 397 | | } 398 | | } 399 | | 400 | | /// @notice Get the number of longs in this option position. 401 | | /// @notice Count the number of legs (out of a maximum of 4) that are long positions. 402 | | /// @param self The TokenId to count longs for 403 | | /// @return The number of long positions in `self` (in the range {0,...,4}) 404 | | function countLongs(TokenId self) internal pure returns (uint256) { 405 | | unchecked { 406 | | return self.isLong(0) + self.isLong(1) + self.isLong(2) + self.isLong(3); 407 | | } 408 | | } 409 | | 410 | | /// @notice Get the option position's nth leg's (index `legIndex`) tick ranges (lower, upper). 411 | | /// @dev NOTE: Does not extract liquidity which is the third piece of information in a LiquidityChunk. 412 | | /// @param self The TokenId to extract the tick range from 413 | | /// @param legIndex The leg index of the position (in {0,1,2,3}) 414 | | /// @return legLowerTick The lower tick of the leg/liquidity chunk 415 | | /// @return legUpperTick The upper tick of the leg/liquidity chunk 416 | * | function asTicks( 417 | | TokenId self, 418 | | uint256 legIndex 419 | * | ) internal pure returns (int24 legLowerTick, int24 legUpperTick) { 420 | * | (legLowerTick, legUpperTick) = PanopticMath.getTicks( 421 | * | self.strike(legIndex), 422 | * | self.width(legIndex), 423 | | self.tickSpacing() 424 | | ); 425 | | } 426 | | 427 | | /// @notice Return the number of active legs in the option position. 428 | | /// @param self The TokenId to count active legs for 429 | | /// @dev ASSUMPTION: There is at least 1 leg in this option position. 430 | | /// @dev ASSUMPTION: For any leg, the option ratio is always > 0 (the leg always has a number of contracts associated with it). 431 | | /// @return The number of active legs in `self` (in the range {0,...,4}) 432 | * | function countLegs(TokenId self) internal pure returns (uint256) { 433 | | // Strip all bits except for the option ratios 434 | * | uint256 optionRatios = TokenId.unwrap(self) & OPTION_RATIO_MASK; 435 | | 436 | | // The legs are filled in from least to most significant 437 | | // Each comparison here is to the start of the next leg's option ratio section 438 | | // Since only the option ratios remain, we can be sure that no bits above the start of the inactive legs will be 1 439 | * | if (optionRatios < 2 ** 64) { 440 | | return 0; 441 | * | } else if (optionRatios < 2 ** 112) { 442 | * | return 1; 443 | * | } else if (optionRatios < 2 ** 160) { 444 | * | return 2; 445 | * | } else if (optionRatios < 2 ** 208) { 446 | * | return 3; 447 | | } 448 | * | return 4; 449 | | } 450 | | 451 | | /// @notice Clear a leg in an option position with index `i`. 452 | | /// @dev set bits of the leg to zero. Also sets the optionRatio and asset to zero of that leg. 453 | | /// @dev NOTE: it's important that the caller fills in the leg details after. 454 | | // - optionRatio is zeroed 455 | | // - asset is zeroed 456 | | // - width is zeroed 457 | | // - strike is zeroed 458 | | // - tokenType is zeroed 459 | | // - isLong is zeroed 460 | | // - riskPartner is zeroed 461 | | /// @param self The TokenId to clear the leg from 462 | | /// @param i The leg index to reset, in {0,1,2,3} 463 | | /// @return `self` with the `i`th leg zeroed including optionRatio and asset 464 | | function clearLeg(TokenId self, uint256 i) internal pure returns (TokenId) { 465 | | if (i == 0) 466 | | return 467 | | TokenId.wrap( 468 | | TokenId.unwrap(self) & 469 | | 0xFFFFFFFFFFFF_FFFFFFFFFFFF_FFFFFFFFFFFF_000000000000_FFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF 470 | | ); 471 | | if (i == 1) 472 | | return 473 | | TokenId.wrap( 474 | | TokenId.unwrap(self) & 475 | | 0xFFFFFFFFFFFF_FFFFFFFFFFFF_000000000000_FFFFFFFFFFFF_FFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF 476 | | ); 477 | | if (i == 2) 478 | | return 479 | | TokenId.wrap( 480 | | TokenId.unwrap(self) & 481 | | 0xFFFFFFFFFFFF_000000000000_FFFFFFFFFFFF_FFFFFFFFFFFF_FFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF 482 | | ); 483 | | if (i == 3) 484 | | return 485 | | TokenId.wrap( 486 | | TokenId.unwrap(self) & 487 | | 0x000000000000_FFFFFFFFFFFF_FFFFFFFFFFFF_FFFFFFFFFFFF_FFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF 488 | | ); 489 | | 490 | | return self; 491 | | } 492 | | 493 | | /*////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// 494 | | VALIDATION 495 | | //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////*/ 496 | | 497 | | /// @notice Validate an option position and all its active legs; return the underlying AMM address. 498 | | /// @dev Used to validate a position tokenId and its legs. 499 | | /// @param self The TokenId to validate 500 | * | function validate(TokenId self) internal pure { 501 | * | if (self.optionRatio(0) == 0) revert Errors.InvalidTokenIdParameter(1); 502 | | 503 | | // loop through the 4 (possible) legs in the tokenId `self` 504 | | unchecked { 505 | | // extract strike, width, and tokenType 506 | * | uint256 chunkData = (TokenId.unwrap(self) & CHUNK_MASK) >> 64; 507 | * | for (uint256 i = 0; i < 4; ++i) { 508 | * | if (self.optionRatio(i) == 0) { 509 | | // final leg in this position identified; 510 | | // make sure any leg above this are zero as well 511 | | // (we don't allow gaps eg having legs 1 and 4 active without 2 and 3 is not allowed) 512 | * | if ((TokenId.unwrap(self) >> (64 + 48 * i)) != 0) 513 | | revert Errors.InvalidTokenIdParameter(1); 514 | | 515 | | break; // we are done iterating over potential legs 516 | | } 517 | | 518 | | // prevent legs touching the same chunks - all chunks in the position must be discrete 519 | * | uint256 numLegs = self.countLegs(); 520 | * | for (uint256 j = i + 1; j < numLegs; ++j) { 521 | * | if (uint48(chunkData >> (48 * i)) == uint48(chunkData >> (48 * j))) { 522 | | revert Errors.InvalidTokenIdParameter(6); 523 | | } 524 | | } 525 | | // now validate this ith leg in the position: 526 | | 527 | | // The width cannot be 0; the minimum is 1 528 | * | if ((self.width(i) == 0)) revert Errors.InvalidTokenIdParameter(5); 529 | | // Strike cannot be MIN_TICK or MAX_TICK 530 | * | if ( 531 | * | (self.strike(i) == Constants.MIN_V3POOL_TICK) || 532 | * | (self.strike(i) == Constants.MAX_V3POOL_TICK) 533 | | ) revert Errors.InvalidTokenIdParameter(4);
