534 | | 535 | | // In the following, we check whether the risk partner of this leg is itself 536 | | // or another leg in this position. 537 | | // Handles case where riskPartner(i) != i ==> leg i has a risk partner that is another leg 538 | * | uint256 riskPartnerIndex = self.riskPartner(i); 539 | * | if (riskPartnerIndex != i) { 540 | | // Ensures that risk partners are mutual 541 | | if (self.riskPartner(riskPartnerIndex) != i) 542 | | revert Errors.InvalidTokenIdParameter(3); 543 | | 544 | | // Ensures that risk partners have 1) the same asset, and 2) the same ratio 545 | | if ( 546 | | (self.asset(riskPartnerIndex) != self.asset(i)) || 547 | | (self.optionRatio(riskPartnerIndex) != self.optionRatio(i)) 548 | | ) revert Errors.InvalidTokenIdParameter(3); 549 | | 550 | | // long/short status of associated legs 551 | | uint256 _isLong = self.isLong(i); 552 | | uint256 isLongP = self.isLong(riskPartnerIndex); 553 | | 554 | | // token type status of associated legs (call/put) 555 | | uint256 _tokenType = self.tokenType(i); 556 | | uint256 tokenTypeP = self.tokenType(riskPartnerIndex); 557 | | 558 | | // if the position is the same i.e both long calls, short put's etc. 559 | | // then this is a regular position, not a defined risk position 560 | | if ((_isLong == isLongP) && (_tokenType == tokenTypeP)) 561 | | revert Errors.InvalidTokenIdParameter(4); 562 | | 563 | | // if the two token long-types and the tokenTypes are both different (one is a short call, the other a long put, e.g.), this is a synthetic position 564 | | // A synthetic long or short is more capital efficient than each leg separated because the long+short premia accumulate proportionally 565 | | // unlike short stranlges, long strangles also cannot be partnered, because there is no reduction in risk (both legs can earn premia simultaneously) 566 | | if (((_isLong != isLongP) || _isLong == 1) && (_tokenType != tokenTypeP)) 567 | | revert Errors.InvalidTokenIdParameter(5); 568 | | } 569 | | } // end for loop over legs 570 | | } 571 | | } 572 | | 573 | | /// @notice Validate that a position `self` and its legs/chunks are exercisable. 574 | | /// @dev At least one long leg must be far-out-of-the-money (i.e. price is outside its range). 575 | | /// @dev Reverts if the position is not exercisable. 576 | | /// @param self The TokenId to validate for exercisability 577 | | /// @param currentTick The current tick corresponding to the current price in the Univ3 pool 578 | | function validateIsExercisable(TokenId self, int24 currentTick) internal pure { 579 | | unchecked { 580 | | uint256 numLegs = self.countLegs(); 581 | | for (uint256 i = 0; i < numLegs; ++i) { 582 | | (int24 rangeDown, int24 rangeUp) = PanopticMath.getRangesFromStrike( 583 | | self.width(i), 584 | | self.tickSpacing() 585 | | ); 586 | | 587 | | int24 _strike = self.strike(i); 588 | | // check if the price is outside this chunk 589 | | if ((currentTick >= _strike + rangeUp) || (currentTick < _strike - rangeDown)) { 590 | | // if this leg is long and the price beyond the leg's range: 591 | | // this exercised ID, `self`, appears valid 592 | | if (self.isLong(i) == 1) return; // validated 593 | | } 594 | | } 595 | | } 596 | | 597 | | // Fail if position has no legs that is far-out-of-the-money 598 | | revert Errors.NoLegsExercisable(); 599 | | } 600 | | } 601 | |

56 | | } 57 | | 58 | | /** 59 | | * @dev Computes the address of a clone deployed using {Clones-cloneDeterministic}. 60 | | */ 61 | | function predictDeterministicAddress( 62 | | address implementation, 63 | | bytes32 salt, 64 | | address deployer 65 | | ) internal pure returns (address predicted) { 66 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 67 | | assembly { 68 | | let ptr := mload(0x40) 69 | | mstore(add(ptr, 0x38), deployer) 70 | | mstore(add(ptr, 0x24), 0x5af43d82803e903d91602b57fd5bf3ff) 71 | | mstore(add(ptr, 0x14), implementation) 72 | | mstore(ptr, 0x3d602d80600a3d3981f3363d3d373d3d3d363d73) 73 | | mstore(add(ptr, 0x58), salt) 74 | | mstore(add(ptr, 0x78), keccak256(add(ptr, 0x0c), 0x37)) 75 | | predicted := keccak256(add(ptr, 0x43), 0x55) 76 | | } 77 | | } 78 | | 79 | | /** 80 | | * @dev Computes the address of a clone deployed using {Clones-cloneDeterministic}. 81 | | */ 82 | | function predictDeterministicAddress(address implementation, bytes32 salt) 83 | | internal 84 | | view 85 | | returns (address predicted) 86 | | { 87 | | return predictDeterministicAddress(implementation, salt, address(this)); 88 | | } 89 | | } 90 | |

Compute the product mod 2^256 and mod 2^256 - 1, then use 62 | | // use the Chinese Remainder Theorem to reconstruct the 512 bit result. The result is stored in two 256 63 | | // variables such that product = prod1 * 2^256 + prod0. 64 | | uint256 prod0; // Least significant 256 bits of the product 65 | | uint256 prod1; // Most significant 256 bits of the product 66 | | assembly { 67 | | let mm := mulmod(x, y, not(0)) 68 | | prod0 := mul(x, y) 69 | | prod1 := sub(sub(mm, prod0), lt(mm, prod0)) 70 | | } 71 | | 72 | | // Handle non-overflow cases, 256 by 256 division. 73 | | if (prod1 == 0) { 74 | | return prod0 / denominator; 75 | | } 76 | | 77 | | // Make sure the result is less than 2^256. Also prevents denominator == 0. 78 | | require(denominator > prod1); 79 | | 80 | | /////////////////////////////////////////////// 81 | | // 512 by 256 division. 82 | | /////////////////////////////////////////////// 83 | | 84 | | // Make division exact by subtracting the remainder from [prod1 prod0]. 85 | | uint256 remainder; 86 | | assembly { 87 | | // Compute remainder using mulmod. 88 | | remainder := mulmod(x, y, denominator) 89 | | 90 | | // Subtract 256 bit number from 512 bit number. 91 | | prod1 := sub(prod1, gt(remainder, prod0)) 92 | | prod0 := sub(prod0, remainder) 93 | | } 94 | | 95 | | // Factor powers of two out of denominator and compute largest power of two divisor of denominator. Always >= 1. 96 | | // See https://cs.stackexchange.com/q/138556/92363. 97 | | 98 | | // Does not overflow because the denominator cannot be zero at this stage in the function. 99 | | uint256 twos = denominator & (~denominator + 1); 100 | | assembly { 101 | | // Divide denominator by twos. 102 | | denominator := div(denominator, twos) 103 | | 104 | | // Divide [prod1 prod0] by twos. 105 | | prod0 := div(prod0, twos) 106 | | 107 | | // Flip twos such that it is 2^256 / twos. If twos is zero, then it becomes one. 108 | | twos := add(div(sub(0, twos), twos), 1) 109 | | } 110 | | 111 | | // Shift in bits from prod1 into prod0. 112 | | prod0 |= prod1 * twos; 113 | | 114 | | // Invert denominator mod 2^256. Now that denominator is an odd number, it has an inverse modulo 2^256 such 115 | | // that denominator * inv = 1 mod 2^256. Compute the inverse by starting with a seed that is correct for 116 | | // four bits. That is, denominator * inv = 1 mod 2^4. 117 | | uint256 inverse = (3 * denominator) ^ 2; 118 | | 119 | | // Use the Newton-Raphson iteration to improve the precision. Thanks to Hensel's lifting lemma, this also works 120 | | // in modular arithmetic, doubling the correct bits in each step. 121 | | inverse *= 2 - denominator * inverse; // inverse mod 2^8 122 | | inverse *= 2 - denominator * inverse; // inverse mod 2^16 123 | | inverse *= 2 - denominator * inverse; // inverse mod 2^32 124 | | inverse *= 2 - denominator * inverse; // inverse mod 2^64 125 | | inverse *= 2 - denominator * inverse; // inverse mod 2^128 126 | | inverse *= 2 - denominator * inverse; // inverse mod 2^256 127 | | 128 | | // Because the division is now exact we can divide by multiplying with the modular inverse of denominator. 129 | | // This will give us the correct result modulo 2^256. Since the preconditions guarantee that the outcome is 130 | | // less than 2^256, this is the final result. We don't need to compute the high bits of the result and prod1 131 | | // is no longer required. 132 | | result = prod0 * inverse; 133 | | return result; 134 | | } 135 | | } 136 | | 137 | | /** 138 | | * @notice Calculates x * y / denominator with full precision, following the selected rounding direction. 139 | | */ 140 | | function mulDiv( 141 | | uint256 x, 142 | | uint256 y, 143 | | uint256 denominator, 144 | | Rounding rounding 145 | | ) internal pure returns (uint256) { 146 | | uint256 result = mulDiv(x, y, denominator); 147 | | if (rounding == Rounding.Up && mulmod(x, y, denominator) > 0) { 148 | | result += 1; 149 | | } 150 | | return result; 151 | | } 152 | | 153 | | /** 154 | | * @dev Returns the square root of a number. If the number is not a perfect square, the value is rounded down. 155 | | * 156 | | * Inspired by Henry S. Warren, Jr.'s "Hacker's Delight" (Chapter 11). 157 | | */ 158 | | function sqrt(uint256 a) internal pure returns (uint256) { 159 | | if (a == 0) { 160 | | return 0; 161 | | } 162 | | 163 | | // For our first guess, we get the biggest power of 2 which is smaller than the square root of the target. 164 | | // 165 | | // We know that the "msb" (most significant bit) of our target number `a` is a power of 2 such that we have 166 | | // `msb(a) <= a < 2*msb(a)`. This value can be written `msb(a)=2**k` with `k=log2(a)`. 167 | | // 168 | | // This can be rewritten `2**log2(a) <= a < 2**(log2(a) + 1)` 169 | | // → `sqrt(2**k) <= sqrt(a) < sqrt(2**(k+1))` 170 | | // → `2**(k/2) <= sqrt(a) < 2**((k+1)/2) <= 2**(k/2 + 1)` 171 | | // 172 | | // Consequently, `2**(log2(a) / 2)` is a good first approximation of `sqrt(a)` with at least 1 correct bit. 173 | | uint256 result = 1 << (log2(a) >> 1); 174 | | 175 | | // At this point `result` is an estimation with one bit of precision. We know the true value is a uint128, 176 | | // since it is the square root of a uint256. Newton's method converges quadratically (precision doubles at 177 | | // every iteration). We thus need at most 7 iteration to turn our partial result with one bit of precision 178 | | // into the expected uint128 result. 179 | | unchecked { 180 | | result = (result + a / result) >> 1; 181 | | result = (result + a / result) >> 1; 182 | | result = (result + a / result) >> 1; 183 | | result = (result + a / result) >> 1; 184 | | result = (result + a / result) >> 1; 185 | | result = (result + a / result) >> 1; 186 | | result = (result + a / result) >> 1; 187 | | return min(result, a / result); 188 | | } 189 | | } 190 | | 191 | | /** 192 | | * @notice Calculates sqrt(a), following the selected rounding direction. 193 | | */ 194 | | function sqrt(uint256 a, Rounding rounding) internal pure returns (uint256) { 195 | | unchecked { 196 | | uint256 result = sqrt(a); 197 | | return result + (rounding == Rounding.Up && result * result < a ? 1 : 0); 198 | | } 199 | | } 200 | | 201 | | /** 202 | | * @dev Return the log in base 2, rounded down, of a positive value. 203 | | * Returns 0 if given 0. 204 | | */ 205 | | function log2(uint256 value) internal pure returns (uint256) { 206 | | uint256 result = 0; 207 | | unchecked { 208 | | if (value >> 128 > 0) { 209 | | value >>= 128; 210 | | result += 128; 211 | | } 212 | | if (value >> 64 > 0) { 213 | | value >>= 64; 214 | | result += 64; 215 | | } 216 | | if (value >> 32 > 0) { 217 | | value >>= 32; 218 | | result += 32; 219 | | } 220 | | if (value >> 16 > 0) { 221 | | value >>= 16; 222 | | result += 16; 223 | | } 224 | | if (value >> 8 > 0) { 225 | | value >>= 8; 226 | | result += 8; 227 | | } 228 | | if (value >> 4 > 0) { 229 | | value >>= 4; 230 | | result += 4; 231 | | } 232 | | if (value >> 2 > 0) { 233 | | value >>= 2; 234 | | result += 2; 235 | | } 236 | | if (value >> 1 > 0) { 237 | | result += 1; 238 | | } 239 | | } 240 | | return result; 241 | | } 242 | | 243 | | /** 244 | | * @dev Return the log in base 2, following the selected rounding direction, of a positive value. 245 | | * Returns 0 if given 0. 246 | | */ 247 | | function log2(uint256 value, Rounding rounding) internal pure returns (uint256) { 248 | | unchecked { 249 | | uint256 result = log2(value); 250 | | return result + (rounding == Rounding.Up && 1 << result < value ? 1 : 0); 251 | | } 252 | | } 253 | | 254 | | /** 255 | | * @dev Return the log in base 10, rounded down, of a positive value. 256 | | * Returns 0 if given 0. 257 | | */ 258 | | function log10(uint256 value) internal pure returns (uint256) { 259 | | uint256 result = 0; 260 | | unchecked { 261 | | if (value >= 10**64) { 262 | | value /= 10**64; 263 | | result += 64; 264 | | } 265 | | if (value >= 10**32) { 266 | | value /= 10**32; 267 | | result += 32; 268 | | } 269 | | if (value >= 10**16) { 270 | | value /= 10**16; 271 | | result += 16; 272 | | } 273 | | if (value >= 10**8) { 274 | | value /= 10**8; 275 | | result += 8; 276 | | } 277 | | if (value >= 10**4) { 278 | | value /= 10**4; 279 | | result += 4; 280 | | } 281 | | if (value >= 10**2) { 282 | | value /= 10**2; 283 | | result += 2; 284 | | } 285 | | if (value >= 10**1) { 286 | | result += 1; 287 | | } 288 | | } 289 | | return result; 290 | | } 291 | | 292 | | /** 293 | | * @dev Return the log in base 10, following the selected rounding direction, of a positive value. 294 | | * Returns 0 if given 0. 295 | | */ 296 | | function log10(uint256 value, Rounding rounding) internal pure returns (uint256) { 297 | | unchecked { 298 | | uint256 result = log10(value); 299 | | return result + (rounding == Rounding.Up && 10**result < value ? 1 : 0); 300 | | } 301 | | } 302 | | 303 | | /** 304 | | * @dev Return the log in base 256, rounded down, of a positive value. 305 | | * Returns 0 if given 0. 306 | | * 307 | | * Adding one to the result gives the number of pairs of hex symbols needed to represent `value` as a hex string. 308 | | */ 309 | | function log256(uint256 value) internal pure returns (uint256) { 310 | | uint256 result = 0; 311 | | unchecked { 312 | | if (value >> 128 > 0) { 313 | | value >>= 128; 314 | | result += 16; 315 | | } 316 | | if (value >> 64 > 0) { 317 | | value >>= 64; 318 | | result += 8; 319 | | } 320 | | if (value >> 32 > 0) { 321 | | value >>= 32; 322 | | result += 4; 323 | | } 324 | | if (value >> 16 > 0) { 325 | | value >>= 16; 326 | | result += 2; 327 | | } 328 | | if (value >> 8 > 0) { 329 | | result += 1; 330 | | } 331 | | } 332 | | return result; 333 | | } 334 | | 335 | | /** 336 | | * @dev Return the log in base 10, following the selected rounding direction, of a positive value. 337 | | * Returns 0 if given 0. 338 | | */ 339 | | function log256(uint256 value, Rounding rounding) internal pure returns (uint256) { 340 | | unchecked { 341 | | uint256 result = log256(value); 342 | | return result + (rounding == Rounding.Up && 1 << (result * 8) < value ? 1 : 0); 343 | | } 344 | | } 345 | | } 346 | |
/home/xshadow/panoptic-v1-core-private/lib/solady/src/utils/Base64.sol 1 | | // SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT 2 | | pragma solidity ^0.8.4; 3 | | 4 | | /// @notice Library to encode strings in Base64. 5 | | /// @author Solady (https://github.com/vectorized/solady/blob/main/src/utils/Base64.sol) 6 | | /// @author Modified from Solmate (https://github.com/transmissions11/solmate/blob/main/src/utils/Base64.sol) 7 | | /// @author Modified from (https://github.com/Brechtpd/base64/blob/main/base64.sol) by Brecht Devos - <brecht@loopring.org>. 8 | | library Base64 { 9 | | /// @dev Encodes `data` using the base64 encoding described in RFC 4648. 10 | | /// See: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc4648 11 | | /// @param fileSafe Whether to replace '+' with '-' and '/' with '_'. 12 | | /// @param noPadding Whether to strip away the padding. 13 | | function encode(bytes memory data, bool fileSafe, bool noPadding) 14 | | internal 15 | | pure 16 | | returns (string memory result) 17 | | { 18 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 19 | | assembly { 20 | | let dataLength := mload(data) 21 | | 22 | | if dataLength { 23 | | // Multiply by 4/3 rounded up. 24 | | // The `shl(2, ...)` is equivalent to multiplying by 4. 25 | | let encodedLength := shl(2, div(add(dataLength, 2), 3)) 26 | | 27 | | // Set `result` to point to the start of the free memory. 28 | | result := mload(0x40) 29 | | 30 | | // Store the table into the scratch space. 31 | | // Offsetted by -1 byte so that the `mload` will load the character. 32 | | // We will rewrite the free memory pointer at `0x40` later with 33 | | // the allocated size. 34 | | // The magic constant 0x0670 will turn "-_" into "+/". 35 | | mstore(0x1f, "ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdef") 36 | | mstore(0x3f, xor("ghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789-_", mul(iszero(fileSafe), 0x0670))) 37 | | 38 | | // Skip the first slot, which stores the length. 39 | | let ptr := add(result, 0x20) 40 | | let end := add(ptr, encodedLength) 41 | | 42 | | let dataEnd := add(add(0x20, data), dataLength) 43 | | let dataEndValue := mload(dataEnd) // Cache the value at the `dataEnd` slot. 44 | | mstore(dataEnd, 0x00) // Zeroize the `dataEnd` slot to clear dirty bits. 45 | | 46 | | // Run over the input, 3 bytes at a time. 47 | | for {} 1 {} { 48 | | data := add(data, 3) // Advance 3 bytes. 49 | | let input := mload(data) 50 | | 51 | | // Write 4 bytes. Optimized for fewer stack operations. 52 | | mstore8(0, mload(and(shr(18, input), 0x3F))) 53 | | mstore8(1, mload(and(shr(12, input), 0x3F))) 54 | | mstore8(2, mload(and(shr(6, input), 0x3F))) 55 | | mstore8(3, mload(and(input, 0x3F))) 56 | | mstore(ptr, mload(0x00)) 57 | | 58 | | ptr := add(ptr, 4) // Advance 4 bytes. 59 | | if iszero(lt(ptr, end)) { break } 60 | | } 61 | | mstore(dataEnd, dataEndValue) // Restore the cached value at `dataEnd`. 62 | | mstore(0x40, add(end, 0x20)) // Allocate the memory. 63 | | // Equivalent to `o = [0, 2, 1][dataLength % 3]`. 64 | | let o := div(2, mod(dataLength, 3)) 65 | | // Offset `ptr` and pad with '='. We can simply write over the end. 66 | | mstore(sub(ptr, o), shl(240, 0x3d3d)) 67 | | // Set `o` to zero if there is padding. 68 | | o := mul(iszero(iszero(noPadding)), o) 69 | | mstore(sub(ptr, o), 0) // Zeroize the slot after the string. 70 | | mstore(result, sub(encodedLength, o)) // Store the length. 71 | | } 72 | | } 73 | | } 74 | | 75 | | /// @dev Encodes `data` using the base64 encoding described in RFC 4648. 76 | | /// Equivalent to `encode(data, false, false)`. 77 | | function encode(bytes memory data) internal pure returns (string memory result) { 78 | | result = encode(data, false, false); 79 | | } 80 | | 81 | | /// @dev Encodes `data` using the base64 encoding described in RFC 4648. 82 | | /// Equivalent to `encode(data, fileSafe, false)`. 83 | | function encode(bytes memory data, bool fileSafe) 84 | | internal 85 | | pure 86 | | returns (string memory result) 87 | | { 88 | | result = encode(data, fileSafe, false); 89 | | } 90 | | 91 | | /// @dev Decodes base64 encoded `data`. 92 | | /// 93 | | /// Supports: 94 | | /// - RFC 4648 (both standard and file-safe mode). 95 | | /// - RFC 3501 (63: ','). 96 | | /// 97 | | /// Does not support: 98 | | /// - Line breaks. 99 | | /// 100 | | /// Note: For performance reasons, 101 | | /// this function will NOT revert on invalid `data` inputs. 102 | | /// Outputs for invalid inputs will simply be undefined behaviour. 103 | | /// It is the user's responsibility to ensure that the `data` 104 | | /// is a valid base64 encoded string. 105 | | function decode(string memory data) internal pure returns (bytes memory result) { 106 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 107 | | assembly { 108 | | let dataLength := mload(data) 109 | | 110 | | if dataLength { 111 | | let decodedLength := mul(shr(2, dataLength), 3) 112 | | 113 | | for {} 1 {} { 114 | | // If padded. 115 | | if iszero(and(dataLength, 3)) { 116 | | let t := xor(mload(add(data, dataLength)), 0x3d3d) 117 | | // forgefmt: disable-next-item 118 | | decodedLength := sub( 119 | | decodedLength, 120 | | add(iszero(byte(30, t)), iszero(byte(31, t))) 121 | | ) 122 | | break 123 | | } 124 | | // If non-padded. 125 | | decodedLength := add(decodedLength, sub(and(dataLength, 3), 1)) 126 | | break 127 | | } 128 | | result := mload(0x40) 129 | | 130 | | // Write the length of the bytes. 131 | | mstore(result, decodedLength) 132 | | 133 | | // Skip the first slot, which stores the length. 134 | | let ptr := add(result, 0x20) 135 | | let end := add(ptr, decodedLength) 136 | | 137 | | // Load the table into the scratch space. 138 | | // Constants are optimized for smaller bytecode with zero gas overhead. 139 | | // `m` also doubles as the mask of the upper 6 bits. 140 | | let m := 0xfc000000fc00686c7074787c8084888c9094989ca0a4a8acb0b4b8bcc0c4c8cc 141 | | mstore(0x5b, m) 142 | | mstore(0x3b, 0x04080c1014181c2024282c3034383c4044484c5054585c6064) 143 | | mstore(0x1a, 0xf8fcf800fcd0d4d8dce0e4e8ecf0f4) 144 | | 145 | | for {} 1 {} { 146 | | // Read 4 bytes. 147 | | data := add(data, 4) 148 | | let input := mload(data) 149 | | 150 | | // Write 3 bytes. 151 | | // forgefmt: disable-next-item 152 | | mstore(ptr, or( 153 | | and(m, mload(byte(28, input))), 154 | | shr(6, or( 155 | | and(m, mload(byte(29, input))), 156 | | shr(6, or( 157 | | and(m, mload(byte(30, input))), 158 | | shr(6, mload(byte(31, input))) 159 | | )) 160 | | )) 161 | | )) 162 | | ptr := add(ptr, 3) 163 | | if iszero(lt(ptr, end)) { break } 164 | | } 165 | | mstore(0x40, add(end, 0x20)) // Allocate the memory. 166 | | mstore(end, 0) // Zeroize the slot after the bytes. 167 | | mstore(0x60, 0) // Restore the zero slot. 168 | | } 169 | | } 170 | | } 171 | | } 172 | |
/home/xshadow/panoptic-v1-core-private/lib/solady/src/utils/LibString.sol 1 | | // SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT 2 | | pragma solidity ^0.8.4; 3 | | 4 | | /// @notice Library for converting numbers into strings and other string operations. 5 | | /// @author Solady (https://github.com/vectorized/solady/blob/main/src/utils/LibString.sol) 6 | | /// @author Modified from Solmate (https://github.com/transmissions11/solmate/blob/main/src/utils/LibString.sol) 7 | | /// 8 | | /// @dev Note: 9 | | /// For performance and bytecode compactness, most of the string operations are restricted to 10 | | /// byte strings (7-bit ASCII), except where otherwise specified. 11 | | /// Usage of byte string operations on charsets with runes spanning two or more bytes 12 | | /// can lead to undefined behavior. 13 | | library LibString { 14 | | /*´:°•.°+.*•´.*:˚.°*.˚•´.°:°•.°•.*•´.*:˚.°*.˚•´.°:°•.°+.*•´.*:*/ 15 | | /* CUSTOM ERRORS */ 16 | | /*.•°:°.´+˚.*°.˚:*.´•*.+°.•°:´*.´•*.•°.•°:°.´:•˚°.*°.˚:*.´+°.•*/ 17 | | 18 | | /// @dev The length of the output is too small to contain all the hex digits. 19 | | error HexLengthInsufficient(); 20 | | 21 | | /// @dev The length of the string is more than 32 bytes. 22 | | error TooBigForSmallString(); 23 | | 24 | | /*´:°•.°+.*•´.*:˚.°*.˚•´.°:°•.°•.*•´.*:˚.°*.˚•´.°:°•.°+.*•´.*:*/ 25 | | /* CONSTANTS */ 26 | | /*.•°:°.´+˚.*°.˚:*.´•*.+°.•°:´*.´•*.•°.•°:°.´:•˚°.*°.˚:*.´+°.•*/ 27 | | 28 | | /// @dev The constant returned when the `search` is not found in the string. 29 | | uint256 internal constant NOT_FOUND = type(uint256).max; 30 | | 31 | | /*´:°•.°+.*•´.*:˚.°*.˚•´.°:°•.°•.*•´.*:˚.°*.˚•´.°:°•.°+.*•´.*:*/ 32 | | /* DECIMAL OPERATIONS */ 33 | | /*.•°:°.´+˚.*°.˚:*.´•*.+°.•°:´*.´•*.•°.•°:°.´:•˚°.*°.˚:*.´+°.•*/ 34 | | 35 | | /// @dev Returns the base 10 decimal representation of `value`. 36 | | function toString(uint256 value) internal pure returns (string memory str) { 37 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 38 | | assembly { 39 | | // The maximum value of a uint256 contains 78 digits (1 byte per digit), but 40 | | // we allocate 0xa0 bytes to keep the free memory pointer 32-byte word aligned. 41 | | // We will need 1 word for the trailing zeros padding, 1 word for the length, 42 | | // and 3 words for a maximum of 78 digits. 43 | | str := add(mload(0x40), 0x80) 44 | | // Update the free memory pointer to allocate. 45 | | mstore(0x40, add(str, 0x20)) 46 | | // Zeroize the slot after the string. 47 | | mstore(str, 0) 48 | | 49 | | // Cache the end of the memory to calculate the length later. 50 | | let end := str 51 | | 52 | | let w := not(0) // Tsk. 53 | | // We write the string from rightmost digit to leftmost digit. 54 | | // The following is essentially a do-while loop that also handles the zero case. 55 | | for { let temp := value } 1 {} { 56 | | str := add(str, w) // `sub(str, 1)`. 57 | | // Write the character to the pointer. 58 | | // The ASCII index of the '0' character is 48. 59 | | mstore8(str, add(48, mod(temp, 10))) 60 | | // Keep dividing `temp` until zero. 61 | | temp := div(temp, 10) 62 | | if iszero(temp) { break } 63 | | } 64 | | 65 | | let length := sub(end, str) 66 | | // Move the pointer 32 bytes leftwards to make room for the length. 67 | | str := sub(str, 0x20) 68 | | // Store the length. 69 | | mstore(str, length) 70 | | } 71 | | } 72 | | 73 | | /// @dev Returns the base 10 decimal representation of `value`. 74 | | function toString(int256 value) internal pure returns (string memory str) { 75 | | if (value >= 0) { 76 | | return toString(uint256(value)); 77 | | } 78 | | unchecked { 79 | | str = toString(~uint256(value) + 1); 80 | | } 81 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 82 | | assembly { 83 | | // We still have some spare memory space on the left, 84 | | // as we have allocated 3 words (96 bytes) for up to 78 digits. 85 | | let length := mload(str) // Load the string length. 86 | | mstore(str, 0x2d) // Store the '-' character. 87 | | str := sub(str, 1) // Move back the string pointer by a byte. 88 | | mstore(str, add(length, 1)) // Update the string length. 89 | | } 90 | | } 91 | | 92 | | /*´:°•.°+.*•´.*:˚.°*.˚•´.°:°•.°•.*•´.*:˚.°*.˚•´.°:°•.°+.*•´.*:*/ 93 | | /* HEXADECIMAL OPERATIONS */ 94 | | /*.•°:°.´+˚.*°.˚:*.´•*.+°.•°:´*.´•*.•°.•°:°.´:•˚°.*°.˚:*.´+°.•*/ 95 | | 96 | | /// @dev Returns the hexadecimal representation of `value`, 97 | | /// left-padded to an input length of `length` bytes. 98 | | /// The output is prefixed with "0x" encoded using 2 hexadecimal digits per byte, 99 | | /// giving a total length of `length * 2 + 2` bytes. 100 | | /// Reverts if `length` is too small for the output to contain all the digits. 101 | | function toHexString(uint256 value, uint256 length) internal pure returns (string memory str) { 102 | | str = toHexStringNoPrefix(value, length); 103 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 104 | | assembly { 105 | | let strLength := add(mload(str), 2) // Compute the length. 106 | | mstore(str, 0x3078) // Write the "0x" prefix. 107 | | str := sub(str, 2) // Move the pointer. 108 | | mstore(str, strLength) // Write the length. 109 | | } 110 | | } 111 | | 112 | | /// @dev Returns the hexadecimal representation of `value`, 113 | | /// left-padded to an input length of `length` bytes. 114 | | /// The output is prefixed with "0x" encoded using 2 hexadecimal digits per byte, 115 | | /// giving a total length of `length * 2` bytes. 116 | | /// Reverts if `length` is too small for the output to contain all the digits. 117 | | function toHexStringNoPrefix(uint256 value, uint256 length) 118 | | internal 119 | | pure 120 | | returns (string memory str) 121 | | { 122 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 123 | | assembly { 124 | | // We need 0x20 bytes for the trailing zeros padding, `length * 2` bytes 125 | | // for the digits, 0x02 bytes for the prefix, and 0x20 bytes for the length. 126 | | // We add 0x20 to the total and round down to a multiple of 0x20. 127 | | // (0x20 + 0x20 + 0x02 + 0x20) = 0x62. 128 | | str := add(mload(0x40), and(add(shl(1, length), 0x42), not(0x1f))) 129 | | // Allocate the memory. 130 | | mstore(0x40, add(str, 0x20)) 131 | | // Zeroize the slot after the string. 132 | | mstore(str, 0) 133 | | 134 | | // Cache the end to calculate the length later. 135 | | let end := str 136 | | // Store "0123456789abcdef" in scratch space. 137 | | mstore(0x0f, 0x30313233343536373839616263646566) 138 | | 139 | | let start := sub(str, add(length, length)) 140 | | let w := not(1) // Tsk. 141 | | let temp := value 142 | | // We write the string from rightmost digit to leftmost digit. 143 | | // The following is essentially a do-while loop that also handles the zero case. 144 | | for {} 1 {} { 145 | | str := add(str, w) // `sub(str, 2)`. 146 | | mstore8(add(str, 1), mload(and(temp, 15))) 147 | | mstore8(str, mload(and(shr(4, temp), 15))) 148 | | temp := shr(8, temp) 149 | | if iszero(xor(str, start)) { break } 150 | | } 151 | | 152 | | if temp { 153 | | mstore(0x00, 0x2194895a) // `HexLengthInsufficient()`. 154 | | revert(0x1c, 0x04) 155 | | } 156 | | 157 | | // Compute the string's length. 158 | | let strLength := sub(end, str) 159 | | // Move the pointer and write the length. 160 | | str := sub(str, 0x20) 161 | | mstore(str, strLength) 162 | | } 163 | | } 164 | | 165 | | /// @dev Returns the hexadecimal representation of `value`. 166 | | /// The output is prefixed with "0x" and encoded using 2 hexadecimal digits per byte. 167 | | /// As address are 20 bytes long, the output will left-padded to have 168 | | /// a length of `20 * 2 + 2` bytes. 169 | | function toHexString(uint256 value) internal pure returns (string memory str) { 170 | | str = toHexStringNoPrefix(value); 171 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 172 | | assembly { 173 | | let strLength := add(mload(str), 2) // Compute the length. 174 | | mstore(str, 0x3078) // Write the "0x" prefix. 175 | | str := sub(str, 2) // Move the pointer. 176 | | mstore(str, strLength) // Write the length. 177 | | } 178 | | } 179 | | 180 | | /// @dev Returns the hexadecimal representation of `value`. 181 | | /// The output is prefixed with "0x". 182 | | /// The output excludes leading "0" from the `toHexString` output. 183 | | /// `0x00: "0x0", 0x01: "0x1", 0x12: "0x12", 0x123: "0x123"`. 184 | | function toMinimalHexString(uint256 value) internal pure returns (string memory str) { 185 | | str = toHexStringNoPrefix(value); 186 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 187 | | assembly { 188 | | let o := eq(byte(0, mload(add(str, 0x20))), 0x30) // Whether leading zero is present. 189 | | let strLength := add(mload(str), 2) // Compute the length. 190 | | mstore(add(str, o), 0x3078) // Write the "0x" prefix, accounting for leading zero. 191 | | str := sub(add(str, o), 2) // Move the pointer, accounting for leading zero. 192 | | mstore(str, sub(strLength, o)) // Write the length, accounting for leading zero. 193 | | } 194 | | } 195 | | 196 | | /// @dev Returns the hexadecimal representation of `value`. 197 | | /// The output excludes leading "0" from the `toHexStringNoPrefix` output. 198 | | /// `0x00: "0", 0x01: "1", 0x12: "12", 0x123: "123"`. 199 | | function toMinimalHexStringNoPrefix(uint256 value) internal pure returns (string memory str) { 200 | | str = toHexStringNoPrefix(value); 201 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 202 | | assembly { 203 | | let o := eq(byte(0, mload(add(str, 0x20))), 0x30) // Whether leading zero is present. 204 | | let strLength := mload(str) // Get the length. 205 | | str := add(str, o) // Move the pointer, accounting for leading zero. 206 | | mstore(str, sub(strLength, o)) // Write the length, accounting for leading zero. 207 | | } 208 | | } 209 | | 210 | | /// @dev Returns the hexadecimal representation of `value`. 211 | | /// The output is encoded using 2 hexadecimal digits per byte. 212 | | /// As address are 20 bytes long, the output will left-padded to have 213 | | /// a length of `20 * 2` bytes. 214 | | function toHexStringNoPrefix(uint256 value) internal pure returns (string memory str) { 215 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 216 | | assembly { 217 | | // We need 0x20 bytes for the trailing zeros padding, 0x20 bytes for the length, 218 | | // 0x02 bytes for the prefix, and 0x40 bytes for the digits. 219 | | // The next multiple of 0x20 above (0x20 + 0x20 + 0x02 + 0x40) is 0xa0. 220 | | str := add(mload(0x40), 0x80) 221 | | // Allocate the memory. 222 | | mstore(0x40, add(str, 0x20)) 223 | | // Zeroize the slot after the string. 224 | | mstore(str, 0) 225 | | 226 | | // Cache the end to calculate the length later. 227 | | let end := str 228 | | // Store "0123456789abcdef" in scratch space. 229 | | mstore(0x0f, 0x30313233343536373839616263646566) 230 | | 231 | | let w := not(1) // Tsk. 232 | | // We write the string from rightmost digit to leftmost digit. 233 | | // The following is essentially a do-while loop that also handles the zero case. 234 | | for { let temp := value } 1 {} { 235 | | str := add(str, w) // `sub(str, 2)`. 236 | | mstore8(add(str, 1), mload(and(temp, 15))) 237 | | mstore8(str, mload(and(shr(4, temp), 15))) 238 | | temp := shr(8, temp) 239 | | if iszero(temp) { break } 240 | | } 241 | | 242 | | // Compute the string's length. 243 | | let strLength := sub(end, str) 244 | | // Move the pointer and write the length. 245 | | str := sub(str, 0x20) 246 | | mstore(str, strLength) 247 | | } 248 | | } 249 | | 250 | | /// @dev Returns the hexadecimal representation of `value`. 251 | | /// The output is prefixed with "0x", encoded using 2 hexadecimal digits per byte, 252 | | /// and the alphabets are capitalized conditionally according to 253 | | /// https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-55 254 | | function toHexStringChecksummed(address value) internal pure returns (string memory str) { 255 | | str = toHexString(value); 256 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 257 | | assembly { 258 | | let mask := shl(6, div(not(0), 255)) // `0b010000000100000000 ...` 259 | | let o := add(str, 0x22) 260 | | let hashed := and(keccak256(o, 40), mul(34, mask)) // `0b10001000 ... ` 261 | | let t := shl(240, 136) // `0b10001000 << 240` 262 | | for { let i := 0 } 1 {} { 263 | | mstore(add(i, i), mul(t, byte(i, hashed))) 264 | | i := add(i, 1) 265 | | if eq(i, 20) { break } 266 | | } 267 | | mstore(o, xor(mload(o), shr(1, and(mload(0x00), and(mload(o), mask))))) 268 | | o := add(o, 0x20) 269 | | mstore(o, xor(mload(o), shr(1, and(mload(0x20), and(mload(o), mask))))) 270 | | } 271 | | } 272 | | 273 | | /// @dev Returns the hexadecimal representation of `value`. 274 | | /// The output is prefixed with "0x" and encoded using 2 hexadecimal digits per byte. 275 | | function toHexString(address value) internal pure returns (string memory str) { 276 | | str = toHexStringNoPrefix(value); 277 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 278 | | assembly { 279 | | let strLength := add(mload(str), 2) // Compute the length. 280 | | mstore(str, 0x3078) // Write the "0x" prefix. 281 | | str := sub(str, 2) // Move the pointer. 282 | | mstore(str, strLength) // Write the length. 283 | | } 284 | | } 285 | | 286 | | /// @dev Returns the hexadecimal representation of `value`. 287 | | /// The output is encoded using 2 hexadecimal digits per byte. 288 | | function toHexStringNoPrefix(address value) internal pure returns (string memory str) { 289 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 290 | | assembly { 291 | | str := mload(0x40) 292 | | 293 | | // Allocate the memory. 294 | | // We need 0x20 bytes for the trailing zeros padding, 0x20 bytes for the length, 295 | | // 0x02 bytes for the prefix, and 0x28 bytes for the digits. 296 | | // The next multiple of 0x20 above (0x20 + 0x20 + 0x02 + 0x28) is 0x80. 297 | | mstore(0x40, add(str, 0x80)) 298 | | 299 | | // Store "0123456789abcdef" in scratch space. 300 | | mstore(0x0f, 0x30313233343536373839616263646566) 301 | | 302 | | str := add(str, 2) 303 | | mstore(str, 40) 304 | | 305 | | let o := add(str, 0x20) 306 | | mstore(add(o, 40), 0) 307 | | 308 | | value := shl(96, value) 309 | | 310 | | // We write the string from rightmost digit to leftmost digit. 311 | | // The following is essentially a do-while loop that also handles the zero case. 312 | | for { let i := 0 } 1 {} { 313 | | let p := add(o, add(i, i)) 314 | | let temp := byte(i, value) 315 | | mstore8(add(p, 1), mload(and(temp, 15))) 316 | | mstore8(p, mload(shr(4, temp))) 317 | | i := add(i, 1) 318 | | if eq(i, 20) { break } 319 | | } 320 | | } 321 | | } 322 | | 323 | | /// @dev Returns the hex encoded string from the raw bytes. 324 | | /// The output is encoded using 2 hexadecimal digits per byte. 325 | | function toHexString(bytes memory raw) internal pure returns (string memory str) { 326 | | str = toHexStringNoPrefix(raw); 327 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 328 | | assembly { 329 | | let strLength := add(mload(str), 2) // Compute the length. 330 | | mstore(str, 0x3078) // Write the "0x" prefix. 331 | | str := sub(str, 2) // Move the pointer. 332 | | mstore(str, strLength) // Write the length. 333 | | } 334 | | } 335 | | 336 | | /// @dev Returns the hex encoded string from the raw bytes. 337 | | /// The output is encoded using 2 hexadecimal digits per byte. 338 | | function toHexStringNoPrefix(bytes memory raw) internal pure returns (string memory str) { 339 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 340 | | assembly { 341 | | let length := mload(raw) 342 | | str := add(mload(0x40), 2) // Skip 2 bytes for the optional prefix. 343 | | mstore(str, add(length, length)) // Store the length of the output. 344 | | 345 | | // Store "0123456789abcdef" in scratch space. 346 | | mstore(0x0f, 0x30313233343536373839616263646566) 347 | | 348 | | let o := add(str, 0x20) 349 | | let end := add(raw, length) 350 | | 351 | | for {} iszero(eq(raw, end)) {} { 352 | | raw := add(raw, 1) 353 | | mstore8(add(o, 1), mload(and(mload(raw), 15))) 354 | | mstore8(o, mload(and(shr(4, mload(raw)), 15))) 355 | | o := add(o, 2) 356 | | } 357 | | mstore(o, 0) // Zeroize the slot after the string. 358 | | mstore(0x40, add(o, 0x20)) // Allocate the memory. 359 | | } 360 | | } 361 | | 362 | | /*´:°•.°+.*•´.*:˚.°*.˚•´.°:°•.°•.*•´.*:˚.°*.˚•´.°:°•.°+.*•´.*:*/ 363 | | /* RUNE STRING OPERATIONS */ 364 | | /*.•°:°.´+˚.*°.˚:*.´•*.+°.•°:´*.´•*.•°.•°:°.´:•˚°.*°.˚:*.´+°.•*/ 365 | | 366 | | /// @dev Returns the number of UTF characters in the string. 367 | | function runeCount(string memory s) internal pure returns (uint256 result) { 368 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 369 | | assembly { 370 | | if mload(s) { 371 | | mstore(0x00, div(not(0), 255)) 372 | | mstore(0x20, 0x0202020202020202020202020202020202020202020202020303030304040506) 373 | | let o := add(s, 0x20) 374 | | let end := add(o, mload(s)) 375 | | for { result := 1 } 1 { result := add(result, 1) } { 376 | | o := add(o, byte(0, mload(shr(250, mload(o))))) 377 | | if iszero(lt(o, end)) { break } 378 | | } 379 | | } 380 | | } 381 | | } 382 | | 383 | | /// @dev Returns if this string is a 7-bit ASCII string. 384 | | /// (i.e. all characters codes are in [0..127]) 385 | | function is7BitASCII(string memory s) internal pure returns (bool result) { 386 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 387 | | assembly { 388 | | let mask := shl(7, div(not(0), 255)) 389 | | result := 1 390 | | let n := mload(s) 391 | | if n { 392 | | let o := add(s, 0x20) 393 | | let end := add(o, n) 394 | | let last := mload(end) 395 | | mstore(end, 0) 396 | | for {} 1 {} { 397 | | if and(mask, mload(o)) { 398 | | result := 0 399 | | break 400 | | } 401 | | o := add(o, 0x20) 402 | | if iszero(lt(o, end)) { break } 403 | | } 404 | | mstore(end, last) 405 | | } 406 | | } 407 | | } 408 | | 409 | | /*´:°•.°+.*•´.*:˚.°*.˚•´.°:°•.°•.*•´.*:˚.°*.˚•´.°:°•.°+.*•´.*:*/ 410 | | /* BYTE STRING OPERATIONS */ 411 | | /*.•°:°.´+˚.*°.˚:*.´•*.+°.•°:´*.´•*.•°.•°:°.´:•˚°.*°.˚:*.´+°.•*/ 412 | | 413 | | // For performance and bytecode compactness, byte string operations are restricted 414 | | // to 7-bit ASCII strings. All offsets are byte offsets, not UTF character offsets. 415 | | // Usage of byte string operations on charsets with runes spanning two or more bytes 416 | | // can lead to undefined behavior. 417 | | 418 | | /// @dev Returns `subject` all occurrences of `search` replaced with `replacement`. 419 | | function replace(string memory subject, string memory search, string memory replacement) 420 | | internal 421 | | pure 422 | | returns (string memory result) 423 | | { 424 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 425 | | assembly { 426 | | let subjectLength := mload(subject) 427 | | let searchLength := mload(search) 428 | | let replacementLength := mload(replacement) 429 | | 430 | | subject := add(subject, 0x20) 431 | | search := add(search, 0x20) 432 | | replacement := add(replacement, 0x20) 433 | | result := add(mload(0x40), 0x20) 434 | | 435 | | let subjectEnd := add(subject, subjectLength) 436 | | if iszero(gt(searchLength, subjectLength)) { 437 | | let subjectSearchEnd := add(sub(subjectEnd, searchLength), 1) 438 | | let h := 0 439 | | if iszero(lt(searchLength, 0x20)) { h := keccak256(search, searchLength) } 440 | | let m := shl(3, sub(0x20, and(searchLength, 0x1f))) 441 | | let s := mload(search) 442 | | for {} 1 {} { 443 | | let t := mload(subject) 444 | | // Whether the first `searchLength % 32` bytes of 445 | | // `subject` and `search` matches. 446 | | if iszero(shr(m, xor(t, s))) { 447 | | if h { 448 | | if iszero(eq(keccak256(subject, searchLength), h)) { 449 | | mstore(result, t) 450 | | result := add(result, 1) 451 | | subject := add(subject, 1) 452 | | if iszero(lt(subject, subjectSearchEnd)) { break } 453 | | continue 454 | | } 455 | | } 456 | | // Copy the `replacement` one word at a time. 457 | | for { let o := 0 } 1 {} { 458 | | mstore(add(result, o), mload(add(replacement, o))) 459 | | o := add(o, 0x20) 460 | | if iszero(lt(o, replacementLength)) { break } 461 | | } 462 | | result := add(result, replacementLength) 463 | | subject := add(subject, searchLength) 464 | | if searchLength { 465 | | if iszero(lt(subject, subjectSearchEnd)) { break } 466 | | continue 467 | | } 468 | | } 469 | | mstore(result, t) 470 | | result := add(result, 1) 471 | | subject := add(subject, 1) 472 | | if iszero(lt(subject, subjectSearchEnd)) { break } 473 | | } 474 | | } 475 | | 476 | | let resultRemainder := result 477 | | result := add(mload(0x40), 0x20) 478 | | let k := add(sub(resultRemainder, result), sub(subjectEnd, subject)) 479 | | // Copy the rest of the string one word at a time. 480 | | for {} lt(subject, subjectEnd) {} { 481 | | mstore(resultRemainder, mload(subject)) 482 | | resultRemainder := add(resultRemainder, 0x20) 483 | | subject := add(subject, 0x20) 484 | | } 485 | | result := sub(result, 0x20) 486 | | let last := add(add(result, 0x20), k) // Zeroize the slot after the string. 487 | | mstore(last, 0) 488 | | mstore(0x40, add(last, 0x20)) // Allocate the memory. 489 | | mstore(result, k) // Store the length. 490 | | } 491 | | } 492 | | 493 | | /// @dev Returns the byte index of the first location of `search` in `subject`, 494 | | /// searching from left to right, starting from `from`. 495 | | /// Returns `NOT_FOUND` (i.e. `type(uint256).max`) if the `search` is not found. 496 | | function indexOf(string memory subject, string memory search, uint256 from) 497 | | internal 498 | | pure 499 | | returns (uint256 result) 500 | | { 501 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 502 | | assembly { 503 | | for { let subjectLength := mload(subject) } 1 {} { 504 | | if iszero(mload(search)) { 505 | | if iszero(gt(from, subjectLength)) { 506 | | result := from 507 | | break 508 | | } 509 | | result := subjectLength 510 | | break 511 | | } 512 | | let searchLength := mload(search) 513 | | let subjectStart := add(subject, 0x20) 514 | | 515 | | result := not(0) // Initialize to `NOT_FOUND`. 516 | | 517 | | subject := add(subjectStart, from) 518 | | let end := add(sub(add(subjectStart, subjectLength), searchLength), 1) 519 | | 520 | | let m := shl(3, sub(0x20, and(searchLength, 0x1f))) 521 | | let s := mload(add(search, 0x20)) 522 | | 523 | | if iszero(and(lt(subject, end), lt(from, subjectLength))) { break } 524 | | 525 | | if iszero(lt(searchLength, 0x20)) { 526 | | for { let h := keccak256(add(search, 0x20), searchLength) } 1 {} { 527 | | if iszero(shr(m, xor(mload(subject), s))) { 528 | | if eq(keccak256(subject, searchLength), h) { 529 | | result := sub(subject, subjectStart) 530 | | break 531 | | } 532 | | } 533 | | subject := add(subject, 1) 534 | | if iszero(lt(subject, end)) { break } 535 | | } 536 | | break 537 | | } 538 | | for {} 1 {} { 539 | | if iszero(shr(m, xor(mload(subject), s))) { 540 | | result := sub(subject, subjectStart) 541 | | break 542 | | } 543 | | subject := add(subject, 1) 544 | | if iszero(lt(subject, end)) { break } 545 | | } 546 | | break 547 | | } 548 | | } 549 | | } 550 | | 551 | | /// @dev Returns the byte index of the first location of `search` in `subject`, 552 | | /// searching from left to right. 553 | | /// Returns `NOT_FOUND` (i.e. `type(uint256).max`) if the `search` is not found. 554 | | function indexOf(string memory subject, string memory search) 555 | | internal 556 | | pure 557 | | returns (uint256 result) 558 | | { 559 | | result = indexOf(subject, search, 0); 560 | | } 561 | | 562 | | /// @dev Returns the byte index of the first location of `search` in `subject`, 563 | | /// searching from right to left, starting from `from`. 564 | | /// Returns `NOT_FOUND` (i.e. `type(uint256).max`) if the `search` is not found. 565 | | function lastIndexOf(string memory subject, string memory search, uint256 from) 566 | | internal 567 | | pure 568 | | returns (uint256 result) 569 | | { 570 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 571 | | assembly { 572 | | for {} 1 {} { 573 | | result := not(0) // Initialize to `NOT_FOUND`. 574 | | let searchLength := mload(search) 575 | | if gt(searchLength, mload(subject)) { break } 576 | | let w := result 577 | | 578 | | let fromMax := sub(mload(subject), searchLength) 579 | | if iszero(gt(fromMax, from)) { from := fromMax } 580 | | 581 | | let end := add(add(subject, 0x20), w) 582 | | subject := add(add(subject, 0x20), from) 583 | | if iszero(gt(subject, end)) { break } 584 | | // As this function is not too often used, 585 | | // we shall simply use keccak256 for smaller bytecode size. 586 | | for { let h := keccak256(add(search, 0x20), searchLength) } 1 {} { 587 | | if eq(keccak256(subject, searchLength), h) { 588 | | result := sub(subject, add(end, 1)) 589 | | break 590 | | } 591 | | subject := add(subject, w) // `sub(subject, 1)`. 592 | | if iszero(gt(subject, end)) { break } 593 | | } 594 | | break 595 | | } 596 | | } 597 | | } 598 | | 599 | | /// @dev Returns the byte index of the first location of `search` in `subject`, 600 | | /// searching from right to left. 601 | | /// Returns `NOT_FOUND` (i.e. `type(uint256).max`) if the `search` is not found. 602 | | function lastIndexOf(string memory subject, string memory search) 603 | | internal 604 | | pure 605 | | returns (uint256 result) 606 | | { 607 | | result = lastIndexOf(subject, search, uint256(int256(-1))); 608 | | } 609 | | 610 | | /// @dev Returns true if `search` is found in `subject`, false otherwise. 611 | | function contains(string memory subject, string memory search) internal pure returns (bool) { 612 | | return indexOf(subject, search) != NOT_FOUND; 613 | | } 614 | | 615 | | /// @dev Returns whether `subject` starts with `search`. 616 | | function startsWith(string memory subject, string memory search) 617 | | internal 618 | | pure 619 | | returns (bool result) 620 | | { 621 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 622 | | assembly { 623 | | let searchLength := mload(search) 624 | | // Just using keccak256 directly is actually cheaper. 625 | | // forgefmt: disable-next-item 626 | | result := and( 627 | | iszero(gt(searchLength, mload(subject))), 628 | | eq( 629 | | keccak256(add(subject, 0x20), searchLength), 630 | | keccak256(add(search, 0x20), searchLength) 631 | | ) 632 | | ) 633 | | } 634 | | } 635 | | 636 | | /// @dev Returns whether `subject` ends with `search`. 637 | | function endsWith(string memory subject, string memory search) 638 | | internal 639 | | pure 640 | | returns (bool result) 641 | | { 642 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 643 | | assembly { 644 | | let searchLength := mload(search) 645 | | let subjectLength := mload(subject) 646 | | // Whether `search` is not longer than `subject`. 647 | | let withinRange := iszero(gt(searchLength, subjectLength)) 648 | | // Just using keccak256 directly is actually cheaper. 649 | | // forgefmt: disable-next-item 650 | | result := and( 651 | | withinRange, 652 | | eq( 653 | | keccak256( 654 | | // `subject + 0x20 + max(subjectLength - searchLength, 0)`. 655 | | add(add(subject, 0x20), mul(withinRange, sub(subjectLength, searchLength))), 656 | | searchLength 657 | | ), 658 | | keccak256(add(search, 0x20), searchLength) 659 | | ) 660 | | ) 661 | | } 662 | | } 663 | | 664 | | /// @dev Returns `subject` repeated `times`. 665 | | function repeat(string memory subject, uint256 times) 666 | | internal 667 | | pure 668 | | returns (string memory result) 669 | | { 670 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 671 | | assembly { 672 | | let subjectLength := mload(subject) 673 | | if iszero(or(iszero(times), iszero(subjectLength))) { 674 | | subject := add(subject, 0x20) 675 | | result := mload(0x40) 676 | | let output := add(result, 0x20) 677 | | for {} 1 {} { 678 | | // Copy the `subject` one word at a time. 679 | | for { let o := 0 } 1 {} { 680 | | mstore(add(output, o), mload(add(subject, o))) 681 | | o := add(o, 0x20) 682 | | if iszero(lt(o, subjectLength)) { break } 683 | | } 684 | | output := add(output, subjectLength) 685 | | times := sub(times, 1) 686 | | if iszero(times) { break } 687 | | } 688 | | mstore(output, 0) // Zeroize the slot after the string. 689 | | let resultLength := sub(output, add(result, 0x20)) 690 | | mstore(result, resultLength) // Store the length. 691 | | // Allocate the memory. 692 | | mstore(0x40, add(result, add(resultLength, 0x20))) 693 | | } 694 | | } 695 | | } 696 | | 697 | | /// @dev Returns a copy of `subject` sliced from `start` to `end` (exclusive). 698 | | /// `start` and `end` are byte offsets. 699 | | function slice(string memory subject, uint256 start, uint256 end) 700 | | internal 701 | | pure 702 | | returns (string memory result) 703 | | { 704 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 705 | | assembly { 706 | | let subjectLength := mload(subject) 707 | | if iszero(gt(subjectLength, end)) { end := subjectLength } 708 | | if iszero(gt(subjectLength, start)) { start := subjectLength } 709 | | if lt(start, end) { 710 | | result := mload(0x40) 711 | | let resultLength := sub(end, start) 712 | | mstore(result, resultLength) 713 | | subject := add(subject, start) 714 | | let w := not(0x1f) 715 | | // Copy the `subject` one word at a time, backwards. 716 | | for { let o := and(add(resultLength, 0x1f), w) } 1 {} { 717 | | mstore(add(result, o), mload(add(subject, o))) 718 | | o := add(o, w) // `sub(o, 0x20)`. 719 | | if iszero(o) { break } 720 | | } 721 | | // Zeroize the slot after the string. 722 | | mstore(add(add(result, 0x20), resultLength), 0) 723 | | // Allocate memory for the length and the bytes, 724 | | // rounded up to a multiple of 32. 725 | | mstore(0x40, add(result, and(add(resultLength, 0x3f), w))) 726 | | } 727 | | } 728 | | } 729 | | 730 | | /// @dev Returns a copy of `subject` sliced from `start` to the end of the string. 731 | | /// `start` is a byte offset. 732 | | function slice(string memory subject, uint256 start) 733 | | internal 734 | | pure 735 | | returns (string memory result) 736 | | { 737 | | result = slice(subject, start, uint256(int256(-1))); 738 | | } 739 | | 740 | | /// @dev Returns all the indices of `search` in `subject`. 741 | | /// The indices are byte offsets. 742 | | function indicesOf(string memory subject, string memory search) 743 | | internal 744 | | pure 745 | | returns (uint256[] memory result) 746 | | { 747 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 748 | | assembly { 749 | | let subjectLength := mload(subject) 750 | | let searchLength := mload(search) 751 | | 752 | | if iszero(gt(searchLength, subjectLength)) { 753 | | subject := add(subject, 0x20) 754 | | search := add(search, 0x20) 755 | | result := add(mload(0x40), 0x20) 756 | | 757 | | let subjectStart := subject 758 | | let subjectSearchEnd := add(sub(add(subject, subjectLength), searchLength), 1) 759 | | let h := 0 760 | | if iszero(lt(searchLength, 0x20)) { h := keccak256(search, searchLength) } 761 | | let m := shl(3, sub(0x20, and(searchLength, 0x1f))) 762 | | let s := mload(search) 763 | | for {} 1 {} { 764 | | let t := mload(subject) 765 | | // Whether the first `searchLength % 32` bytes of 766 | | // `subject` and `search` matches. 767 | | if iszero(shr(m, xor(t, s))) { 768 | | if h { 769 | | if iszero(eq(keccak256(subject, searchLength), h)) { 770 | | subject := add(subject, 1) 771 | | if iszero(lt(subject, subjectSearchEnd)) { break } 772 | | continue 773 | | } 774 | | } 775 | | // Append to `result`. 776 | | mstore(result, sub(subject, subjectStart)) 777 | | result := add(result, 0x20) 778 | | // Advance `subject` by `searchLength`. 779 | | subject := add(subject, searchLength) 780 | | if searchLength { 781 | | if iszero(lt(subject, subjectSearchEnd)) { break } 782 | | continue 783 | | } 784 | | } 785 | | subject := add(subject, 1) 786 | | if iszero(lt(subject, subjectSearchEnd)) { break } 787 | | } 788 | | let resultEnd := result 789 | | // Assign `result` to the free memory pointer. 790 | | result := mload(0x40) 791 | | // Store the length of `result`. 792 | | mstore(result, shr(5, sub(resultEnd, add(result, 0x20)))) 793 | | // Allocate memory for result. 794 | | // We allocate one more word, so this array can be recycled for {split}. 795 | | mstore(0x40, add(resultEnd, 0x20)) 796 | | } 797 | | } 798 | | } 799 | | 800 | | /// @dev Returns a arrays of strings based on the `delimiter` inside of the `subject` string. 801 | | function split(string memory subject, string memory delimiter) 802 | | internal 803 | | pure 804 | | returns (string[] memory result) 805 | | { 806 | | uint256[] memory indices = indicesOf(subject, delimiter); 807 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 808 | | assembly { 809 | | let w := not(0x1f) 810 | | let indexPtr := add(indices, 0x20) 811 | | let indicesEnd := add(indexPtr, shl(5, add(mload(indices), 1))) 812 | | mstore(add(indicesEnd, w), mload(subject)) 813 | | mstore(indices, add(mload(indices), 1)) 814 | | let prevIndex := 0 815 | | for {} 1 {} { 816 | | let index := mload(indexPtr) 817 | | mstore(indexPtr, 0x60) 818 | | if iszero(eq(index, prevIndex)) { 819 | | let element := mload(0x40) 820 | | let elementLength := sub(index, prevIndex) 821 | | mstore(element, elementLength) 822 | | // Copy the `subject` one word at a time, backwards. 823 | | for { let o := and(add(elementLength, 0x1f), w) } 1 {} { 824 | | mstore(add(element, o), mload(add(add(subject, prevIndex), o))) 825 | | o := add(o, w) // `sub(o, 0x20)`. 826 | | if iszero(o) { break } 827 | | } 828 | | // Zeroize the slot after the string. 829 | | mstore(add(add(element, 0x20), elementLength), 0) 830 | | // Allocate memory for the length and the bytes, 831 | | // rounded up to a multiple of 32. 832 | | mstore(0x40, add(element, and(add(elementLength, 0x3f), w))) 833 | | // Store the `element` into the array. 834 | | mstore(indexPtr, element) 835 | | } 836 | | prevIndex := add(index, mload(delimiter)) 837 | | indexPtr := add(indexPtr, 0x20) 838 | | if iszero(lt(indexPtr, indicesEnd)) { break } 839 | | } 840 | | result := indices 841 | | if iszero(mload(delimiter)) { 842 | | result := add(indices, 0x20) 843 | | mstore(result, sub(mload(indices), 2)) 844 | | } 845 | | } 846 | | } 847 | | 848 | | /// @dev Returns a concatenated string of `a` and `b`. 849 | | /// Cheaper than `string.concat()` and does not de-align the free memory pointer. 850 | | function concat(string memory a, string memory b) 851 | | internal 852 | | pure 853 | | returns (string memory result) 854 | | { 855 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 856 | | assembly { 857 | | let w := not(0x1f) 858 | | result := mload(0x40) 859 | | let aLength := mload(a) 860 | | // Copy `a` one word at a time, backwards. 861 | | for { let o := and(add(aLength, 0x20), w) } 1 {} { 862 | | mstore(add(result, o), mload(add(a, o))) 863 | | o := add(o, w) // `sub(o, 0x20)`. 864 | | if iszero(o) { break } 865 | | } 866 | | let bLength := mload(b) 867 | | let output := add(result, aLength) 868 | | // Copy `b` one word at a time, backwards. 869 | | for { let o := and(add(bLength, 0x20), w) } 1 {} { 870 | | mstore(add(output, o), mload(add(b, o))) 871 | | o := add(o, w) // `sub(o, 0x20)`. 872 | | if iszero(o) { break } 873 | | } 874 | | let totalLength := add(aLength, bLength) 875 | | let last := add(add(result, 0x20), totalLength) 876 | | // Zeroize the slot after the string. 877 | | mstore(last, 0) 878 | | // Stores the length. 879 | | mstore(result, totalLength) 880 | | // Allocate memory for the length and the bytes, 881 | | // rounded up to a multiple of 32. 882 | | mstore(0x40, and(add(last, 0x1f), w)) 883 | | } 884 | | } 885 | | 886 | | /// @dev Returns a copy of the string in either lowercase or UPPERCASE. 887 | | /// WARNING! This function is only compatible with 7-bit ASCII strings. 888 | | function toCase(string memory subject, bool toUpper) 889 | | internal 890 | | pure 891 | | returns (string memory result) 892 | | { 893 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 894 | | assembly { 895 | | let length := mload(subject) 896 | | if length { 897 | | result := add(mload(0x40), 0x20) 898 | | subject := add(subject, 1) 899 | | let flags := shl(add(70, shl(5, toUpper)), 0x3ffffff) 900 | | let w := not(0) 901 | | for { let o := length } 1 {} { 902 | | o := add(o, w) 903 | | let b := and(0xff, mload(add(subject, o))) 904 | | mstore8(add(result, o), xor(b, and(shr(b, flags), 0x20))) 905 | | if iszero(o) { break } 906 | | } 907 | | result := mload(0x40) 908 | | mstore(result, length) // Store the length. 909 | | let last := add(add(result, 0x20), length) 910 | | mstore(last, 0) // Zeroize the slot after the string. 911 | | mstore(0x40, add(last, 0x20)) // Allocate the memory. 912 | | } 913 | | } 914 | | } 915 | | 916 | | /// @dev Returns a string from a small bytes32 string. 917 | | /// `s` must be null-terminated, or behavior will be undefined. 918 | | function fromSmallString(bytes32 s) internal pure returns (string memory result) { 919 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 920 | | assembly { 921 | | result := mload(0x40) 922 | | let n := 0 923 | | for {} byte(n, s) { n := add(n, 1) } {} // Scan for '\0'. 924 | | mstore(result, n) 925 | | let o := add(result, 0x20) 926 | | mstore(o, s) 927 | | mstore(add(o, n), 0) 928 | | mstore(0x40, add(result, 0x40)) 929 | | } 930 | | } 931 | | 932 | | /// @dev Returns the small string, with all bytes after the first null byte zeroized. 933 | | function normalizeSmallString(bytes32 s) internal pure returns (bytes32 result) { 934 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 935 | | assembly { 936 | | for {} byte(result, s) { result := add(result, 1) } {} // Scan for '\0'. 937 | | mstore(0x00, s) 938 | | mstore(result, 0x00) 939 | | result := mload(0x00) 940 | | } 941 | | } 942 | | 943 | | /// @dev Returns the string as a normalized null-terminated small string. 944 | | function toSmallString(string memory s) internal pure returns (bytes32 result) { 945 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 946 | | assembly { 947 | | result := mload(s) 948 | | if iszero(lt(result, 33)) { 949 | | mstore(0x00, 0xec92f9a3) // `TooBigForSmallString()`. 950 | | revert(0x1c, 0x04) 951 | | } 952 | | result := shl(shl(3, sub(32, result)), mload(add(s, result))) 953 | | } 954 | | } 955 | | 956 | | /// @dev Returns a lowercased copy of the string. 957 | | /// WARNING! This function is only compatible with 7-bit ASCII strings. 958 | | function lower(string memory subject) internal pure returns (string memory result) { 959 | | result = toCase(subject, false); 960 | | } 961 | | 962 | | /// @dev Returns an UPPERCASED copy of the string. 963 | | /// WARNING! This function is only compatible with 7-bit ASCII strings. 964 | | function upper(string memory subject) internal pure returns (string memory result) { 965 | | result = toCase(subject, true); 966 | | } 967 | | 968 | | /// @dev Escapes the string to be used within HTML tags. 969 | | function escapeHTML(string memory s) internal pure returns (string memory result) { 970 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 971 | | assembly { 972 | | let end := add(s, mload(s)) 973 | | result := add(mload(0x40), 0x20) 974 | | // Store the bytes of the packed offsets and strides into the scratch space. 975 | | // `packed = (stride << 5) | offset`. Max offset is 20. Max stride is 6. 976 | | mstore(0x1f, 0x900094) 977 | | mstore(0x08, 0xc0000000a6ab) 978 | | // Store "&quot;&amp;&#39;&lt;&gt;" into the scratch space. 979 | | mstore(0x00, shl(64, 0x2671756f743b26616d703b262333393b266c743b2667743b)) 980 | | for {} iszero(eq(s, end)) {} { 981 | | s := add(s, 1) 982 | | let c := and(mload(s), 0xff) 983 | | // Not in `["\"","'","&","<",">"]`. 984 | | if iszero(and(shl(c, 1), 0x500000c400000000)) { 985 | | mstore8(result, c) 986 | | result := add(result, 1) 987 | | continue 988 | | } 989 | | let t := shr(248, mload(c)) 990 | | mstore(result, mload(and(t, 0x1f))) 991 | | result := add(result, shr(5, t)) 992 | | } 993 | | let last := result 994 | | mstore(last, 0) // Zeroize the slot after the string. 995 | | result := mload(0x40) 996 | | mstore(result, sub(last, add(result, 0x20))) // Store the length. 997 | | mstore(0x40, add(last, 0x20)) // Allocate the memory. 998 | | } 999 | | } 1000 | | 1001 | | /// @dev Escapes the string to be used within double-quotes in a JSON. 1002 | | /// If `addDoubleQuotes` is true, the result will be enclosed in double-quotes. 1003 | | function escapeJSON(string memory s, bool addDoubleQuotes) 1004 | | internal 1005 | | pure 1006 | | returns (string memory result) 1007 | | { 1008 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 1009 | | assembly { 1010 | | let end := add(s, mload(s)) 1011 | | result := add(mload(0x40), 0x20) 1012 | | if addDoubleQuotes { 1013 | | mstore8(result, 34) 1014 | | result := add(1, result) 1015 | | } 1016 | | // Store "\\u0000" in scratch space. 1017 | | // Store "0123456789abcdef" in scratch space. 1018 | | // Also, store `{0x08:"b", 0x09:"t", 0x0a:"n", 0x0c:"f", 0x0d:"r"}`. 1019 | | // into the scratch space. 1020 | | mstore(0x15, 0x5c75303030303031323334353637383961626364656662746e006672) 1021 | | // Bitmask for detecting `["\"","\\"]`. 1022 | | let e := or(shl(0x22, 1), shl(0x5c, 1)) 1023 | | for {} iszero(eq(s, end)) {} { 1024 | | s := add(s, 1) 1025 | | let c := and(mload(s), 0xff) 1026 | | if iszero(lt(c, 0x20)) { 1027 | | if iszero(and(shl(c, 1), e)) { 1028 | | // Not in `["\"","\\"]`. 1029 | | mstore8(result, c) 1030 | | result := add(result, 1) 1031 | | continue 1032 | | } 1033 | | mstore8(result, 0x5c) // "\\". 1034 | | mstore8(add(result, 1), c) 1035 | | result := add(result, 2) 1036 | | continue 1037 | | } 1038 | | if iszero(and(shl(c, 1), 0x3700)) { 1039 | | // Not in `["\b","\t","\n","\f","\d"]`. 1040 | | mstore8(0x1d, mload(shr(4, c))) // Hex value. 1041 | | mstore8(0x1e, mload(and(c, 15))) // Hex value. 1042 | | mstore(result, mload(0x19)) // "\\u00XX". 1043 | | result := add(result, 6) 1044 | | continue 1045 | | } 1046 | | mstore8(result, 0x5c) // "\\". 1047 | | mstore8(add(result, 1), mload(add(c, 8))) 1048 | | result := add(result, 2) 1049 | | } 1050 | | if addDoubleQuotes { 1051 | | mstore8(result, 34) 1052 | | result := add(1, result) 1053 | | } 1054 | | let last := result 1055 | | mstore(last, 0) // Zeroize the slot after the string. 1056 | | result := mload(0x40) 1057 | | mstore(result, sub(last, add(result, 0x20))) // Store the length. 1058 | | mstore(0x40, add(last, 0x20)) // Allocate the memory. 1059 | | } 1060 | | } 1061 | | 1062 | | /// @dev Escapes the string to be used within double-quotes in a JSON. 1063 | | function escapeJSON(string memory s) internal pure returns (string memory result) { 1064 | | result = escapeJSON(s, false); 1065 | | } 1066 | | 1067 | | /// @dev Returns whether `a` equals `b`. 1068 | | function eq(string memory a, string memory b) internal pure returns (bool result) { 1069 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 1070 | | assembly { 1071 | | result := eq(keccak256(add(a, 0x20), mload(a)), keccak256(add(b, 0x20), mload(b))) 1072 | | } 1073 | | } 1074 | | 1075 | | /// @dev Returns whether `a` equals `b`, where `b` is a null-terminated small string. 1076 | | function eqs(string memory a, bytes32 b) internal pure returns (bool result) { 1077 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 1078 | | assembly { 1079 | | // These should be evaluated on compile time, as far as possible. 1080 | | let m := not(shl(7, div(not(iszero(b)), 255))) // `0x7f7f ...`. 1081 | | let x := not(or(m, or(b, add(m, and(b, m))))) 1082 | | let r := shl(7, iszero(iszero(shr(128, x)))) 1083 | | r := or(r, shl(6, iszero(iszero(shr(64, shr(r, x)))))) 1084 | | r := or(r, shl(5, lt(0xffffffff, shr(r, x)))) 1085 | | r := or(r, shl(4, lt(0xffff, shr(r, x)))) 1086 | | r := or(r, shl(3, lt(0xff, shr(r, x)))) 1087 | | // forgefmt: disable-next-item 1088 | | result := gt(eq(mload(a), add(iszero(x), xor(31, shr(3, r)))), 1089 | | xor(shr(add(8, r), b), shr(add(8, r), mload(add(a, 0x20))))) 1090 | | } 1091 | | } 1092 | | 1093 | | /// @dev Packs a single string with its length into a single word. 1094 | | /// Returns `bytes32(0)` if the length is zero or greater than 31. 1095 | | function packOne(string memory a) internal pure returns (bytes32 result) { 1096 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 1097 | | assembly { 1098 | | // We don't need to zero right pad the string, 1099 | | // since this is our own custom non-standard packing scheme. 1100 | | result := 1101 | | mul( 1102 | | // Load the length and the bytes. 1103 | | mload(add(a, 0x1f)), 1104 | | // `length != 0 && length < 32`. Abuses underflow. 1105 | | // Assumes that the length is valid and within the block gas limit. 1106 | | lt(sub(mload(a), 1), 0x1f) 1107 | | ) 1108 | | } 1109 | | } 1110 | | 1111 | | /// @dev Unpacks a string packed using {packOne}. 1112 | | /// Returns the empty string if `packed` is `bytes32(0)`. 1113 | | /// If `packed` is not an output of {packOne}, the output behavior is undefined. 1114 | | function unpackOne(bytes32 packed) internal pure returns (string memory result) { 1115 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 1116 | | assembly { 1117 | | // Grab the free memory pointer. 1118 | | result := mload(0x40) 1119 | | // Allocate 2 words (1 for the length, 1 for the bytes). 1120 | | mstore(0x40, add(result, 0x40)) 1121 | | // Zeroize the length slot. 1122 | | mstore(result, 0) 1123 | | // Store the length and bytes. 1124 | | mstore(add(result, 0x1f), packed) 1125 | | // Right pad with zeroes. 1126 | | mstore(add(add(result, 0x20), mload(result)), 0) 1127 | | } 1128 | | } 1129 | | 1130 | | /// @dev Packs two strings with their lengths into a single word. 1131 | | /// Returns `bytes32(0)` if combined length is zero or greater than 30. 1132 | | function packTwo(string memory a, string memory b) internal pure returns (bytes32 result) { 1133 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 1134 | | assembly { 1135 | | let aLength := mload(a) 1136 | | // We don't need to zero right pad the strings, 1137 | | // since this is our own custom non-standard packing scheme. 1138 | | result := 1139 | | mul( 1140 | | // Load the length and the bytes of `a` and `b`. 1141 | | or( 1142 | | shl(shl(3, sub(0x1f, aLength)), mload(add(a, aLength))), 1143 | | mload(sub(add(b, 0x1e), aLength)) 1144 | | ), 1145 | | // `totalLength != 0 && totalLength < 31`. Abuses underflow. 1146 | | // Assumes that the lengths are valid and within the block gas limit. 1147 | | lt(sub(add(aLength, mload(b)), 1), 0x1e) 1148 | | ) 1149 | | } 1150 | | } 1151 | | 1152 | | /// @dev Unpacks strings packed using {packTwo}. 1153 | | /// Returns the empty strings if `packed` is `bytes32(0)`. 1154 | | /// If `packed` is not an output of {packTwo}, the output behavior is undefined. 1155 | | function unpackTwo(bytes32 packed) 1156 | | internal 1157 | | pure 1158 | | returns (string memory resultA, string memory resultB) 1159 | | { 1160 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 1161 | | assembly { 1162 | | // Grab the free memory pointer. 1163 | | resultA := mload(0x40) 1164 | | resultB := add(resultA, 0x40) 1165 | | // Allocate 2 words for each string (1 for the length, 1 for the byte). Total 4 words. 1166 | | mstore(0x40, add(resultB, 0x40)) 1167 | | // Zeroize the length slots. 1168 | | mstore(resultA, 0) 1169 | | mstore(resultB, 0) 1170 | | // Store the lengths and bytes. 1171 | | mstore(add(resultA, 0x1f), packed) 1172 | | mstore(add(resultB, 0x1f), mload(add(add(resultA, 0x20), mload(resultA)))) 1173 | | // Right pad with zeroes. 1174 | | mstore(add(add(resultA, 0x20), mload(resultA)), 0) 1175 | | mstore(add(add(resultB, 0x20), mload(resultB)), 0) 1176 | | } 1177 | | } 1178 | | 1179 | | /// @dev Directly returns `a` without copying. 1180 | | function directReturn(string memory a) internal pure { 1181 | | assembly { 1182 | | // Assumes that the string does not start from the scratch space. 1183 | | let retStart := sub(a, 0x20) 1184 | | let retUnpaddedSize := add(mload(a), 0x40) 1185 | | // Right pad with zeroes. Just in case the string is produced 1186 | | // by a method that doesn't zero right pad. 1187 | | mstore(add(retStart, retUnpaddedSize), 0) 1188 | | // Store the return offset. 1189 | | mstore(retStart, 0x20) 1190 | | // End the transaction, returning the string. 1191 | | return(retStart, and(not(0x1f), add(0x1f, retUnpaddedSize))) 1192 | | } 1193 | | } 1194 | | } 1195 | |
/home/xshadow/panoptic-v1-core-private/lib/solady/src/utils/LibZip.sol 1 | | // SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT 2 | | pragma solidity ^0.8.4; 3 | | 4 | | /// @notice Library for compressing and decompressing bytes. 5 | | /// @author Solady (https://github.com/vectorized/solady/blob/main/src/utils/LibZip.sol) 6 | | /// @author Calldata compression by clabby (https://github.com/clabby/op-kompressor) 7 | | /// @author FastLZ by ariya (https://github.com/ariya/FastLZ) 8 | | /// 9 | | /// @dev Note: 10 | | /// The accompanying solady.js library includes implementations of 11 | | /// FastLZ and calldata operations for convenience. 12 | | library LibZip { 13 | | /*´:°•.°+.*•´.*:˚.°*.˚•´.°:°•.°•.*•´.*:˚.°*.˚•´.°:°•.°+.*•´.*:*/ 14 | | /* FAST LZ OPERATIONS */ 15 | | /*.•°:°.´+˚.*°.˚:*.´•*.+°.•°:´*.´•*.•°.•°:°.´:•˚°.*°.˚:*.´+°.•*/ 16 | | 17 | | // LZ77 implementation based on FastLZ. 18 | | // Equivalent to level 1 compression and decompression at the following commit: 19 | | // https://github.com/ariya/FastLZ/commit/344eb4025f9ae866ebf7a2ec48850f7113a97a42 20 | | // Decompression is backwards compatible. 21 | | 22 | | /// @dev Returns the compressed `data`. 23 | | function flzCompress(bytes memory data) internal pure returns (bytes memory result) { 24 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 25 | | assembly { 26 | | function ms8(d_, v_) -> _d { 27 | | mstore8(d_, v_) 28 | | _d := add(d_, 1) 29 | | } 30 | | function u24(p_) -> _u { 31 | | _u := mload(p_) 32 | | _u := or(shl(16, byte(2, _u)), or(shl(8, byte(1, _u)), byte(0, _u))) 33 | | } 34 | | function cmp(p_, q_, e_) -> _l { 35 | | for { e_ := sub(e_, q_) } lt(_l, e_) { _l := add(_l, 1) } { 36 | | e_ := mul(iszero(byte(0, xor(mload(add(p_, _l)), mload(add(q_, _l))))), e_) 37 | | } 38 | | } 39 | | function literals(runs_, src_, dest_) -> _o { 40 | | for { _o := dest_ } iszero(lt(runs_, 0x20)) { runs_ := sub(runs_, 0x20) } { 41 | | mstore(ms8(_o, 31), mload(src_)) 42 | | _o := add(_o, 0x21) 43 | | src_ := add(src_, 0x20) 44 | | } 45 | | if iszero(runs_) { leave } 46 | | mstore(ms8(_o, sub(runs_, 1)), mload(src_)) 47 | | _o := add(1, add(_o, runs_)) 48 | | } 49 | | function mt(l_, d_, o_) -> _o { 50 | | for { d_ := sub(d_, 1) } iszero(lt(l_, 263)) { l_ := sub(l_, 262) } { 51 | | o_ := ms8(ms8(ms8(o_, add(224, shr(8, d_))), 253), and(0xff, d_)) 52 | | } 53 | | if iszero(lt(l_, 7)) { 54 | | _o := ms8(ms8(ms8(o_, add(224, shr(8, d_))), sub(l_, 7)), and(0xff, d_)) 55 | | leave 56 | | } 57 | | _o := ms8(ms8(o_, add(shl(5, l_), shr(8, d_))), and(0xff, d_)) 58 | | } 59 | | function setHash(i_, v_) { 60 | | let p_ := add(mload(0x40), shl(2, i_)) 61 | | mstore(p_, xor(mload(p_), shl(224, xor(shr(224, mload(p_)), v_)))) 62 | | } 63 | | function getHash(i_) -> _h { 64 | | _h := shr(224, mload(add(mload(0x40), shl(2, i_)))) 65 | | } 66 | | function hash(v_) -> _r { 67 | | _r := and(shr(19, mul(2654435769, v_)), 0x1fff) 68 | | } 69 | | function setNextHash(ip_, ipStart_) -> _ip { 70 | | setHash(hash(u24(ip_)), sub(ip_, ipStart_)) 71 | | _ip := add(ip_, 1) 72 | | } 73 | | result := mload(0x40) 74 | | codecopy(result, codesize(), 0x8000) // Zeroize the hashmap. 75 | | let op := add(result, 0x8000) 76 | | let a := add(data, 0x20) 77 | | let ipStart := a 78 | | let ipLimit := sub(add(ipStart, mload(data)), 13) 79 | | for { let ip := add(2, a) } lt(ip, ipLimit) {} { 80 | | let r := 0 81 | | let d := 0 82 | | for {} 1 {} { 83 | | let s := u24(ip) 84 | | let h := hash(s) 85 | | r := add(ipStart, getHash(h)) 86 | | setHash(h, sub(ip, ipStart)) 87 | | d := sub(ip, r) 88 | | if iszero(lt(ip, ipLimit)) { break } 89 | | ip := add(ip, 1) 90 | | if iszero(gt(d, 0x1fff)) { if eq(s, u24(r)) { break } } 91 | | } 92 | | if iszero(lt(ip, ipLimit)) { break } 93 | | ip := sub(ip, 1) 94 | | if gt(ip, a) { op := literals(sub(ip, a), a, op) } 95 | | let l := cmp(add(r, 3), add(ip, 3), add(ipLimit, 9)) 96 | | op := mt(l, d, op) 97 | | ip := setNextHash(setNextHash(add(ip, l), ipStart), ipStart) 98 | | a := ip 99 | | } 100 | | // Copy the result to compact the memory, overwriting the hashmap. 101 | | let end := sub(literals(sub(add(ipStart, mload(data)), a), a, op), 0x7fe0) 102 | | let o := add(result, 0x20) 103 | | mstore(result, sub(end, o)) // Store the length. 104 | | for {} iszero(gt(o, end)) { o := add(o, 0x20) } { mstore(o, mload(add(o, 0x7fe0))) } 105 | | mstore(end, 0) // Zeroize the slot after the string. 106 | | mstore(0x40, add(end, 0x20)) // Allocate the memory. 107 | | } 108 | | } 109 | | 110 | | /// @dev Returns the decompressed `data`. 111 | | function flzDecompress(bytes memory data) internal pure returns (bytes memory result) { 112 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 113 | | assembly { 114 | | result := mload(0x40) 115 | | let op := add(result, 0x20) 116 | | let end := add(add(data, 0x20), mload(data)) 117 | | for { data := add(data, 0x20) } lt(data, end) {} { 118 | | let w := mload(data) 119 | | let c := byte(0, w) 120 | | let t := shr(5, c) 121 | | if iszero(t) { 122 | | mstore(op, mload(add(data, 1))) 123 | | data := add(data, add(2, c)) 124 | | op := add(op, add(1, c)) 125 | | continue 126 | | } 127 | | for { 128 | | let g := eq(t, 7) 129 | | let l := add(2, xor(t, mul(g, xor(t, add(7, byte(1, w)))))) // M 130 | | let s := add(add(shl(8, and(0x1f, c)), byte(add(1, g), w)), 1) // R 131 | | let r := sub(op, s) 132 | | let f := xor(s, mul(gt(s, 0x20), xor(s, 0x20))) 133 | | let j := 0 134 | | } 1 {} { 135 | | mstore(add(op, j), mload(add(r, j))) 136 | | j := add(j, f) 137 | | if lt(j, l) { continue } 138 | | data := add(data, add(2, g)) 139 | | op := add(op, l) 140 | | break 141 | | } 142 | | } 143 | | mstore(result, sub(op, add(result, 0x20))) // Store the length. 144 | | mstore(op, 0) // Zeroize the slot after the string. 145 | | mstore(0x40, add(op, 0x20)) // Allocate the memory. 146 | | } 147 | | } 148 | | 149 | | /*´:°•.°+.*•´.*:˚.°*.˚•´.°:°•.°•.*•´.*:˚.°*.˚•´.°:°•.°+.*•´.*:*/ 150 | | /* CALLDATA OPERATIONS */ 151 | | /*.•°:°.´+˚.*°.˚:*.´•*.+°.•°:´*.´•*.•°.•°:°.´:•˚°.*°.˚:*.´+°.•*/ 152 | | 153 | | // Calldata compression and decompression using selective run length encoding: 154 | | // - Sequences of 0x00 (up to 128 consecutive). 155 | | // - Sequences of 0xff (up to 32 consecutive). 156 | | // 157 | | // A run length encoded block consists of two bytes: 158 | | // (0) 0x00 159 | | // (1) A control byte with the following bit layout: 160 | | // - [7] `0: 0x00, 1: 0xff`. 161 | | // - [0..6] `runLength - 1`. 162 | | // 163 | | // The first 4 bytes are bitwise negated so that the compressed calldata 164 | | // can be dispatched into the `fallback` and `receive` functions. 165 | | 166 | | /// @dev Returns the compressed `data`. 167 | | function cdCompress(bytes memory data) internal pure returns (bytes memory result) { 168 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 169 | | assembly { 170 | | function rle(v_, o_, d_) -> _o, _d { 171 | | mstore(o_, shl(240, or(and(0xff, add(d_, 0xff)), and(0x80, v_)))) 172 | | _o := add(o_, 2) 173 | | } 174 | | result := mload(0x40) 175 | | let o := add(result, 0x20) 176 | | let z := 0 // Number of consecutive 0x00. 177 | | let y := 0 // Number of consecutive 0xff. 178 | | for { let end := add(data, mload(data)) } iszero(eq(data, end)) {} { 179 | | data := add(data, 1) 180 | | let c := byte(31, mload(data)) 181 | | if iszero(c) { 182 | | if y { o, y := rle(0xff, o, y) } 183 | | z := add(z, 1) 184 | | if eq(z, 0x80) { o, z := rle(0x00, o, 0x80) } 185 | | continue 186 | | } 187 | | if eq(c, 0xff) { 188 | | if z { o, z := rle(0x00, o, z) } 189 | | y := add(y, 1) 190 | | if eq(y, 0x20) { o, y := rle(0xff, o, 0x20) } 191 | | continue 192 | | } 193 | | if y { o, y := rle(0xff, o, y) } 194 | | if z { o, z := rle(0x00, o, z) } 195 | | mstore8(o, c) 196 | | o := add(o, 1) 197 | | } 198 | | if y { o, y := rle(0xff, o, y) } 199 | | if z { o, z := rle(0x00, o, z) } 200 | | // Bitwise negate the first 4 bytes. 201 | | mstore(add(result, 4), not(mload(add(result, 4)))) 202 | | mstore(result, sub(o, add(result, 0x20))) // Store the length. 203 | | mstore(o, 0) // Zeroize the slot after the string. 204 | | mstore(0x40, add(o, 0x20)) // Allocate the memory. 205 | | } 206 | | } 207 | | 208 | | /// @dev Returns the decompressed `data`. 209 | | function cdDecompress(bytes memory data) internal pure returns (bytes memory result) { 210 | | /// @solidity memory-safe-assembly 211 | | assembly { 212 | | if mload(data) { 213 | | result := mload(0x40) 214 | | let o := add(result, 0x20) 215 | | let s := add(data, 4) 216 | | let v := mload(s) 217 | | let end := add(data, mload(data)) 218 | | mstore(s, not(v)) // Bitwise negate the first 4 bytes. 219 | | for {} lt(data, end) {} { 220 | | data := add(data, 1) 221 | | let c := byte(31, mload(data)) 222 | | if iszero(c) { 223 | | data := add(data, 1) 224 | | let d := byte(31, mload(data)) 225 | | // Fill with either 0xff or 0x00. 226 | | mstore(o, not(0)) 227 | | if iszero(gt(d, 0x7f)) { codecopy(o, codesize(), add(d, 1)) } 228 | | o := add(o, add(and(d, 0x7f), 1)) 229 | | continue 230 | | } 231 | | mstore8(o, c) 232 | | o := add(o, 1) 233 | | } 234 | | mstore(s, v) // Restore the first 4 bytes. 235 | | mstore(result, sub(o, add(result, 0x20))) // Store the length. 236 | | mstore(o, 0) // Zeroize the slot after the string. 237 | | mstore(0x40, add(o, 0x20)) // Allocate the memory. 238 | | } 239 | | } 240 | | } 241 | | 242 | | /// @dev To be called in the `fallback` function. 243 | | /// ``` 244 | | /// fallback() external payable { LibZip.cdFallback(); } 245 | | /// receive() external payable {} // Silence compiler warning to add a `receive` function. 246 | | /// ``` 247 | | /// For efficiency, this function will directly return the results, terminating the context. 248 | | /// If called internally, it must be called at the end of the function. 249 | | function cdFallback() internal { 250 | | assembly { 251 | | if iszero(calldatasize()) { return(calldatasize(), calldatasize()) } 252 | | let o := 0 253 | | let f := not(3) // For negating the first 4 bytes. 254 | | for { let i := 0 } lt(i, calldatasize()) {} { 255 | | let c := byte(0, xor(add(i, f), calldataload(i))) 256 | | i := add(i, 1) 257 | | if iszero(c) { 258 | | let d := byte(0, xor(add(i, f), calldataload(i))) 259 | | i := add(i, 1) 260 | | // Fill with either 0xff or 0x00. 261 | | mstore(o, not(0)) 262 | | if iszero(gt(d, 0x7f)) { codecopy(o, codesize(), add(d, 1)) } 263 | | o := add(o, add(and(d, 0x7f), 1)) 264 | | continue 265 | | } 266 | | mstore8(o, c) 267 | | o := add(o, 1) 268 | | } 269 | | let success := delegatecall(gas(), address(), 0x00, o, codesize(), 0x00) 270 | |
